[F01] ENGINES OR PUMPS -- F01, under the Mechanical Engineering section, covers patents for engines and pumps, encompassing the design, operation, and auxiliary components of devices that convert energy into mechanical work or transport fluids. This class includes internal-combustion, steam, electric, and hydraulic engines as well as a wide range of pump types and their control systems.
[F01B] MACHINES OR ENGINES, IN GENERAL OR OF POSITIVE-DISPLACEMENT TYPE, e.g. STEAM ENGINES -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines or engines, in general or of positive-displacement type.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Rotary-piston or oscillating-piston machines or engines -> F01C - Non-positive-displacement machines or engines, e.g. steam turbines -> F01D - Internal-combustion engines -> F02B - Combustion-product engine plants -> F02G - Machines or engines, other than of positive-displacement type, for liquids -> F03B - Positive-displacement engines driven by liquids -> F03C - Wind motors -> F03D - Positive-displacement machines for liquids -> F04B - Rotary-piston, or oscillating-piston, positive-displacement machines for liquids -> F04C  **Glossary:** - engine: a device for continuously converting fluid energy into mechanical power, thus, this term includes, for example, steam piston engines or steam turbines, per se, or internal-combustion piston engines, but it excludes single-stroke devices. - machine: a device which could equally be an engine and a pump, and not a device which is restricted to an engine or one which is restricted to a pump. - pump: a device for continuously raising, forcing, compressing, or exhausting fluid by mechanical or other means. Thus, this term includes fans or blowers. - positive displacement: the way the energy of a working fluid is transformed into mechanical energy, in which variations of volume created by the working fluid in a working chamber produce equivalent displacements of the mechanical member transmitting the energy, the dynamic effect of the fluid being of minor importance, and vice versa. - non-positive displacement: the way the energy of a working fluid is transformed into mechanical energy, by transformation of the energy of the working fluid into kinetic energy, and vice versa. - oscillating-piston machine: a positive-displacement machine in which a fluid-engaging work-transmitting member oscillates. This definition applies also to engines and pumps. - rotary-piston machine: a positive-displacement machine in which a fluid-engaging work-transmitting member rotates about a fixed axis or about an axis moving along a circular or similar orbit. This definition applies also to engines and pumps. - rotary piston: the work-transmitting member of a rotary-piston machine and may be of any suitable form, e.g., like a toothed gear. - free piston: a piston of which the length of stroke is not defined by any member driven thereby. - cylinders: positive-displacement working chambers in general. Thus, this term is not restricted to cylinders of circular cross-section. - main shaft: the shaft which converts reciprocating piston motion into rotary motion or vice versa. - plant: an engine together with such additional apparatus as is necessary to run the engine. For example, a steam engine plant includes a steam engine and means for generating the steam. - working fluid: the driven fluid in a pump or the driving fluid in an engine. The working fluid can be in a compressible, gaseous state, called elastic fluid, e.g. steam; in a liquid state; or in a state where there is coexistence of an elastic fluid and liquid phase.
[F01B 1/00] Reciprocating-piston machines or engines characterised by number or relative disposition of cylinders or by being built-up from separate cylinder-crankcase elements -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Reciprocating-piston machines or engines with cylinder axes coaxial with, or parallel or inclined to, main shaft -> F01B3/00 - Reciprocating-piston machines or engines with cylinder axes arranged substantially tangentially to a circle centred on main shaft axis -> F01B5/00
[F01B 1/01] with one single cylinder -- This subclass covers reciprocating-piston engines that contain a single cylinder, including both steam and internal-combustion types. It addresses the design, construction and operation of machines built around one cylinder-crankcase unit.
[F01B 1/02] with cylinders all in one line -- F01B 1/02 classifies reciprocating-piston machines or engines whose cylinders are arranged in a single straight line, covering designs where the cylinder-crankcase elements are aligned linearly rather than in staggered or radial configurations. This sub-group falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and general positive-displacement machines such as steam engines.
[F01B 1/04] with cylinders in V-arrangement -- F01B 1/04 covers reciprocating-piston engines whose cylinders are arranged in a V configuration, meaning two banks of cylinders are set at an angle to each other and share a common crankcase. This classification distinguishes V-type layouts from inline or radial cylinder arrangements.
[F01B 1/06] with cylinders in star or fan arrangement -- F01B 1/06 covers reciprocating piston engines or machines where the cylinders are arranged in a star or fan configuration, meaning the cylinders are positioned radially outward from a common crankcase like spokes of a wheel. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, machines or engines in general or of positive-displacement type.
[F01B 1/0603] the connection of the pistons with an element being at the outer ends of the cylinders -- The F01B 1/0603 entry covers reciprocating-piston engines or machines where the pistons are joined to a component located at the outer ends of the cylinders, such as a crosshead, rod or bearing. It applies to designs that define the piston-to-outer-end connection, detailing arrangements of cylinders, crankcases or related elements that affect the motion transmission and sealing at the cylinder ends. This classification falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and general positive-displacement machines.
[F01B 1/0606] with cam-actuated distribution member(s) -- F01B 1/0606 classifies reciprocating-piston engines or machines distinguished by the number or arrangement of their cylinders, where the intake or exhaust distribution is driven by cam-actuated valves or ports. It falls under Mechanical Engineering → Engines or Pumps → General or positive-displacement machines such as steam engines.
[F01B 1/061] with two or more series radial piston-cylinder units -- The subclass F01B 1/061 covers reciprocating-piston engines that have two or more series of radially arranged piston-cylinder units, meaning the cylinders are set in multiple concentric rows around the crankshaft. It applies to positive-displacement machines such as multi-row radial aircraft engines, where each series forms a distinct ring of cylinders.
[F01B 1/0613] directly located side by side -- F01B 1/0613 covers reciprocating-piston machines whose cylinders are positioned directly side by side, without intervening spacing or separate crankcase elements, so that the pistons share a common bank. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and general positive-displacement machines such as steam engines.
[F01B 1/0617] coupling of several cylinders-barrels -- F01B 1/0617 covers reciprocating-piston engines in which two or more cylinders (or barrels) are mechanically linked or combined, defining the overall arrangement, number and relative disposition of the cylinders within a single engine assembly. It is used for classifying designs where separate cylinder-crankcase units are united to form a coupled cylinder group.
[F01B 1/062] the connection of the pistons with an actuating or actuated element being at the inner ends of the cylinders -- This subclass concerns reciprocating-piston machines or engines whose pistons are linked to an actuating or actuated element at the inner ends of the cylinders, such as inner-end crankshaft connections or similar mechanisms housed within the cylinder block. It is part of the hierarchy for mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and general or positive-displacement machines.
[F01B 1/0624] with cam-actuated distribution member(s) -- A CPC subclass for reciprocating-piston engines that use cam-actuated distribution members, such as valves driven by cam shafts. It falls under mechanical engineering → engines or pumps → general positive-displacement machines, and specifies the arrangement or number of cylinders built from separate cylinder-crankcase elements.
[F01B 1/0627] each machine piston being provided with channels, which are coacting with the cylinder and are used as a distribution member for another piston-cylinder unit -- This subclass covers reciprocating piston engines in which each piston incorporates one or more channels that align with the cylinder wall and serve to convey fluid or pressure to a second piston-cylinder assembly, effectively acting as a distribution member for that unit. It is used for engines where pistons are built up from separate cylinder-crankcase elements.
[F01B 1/0631] the piston-driving or -driven cam being provided with an inlet or an outlet -- F01B 1/0631 relates to reciprocating-piston engines or machines whose piston-driving or piston-driven cam incorporates an inlet or an outlet, typically a cam with a port that allows fluid to enter or leave the cam mechanism. It falls within the CPC group for mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and positive-displacement machines such as steam engines.
[F01B 1/0634] with two or more series radial piston-cylinder units -- The classification refers to reciprocating-piston engines that use two or more series of radial piston-cylinder units, meaning the cylinders are arranged in multiple rows radiating from the crankshaft rather than a single circle. This arrangement is typical of multi-row radial engines used in aviation and some industrial applications.
[F01B 1/0637] directly located side by side -- This subclass concerns reciprocating-piston engines whose cylinders are arranged directly side by side, sharing a common wall or positioned immediately adjacent to each other, as opposed to staggered or spaced configurations. It falls under the broader category of positive-displacement engines characterized by cylinder number or relative disposition.
[F01B 1/0641] Details, component parts specially adapted for such machines -- This subclass covers the detailed designs and specially adapted component parts of reciprocating-piston machines or engines that are distinguished by the number or arrangement of cylinders or by being assembled from separate cylinder-crankcase elements, such as liners, pistons, valves, bearings and related accessories.
[F01B 1/0644] Pistons -- F01B 1/0644 is the CPC subclass that covers pistons used in reciprocating-piston machines or engines, addressing their design, arrangement and construction within positive-displacement types such as steam and internal-combustion engines. The entry sits under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and machines or engines in general.
[F01B 1/0648] Cams -- Cams for reciprocating-piston machines or engines, where the cam element is used to control valve timing, fuel-injection timing or other periodic actions, are classified under F01B 1/0648. This sub-group covers cams distinguished by the number of lobes, shape, relative placement or modular construction within the cylinder-crankcase assembly.
[F01B 1/0651] consisting of several cylindrical elements, e.g. rollers -- F01B 1/0651 covers reciprocating-piston machines in which the cylinder arrangement consists of several separate cylindrical elements, such as rollers, defining the number and relative disposition of cylinders in positive-displacement engines.
[F01B 1/0655] cylinders -- This CPC subclass pertains to cylinders in reciprocating-piston machines, defined by the number, relative disposition, or modular construction of separate cylinder-crankcase elements. It falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, general positive-displacement machines such as steam engines. The code F01B 1/0655 classifies inventions relating to the cylinder configuration of such engines.
[F01B 1/0658] Arrangements for pressing or connecting the pistons against the actuating or actuated cam -- F01B 1/0658 covers arrangements that press or connect pistons to an actuating or actuated cam in reciprocating-piston engines, including devices such as pins, links, levers or other mechanisms that ensure the pistons follow the cam's motion.
[F01B 1/0662] hydraulically -- F01B 1/0662 covers reciprocating-piston engines or machines in which the cylinders are arranged or connected by hydraulic means, such as hydraulic coupling of separate cylinder-crankcase units or hydraulic synchronization of multiple cylinders.
[F01B 1/0665] Disconnecting the pistons from the actuating or actuated cam -- The F01B 1/0665 subclass covers designs in reciprocating-piston engines where the pistons are mechanically separated from the cam that drives or is driven by them. It includes mechanisms that allow the piston to be disengaged from the cam for timing adjustments, maintenance, or alternative operation modes.
[F01B 1/0668] Supporting and guiding means for the piston -- The entry F01B 1/0668 covers devices that support and guide the piston in reciprocating-piston engines, such as pins, bearings, guide bushes or other elements that keep the piston aligned and control its motion within the cylinder.
[F01B 1/0672] Draining of the machine housing; arrangements dealing with leakage fluid -- This subclass deals with means for removing fluid that has entered a machine housing, including drain openings, passages, collection chambers or other arrangements that handle oil, water or other leakage fluids, and the associated methods for safely disposing of or recycling the drained fluid.
[F01B 1/0675] Controlling -- This CPC subclass covers control systems applied to reciprocating-piston engines whose design is defined by cylinder count, arrangement or modular cylinder-crankcase construction. It includes devices that regulate speed, power output, fuel supply or valve timing in steam, internal-combustion or other positive-displacement engines.
[F01B 1/0679] by using a valve in a system with several pump or motor chambers, wherein the flow path through the chambers can be changed, e.g. series-parallel -- F01B 1/0679 covers reciprocating-piston engines or pumps that use a valve to connect several cylinder or motor chambers, allowing the fluid flow to be rearranged in series, parallel or mixed configurations. The classification applies to positive-displacement machines where the cylinder arrangement is modified by valve-controlled flow paths.
[F01B 1/0682] by changing the effective cross sectional piston working surface -- F01B 1/0682 designates reciprocating-piston engines that achieve performance changes by varying the effective cross-sectional area of the piston's working surface, for example through adjustable cylinder liners, piston skirts or variable-geometry chambers. The subclass falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and covers machines built from separate cylinder-crankcase elements where cylinder number or arrangement is a defining feature.
[F01B 1/0686] by changing the effective piston stroke -- This subclass pertains to reciprocating-piston engines whose power or speed is adjusted by varying the effective stroke of the pistons, for example through mechanisms that change crank radius, alter connecting-rod geometry or modify piston travel length during operation.
[F01B 1/0689] by changing the excentricity of one element relative to another element -- A reciprocating-piston engine in which the operating characteristics are modified by varying the offset between two components, such as changing the eccentricity of a crankshaft relative to a cylinder or other element, thereby altering stroke, timing or force transmission within the engine.
[F01B 1/0693] by changing the phase relationship between two actuating or actuated cams -- A reciprocating-piston engine or machine whose operation is adjusted by varying the phase relationship between two actuating or actuated cams, altering timing of valve or piston movement for performance or control purposes. This falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and specifically machines or engines of positive-displacement type.
[F01B 1/0696] by changing the phase relationship between the actuating or actuated cam and the distributing means -- This subgroup deals with reciprocating piston engines in which the timing between the cam that drives or is driven and the valve-distribution system is varied by adjusting their phase relationship. Such arrangements are used to modify valve opening and closing events for performance or efficiency, for example variable cam-phasing or adjustable timing mechanisms.
[F01B 1/08] with cylinders arranged oppositely relative to main shaft and of "flat" type -- F01B 1/08 covers reciprocating-piston engines in which the cylinders are positioned opposite one another on either side of the main shaft, forming a flat, opposed-cylinder layout. This arrangement is typical of flat or boxer engines where each piston's motion mirrors that of its counterpart across the crankcase.
[F01B 1/10] with more than one main shaft, e.g. coupled to common output shaft -- F01B 1/10 covers reciprocating-piston engines that employ two or more main crankshafts, which may be linked to a shared output shaft. This arrangement is used to balance forces, increase power-transmission flexibility, or allow larger engines to be constructed from multiple crankcase sections.
[F01B 11/00] Reciprocating-piston machines or engines without rotary main shaft, e.g. of free-piston type -- F01B 11/00 covers reciprocating-piston machines or engines that operate without a rotary main shaft, such as free-piston engines, and includes their specific structural and functional features within the broader class of general positive-displacement engines.
[F01B 11/001] in which the movement in the two directions is obtained by one double acting piston motor -- F01B 11/001 covers reciprocating-piston machines or engines that use a single double-acting piston to produce motion in both forward and reverse directions. The class applies to positive-displacement engines such as steam or free-piston types where the two-way movement is generated by one piston acting alternately on each side.
[F01B 11/002] one side of the double acting piston motor being always under the influence of the fluid under pressure -- This CPC subclass concerns double-acting piston motors in which one side of the piston is permanently exposed to pressurised fluid while the other side receives no fluid pressure, so the motor is driven continuously by a single pressure source.
[F01B 11/003] the fluid under pressure being continuously delivered to one motor chamber and reacting the other chamber through a valve located in the piston, to bring the piston back in its start-position -- This subclass covers reciprocating-piston machines in which pressurised fluid is continuously supplied to one cylinder chamber while a valve in the piston directs fluid to the opposite chamber, driving the piston back to its start position. It applies to free-piston or similar engines that lack a rotary main shaft.
[F01B 11/004] in which the movement in the two directions is obtained by two single acting piston motors, each acting in one direction -- The subclass F01B 11/004 concerns reciprocating-piston machines that generate motion in both directions by employing two single-acting piston motors, each delivering power on one stroke only. It applies to positive-displacement engines without a rotary crankshaft, such as free-piston or linear-motor designs.
[F01B 11/006] one single acting piston motor being always under the influence of the fluid under pressure -- F01B 11/006 covers single-acting piston motors in which the piston is continually acted on by a pressurised fluid on one side, without a counter-acting force from a rotary shaft. These devices belong to the class of reciprocating-piston machines such as free-piston engines, where the working fluid provides both power and the constant pressure that drives the piston's motion.
[F01B 11/007] in which the movement in only one direction is obtained by a single acting piston motor, e.g. with actuation in the other direction by spring means -- F01B 11/007 covers reciprocating-piston machines in which the piston is driven in only one direction by a single-acting motor, while the opposite stroke is achieved by a spring or other passive means. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and positive-displacement machines such as steam engines.
[F01B 11/008] with actuation in the other direction by gravity
[F01B 11/009] in which the movement in two directions is obtained by two or more double acting piston motors -- F01B 11/009 classifies reciprocating-piston machines that generate motion in opposite directions by employing two or more double-acting piston motors, a design used in free-piston engines or similar devices that produce linear movement without a rotating main shaft. This falls under mechanical engineering → engines or pumps → general or positive-displacement machines.
[F01B 11/02] Equalising or cushioning devices -- F01B 11/02 covers devices that equalise pressure fluctuations or provide cushioning in reciprocating-piston machines that operate without a rotary main shaft, such as free-piston engines. The devices mitigate shock loads on pistons and supporting structures, improving smooth operation and prolonging component life. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and general positive-displacement machines.
[F01B 11/04] Engines combined with reciprocatory driven devices, e.g. hammers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:
[F01B 11/06] for generating vibration only
[F01B 11/08] with direct fluid transmission link -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:
[F01B 1/12] Separate cylinder-crankcase elements coupled together to form a unit
[F01B 13/00] Reciprocating-piston machines or engines with rotating cylinders in order to obtain the reciprocating-piston motion -- F01B 13/00 covers reciprocating-piston machines or engines that achieve piston motion by rotating the cylinder, a design used in certain steam or internal-combustion engines. The subclass falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and general positive-displacement machines.
[F01B 13/02] with one cylinder only -- F01B 13/02 covers reciprocating-piston machines that achieve piston motion by rotating a single cylinder, i.e., single-cylinder rotary engines or pumps. It applies to any positive-displacement engine or pump where the cylinder itself turns to create the reciprocating action, such as a one-cylinder steam or hydraulic rotary engine.
[F01B 13/04] with more than one cylinder -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:
[F01B 13/045] with cylinder axes arranged substantially tangentially to a circle centred on main shaft axis -- This CPC subclass covers reciprocating-piston machines in which the cylinder axes are placed substantially tangentially to an imaginary circle centred on the main shaft axis, a layout typical of radial-type engines. The arrangement enables the rotating cylinder block to drive pistons with a cyclical reciprocating motion while the shaft remains stationary.
[F01B 13/06] in star arrangement -- F01B 13/06 covers reciprocating-piston engines or machines where the cylinders are mounted on a rotating star-shaped carrier, allowing the pistons to move in a reciprocating fashion while the whole assembly rotates. The star arrangement is used to achieve balanced forces and compact layout in positive-displacement engines.
[F01B 13/061] the connection of the pistons with the actuated or actuating element being at the outer ends of the cylinders -- F01B 13/061 concerns reciprocating-piston machines in which the pistons are linked to the actuating or driven element at the outer ends of the cylinders, allowing the piston motion to be transmitted to a crank, gear or similar component via an external connection.
[F01B 13/062] cylinder block and actuating or actuated cam both rotating -- F01B 13/062 covers a cylinder block in which the block itself and the cam that controls piston motion are mounted to rotate together, typically found in reciprocating-piston engines that achieve piston movement by rotating a set of cylinders rather than by a stationary block. This arrangement synchronizes cam actuation with block rotation for compact, high-speed power units.
[F01B 13/063] with two or more series radial piston-cylinder units -- The CPC subclass F01B 13/063 covers reciprocating-piston machines that use two or more series of radial piston-cylinder units, meaning engines in which multiple rows of cylinders are arranged radially around a common crankshaft, typical of multi-row radial engines. It lies within the hierarchy of mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and general or positive-displacement machines.
[F01B 13/064] cylinder block and actuating or actuated cam both rotating  F01B13/066  takes precedence -- F01B 13/064 classifies cylinder blocks that incorporate a cam which rotates to actuate or is itself actuated, a design used in reciprocating-piston engines such as steam or internal-combustion types. It falls under Mechanical Engineering → Engines or Pumps → General Positive-Displacement Machines, and is superseded by the narrower F01B 13/066.
[F01B 13/065] directly located side by side -- F01B 13/065 classifies reciprocating-piston machines whose cylinders are positioned directly side by side, rather than staggered or offset, to achieve the piston motion. It applies to positive-displacement engines such as steam or internal-combustion types where the cylinder array is aligned laterally.
[F01B 13/066] cylinder block and actuating or actuated cam both rotating -- This subclass covers reciprocating-piston engines in which the cylinder block itself rotates together with an actuating cam that drives the valve train. The rotating cam is integral to the block or attached so that both parts turn as a unit, providing synchronized valve timing for the engine.
[F01B 13/067] with pistons and cylinders having two different parallel axis of rotation -- A reciprocating-piston engine in which the pistons and their cylinders rotate about two distinct, parallel axes rather than a single common axis. This arrangement allows the pistons to follow a reciprocating motion while the entire cylinder assembly turns on a separate, parallel shaft, offering alternative balance and power-transmission characteristics within positive-displacement engines.
[F01B 13/068] the connection of the pistons with an actuated or actuating element being at the inner ends of the cylinders -- This classification covers arrangements where pistons are linked to an actuation element located at the inner ends of the cylinder, such as rods, pins or levers that transmit motion to the piston from within the cylinder. It applies to reciprocating-piston machines with rotating cylinders, including steam and other positive-displacement engines.
[F01B 15/00] Reciprocating-piston machines or engines with movable cylinders other than provided for in group -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Reciprocating-piston machines or engines with movable cylinders other than provided for in group F01B13/00 , e.g. reciprocating-piston machines or engines having reciprocating cylinders or pivoting and oscillating cylinders.
[F01B 15/002] having cylinders in star or fan arrangement, the connection of the pistons with the actuated or actuating element being at the outer ends of the cylinders -- F01B 15/002 covers reciprocating-piston machines whose cylinders are positioned in a star- or fan-shaped layout, with each piston linked to the crank or other actuating element at the outer end of its cylinder rather than centrally. This arrangement is used in certain positive-displacement engines.
[F01B 15/005] having cylinders in star or fan arrangement, the connection of the pistons with the actuated or actuating element being at the inner ends of the cylinders -- F01B 15/005 covers reciprocating-piston machines whose cylinders are positioned in a star or fan layout, with the pistons connected to the crank or other actuating element at the inner ends of the cylinders rather than at the outer ends. This arrangement is used to achieve compactness or specific force transmission characteristics in engines and pumps. It falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and general positive-displacement machines.
[F01B 15/007] having spinning cylinders, i.e. the cylinders rotating about their longitudinal axis -- F01B 15/007 covers positive-displacement piston engines whose cylinders rotate around their longitudinal axis while the pistons move, a configuration found in certain rotary or spinning-cylinder engines. The class is part of the mechanical-engineering subgroup for general machines or engines of the positive-displacement type.
[F01B 15/02] with reciprocating cylinders -- The subclass F01B 15/02 covers machines or engines that employ reciprocating cylinders for power generation where the cylinder arrangement is not covered by other groups. It includes positive-displacement engines such as steam, gas or hydraulic types with movable cylinders that fall outside the more specific categories.
[F01B 15/04] with oscillating cylinder -- F01B 15/04 classifies reciprocating-piston engines in which at least one cylinder is mounted so that it oscillates about a pivot during operation. The oscillating cylinder replaces the usual fixed cylinder and crank mechanisms to produce reciprocating motion, as found in certain steam or internal-combustion engine designs.
[F01B 15/06] Control of working-fluid admission or discharge peculiar thereto -- F01B 15/06 covers the control systems that regulate admission or discharge of the working fluid in reciprocating-piston engines and machines, such as steam, gas or hydraulic engines. It includes specialized valve arrangements, timing devices, governors and other mechanisms that manage intake, exhaust or injection uniquely for these machines. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering for positive-displacement engines.
[F01B 15/065] by cam-actuated distribution members -- The subclass F01B 15/065 covers reciprocating-piston engines in which the intake or exhaust distribution system is operated by cam-actuated members such as valves, ports or distributors. It applies to engines where the cam mechanism directly controls the timing and opening of these flow passages.
[F01B 17/00] Reciprocating-piston machines or engines characterised by use of uniflow principle -- **Definition:** This place covers: Reciprocating-piston machines or engines characterised by use of uniflow principle: E.g. compressed gas engines where the working fluid enter a working cylinder, expands and then exits the cylinder.  **Glossary:** In this place, the following terms or expressions are used with the meaning indicated: In patent document the word "uniflow" is often used with the meaning "open circuit where gas enters cylinder, expands, and is then released"
[F01B 17/02] Engines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Compressed gas engines.
[F01B 17/022] with fluid heating -- F01B 17/022 covers reciprocating-piston engines that operate on the uniflow principle and incorporate a system for heating the working fluid, such as pre-heating intake air or fuel to improve combustion efficiency. The classification applies to steam, internal-combustion or other positive-displacement engines where fluid heating is integral to engine operation.
[F01B 17/025] using liquid air -- This CPC subclass covers reciprocating-piston engines that employ the uniflow principle, wherein fresh working fluid enters at one end and exhaust leaves at the opposite end, and specifically those that use liquid air as the power-producing medium.
[F01B 17/027] using separators -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Separators for separating liquid or oil from compressed gas for gas engines.
[F01B 17/04] Steam engines -- The CPC subclass F01B 17/04 covers reciprocating-piston steam engines that employ the uniflow principle, in which steam enters at one end of the cylinder and exhausts at the opposite end, flowing in a single direction. It includes designs and valve arrangements that use this flow pattern to improve efficiency in positive-displacement steam machines.
[F01B 19/00] Positive-displacement machines or engines of flexible-wall type -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Positive-displacement machines or engines of flexible-wall type. E.g. where the piston is made of a diaphragm or bellows.
[F01B 19/02] with plate-like flexible members -- The CPC subclass F01B 19/02 pertains to positive-displacement machines or engines that employ plate-like flexible members to create the moving chamber, such as certain hydraulic or pneumatic pumps and engines of flexible-wall type. It is nested under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and positive-displacement machines of flexible-wall construction.
[F01B 19/04] with tubular flexible members -- F01B 19/04 classifies positive-displacement machines or engines of the flexible-wall type that employ tubular flexible members as the moving chambers. The tubes expand and contract to draw in and discharge fluid, a principle used in peristaltic pumps, flexible-tube engines and similar devices. This subclass groups all such designs within the broader category of mechanical engines and pumps.
[F01B 2003/0097] Z-shafts, i.e. driven or driving shafts in Z-form -- F01B 2003/0097 covers Z-shafts, which are shafts shaped in a Z profile used as either driven or driving components in machinery. The classification pertains to mechanical power-transmission elements where the Z-form provides specific alignment or load-bearing advantages.
[F01B 2009/045] Planetary gearings -- Planetary gearings consist of a central sun gear, multiple planet gears mounted on a carrier, and an outer ring gear, providing high-torque density and variable speed ratios in a compact layout, commonly used in automotive transmissions, wind turbines, and industrial machinery.
[F01B 2009/061] by cams -- F01B 2009/061 classifies pumps that are driven or regulated by cam mechanisms, such as reciprocating or rotary pumps where cams convert rotational motion into the pumping action. It covers designs in which the cam profile controls valve timing, displacement or flow rate, and includes cam-actuated piston, diaphragm or gear-type pump arrangements.
[F01B 2009/063] Mono-lobe cams -- F01B/2009/063 covers mono-lobe cams, which are single-lobed cam elements used to convert rotary motion into a reciprocating action in pump, compressor or hydraulic mechanisms. The entry includes the design, geometry, manufacturing methods and functional use of such cams for timing and controlling valve or piston strokes.
[F01B 2009/065] Bi-lobe cams -- The CPC entry F01B 2009/065 covers the design, construction and use of bi-lobe cams that have two lobed profiles to convert rotary motion into reciprocating motion, typically for valve timing and other motion-control applications in reciprocating engines and related machines.
[F01B 2009/066] Tri-lobe cams -- Tri-lobe cams are cam profiles with three equally spaced lobes that convert continuous rotary motion into intermittent or variable axial motion, often used for timing valve gear in internal-combustion engines, compressors, and indexing mechanisms. They fall under CPC class F01B for devices that transform motion in reciprocating pumps and similar machinery.
[F01B 2009/068] Quadri-lobe cams -- Quadri-lobe cams are cam mechanisms that feature four lobes on a single camshaft to convert rotary motion into precisely timed reciprocating motion of engine valves or other actuators, enabling control of valve opening, lift and duration in multi-valve or multi-cylinder internal-combustion engines. The CPC entry covers the design, geometry, manufacturing and functional aspects of such four-lobed cam profiles.
[F01B 2011/005] with oscillating pistons, i.e. the pistons are arranged in ring like cylinder sections and oscillate with respect to the center of the ring -- In the CPC class F01B, code 2011/005 refers to reciprocating internal-combustion engines whose pistons are mounted in a circular arrangement around a central point and move back-and-forth (oscillate) relative to that centre, rather than in a traditional linear cylinder layout. This subgroup covers designs where the pistons form ring-like cylinder sections that oscillate about the ring's centre.
[F01B 21/00] Combinations of two or more machines or engines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Combinations of two or more machines or engines, e.g. a combined internal combustion engine and steam engine.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Adaptations of machines or engines for special use; Combinations of engines with devices driven thereby -> F01B23/00
[F01B 21/02] the machines or engines being all of reciprocating-piston type -- F01B 21/02 covers arrangements that combine two or more machines or engines, all of which operate on the reciprocating-piston principle. It applies to any configuration where multiple piston-type engines are linked or integrated to work together, such as combined-drive systems or hybrid power units.
[F01B 21/04] the machines or engines being not all of reciprocating-piston type, e.g. of reciprocating steam engine with steam turbine -- F01B 21/04 covers machines or engines that are not purely reciprocating-piston type, such as arrangements where a reciprocating steam engine is coupled with a steam turbine or other non-reciprocating power units. The classification is used for hybrid power plants that combine piston engines with turbines or similar devices.
[F01B 2170/00] Steam engines, e.g. for locomotives or ships -- F01B 2170/00 refers to steam engines, including those employed in locomotives or ships, classified under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and general positive-displacement machines.
[F01B 2170/04] To-be-deleted with administrative transfer to parent group -- F01B 2170/04 was a subclass under mechanical engineering for steam engines such as those used in locomotives or ships, nested within engines or pumps, machines or engines of positive-displacement type. The entry has been withdrawn and its subject matter transferred administratively to its parent group.
[F01B 2170/0405] To-be-deleted with administrative transfer to parent group -- F01B 2170/0405 is a CPC subgroup under mechanical engineering → engines or pumps → machines or engines, in general or of positive-displacement type, specifically steam engines such as those used in locomotives or ships. The entry is marked for deletion, with its subject matter administratively transferred to the parent group.
[F01B 2170/0411] for locomotives -- The CPC subclass F01B 2170/0411 covers steam-engine arrangements specifically designed for locomotive applications, including railway and marine locomotives that use positive-displacement steam power. It pertains to the mechanical engineering field of engines or pumps, focusing on general or positive-displacement machines such as steam engines adapted for propulsion of vehicles.
[F01B 2170/0417] for locomobiles driven by small motors -- The CPC subclass F01B 2170/0417 covers steam-engine locomotives, such as railway or marine engines, that are propelled by auxiliary small motors rather than directly by the main steam cylinder. It pertains to positive-displacement machines where a compact motor assists the primary engine in moving the vehicle.
[F01B 2170/0423] Single acting steam engines with 1, 2 or 3 cylinders -- The entry covers single-acting steam engines that use one, two or three cylinders, where pressure acts on only one side of each piston to produce motion. These engines are typical of locomotive and ship propulsion systems and fall under the broader category of positive-displacement mechanical engines.
[F01B 2170/0429] Double acting high pressure machines -- The CPC subclass F01B 2170/0429 covers double-acting high-pressure machines, a type of steam engine in which pressure is applied to both sides of the piston during each cycle, providing power on both strokes. These machines are typically used in locomotives or ship propulsion and fall under the broader categories of mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and general or positive-displacement steam engines.
[F01B 2170/0435] Compound machines with double or plural expansion -- The CPC subclass F01B 2170/0435 covers compound steam machines that employ two or more successive expansion stages of the working fluid, such as double- or triple-expansion engines. It includes designs where steam is expanded in a series of cylinders of increasing size to improve efficiency, typically applied to locomotive or marine propulsion systems.
[F01B 2170/0441] Compound engines with monolytic pistons in same cylinder -- The CPC entry F01B 2170/0441 covers steam engines in which two or more stages of expansion are performed in a single cylinder using monolytic pistons that separate the working spaces. This design combines the high- and low-pressure phases in one cylinder, typical of compound steam engines for locomotives or ships, and aims to improve efficiency while reducing mechanical complexity.
[F01B 2170/0447] Machines with more than one piston in a cylinder and with counter moving pistons -- This CPC subclass covers steam or other positive-displacement engines that have multiple pistons operating in the same cylinder, arranged so they move in opposite directions to each other, providing balanced forces and improved efficiency. It applies to designs such as compound or opposed-piston engines used in locomotives or marine propulsion.
[F01B 2170/0452] Engines without connecting rods -- This CPC subclass covers engines that generate power without connecting rods, using mechanisms such as rotary pistons, wobble plates or other direct-drive arrangements that transfer force directly to the crankshaft. It is positioned under Mechanical Engineering - Engines or Pumps - Machines or Engines, General or Positive-Displacement Type - Steam Engines and is identified by the code F01B 2170/0452.
[F01B 2170/0458] Moving cylinders for steam engines, e.g. with telescopic cylinder arrangements -- The subclass F01B 2170/0458 concerns moving cylinders employed in steam-engine applications, such as telescopic-cylinder arrangements that allow the cylinder length to vary during operation, improving efficiency and adaptability in locomotives, ships and other steam-driven machinery.
[F01B 2170/0464] Oscillating cylinders for steam engines -- The CPC entry F01B 2170/0464 covers oscillating cylinders used in steam engines, where the cylinder pivots about a pin so that the piston rod moves in a fixed direction while the cylinder swings with the crank. This arrangement simplifies the connection between piston and crank and is employed in locomotive and marine steam engines.
[F01B 2170/047] General arrangements for steam engines -- The CPC subclass F01B 2170/047 pertains to the overall layout and integration of components in steam engines, such as the arrangement of boilers, cylinders, condensers and drive mechanisms within a single machine. It includes design choices that affect space utilization, accessibility and performance for locomotives, ships and other steam-powered installations.
[F01B 2170/0476] Components or parts for steam engines -- This CPC subclass covers components or parts used in steam engines, such as those for locomotives or ships, including any mechanical elements that form or support the engine's operation. It is nested under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, machines or engines in general, positive-displacement type, steam engines.
[F01B 2170/0482] with toroidal cylinder space -- This subclass covers steam engines, for example in locomotives or ships, that incorporate a toroidal cylinder space-a doughnut-shaped chamber within the cylinder designed to improve steam flow or expansion.
[F01B 2170/0488] To-be-deleted with administrative transfer to parent group -- The CPC subclass F01B 2170/0488 belongs to the mechanical-engineering section for steam engines used in locomotives or ships. It is marked for deletion, with its subject matter being administratively transferred to the broader parent group covering general steam-engine machines. This reflects a re-organisation of the classification hierarchy rather than a distinct technical concept.
[F01B 2170/0494] with fixed cylinder space -- The subclass F01B 2170/0494 covers steam engines, such as those used in locomotives or ships, whose cylinder space is of fixed volume and does not vary during operation. It includes designs where the displacement is set by a stationary cylinder geometry, distinguishing them from engines with adjustable or variable cylinder spaces.
[F01B 2250/00] Accessories of steam engines -- F01B 2250/00 covers accessories of steam engines, including condensers, superheaters, feed-water pumps and other auxiliary devices that support engine operation. It belongs to the CPC hierarchy for mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and specifically to machines or engines in general or of positive-displacement type such as steam engines.
[F01B 2250/001] Valves for steam inlet or outlet -- The CPC subclass F01B 2250/001 concerns valves that regulate the admission or exhaust of steam in steam-engine accessories. It includes inlet and outlet valve designs used to control steam flow in positive-displacement machines such as steam engines. This falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and general steam-engine accessories.
[F01B 2250/002] Valves, brakes, control or safety devices for steam engines -- F01B 2250/002 covers the valves, braking systems and control or safety devices installed on steam engines. It includes mechanisms that regulate steam admission, exhaust or pressure, as well as devices that stop or limit engine motion and protect against over-speed or over-pressure conditions. These components are considered accessories of steam engines within the mechanical engineering field.
[F01B 2250/003] Apparatus for control or receiver or condensor pressure -- An apparatus designed to regulate, receive, or reduce the pressure within a steam-engine system, such as pressure-control valves, condensate receivers, or condenser-pressure regulators. It is used to maintain optimal operating conditions and protect components from excess pressure in positive-displacement engines.
[F01B 2250/004] Devices for draining or idling of steam cylinders or for uncoupling piston and connecting rod -- F01B 2250/004 covers devices that drain or idle steam-cylinder pressure and mechanisms that disconnect the piston from the connecting rod, such as special valves, release gears or uncoupling links. These components are classified as accessories of steam engines within the broader mechanical-engineering hierarchy for engines and pumps.
[F01B 2250/005] Oil separators for steam engines -- F01B 2250/005 covers oil separation devices designed for steam engines, used to remove oil particles from steam and lubricating oil circuits to prevent fouling and improve efficiency. These accessories are part of the steam engine's auxiliary systems, ensuring cleaner steam flow and reduced wear.
[F01B 2250/006] Arrangement of or controlling of piston pumps or compressors without crank shaft -- This CPC subclass relates to designs and control methods for piston-type pumps or compressors that operate without a crankshaft, typically in steam-engine contexts. It includes alternative drive or timing arrangements that replace the conventional crank mechanism.
[F01B 2250/007] Condensing devices for steam engines -- The CPC class F01B 2250/007 covers condensing devices for steam engines, which recover exhaust steam by cooling it to a liquid state and creating a partial vacuum that improves the engine's thermodynamic efficiency. This subclass includes condensers, condensate collectors and related auxiliary components used in positive-displacement steam machinery. It falls under Mechanical Engineering → Engines or Pumps → Machines or Engines, in General or of Positive-Displacement Type.
[F01B 2250/008] Surface condensors for so far as they influence the functioning of the engine
[F01B 2250/009] Condenser pumps for steam engines -- This subclass covers pumps that are part of the condenser system of steam engines, used to move condensate back to the boiler or to a feedwater tank. The pumps are specifically designed for integration with steam engine condensers, handling low-pressure condensate and facilitating efficient heat recovery.
[F01B 23/00] Adaptations of machines or engines for special use -- F01B 23/00 covers modifications made to general-purpose engines or machines to enable them to perform specific tasks not addressed by the basic design. It includes special-purpose attachments, control system alterations, and structural changes that tailor the device for unique operating conditions or applications.
[F01B 23/02] Adaptations for driving vehicles, e.g. locomotives -- F01B 23/02 covers modifications to positive-displacement engines or machines that enable them to propel vehicles, especially railway locomotives. The subclass includes alterations of drive mechanisms, transmissions and supporting components designed for mobile rather than stationary operation.
[F01B 23/04] the vehicles being waterborne vessels -- F01B 23/04 covers the adaptation of machines or engines-especially positive-displacement types such as steam engines-for use in waterborne vessels. It addresses modifications that enable propulsion or other functions of ships, boats, and similar craft.
[F01B 23/06] Adaptations for driving, or combinations with, hand-held tools or the like -- The subclass concerns modifications to engines or pumps that enable them to power hand-held tools such as drills, sanders or similar devices, including integrated drive mechanisms or couplings. It also covers combinations where the engine is built into the tool or attached as a separate unit to supply rotary or reciprocating motion.
[F01B 23/08] Adaptations for driving, or combinations with, pumps -- F01B 23/08 covers technical modifications that enable a machine or engine to drive a pump, or that integrate a pump directly with the machine's power source. It includes arrangements such as gear drives, belt drives, direct coupling and combined unit designs that optimise power transmission to pumping equipment.
[F01B 23/10] Adaptations for driving, or combinations with, electric generators -- F01B 23/10 covers technical modifications that enable a mechanical engine or pump to drive an electric generator, or that combine the engine and generator into an integrated unit. It includes adaptations for coupling, power transmission and control systems that link the prime mover with the generator.
[F01B 23/12] Adaptations for driving rolling mills or other heavy reversing machinery -- F01B 23/12 covers modifications to engines or machines that allow them to power rolling mills or other heavy equipment requiring frequent reversal of motion. These adaptations typically involve gearing, clutch, or drive-control arrangements designed to handle high torque and changing direction.
[F01B 25/00] Regulating, controlling or safety means -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Controlling combustion engines -> F02D
[F01B 25/02] Regulating or controlling by varying working-fluid admission or exhaust, e.g. by varying pressure or quantity -- F01B 25/02 covers devices that regulate or control a machine's operation by changing the admission or exhaust of the working fluid, such as throttles, inlet and exhaust valves, or other means that vary fluid pressure or quantity to adjust speed or power.
[F01B 25/04] Sensing elements -- F01B 25/04 covers sensing elements used to regulate, control or provide safety for engines or pumps, especially positive-displacement machines. It includes devices that detect physical quantities such as temperature, pressure, flow or speed and generate signals for automatic or manual adjustment of operation.
[F01B 25/06] responsive to speed -- F01B 25/06 deals with speed-responsive regulating, controlling or safety devices for engines and pumps, such as governors, speed sensors and mechanisms that adjust fuel flow, valve timing or other operating parameters in proportion to the rotational speed to maintain a set speed or protect against overspeed.
[F01B 25/08] Final actuators -- F01B 25/08 refers to final actuators in mechanical engines or pumps, particularly those of the positive-displacement type such as steam engines. It covers devices that receive control signals and convert them into mechanical motion to adjust valves, throttles or other elements for regulation, control or safety purposes.
[F01B 25/10] Arrangements or adaptations of working-fluid admission or discharge valves -- The code F01B 25/10 covers the design and modification of inlet and outlet valves that admit or discharge the working fluid in engines and pumps, including their timing, positioning and actuation mechanisms. It addresses configurations that improve flow control, efficiency, safety and overall engine performance.
[F01B 25/12] Devices dealing with sensing elements or final actuators or transmitting means between them, e.g. power-assisted -- F01B 25/12 classifies devices that interface sensing elements with final actuators, including the transmission mechanisms that convey control signals or power, such as power-assisted systems for engines and pumps. These are used for regulation, control or safety functions in positive-displacement machines.
[F01B 25/14] peculiar to particular kinds of machines or engines -- F01B 25/14 covers control, regulation or safety devices that are uniquely designed for specific kinds of machines or engines, such as particular steam, internal-combustion or other positive-displacement engines. These features are tailored to the distinctive operating characteristics or construction of the individual engine type.
[F01B 25/16] Safety means responsive to specific conditions -- This subclass covers safety devices that monitor a particular operating condition of an engine, pump or similar machine and automatically intervene when a preset limit is exceeded. Examples include pressure-relief valves, temperature-triggered shut-offs and overspeed protectors that act only under the defined condition to prevent damage or hazardous operation.
[F01B 25/18] preventing rotation in wrong direction -- This CPC subclass concerns devices or methods that stop an engine or pump from turning in the opposite direction, such as reverse-run protection, lock-out clutches, or braking arrangements. It falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, general positive-displacement machines, regulating, controlling or safety means.
[F01B 25/20] Checking operation on safety devices -- The F01B 25/20 subclass covers techniques for verifying that safety devices on engines, pumps or other positive-displacement machines are functioning properly, including tests or inspections of interlocks, pressure-release, or other protective mechanisms. It is applied to methods that check operation, detect faults, and confirm compliance with safety requirements.
[F01B 25/22] Braking by redirecting working-fluid -- F01B 25/22 covers braking methods for engines or pumps that create resistance by diverting the working fluid, such as using valves, passages or ducts to redirect flow and produce a decelerating force, rather than applying mechanical friction or external brakes. This approach is used to control speed or stop motion in positive-displacement machines.
[F01B 25/24] thereby regenerating energy -- F01B 25/24 covers machines or engines that incorporate regenerative energy systems, wherein part of the output power is recovered and reused to improve overall efficiency. Typical implementations include waste-heat recovery, regenerative braking, or hydraulic/electrical storage that feeds back into the engine's operation.
[F01B 25/26] Warning devices -- F01B 25/26 covers warning devices used on steam or other positive-displacement engines to alert operators of unsafe conditions such as overpressure, overheating, or malfunction. These devices may include alarms, lights, whistles or other signals that indicate when the machine requires attention or must be stopped.
[F01B 27/00] Starting of machines or engines -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Starting combustion engines -> F02N
[F01B 27/02] of reciprocating-piston engines
[F01B 27/04] by directing working-fluid supply, e.g. by aid of by-pass steam conduits -- This CPC sub-group covers methods of starting machines or engines by directing the supply of the working fluid, for example through bypass steam conduits that feed the cylinder before normal operation. It includes arrangements where a temporary fluid path is used to spin the piston or rotor and bring the device up to speed before the main supply is engaged.
[F01B 27/06] specially for compound engines -- F01B 27/06 designates components or arrangements that are specifically adapted for compound engines, which utilise multiple stages of expansion to improve efficiency. The subclass falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and covers general-type positive-displacement machines such as steam engines.
[F01B 27/08] Means for moving crank off dead-centre -- F01B 27/08 covers devices that rotate a crank away from dead-centre to start a machine or engine. It includes mechanisms such as flywheels, spring-loaded levers, pneumatic or hydraulic jacks, and electric starters that supply the initial torque needed for engines or pumps that cannot begin moving under their own power.
[F01B 29/00] Machines or engines with pertinent characteristics other than those provided for in preceding main groups -- F01B 29/00 pertains to machines or engines that exhibit specific characteristics not addressed in the preceding main groups of positive-displacement engines, such as unconventional configurations, auxiliary mechanisms, or distinctive design features.
[F01B 29/02] Atmospheric engines, i.e. atmosphere acting against vacuum -- Atmospheric engines are power-producing machines that rely on atmospheric pressure acting against a vacuum created within a cylinder or chamber, causing a piston or other moving element to be driven by the pressure differential. The CPC subclass F01B 29/02 covers these types of engines and related mechanisms not otherwise classified in preceding groups.
[F01B 29/04] characterised by means for converting from one type to a different one -- F01B 29/04 covers machines or engines that incorporate means for converting one type of energy, fluid, or motion into another, such as devices that change steam into compressed air or transform rotary motion into reciprocating motion. This subgroup applies to general positive-displacement engines where the conversion function is not specified in more detailed groups.
[F01B 29/06] from steam engine into combustion engine
[F01B 29/08] Reciprocating-piston machines or engines not otherwise provided for -- F01B 29/08 covers reciprocating-piston machines or engines that are not otherwise classified elsewhere in the CPC, including piston-driven engines with unusual or hybrid configurations that do not fit the more specific main-group definitions.
[F01B 29/10] Engines -- F01B 29/10 covers engines, machines that transform energy into mechanical work, encompassing a broad range of types such as internal-combustion, steam, gas-turbine and other engine concepts not specifically singled out in narrower groups. It addresses general engine characteristics and functions rather than detailed designs of particular engine families.
[F01B 29/12] Steam engines -- The CPC subclass F01B 29/12 pertains to steam engines of the positive-displacement type, covering general constructions and operating principles of steam-driven machines that are not specifically addressed in other more detailed groups. It includes both reciprocating and rotary steam engine configurations and related components used for converting steam power into mechanical work.
[F01B 3/00] Reciprocating-piston machines or engines with cylinder axes coaxial with, or parallel or inclined to, main shaft axis -- This subclass covers piston machines or engines whose cylinders are arranged coaxially with, parallel to, or at an angle to the main shaft, describing configurations where the cylinder axes are aligned, side-by-side, or inclined relative to the crankshaft in reciprocating-piston power units.
[F01B 3/0002] having stationary cylinders -- This CPC subgroup concerns reciprocating-piston machines whose cylinder axes are aligned with, parallel to, or inclined from the main shaft and where the cylinders remain fixed in place while the pistons move inside them. It applies to conventional steam, internal-combustion and similar engines with stationary cylinders.
[F01B 3/0005] having two or more sets of cylinders or pistons -- F01B 3/0005 classifies reciprocating-piston machines whose cylinders or pistons are arranged in two or more separate groups, such as twin-cylinder steam engines or multi-bank internal-combustion engines, where each set operates on a common shaft but is distinct from the others. This falls under Mechanical Engineering → Engines or Pumps → Machines or Engines, in general or of positive-displacement type.
[F01B 3/0008] having self-acting distribution members, e.g. actuated by working fluid -- F01B 3/0008 covers reciprocating-piston machines in which the inlet and exhaust distribution members operate automatically, driven by the working fluid itself rather than by external mechanical linkages. Examples include pressure-or steam-actuated valves that open and close in response to fluid pressure changes.
[F01B 3/0011] Cylindrical distribution members -- Cylindrical distribution members are tubular components used in reciprocating-piston engines to channel fluids such as fuel, air, steam, oil or exhaust gases to and from the cylinders. They typically take the form of manifolds, pipes or distributors mounted coaxially or parallel to the crankshaft and ensure even distribution and collection of the working medium throughout the engine.
[F01B 3/0014] Conical distribution members -- Conical distribution members are funnel-shaped components used in reciprocating-piston engines to direct steam, gas, or lubricant from a supply manifold to the cylinder or vice versa, ensuring even flow across the piston chambers. They are typically mounted on or near the cylinder head and may serve as ports, relief valves or distribution rings in steam and other positive-displacement engines.
[F01B 3/0017] Component parts, details, e.g. sealings, lubrication -- F01B 3/0017 covers the component parts and details of reciprocating-piston machines whose cylinder axes are coaxial with, or parallel or inclined to, the main shaft, including items such as sealing arrangements, lubrication devices and other auxiliary elements. It falls under the mechanical-engineering group for general positive-displacement engines such as steam engines.
[F01B 3/002] Cylinders -- F01B 3/002 covers the design and construction of cylinders used in reciprocating-piston engines and machines where the cylinder axes are coaxial with, parallel to, or inclined relative to the main shaft. It includes features such as cylinder liners, cooling passages, sealing arrangements and mounting methods specific to positive-displacement engines.
[F01B 3/0023] Actuating or actuated elements -- The CPC subclass F01B 3/0023 concerns the actuating or actuated elements of reciprocating-piston machines whose cylinder axes are coaxial, parallel or inclined to the main shaft. It includes devices that impart motion to the piston or are driven by it, such as valves, cams, linkages, control mechanisms and auxiliary drives used in steam, internal-combustion or other positive-displacement engines.
[F01B 3/0026] Actuating or actuated element bearing means or driving or driven axis bearing means -- F01B 3/0026 relates to the bearing arrangements that support either the actuating component or the component being actuated, as well as the bearings that locate the driving or driven shaft in reciprocating-piston machines whose cylinder axes are coaxial or parallel to the main shaft. These bearings ensure proper alignment and load transmission within engines such as steam or internal-combustion types.
[F01B 3/0029] Casings, housings -- F01B 3/0029 pertains to the protective casings and housings that enclose reciprocating-piston machines whose cylinder axes are coaxial with, parallel to, or inclined from the main shaft, such as steam or positive-displacement engines. It classifies the structural components that contain and support the moving parts of these engines.
[F01B 3/0032] having rotary cylinder block -- F01B 3/0032 is a CPC subclass for reciprocating-piston engines in which the cylinder block itself rotates during operation, forming a single rotating assembly rather than fixed cylinders. The classification covers designs such as rotary-type engines and other engines where the cylinder axes are coaxial, parallel or inclined to the main shaft and the entire cylinder block turns as a unit. This feature distinguishes them from conventional fixed-cylinder machines.
[F01B 3/0035] having two or more sets of cylinders or pistons -- The classification F01B 3/0035 applies to reciprocating-piston engines that contain two or more separate groups of cylinders or pistons, each set arranged coaxially, parallel or inclined to the main shaft and driven by the same crankshaft.
[F01B 3/0038] inclined to main shaft axis -- The subclass F01B 3/0038 covers reciprocating-piston machines whose cylinder axes are set at an angle to the main shaft axis, including engines with inclined or slanted cylinders. It addresses the geometric arrangement of the pistons relative to the crankshaft, which influences engine layout, balance and packaging in both steam and internal-combustion designs.
[F01B 3/0041] Arrangements for pressing the cylinder barrel against the valve plate, e.g. fluid pressure -- F01B 3/0041 pertains to reciprocating-piston machines in which the cylinder barrel is forced against the valve plate, usually by means of fluid pressure. The classification covers the specific arrangement of this pressing action in positive-displacement engines such as steam or other piston engines. It is a subcategory under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and machines or engines in general.
[F01B 3/0044] Component parts, details, e.g. valves, sealings, lubrication -- F01B 3/0044 covers the individual components and detailed parts of reciprocating-piston engines whose cylinder axes are coaxial, parallel or inclined to the main shaft. It includes items such as valves, sealing elements, and lubrication devices that are integral to the operation of these machines.
[F01B 3/0047] Particularities in the contacting area between cylinder barrel and valve plate -- F01B 3/0047 covers design details of the interface between the cylinder barrel and the valve plate in reciprocating-piston engines whose cylinder axes are coaxial or parallel/inclined to the main shaft. It includes surface geometry, material choice, lubrication and wear-control features that affect sealing and heat transfer.
[F01B 3/005] Bearing arrangements -- F01B 3/005 covers the design and placement of bearings that support the crankshaft and other rotating parts in reciprocating-piston machines whose cylinder axes are coaxial with, parallel to, or inclined toward the main shaft. It includes bearing types, mounting arrangements, and lubrication schemes intended to ensure proper alignment and load distribution.
[F01B 3/0052] Cylinder barrel -- Cylinder barrel refers to the cylindrical housing in a reciprocating-piston engine that encloses the piston and forms the combustion chamber, providing a sealed surface for pressure transmission and supporting the piston's motion. It may be integral with the crankcase or a separate component and is designed to withstand high temperatures and pressures.
[F01B 3/0055] Valve means, e.g. valve plate -- This CPC subclass covers valve mechanisms such as valve plates employed in reciprocating-piston machines whose cylinder axes are coaxial, parallel or inclined to the main shaft. It includes designs that regulate the admission or exhaust of working fluid in engines or pumps.
[F01B 3/0058] Cylindrical valve means -- The CPC entry F01B 3/0058 covers cylindrical valve means used in reciprocating-piston machines or engines, such as steam engines, where the cylinder axes are coaxial, parallel, or inclined to the main shaft. It pertains to valve designs that are cylindrical in shape and employed to control the admission and exhaust of working fluid within the engine's cylinders.
[F01B 3/0061] Conical valve means -- Conical valve means are valve components shaped like a cone that move axially or radially to open or close fluid passages in reciprocating-piston engines whose cylinders are aligned with or inclined to the main shaft. They are used to control intake, exhaust or pressure-oil flow in steam, internal-combustion or other positive-displacement engines.
[F01B 3/0064] Machine housing -- The entry refers to the protective or supporting enclosure that contains a reciprocating-piston engine whose cylinders are coaxial with, parallel to, or inclined from the main shaft. It covers the design and construction of housings, casings, frames or bodies that secure the moving parts, provide structural rigidity and isolate the engine from the external environment. This classification is used for machine housings in general or positive-displacement engines such as steam or internal-combustion types.
[F01B 3/0067] cylinder barrel bearing means -- This CPC subclass defines bearing arrangements situated on the cylinder barrel of reciprocating-piston engines whose cylinder axes are coaxial, parallel or inclined to the main shaft. It covers various bearing types-plain, roller, hydrodynamic or combined-that support the barrel against radial and axial loads and maintain alignment during operation.
[F01B 3/007] Swash plate -- A swash plate is a slotted, rotating disc that converts the angular motion of a crankshaft into the reciprocating motion of pistons in engines whose cylinder axes are coaxial, parallel or inclined to the main shaft. It is used in reciprocating-piston machines such as steam or internal-combustion engines to drive pistons with a single rotating component, simplifying the linkage and reducing size.
[F01B 3/0073] swash plate bearing means or driving or driven axis bearing means -- The code F01B 3/0073 covers bearing arrangements that support a swash-plate mechanism or the bearings for the driving or driven shafts in reciprocating-piston machines whose cylinder axes are coaxial, parallel or inclined to the main shaft, such as positive-displacement engines including steam engines. It includes designs where the bearing members directly accommodate the swash plate or the axial thrust loads of the crank or connecting-rod shafts.
[F01B 3/0076] Connection between cylinder barrel and inclined swash plate
[F01B 3/0079] having pistons with rotary and reciprocating motion, i.e. spinning pistons -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Reciprocating-piston machines or engines with cylinder axes coaxial with, or parallel or inclined to, main shaft axis, wherein the pistons rotate (spin) about their longitudinal axis as they reciprocate.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:
[F01B 3/0082] Details -- F01B 3/0082 pertains to the detailed design and construction features of reciprocating-piston machines or engines whose cylinder axes are coaxial with, parallel to, or inclined relative to the main shaft axis. The entry covers aspects such as bearing arrangements, lubrication, cooling, sealing and other specific components that define the operation of positive-displacement engines such as steam or internal-combustion types.
[F01B 3/0085] Pistons -- The CPC subgroup F01B 3/0085 encompasses pistons employed in reciprocating-piston machines or engines whose cylinder axes are coaxial with, parallel to, or inclined from the main shaft axis. It covers the design, construction, material selection, sealing, cooling and related features of pistons used in such engines, including steam, internal-combustion and other positive-displacement types.
[F01B 3/0088] Piston shoe retaining means -- A piston shoe retaining means is a component that secures the piston shoe- the bearing surface that slides within the cylinder liner- to the piston or connecting rod in reciprocating-piston engines. It ensures precise axial positioning, prevents axial movement during operation, and typically comprises clamps, pins, or fastening brackets designed to withstand high pressures and thermal loads. This device is essential for maintaining engine efficiency and durability.
[F01B 3/0091] Casings, housings -- F01B 3/0091 covers the protective casings or housings that enclose reciprocating-piston machines or engines whose cylinder axes are coaxial, parallel or inclined to the main shaft, such as steam or positive-displacement engines. The classification addresses the structural design, materials and attachment methods of the enclosure that contains the moving components and maintains alignment and safety. It is part of the broader category for mechanical engineering engines and pumps.
[F01B 3/0094] Driving or driven means -- The F01B 3/0094 entry covers the components that transmit power to or from reciprocating-piston machines whose cylinder axes are coaxial, parallel or inclined to the main shaft. It includes gears, belts, couplings, shafts and other mechanical devices that serve as driving or driven means for such engines.
[F01B 3/02] with wobble-plate -- F01B 3/02 covers reciprocating-piston engines or machines whose cylinders are aligned coaxially, parallel or at an angle to the main shaft and that employ a wobble-plate (swash-plate) to transform the piston strokes into rotary motion, typical of wobble-plate engines.
[F01B 3/04] the piston motion being transmitted by curved surfaces -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Piston motion being transmitted by curved surfaces, e.g. by cams or grooves.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:
[F01B 3/045] by two or more curved surfaces, e.g. for two or more pistons in one cylinder -- F01B 3/045 covers reciprocating piston engines where the cylinder contains two or more pistons that move within the same bore and are guided by at least two curved surfaces, for example two pistons sharing a single cylinder.
[F01B 3/06] by multi-turn helical surfaces and automatic reversal -- F01B 3/06 pertains to reciprocating-piston machines whose cylinder axes are coaxial, parallel or inclined to the main shaft and that use multi-turn helical surfaces to control piston motion together with an automatic reversal mechanism. This subclass addresses engines that achieve stroke reversal without external cams or gear trains, employing helical screw-type guides to change direction of the piston travel.
[F01B 3/08] the helices being arranged on the pistons -- This subclass covers reciprocating-piston machines in which helical grooves, ridges or other screw-shaped features are formed on the pistons themselves. The helices are used to improve lubrication, reduce friction, aid sealing or influence gas flow during piston motion.
[F01B 3/10] Control of working-fluid admission or discharge peculiar thereto -- F01B 3/10 classifies mechanisms that control the admission or discharge of working fluid in reciprocating-piston machines whose cylinder axes are coaxial with, parallel to, or inclined from the main shaft. It includes valve systems, timing devices and other special arrangements for managing intake and exhaust flow.
[F01B 31/00] Component parts, details or accessories not provided for in, or of interest apart from, other groups -- F01B 31/00 covers miscellaneous components, details and accessories for positive-displacement engines and steam engines that are not classified in other specific groups. It includes parts such as brackets, couplings, seals and ancillary devices used in engine assemblies but not otherwise listed.
[F01B 31/005] Silencing equipment  F01B31/16  takes precedence -- F01B 31/005 pertains to noise-reduction devices for positive-displacement engines or pumps, including silencers, mufflers, acoustic enclosures and related accessories that are not classified elsewhere. It covers component parts and details intended to dampen or attenuate sound generated by such machines. This subgroup falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and general-type machines.
[F01B 3/101] for machines with stationary cylinders -- F01B 3/101 covers reciprocating-piston machines whose cylinders are stationary, meaning the cylinders do not rotate with the crankshaft. It applies to engines with cylinder axes that are coaxial, parallel or inclined to the main shaft, such as steam or internal-combustion engines where the pistons move within fixed cylinders. This subclass sits under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and general positive-displacement machines.
[F01B 3/102] Changing the piston stroke by changing the position of the swash plate -- F01B 3/102 relates to reciprocating-piston engines in which the piston travel is varied by moving the swash plate that controls the connecting-rod angle. By adjusting the swash-plate position the stroke length can be increased or decreased, enabling variable-displacement operation in engines such as steam or positive-displacement types.
[F01B 31/02] De-icing means for engines having icing phenomena -- F01B 31/02 covers devices, systems or methods that prevent or remove ice buildup on engines that are subject to icing, including inlet-air heating, blade-tip de-icing, spray or electrical heating arrangements and related accessories intended to keep engine components clear of ice during operation.
[F01B 3/103] for machines with rotary cylinder block -- F01B 3/103 relates to positive-displacement reciprocating-piston engines in which the cylinder block rotates relative to the crankshaft. The classification includes machines where the cylinder axes are coaxial, parallel or inclined to the main shaft and the entire cylinder assembly turns as a unit, covering their construction and operation.
[F01B 3/104] by turning the valve plate -- The CPC subclass F01B 3/104 concerns reciprocating-piston engines that regulate intake and exhaust by rotating the valve plate instead of moving it linearly. It applies to positive-displacement machines such as steam engines whose cylinder axes are coaxial, parallel or inclined to the main shaft.
