[F01C 21/0827] by mechanical means -- F01C 21/0827 covers components, details or accessories of rotary or oscillating piston engines that are driven by mechanical force rather than by fluid power or electricity. It includes mechanisms such as gear-driven couplings, levers, cams or springs that control engine functions. These parts are not allocated to more specific groups within the CPC.
[F01C 21/0836] comprising guiding means, e.g. cams, rollers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Guiding means including those for vanes guided or driven in synchronism with the piston movement.
[F01C 21/0845] comprising elastic means, e.g. springs -- F01C 21/0845 is a CPC subgroup for component parts of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston engines and pumps that incorporate elastic elements, such as springs, to provide flexibility, load compensation or shock absorption. It applies to accessories and details not covered by other, more specific groups.
[F01C 21/0854] by fluid means -- A rotary-piston or oscillating-piston engine in which power or motion is transmitted, assisted, or controlled by a fluid such as hydraulic or pneumatic pressure, rather than by mechanical linkages. This covers components, details or accessories that use fluid power to drive or regulate the piston movement.
[F01C 21/0863] the fluid being the working fluid -- This subclass covers component parts, details or accessories of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston machines where the fluid itself is the working fluid, and which are not otherwise provided for in the other groups of the same section.
[F01C 21/0872] the fluid being other than the working fluid -- F01C 21/0872 classifies components or accessories of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston engines in which a fluid other than the main working fluid is used, for example for lubrication, cooling or secondary power transmission. The entry covers such parts that are not allocated to more specific groups within the CPC hierarchy.
[F01C 21/0881] the vanes consisting of two or more parts -- The entry F01C 21/0881 covers vanes used in rotary-piston or oscillating-piston engines that are built from two or more separate parts, such as split-leaf, hinged, or interlocking sections. It applies to component parts, details or accessories of these machines that are not listed in other more specific groups.
[F01C 21/089] for synchronised movement of the vanes -- This subgroup covers mechanisms that keep the vanes of a rotary-piston or oscillating-piston machine moving in a coordinated way, such as gears, cams, linkages or control systems that maintain equal spacing or phase during rotation. It relates to components that synchronise vane motion to improve sealing, balance and overall efficiency.
[F01C 21/10] Outer members for co-operation with rotary pistons -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Details relating to the casing or to the stator of engines but also of pumps and compressors of the type other than intermeshing engagement.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Details relating to the casing or to the stator of machines of the intermeshing engagement type -> F01C1/086
[F01C 21/102] Adjustment of the interstices between moving and fixed parts of the machine by means other than fluid pressure -- F01C 21/102 covers devices that adjust the clearances between moving and stationary parts of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston engines or pumps by means other than fluid pressure, such as mechanical shims, springs or adjustable screws. The adjustment compensates for wear or thermal expansion to maintain proper operation.
[F01C 21/104] Stators; Members defining the outer boundaries of the working chamber -- The subclass covers stators and other members that form the outer wall of the working chamber in rotary-piston or oscillating-piston engines and pumps, defining the chamber's shape and limits. It includes structural components that are not classified in more specific groups.
[F01C 21/106] with a radial surface, e.g. cam rings -- F01C 21/106 pertains to component parts of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston engines that feature a radial surface, such as cam rings used to guide or time piston movement. The provision applies when such parts are not specifically covered by other groups in the classification.
[F01C 21/108] with an axial surface, e.g. side plates -- The CPC subclass F01C 21/108 covers components of reciprocating piston or rotary-piston engines that have an axial surface, such as side plates used for mounting, sealing or supporting other engine parts.
[F01C 21/18] Arrangements for admission or discharge of the working fluid, e.g. constructional features of the inlet or outlet -- F01C 21/18 pertains to the design of inlet and outlet structures in rotary-piston or oscillating-piston engines, covering any features that control how the working fluid enters or leaves the machine. It includes specific geometry, valves, ports or related accessories that facilitate fluid admission or discharge.
[F01C 21/183] Arrangements for supercharging the working space  similar arrangements for internal combustion engines  F02B33/00 ,  F02B27/00 -- F01C 21/183 covers designs that introduce supercharging systems into the working space of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston engines, aiming to increase charge density and power output. It includes configurations comparable to those used in conventional internal-combustion engines as classified under F02B33/00 and F02B27/00.
[F01C 21/186] for variable fluid distribution -- F01C 21/186 covers components of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston engines and pumps that provide a variable distribution of working fluid. It includes mechanisms that adjust flow rates, timing, or direction of fluid to different chambers or ports, such as variable-injection systems, adaptable passages, and controllable distribution valves. These features are used to optimise performance, efficiency, or load handling in the machine.
[F01C 3/00] Rotary-piston machines or engines with non-parallel axes of movement of co-operating members -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Rotary-piston machines with the working-chamber walls being at least partly resiliently deformable -> F01C5/00
[F01C 3/02] the axes being arranged at an angle of 90 degrees -- F01C 3/02 covers rotary-piston or oscillating-piston engines in which the cooperating members move on axes that are not parallel but intersect at right angles. This sub-class deals specifically with designs where the pistons' stroke axis is set 90 degrees to the rotation axis, a geometry used in certain compact power units and specialized pump arrangements.
[F01C 3/025] of intermeshing engagement type, i.e. with engagement of co-operating members similar to that of toothed gearing -- F01C 3/025 covers rotary-piston or oscillating-piston engines in which the cooperating members engage by intermeshing, similar to the teeth of a gear, while moving on non-parallel axes. The classification applies to mechanical-engineering devices that use this gear-like engagement to transmit power within the rotary-piston machine.
[F01C 3/04] with axially sliding vanes -- F01C 3/04 classifies rotary-piston or oscillating-piston machines whose cooperating members move on non-parallel axes and incorporate vanes that slide axially within the rotor, typically to adjust volume or improve sealing during rotation. This sub-group falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and specifically rotary-piston engines.
[F01C 3/06] the axes being arranged otherwise than at an angle of 90 degrees -- F01C 3/06 designates rotary-piston or oscillating-piston engines in which the shafts or axes of the moving components are set at an angle other than 90 degrees to one another. This includes configurations where the axes are inclined, skewed, or otherwise non-perpendicular, affecting the kinematics and sealing of the machine.
[F01C 3/08] of intermeshing-engagement type, i.e. with engagement of co-operating members similar to that of toothed gearing -- F01C 3/08 covers rotary-piston machines or engines whose cooperating members engage by intermeshing, much like the teeth of a gear. The classification applies to designs where the pistons move on non-parallel axes and achieve power transmission through this gear-like interaction. It falls under mechanical engineering, specifically engines or pumps, rotary-piston or oscillating-piston machines.
[F01C 3/085] the axes of cooperating members being on the same plane -- F01C 3/085 relates to rotary-piston or oscillating-piston engines in which the axes of the cooperating members lie in the same geometric plane, rather than being offset or intersecting at different angles. This subgroup covers designs where the movement of the pistons or rotors is constrained to a common plane, simplifying the engine's geometry and often improving balance and manufacturing efficiency.
[F01C 5/00] Rotary-piston machines or engines with the working-chamber walls at least partly resiliently deformable -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Rotary-piston peristaltic machines -> F04B43/12
[F01C 5/02] the resiliently-deformable wall being part of the inner member, e.g. of a rotary piston -- This subgroup covers rotary-piston or oscillating-piston engines whose working-chamber wall that can flex or compress is integrated into the inner rotating member, such as a flexible piston sleeve. The resilient wall forms part of the rotor, allowing it to deform during operation to maintain sealing or volume changes.
[F01C 5/04] the resiliently-deformable wall being part of the outer member, e.g. of a housing -- F01C 5/04 designates rotary-piston or oscillating-piston engines whose working-chamber wall is at least partly resilient and formed as part of the outer member, such as the housing, providing a flexible, sealing surface integrated into the engine's external structure.
[F01C 5/06] the resiliently-deformable wall being a separate member -- This subclass covers rotary-piston or oscillating-piston engines whose working-chamber walls are at least partly resiliently deformable, where the deformable wall is provided as a separate member rather than being integral to the cylinder or housing.
[F01C 5/08] of tubular form, e.g. hose -- The subclass F01C 5/08 covers rotary-piston or oscillating-piston engines where the working chamber is formed by a tubular element, such as a hose, that flexes under pressure. These flexible tubes act as part of the deformable chamber wall, allowing volume changes as the piston rotates or oscillates.
[F01C 7/00] Rotary-piston machines or engines with fluid ring or the like -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Rotary-piston machines or engines in which the rotary-piston is sealed by a mass of liquid rotating inside the housing.
[F01C 9/00] Oscillating-piston machines or engines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Rotary-piston machines or engines in which the rotary-piston moves back and forth inside the working chamber.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Rotary-piston machines with coaxially mounted members having continuously-changing circumferential spacing between them -> F01C1/063
[F01C 9/002] the piston oscillating around a fixed axis -- F01C 9/002 classifies engines or machines in which the piston swings about a fixed axis rather than moving linearly. The oscillating motion is used to convert pressure changes into rotary output, typical of certain steam or gas engines. This subgroup falls under rotary-piston or oscillating-piston machines within mechanical engineering.
[F01C 9/005] the piston oscillating in the space, e.g. around a fixed point  rotary piston machines or engines with non-parallel axes of rotation between co-operating members  F01C3/00 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Rotary-piston machines or engines with non-parallel axes of rotation between co-operating members -> F01C3/00 - Rotary-piston machines where the working fluid is expanded in a flexible chamber -> F04B43/12
[F01C 9/007] the points of the moving element describing approximately an alternating movement in axial direction with respect to the other element -- The F01C 9/007 code refers to oscillating-piston machines or engines. It covers machines where the moving element moves in an alternating axial direction. This is a subset of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston machines or engines.
[F01D] NON-POSITIVE DISPLACEMENT MACHINES OR ENGINES, e.g. STEAM TURBINES -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Machines or engines for liquids -> F03 - Non-positive-displacement pumps -> F04D
[F01D 1/00] Non-positive-displacement machines or engines, e.g. steam turbines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Non-positive-displacement machines or engines, e.g. steam turbines: i.e. machine or engine that uses an impeller or propeller and through which fluid is moved  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Machines or engines with working-fluid flows in opposite axial directions for balancing axial thrust -> F01D3/02 - Machines or engines with other than pure rotation -> F01D23/00 - Turbines characterised by their use in special steam systems, cycles, or processes, regulating devices therefor -> F01K
[F01D 1/02] with stationary working-fluid guiding means and bladed or like rotor, -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Machines or engines without stationary working-fluid guiding means -> F01D1/18
[F01D 1/023] the working-fluid being divided into several separate flows  F01D3/02  takes precedence ; several separate fluid flows being united in a single flow; the machine or engine having provision for two or more different possible fluid flow paths -- The subclass F01D 1/023 relates to non-positive-displacement machines such as steam turbines in which the working fluid is divided into several separate streams, or where separate streams are merged into one, and the machine includes provisions for two or more alternative fluid-flow paths. This feature is subordinate to F01D 3/02, which deals with multiple fluid-flow arrangements.
[F01D 1/026] Impact turbines with buckets, i.e. impulse turbines, e.g. Pelton turbines  F01D1/16 ,  F01D1/34  take precedence
[F01D 1/04] traversed by the working-fluid substantially axially -- F01D 1/04 covers non-positive-displacement machines, such as steam turbines, in which the working fluid moves primarily along the axis of the rotor. The classification is applied to designs where the fluid flow is substantially axial rather than radial or mixed.
[F01D 1/06] traversed by the working-fluid substantially radially -- This subgroup covers non-positive-displacement machines such as steam turbines where the working fluid passes through the rotors in a substantially radial direction, typical of radial-flow turbine stages. It includes designs in which the fluid moves outward from the shaft or inward toward it while delivering power.
[F01D 1/08] having inward flow -- F01D 1/08 refers to non-positive-displacement machines, such as steam turbines, that operate with an inward flow of the working fluid, meaning the fluid moves from the outer radius toward the centre of the rotating rotor. This sub-class groups turbines whose design relies on that inward-flow arrangement for energy conversion.
[F01D 1/10] having two or more stages subjected to working-fluid flow without essential intermediate pressure change, i.e. with velocity stages -- The code F01D 1/10 covers non-positive-displacement machines such as steam turbines that contain two or more velocity stages. In these stages the working fluid passes through successive rotor or stator rows without a significant pressure rise, so the energy conversion relies on fluid velocity changes rather than compression.
[F01D 11/00] Preventing or minimising internal leakage of working-fluid, e.g. between stages -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Sealing issues related to centrifugal compressors -> F04D - Sealing in general -> F16J
[F01D 11/001] for sealing space between stator blade and rotor -- F01D 11/001 covers methods or devices for sealing the gap between a stator blade and a rotor in non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines, aiming to prevent or reduce internal leakage of the working fluid between stages.
[F01D 11/003] by packing rings; Mechanical seals -- This subclass deals with sealing solutions that use packing rings or mechanical seals to prevent fluid leakage between stages of non-positive-displacement machines such as steam turbines. It covers the design, arrangement, and material selection of these seals to maintain pressure integrity and reduce internal losses.
[F01D 11/005] Sealing means between non relatively rotating elements -- F01D 11/005 covers sealing arrangements used in non-positive displacement machines, such as steam turbines, where the sealing surfaces do not rotate relative to each other. It addresses static or quasi-static seals-labyrinth, contact, or non-contact types-designed to prevent or minimise fluid leakage between stages of the engine.
[F01D 11/006] Sealing the gap between rotor blades or blades and rotor -- The classification F01D 11/006 addresses methods and devices for sealing the clearance between rotor blades or between blades and the rotor in non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines, aimed at reducing internal fluid leakage across stages.
[F01D 11/008] by spacer elements between the blades, e.g. independent interblade platforms -- F01D 11/008 covers measures that reduce internal fluid leakage in non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines by inserting spacer elements between the blades, for example independent interblade platforms that keep the flow path sealed.
[F01D 11/02] by non-contact sealings, e.g. of labyrinth type -- F01D 11/02 covers the use of non-contact sealing methods such as labyrinth seals to prevent or minimise internal fluid leakage in non-positive displacement machines or engines, for example steam turbines. These techniques avoid direct rubbing between sealing surfaces and are applied between stages of the machine.
[F01D 11/025] Seal clearance control; Floating assembly; Adaptation means to differential thermal dilatations -- The entry F01D 11/025 covers methods for controlling seal clearance in non-positive-displacement machines such as steam turbines, including floating seal assemblies and devices that compensate for differential thermal expansion to prevent internal fluid leakage.
[F01D 11/04] using sealing fluid, e.g. steam -- F01D 11/04 covers methods of preventing or reducing internal leakage of working fluid in non-positive displacement engines such as steam turbines by introducing a sealing fluid, for example steam, into clearance gaps. The approach uses the sealing fluid to occupy leak paths and minimize loss of the primary working medium.
[F01D 11/06] Control thereof -- This subclass deals with mechanisms or methods that regulate seals, bearings, or other devices to prevent or reduce internal leakage of working fluid between stages of non-positive displacement engines such as steam turbines. It includes control systems, monitoring, and adjustments that maintain fluid tightness during operation.
[F01D 11/08] for sealing space between rotor blade tips and stator -- F01D 11/08 covers devices or methods that seal the clearance between the tips of rotating blades and the surrounding stationary casing in non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines, aiming to prevent or reduce leakage of the working fluid between stages.
[F01D 11/10] using sealing fluid, e.g. steam -- F01D 11/10 covers methods of preventing or reducing internal fluid leakage in non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines by introducing a sealing fluid, often steam itself, into gaps between moving parts or stages. The classification includes systems that supply, distribute or control the sealing fluid to maintain a barrier that limits leakage and improves efficiency.
[F01D 11/12] using a rubstrip, e.g. erodible. deformable or resiliently-biased part -- The entry covers the use of a rubstrip-a sacrificial, deformable, erodible or resiliently-biased sealing element-to prevent or minimise internal leakage of working fluid between stages of non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines. The rubstrip maintains contact with mating surfaces to seal gaps.
[F01D 11/122] with erodable or abradable material  blades having cutting or grinding tips  F01D5/20 -- F01D 11/122 covers steam turbine or other non-positive displacement machines in which internal leakage of working fluid is reduced by using blades made of erodable or abradeable material that have cutting or grinding tips. The design lets the blade tips wear away to seal gaps between stages, improving efficiency and preventing fluid bypass.
[F01D 11/125] with a reinforcing structure -- This subcategory covers designs for steam turbines or other non-positive displacement machines that incorporate a reinforcing structure to reduce or eliminate internal fluid leakage between stages. It relates to methods or apparatus that strengthen casings, diaphragms or sealing components to maintain pressure integrity.
[F01D 11/127] with a deformable or crushable structure, e.g. honeycomb -- This subclass covers sealing measures for non-positive displacement engines or turbines that use a deformable or crushable element, such as a honeycomb structure, to prevent or minimise internal fluid leakage between stages. The design relies on the element's ability to compress and conform, creating an effective barrier against working-fluid escape.
[F01D 11/14] Adjusting or regulating tip-clearance, i.e. distance between rotor-blade tips and stator casing -- F01D 11/14 covers mechanisms for adjusting or regulating the tip clearance between rotor-blade tips and the surrounding stator casing in non-positive-displacement machines such as steam turbines. The purpose is to prevent or reduce internal leakage of the working fluid across stages, improving efficiency and performance.
[F01D 11/16] by self-adjusting means -- The class relates to self-adjusting mechanisms that prevent or reduce internal fluid leakage between stages of non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines. It includes devices that automatically adapt to operating conditions to maintain sealing without manual intervention.
[F01D 11/18] using stator or rotor components with predetermined thermal response, e.g. selective insulation, thermal inertia, differential expansion -- In steam turbines or similar non-positive displacement machines, it covers techniques that employ stator or rotor components engineered to exhibit a predetermined thermal response-such as selective insulation, added thermal inertia, or differential expansion-to prevent or minimise internal leakage of working fluid between stages.
[F01D 1/12] with repeated action on same blade ring -- F01D 1/12 classifies non-positive-displacement machines such as steam turbines in which the same ring of blades is acted on repeatedly during operation, for example for variable pitch or cyclic loading. The subclass deals with mechanisms that repeatedly engage a single blade ring to control flow or power output.
[F01D 11/20] Actively adjusting tip-clearance -- F01D 11/20 covers systems that dynamically control the clearance between rotor tips and the surrounding housing in non-positive-displacement machines such as steam turbines. By actively adjusting tip-clearance during operation, these devices reduce internal fluid leakage between stages, improving efficiency and reducing wear.
[F01D 11/22] by mechanically actuating the stator or rotor components, e.g. moving shroud sections relative to the rotor -- F01D 11/22 covers non-positive displacement machines, such as steam turbines, in which internal fluid leakage between stages is reduced by mechanically moving stator or rotor parts. The entry includes solutions where shroud sections are shifted relative to the rotor to seal gaps and limit leakage.
[F01D 11/24] by selectively cooling-heating stator or rotor components -- The CPC subclass F01D 11/24 covers measures for reducing internal leakage in non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines, focusing on techniques that selectively heat or cool stator or rotor parts to control fluid flow gaps and improve sealing performance.
[F01D 1/14] traversed by the working-fluid substantially radially -- F01D 1/14 covers non-positive-displacement machines, such as steam turbines, in which the working fluid passes through the rotor mainly in a radial direction, crossing the flow path from the inlet to the outlet substantially across the radius of the machine.
[F01D 1/16] characterised by having both reaction stages and impulse stages -- This subclass covers steam turbines that combine reaction and impulse blade stages in a single machine. The design uses pressure rise across moving blades (reaction) and high-velocity jet impact (impulse) to extract work from the fluid, typical of power-generation and industrial drive applications.
[F01D 1/18] without stationary working-fluid guiding means; -- The CPC subclass F01D 1/18 covers non-positive-displacement engines such as steam turbines that operate without any stationary guide vanes or other fixed fluid-directing structures; the fluid flow is managed solely by rotating blades or other moving components.
[F01D 1/20] traversed by the working-fluid substantially axially -- F01D 1/20 refers to non-positive-displacement machines such as steam turbines in which the working fluid passes mainly along the axis of the rotor. The classification includes designs where the fluid flow is essentially axial rather than radial, covering axial-flow turbines, compressors and similar devices.
[F01D 1/22] traversed by the working-fluid substantially radially -- The F01D 1/22 subgroup covers non-positive-displacement machines, such as steam turbines, in which the working fluid passes through the rotating component mainly in a radial direction across the rotor or stator, rather than axially. This radial flow arrangement is characteristic of many turbine designs.
[F01D 1/24] characterised by counter-rotating rotors subjected to same working fluid stream without intermediate stator blades or the like -- F01D 1/24 covers non-positive-displacement machines such as steam turbines where two rotors rotate in opposite directions and are driven by the same fluid stream, without any intermediate stator blades or similar guiding components. This arrangement enables the fluid to act directly on both rotors, simplifying the flow path and potentially improving efficiency.
[F01D 1/26] traversed by the working-fluid substantially axially -- F01D 1/26 covers non-positive-displacement machines, such as steam or gas turbines, in which the working fluid passes mainly along the axis of the rotor. The classification is applied to axial-flow turbine designs where the fluid enters and exits parallel to the shaft, distinguishing them from radial-flow or mixed-flow arrangements. This sub-group refines the broader category of engines or pumps that operate without positive displacement.
[F01D 1/28] traversed by the working-fluid substantially radially -- F01D 1/28 covers non-positive-displacement machines such as steam turbines in which the working fluid crosses the rotor mainly along a radial path, moving outward or inward from the centre rather than axially. It classifies designs where the fluid flow is substantially radial during energy conversion.
[F01D 1/30] characterised by having a single rotor operable in either direction of rotation, e.g. by reversing of blades -- This CPC subclass covers non-positive-displacement machines such as steam turbines that employ a single rotor capable of rotating in either direction, typically achieved by reversible or adjustable blades. It is nested under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and specifically non-positive-displacement engines.
[F01D 13/00] Combinations of two or more machines or engines -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Adaptations of machines or engines for special use; Combinations of engines with devices driven thereby -> F01D15/00
[F01D 13/003] with at least two independent shafts, i.e. cross-compound -- The subclass F01D 13/003 covers steam turbine or similar non-positive-displacement machines that are combined in a cross-compound arrangement, meaning two or more turbine units are mounted on separate, independent shafts. This configuration allows staged expansion of the working fluid and split power output, improving overall efficiency and operational flexibility.
[F01D 13/006] one being a reverse turbine -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines or engines containing a separate turbine for reverse operation  taken from US4245467
[F01D 13/02] Working-fluid interconnection of machines or engines -- F01D 13/02 relates to the way working fluid is linked between two or more non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines, covering pipes, ducts, valves and other coupling arrangements that allow the fluid to pass from one engine to another.
[F01D 1/32] with pressure velocity transformation exclusively in rotor, e.g. the rotor rotating under the influence of jets issuing from the rotor, -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Aspects of working fluid being guided through a rotor construction such that the action of the expelled working fluid jet creates a reaction on the rotor, i.e. a turning motion is induced upon the rotor  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - The working fluid being a combustion product -> F02C3/165 - Jet propulsion plants per se -> F02K
[F01D 1/34] characterised by non-bladed rotor, e.g. with drilled holes -- The code F01D 1/34 covers non-positive-displacement machines such as steam turbines that are characterised by a rotor without blades, for example a rotor formed with drilled holes, to transfer energy to the working fluid.
[F01D 1/36] using fluid friction -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Aspects of extracting power from working fluid by making use of the viscous forces within the fluid, usually by the provision of a stack of closely arranged plates through which the working fluid is forced, such as e.g. a Tesla turbine
[F01D 1/38] of the screw type -- This subclass covers non-positive-displacement turbines whose rotor or flow path is formed as a screw, such as screw-type steam turbines that convert fluid energy into rotary motion through a helical geometry. The design enables continuous energy transfer without the reciprocating motion typical of positive-displacement devices.
[F01D 15/00] Adaptations of machines or engines for special use -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Adaptation off machines or engines for special use, combinations of engines with devices driven thereby: gas turbines in combination with pumps, electrical generators, turbines specifically adapted for driving vehicles, air turbines used in compressed air driven power tools or dentistry applications
[F01D 15/005] Adaptations for refrigeration plants -- This CPC subclass (F01D 15/005) deals with adaptations of non-positive displacement machines, such as steam turbines, specifically for use in refrigeration plants. It covers structural or functional modifications that enable the machine to operate within cooling cycles, incorporate condensers, heat exchangers or other refrigeration-related components.
[F01D 15/02] Adaptations for driving vehicles, e.g. locomotives
[F01D 15/04] the vehicles being waterborne vessels -- F01D 15/04 covers the adaptation of non-positive displacement machines, such as steam turbines, for propulsion or auxiliary power in waterborne vessels. It includes design features, mounting arrangements and integration methods specific to ships, boats and submarines.
[F01D 15/045] Control thereof -- Relates to devices and methods that regulate the performance of non-positive displacement engines such as steam turbines, including speed, power output and load handling. Covers governors, feedback loops, control circuitry and adaptive systems used to optimize operation for particular applications.
[F01D 15/06] Adaptations for driving, or combinations with, hand-held tools or the like
[F01D 15/062] Controlling means specially adapted therefor -- A controlling means specially adapted for non-positive displacement machines, such as steam turbines, comprises devices or systems designed to regulate operating parameters like speed, load, or steam flow in engines that are modified for particular applications. The adaptation ensures precise control and optimal performance under the specific conditions for which the machine has been customized.
[F01D 15/065] with pressure-velocity transformation exclusively in rotor -- This subclass relates to non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines that are adapted so the pressure-to-velocity energy conversion occurs entirely within the rotor, without any separate stator or diffuser stages.
[F01D 15/067] characterised by non-bladed rotor -- F01D 15/067 covers adaptations of non-positive-displacement engines such as steam turbines that employ a rotor lacking conventional blades. The classification is used for machines whose rotors rely on alternative geometry-cavities, slots or other shaping-to convert fluid energy, typically for specialized or niche applications.
[F01D 15/08] Adaptations for driving, or combinations with, pumps -- F01D 15/08 covers technical modifications that enable a non-positive-displacement machine, such as a steam turbine, to drive a pump or to be integrated with a pump in a combined unit. The subclass includes coupling arrangements, power-transfer devices and other adaptations designed for special or auxiliary pump operation.
[F01D 15/10] Adaptations for driving, or combinations with, electric generators -- F01D 15/10 covers adaptations that enable non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines to drive electric generators or to be combined with them. It includes coupling arrangements, gearing, shaft-speed regulation and other modifications required for efficient power conversion from the engine to a generator. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and specifically non-positive displacement machines.
[F01D 15/12] Combinations with mechanical gearing -- F01D 15/12 deals with non-positive displacement machines or engines, especially steam turbines, that are combined with mechanical gearing. The code covers configurations where gear trains are used to transmit, change or adapt the rotational speed or torque of the turbine for special applications.
[F01D 17/00] Regulating or controlling by varying flow -- F01D 17/00 covers methods and devices for regulating or controlling the operation of non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines by varying the flow of working fluid. It includes flow-adjustment mechanisms, control valves and similar systems that modulate fluid volume to achieve desired speed, power output or efficiency. This subclass is part of the broader mechanical-engineering category for engines or pumps.
[F01D 17/02] Arrangement of sensing elements -- This subclass covers the layout and positioning of sensing elements used to monitor and regulate flow in non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines. It includes arrangements of pressure, temperature, speed or other sensors to provide accurate feedback for controlling turbine performance.
[F01D 17/04] responsive to load -- A non-positive displacement machine, such as a steam turbine, that adjusts its operation by varying the flow of working fluid in response to changes in load. The control system modulates the inlet or outlet flow to match the demanded power output, maintaining stable performance under varying load conditions.
[F01D 17/06] responsive to speed -- The subgroup covers non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines that regulate or control operation by varying fluid flow in response to the shaft speed. It includes mechanisms that adjust flow based on speed-sensing to maintain desired performance.
[F01D 17/08] responsive to condition of working-fluid, e.g. pressure -- F01D 17/08 relates to non-positive-displacement machines such as steam turbines in which regulation or control is attained by varying the flow in response to the condition of the working fluid, for example its pressure.
[F01D 17/085] to temperature
[F01D 17/10] Final actuators -- F01D 17/10 covers the final actuating mechanisms that directly move control elements in non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines, converting a control signal into the mechanical motion needed to vary fluid flow. Examples include valve positioners, hydraulic or electric pistons that drive the valve stem to adjust turbine inlet or exhaust flow.
[F01D 17/105] by passing part of the fluid -- This subclass covers ways of controlling the output of a non-positive displacement machine, such as a steam turbine, by diverting a portion of the working fluid away from the main flow path, for example through bypass valves, ducts or passages that reduce the effective flow to the rotor.
[F01D 17/12] arranged in stator parts -- The subclass covers non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines where regulation or control is achieved by varying the flow through stationary components. It includes designs that use stator parts-blades, guide vanes or other fixed elements-to adjust fluid direction or speed for speed or power control.
[F01D 17/14] varying effective cross-sectional area of nozzles or guide conduits -- F01D 17/14 pertains to non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines and covers regulation by varying the effective cross-sectional area of nozzles or guide conduits to control flow.
[F01D 17/141] by means of shiftable members or valves obturating part of the flow path -- F01D 17/141 relates to flow-regulating or controlling techniques for non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines, where the method employs shiftable members or valves that partially obstruct the flow path to vary the fluid flow. This approach adjusts performance by mechanically opening or closing sections of the circuit.
[F01D 17/143] the shiftable member being a wall, or part thereof of a radial diffuser -- F01D 17/143 covers steam or other non-positive displacement turbines where flow regulation is achieved by moving a wall or part of a radial diffuser. The shiftable member adjusts the diffuser geometry to control the working fluid's velocity and pressure, thereby influencing turbine output.
[F01D 17/145] by means of valves, e.g. for steam turbines  valves in general  F16K -- The subclass F01D 17/145 covers regulation or control of non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines by means of valves, encompassing general valve types classified under F16K. It pertains to the method of varying flow to adjust turbine performance.
[F01D 17/146] by throttling the volute inlet of radial machines or engines -- A subclass for regulating or controlling non-positive displacement radial machines, such as steam turbines, by throttling the volute inlet. It covers designs that vary the inlet cross-section or restrict flow to adjust performance, rather than using variable geometry elsewhere in the machine.
[F01D 17/148] by means of rotatable members, e.g. butterfly valves -- This subclass addresses flow-control techniques for non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines that use rotatable components, exemplified by butterfly valves, to vary the fluid passage and regulate engine operation.
[F01D 17/16] by means of nozzle vanes -- F01D 17/16 covers the regulation of non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines by varying the flow with nozzle vanes. The nozzle vanes modify fluid velocity or direction, allowing the speed or power output to be controlled without altering the rotor geometry.
[F01D 17/162] for axial flow, i.e. the vanes turning around axes which are essentially perpendicular to the rotor centre line  F01D17/167  takes precedence -- This group covers regulating or controlling non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines by varying the flow, specifically using axial flow arrangements where the vanes turn about axes essentially perpendicular to the rotor centre line. It is a subordinate subgroup to F01D 17/167, which takes precedence.
[F01D 17/165] for radial flow, i.e. the vanes turning around axes which are essentially parallel to the rotor centre line  F01D17/167  takes precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Vanes assemblies usually activated by a unison ring or the like in which the vanes are turning uniformly around an axis that is parallel to the rotor main axis, e.g. like in a conventional automotive turbocharger assembly
[F01D 17/167] of vanes moving in translation -- F01D 17/167 covers steam turbine or other non-positive displacement engines in which the regulating vanes move linearly rather than rotating, adjusting the fluid flow to control power output. The translation of the vanes is used for flow regulation in turbines.
[F01D 17/18] varying effective number of nozzles or guide conduits -- This subclass deals with non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines that regulate output by altering how many nozzles or guide conduits are effectively used, thereby varying the flow paths. It covers control methods that change the number of active fluid-directing elements to adjust performance.
[F01D 17/20] Devices dealing with sensing elements or final actuators or transmitting means between them, e.g. power-assisted -- The subclass F01D 17/20 covers devices that link sensing elements to final actuators or transmit signals between them in non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines, allowing regulation of flow or power-assist functions. Typical examples include hydraulic or pneumatic power-assist systems, electronic controllers and signal-conditioning units that convert sensor outputs into actuator commands.
[F01D 17/205] Centrifugal governers directly linked to valves -- F01D 17/205 covers centrifugal governors that are directly coupled to control valves for regulating fluid flow in non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines. The devices use centrifugal force to adjust valve opening proportionally to speed, providing automatic flow control in engine or pump systems.
[F01D 17/22] the operation or power assistance being predominantly non-mechanical -- F01D 17/22 covers steam turbines and similar non-positive-displacement machines in which the regulation of speed or power output is achieved mainly by non-mechanical means, such as fluid, hydraulic, pneumatic or electrical assistance, rather than by direct mechanical couplings.
[F01D 17/24] electrical -- F01D 17/24 covers electrical devices or systems used to regulate or control fluid flow in non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines. It sits under mechanical engineering → engines or pumps → non-positive displacement machines, focusing on flow-control mechanisms powered by electricity.
[F01D 17/26] fluid, e.g. hydraulic -- F01D 17/26 relates to the use of fluids, such as hydraulic liquids, to regulate or control non-positive displacement machines or engines-examples include steam turbines-by varying the flow of the fluid.
[F01D 19/00] Starting of machines or engines -- The F01D 19/00 class covers methods, devices and arrangements for initiating the operation of non-positive displacement machines or engines, such as steam turbines. It includes starters, auxiliary drives, control systems and procedures used to bring these engines from rest to their running speed.
[F01D 19/02] dependent on temperature of component parts, e.g. of turbine-casing -- F01D 19/02 covers start-up control of non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines where the initiation is regulated by the temperature of component parts, for example the turbine casing.
[F01D 21/00] Shutting-down of machines or engines, e.g. in emergency -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Shutting-down of machines or engines, e.g. in emergency; regulating, controlling, or safety means not otherwise provided for, e.g. shutting down operations or methods should be classified in here if none of the sub groups apply
[F01D 21/003] Arrangements for testing or measuring  for measuring vibrations  G01H
[F01D 21/006] Arrangements of brakes  brakes  per se   F16D -- F01D 21/006 covers the design and configuration of braking systems directly applied to non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines for emergency shutdown, including the placement, actuation mechanisms and integration of the brakes themselves.
[F01D 21/02] Shutting-down responsive to overspeed -- This subclass deals with automatic shut-down mechanisms that stop a non-positive displacement machine, such as a steam turbine, when its speed exceeds a predefined limit. It includes sensors, control logic and actuation devices designed for overspeed protection and emergency stopping of engines.
[F01D 21/04] responsive to undesired position of rotor relative to stator , e.g. indicating such position -- The CPC subclass F01D 21/04 covers devices that detect an unwanted relative position of the rotor to the stator in non-positive-displacement machines such as steam turbines, and provide an indication or signal of that condition. It includes sensors, transducers, and indicator mechanisms that monitor misalignment, overspeed, or other abnormal rotor positions for safety or shutdown purposes. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and emergency-shutdown related technologies.
[F01D 21/045] special arrangements in stators or in rotors dealing with breaking-off of part of rotor -- The F01D 21/045 subclass covers special arrangements in the stator or rotor of non-positive displacement machines, such as steam-turbine engines, that deal with the breaking-off of part of the rotor. It includes designs that contain, lock, or otherwise manage rotor fragments to prevent damage during an emergency or shutdown condition.
[F01D 21/06] Shutting-down -- The F01D 21/06 subclass pertains to methods and apparatus for stopping or shutting down engines, pumps or similar non-positive displacement machines, such as steam turbines. It includes emergency-stop mechanisms, control systems and procedures that bring the machine to a safe halt. This covers both automatic and manual shutdown techniques.
[F01D 21/08] Restoring position -- This subclass covers techniques for returning a non-positive-displacement engine or turbine to its original or a safe operating position after it has been stopped, such as re-aligning rotors or repositioning shafts. It includes mechanisms that automatically or manually move components back to preset positions during or after emergency shutdowns.
[F01D 21/10] responsive to unwanted deposits on blades, in working-fluid conduits or the like -- F01D 21/10 covers systems for detecting unwanted deposits on turbine blades or in fluid conduits of non-positive-displacement machines and then triggering a shutdown or other protective response. It includes methods that monitor fouling, scale or debris accumulation and automatically halt operation to avoid damage or reduced efficiency. This subclass is part of the broader group for engines or pumps, specifically steam-turbine shutdown mechanisms.
[F01D 21/12] responsive to temperature -- The CPC subclass F01D 21/12 relates to control devices that stop non-positive displacement machines or engines, such as steam turbines, when a temperature condition is reached. It covers systems that trigger a shutdown in response to overheating, fire, or other temperature-based emergencies.
[F01D 21/14] responsive to other specific conditions -- The class covers devices or control methods that automatically stop non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines when particular conditions arise, for example an emergency situation or other predefined triggers. It includes systems that monitor parameters and initiate shutdowns to protect the equipment or ensure safety.
[F01D 21/16] Trip gear -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Trip mechanisms for trip valves  taken from US4379544
[F01D 21/18] involving hydraulic means -- F01D 21/18 pertains to the shutdown of non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines by using hydraulic means, including emergency stop or controlled stopping systems that employ fluid pressure or flow to bring the machine to rest.
[F01D 21/20] Checking operation of shut-down devices -- The classification F01D 21/20 covers methods and devices for verifying that shut-down mechanisms of non-positive displacement machines, such as steam turbines, have operated correctly, including testing, monitoring, and diagnostic procedures after an emergency stop.
[F01D 23/00] Non-positive-displacement machines or engines with movement other than pure rotation, e.g. of endless-chain type -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group:  taken from DE3139802
[F01D 25/00] Component parts, details, or accessories, not provided for in, or of interest apart from, other groups -- F01D 25/00 covers component parts, details and accessories for non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines that are not classified in other specific groups. Items may include casings, shaft couplings, bearings, sealing elements, control or monitoring accessories that serve a supporting or auxiliary function for the turbine or similar engine.
[F01D 25/002] Cleaning of turbomachines -- F01D 25/002 covers techniques and equipment for cleaning turbomachines, including steam turbines and other non-positive displacement engines. The classification includes methods for removing deposits, scale, fouling or other contaminants from blades, casings and internal passages, as well as related cleaning accessories.
[F01D 25/005] Selecting particular materials -- The subclass addresses the choice of particular materials for components of non-positive-displacement machines such as steam turbines, covering criteria, guidelines and considerations for material selection of parts, details or accessories that are not covered elsewhere in the classification.
[F01D 25/007] Preventing corrosion -- F01D 25/007 covers measures and devices for preventing corrosion of components in non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines. It includes protective coatings, inhibitors, material selection and design features that mitigate corrosion in parts, details, or accessories of these engines.
[F01D 25/02] De-icing means for engines having icing phenomena -- This subclass covers devices and methods for preventing or removing ice buildup on engines, such as steam turbines, where ice formation can impair operation. It includes de-icing systems integrated into the engine or attached as accessories, not covered in other groups.
[F01D 25/04] Antivibration arrangements -- The group F01D 25/04 covers antiviibration arrangements for non-positive-displacement machines such as steam turbines, including mounts, dampers, isolators and other devices or methods that reduce or control vibration of the turbine or its components.
[F01D 25/06] for preventing blade vibration -- F01D 25/06 is a CPC subgroup covering devices, components or accessories fitted to non-positive displacement engines such as steam turbines that are intended to prevent or reduce blade vibration; examples include dampers, shrouds, tuners and other vibration-control features.
[F01D 25/08] Cooling -- The F01D 25/08 group pertains to cooling arrangements for non-positive-displacement machines such as steam turbines. It includes devices, components and accessories that remove heat from the turbine or its parts, for example cooling jackets, water-spray systems, air-flow or other heat-dissipation means.
[F01D 25/10] Heating, e.g. warming-up before starting -- The subclass covers heating arrangements for non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines, intended to warm components before start-up. It includes burners, electric heaters, heat-exchanger units and associated control devices used to raise temperature of rotors, casings or other parts prior to operation.
[F01D 25/12] Cooling -- F01D 25/12 covers cooling elements, systems or accessories specially designed for non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines. It includes components like heat exchangers, water jackets and other devices that remove excess heat from the turbine's working parts, but excludes broader turbine assemblies.
[F01D 25/125] of bearings -- F01D 25/125 covers the bearings that support rotating shafts in non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines. It includes the design, arrangement and auxiliary elements of bearing assemblies that are not covered in other groups, focusing on component parts and accessories.
[F01D 25/14] Casings modified therefor -- F01D 25/14 classifies modified casings used in non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines, covering component parts, details or accessories that are not listed elsewhere in the subclass.
[F01D 25/145] Thermally insulated casings -- This group covers casings for steam turbines and other non-positive displacement machines that are provided with thermal insulation to reduce heat loss or protect surrounding components. It includes designs, material choices, and construction details of insulated housings not otherwise classified elsewhere.
[F01D 25/16] Arrangement of bearings -- F01D 25/16 concerns the layout and positioning of bearings in non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines, detailing how bearing components are arranged within the engine or pump structure. It addresses design aspects of bearing placement that are not covered by other groups in the mechanical engineering hierarchy.
[F01D 25/162] Bearing supports -- Bearing supports are structural elements that secure and guide rotating shafts in non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines. They transmit loads, maintain precise alignment, and often incorporate lubricated bearing housings or brackets. These components are essential for reliable operation of turbine shafts and related rotating assemblies.
[F01D 25/164] Flexible supports; Vibration damping means associated with the bearing -- F01D 25/164 pertains to flexible supports and vibration-damping devices incorporated with bearings in non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines, covering components, details or accessories specifically designed to reduce vibration and provide flexible mounting.
[F01D 25/166] Sliding contact bearing  gas bearings  F01D25/22 -- F01D 25/166 covers sliding-contact gas bearings used as component parts in non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines. It includes designs, construction and operation of gas-filled bearings that support rotating shafts without liquid lubrication, focusing on bearing geometry, pressure generation and sealing features. The classification is applied to accessories that are not otherwise covered in broader groups.
[F01D 25/168] for axial load mainly -- F01D 25/168 covers parts of non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines that are primarily designed to carry axial loads. These components include shafts, bearings, thrust plates or other accessories that resist forces acting along the machine's axis rather than radial forces. The classification is used when the axial-load function is the main purpose of the part.
[F01D 25/18] Lubricating arrangements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Aspects related to lubrication of the engine such as lubrication circuits, lubricant supply, scavenging and the like, covers also features relating to turbochargers and lubrication thereof
[F01D 25/183] Sealing means -- A sealing means for non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines, covering component parts, details or accessories that are not otherwise specified. It includes devices that prevent leakage of fluids or gases at shaft, bearing, casing or inter-stage interfaces, ensuring isolation between high-pressure and low-pressure zones within the turbine assembly.
[F01D 25/186] for sliding contact bearing -- F01D 25/186 covers sliding-contact bearings used in non-positive-displacement machines such as steam turbines. The classification applies to bearing components, details or accessories that provide a lubricated surface where one part slides over another, rather than rolling contact. It is used for describing these bearing parts when they are not covered by more specific groups within the mechanical-engineering hierarchy.
[F01D 25/20] using lubrication pumps -- F01D 25/20 covers lubrication-pump applications in non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines, describing how the pump supplies oil or other lubricants to bearings and moving parts. It is classified under component parts, details or accessories that are not covered in other groups within the mechanical-engineering hierarchy.
[F01D 25/22] using working-fluid or other gaseous fluid as lubricant -- F01D 25/22 relates to component parts, details or accessories for non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines, specifically those that employ the working fluid or another gaseous fluid as a lubricant. It falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and addresses lubrication methods that use the process fluid itself.
[F01D 25/24] Casings -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Casings for turbomachinery and also features relating to turbocharger casings.
[F01D 25/243] Flange connections; Bolting arrangements  F01D25/265  takes precedence -- The subclass F01D 25/243 covers the design and arrangement of flanged joints and the bolting methods used to fasten components in non-positive-displacement machines such as steam turbines, where the parts are not treated in other more specific groups. It addresses the geometry, spacing, tightening sequences and related features of flange-to-flange connections, while being superseded by the more detailed subclass F01D 25/265.
[F01D 25/246] Fastening of diaphragms or stator-rings -- F01D 25/246 concerns the means used to secure diaphragms or stator-rings within non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines. It covers fastening devices, clamps, bolts, welding or other joining techniques employed to attach these components to the machine housing or shaft. The classification applies to component parts and accessories specifically related to the fastening of these elements.
[F01D 25/26] Double casings -- F01D 25/26 covers double-casing structures used as component parts for non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines. The classification refers to a pair of concentric casings that provide additional sealing, insulation, structural support or vibration damping for the turbine rotor and associated hardware. It is applied when the double-casing element is of specific interest separate from other turbine accessories.
[F01D 25/265] Vertically split casings; Clamping arrangements therefor -- The classification covers component parts for non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines that have casings split vertically, including the design and features of the split sections. It also includes the various clamping or fastening arrangements used to hold the split casing halves together during operation.
[F01D 25/28] Supporting or mounting arrangements, e.g. for turbine casing -- F01D 25/28 covers structures and devices that secure, support, or mount turbine casings and related components, such as brackets, frames, and fastening systems. It includes arrangements that hold the casing in position, absorb vibrations, or facilitate alignment, without being part of the turbine's internal rotating or fluid-handling parts.
[F01D 25/285] Temporary support structures, e.g. for testing, assembling, installing, repairing; Assembly methods using such structures -- F01D 25/285 covers temporary support structures used in the testing, assembly, installation or repair of non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines, and the methods of assembling components with these structures.
[F01D 25/30] Exhaust heads, chambers, or the like -- F01D 25/30 pertains to the design and construction of exhaust heads, chambers and related components for non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines. It covers accessories that direct or discharge working fluid from the turbine cylinder, including exhaust nozzles, ducts and liners.
[F01D 25/305] with fluid, e.g. liquid injection -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Exhaust heads or chambers or diffusers comprising an additional injection of fluid such as air or liquids for any desired purpose such as cooling, cleaning of exhaust, deswirling etc.
[F01D 25/32] Collecting of condensation water -- F01D 25/32 covers devices and arrangements for gathering condensate that forms on steam-turbine components or in related piping, such as drains, condensate collectors, separators and associated conduits that channel the water away from the turbine and its auxiliaries. This sub-group addresses the means of collecting, directing, or storing the condensate for reuse, removal or treatment within non-positive displacement machines.
[F01D 25/34] Turning or inching gear -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Auxiliary equipment for rotating the rotor of a gas or steam turbine at low rpm, e.g. for inspection purposes or prevention of sagging and the like  taken from US4430575
[F01D 25/36] using electric motors -- The F01D 25/36 class covers components, details or accessories for non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines in which an electric motor is used to drive the machine or a part of it. It falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, non-positive displacement machines.
[F01D 3/00] Machines or engines with axial-thrust balancing effected by working-fluid -- F01D 3/00 covers machines or engines, such as steam turbines, that use the working-fluid itself to balance axial thrust forces generated during operation. The classification applies to non-positive-displacement engines where fluid flow is directed or staged to produce a counteracting axial force, improving stability and efficiency.
[F01D 3/02] characterised by having one fluid flow in one axial direction and another fluid flow in the opposite direction -- F01D 3/02 covers non-positive displacement machines, such as steam turbines, that balance axial thrust by directing one working fluid in one axial direction while a second fluid flows opposite, providing counter-acting thrust to reduce net axial load.
[F01D 3/025] with a centrally disposed radial stage -- F01D 3/025 covers steam or other non-positive displacement turbines that balance axial thrust using the working fluid and incorporate a radial stage mounted in the centre of the rotor. The central radial stage receives fluid flow radially and contributes to overall power output.
[F01D 3/04] axial thrust being compensated by thrust-balancing dummy piston or the like -- The code F01D 3/04 refers to non-positive-displacement machines, such as steam turbines, that incorporate a thrust-balancing dummy piston or similar device to counteract axial thrust generated by the working fluid. This arrangement helps maintain axial stability without requiring additional bearings or thrust loads. It falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and specifically non-positive-displacement machines.
[F01D 5/00] Blades -- F01D 5/00 covers the blades used in non-positive displacement engines such as steam turbines. It includes the design, shape, arrangement and mounting of rotor and stator blades that guide working fluid to generate torque, as well as the materials and cooling features needed for high-temperature operation.
[F01D 5/005] Repairing methods or devices -- F01D 5/005 covers methods and apparatus for repairing the blades of non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines. It includes techniques for inspecting, cleaning, reshaping, coating, and re-attaching turbine blades, as well as tools and devices that facilitate these maintenance operations.
[F01D 5/02] Blade-carrying members, e.g. rotors -- F01D 5/02 concerns the components that support or carry blades in non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines, including rotors, disks and other blade-holding structures. It addresses the design, mounting and integration of these blade-carrying members within the turbine assembly.
[F01D 5/021] for flow machines or engines with only one axial stage  for more than one stage  F01D5/06
[F01D 5/022] with concentric rows of axial blades -- The subclass defines turbine rotors that incorporate several concentric rows of axial blades, meaning the blades are arranged in multiple rings parallel to the shaft axis, as found in steam turbines and other non-positive displacement engines.
[F01D 5/023] of the screw type -- The F01D 5/023 subclass covers screw-type blades employed in non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines. These blades have a helical or screw-like geometry that converts fluid flow into rotational motion, improving efficiency and pressure handling in turbomachinery.
[F01D 5/025] Fixing blade carrying members on shafts  attachment of a member on a shaft in general  F16D1/06 ; for non-positive displacement pumps  F04D29/00 -- This subclass deals with the means of securing blade-carrying members to a rotating shaft, addressing general attachment methods used in non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines and related pumps. It covers various fixation techniques and their mechanical implementation.
[F01D 5/026] Shaft to shaft connections -- The CPC subclass F01D 5/026 covers the means of connecting one shaft to another within the blade assemblies of non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines. It includes couplings, gears, splines and other mechanical devices that transmit torque between shafts in the turbine's rotating system. This falls under the broader categories of mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and non-positive displacement machines.
[F01D 5/027] Arrangements for balancing  for balancing rotating bodies in general  F16F15/32 ; for compensating imbalance  G01M1/36 -- F01D 5/027 pertains to mechanical arrangements that balance rotating bodies, encompassing general methods for achieving dynamic equilibrium and specific devices for compensating imbalance in engines or turbines. It includes systems such as counter-rotating masses, adjustable blades or hydraulic dampers, and links to related balancing technologies classified under F16F15/32 and G01M1/36.
[F01D 5/028] the rotor disc being formed of sheet laminae  rotor blade aggregates of unitary construction  F01D5/34 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Rotor discs which are made by stacking several sheets of metallic or composite plate like elements together and attaching them to each other, e.g. by gluing or clamping in order to form a single rotor disk or a stacked rotor assembly.
[F01D 5/03] Annular blade-carrying members having blades on the inner periphery of the annulus and extending inwardly radially, i.e. inverted rotors -- F01D 5/03 refers to annular blade carriers in which the blades are mounted on the inner side of the ring and extend inward radially, forming an inverted rotor arrangement. This design is used in non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines, where the blade-carrying disc rotates about a central axis.
[F01D 5/04] for radial-flow machines or engines -- The CPC entry F01D 5/04 refers to the blade arrangements used in radial-flow machines or engines, such as steam turbines, within the broader group of non-positive displacement engines. It covers design features that direct fluid flow radially through the rotor.
[F01D 5/041] of the Ljungström type -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ljungström type machines in which the working fluid usually flows radially outwardly between two counter rotating bladed plates or more generally speaking turbines in which the fluid is expanded in a radial direction passing through consecutive turbine stages, wherein the turbine blades are mounted in circular alternating rows on plate like structures
[F01D 5/043] of the axial inlet- radial outlet, or  vice versa , type -- F01D 5/043 classifies turbine blade designs in which the working fluid enters the blade axially and leaves radially, or conversely enters radially and exits axially. It pertains to non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines and covers the geometry and flow direction of the blades.
[F01D 5/045] the wheel comprising two adjacent bladed wheel portions, e.g. with interengaging blades for damping vibrations -- This subclass covers wheels made of two side-by-side bladed sections whose blades interlock to provide vibration damping in non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines. It falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, non-positive displacement machines, blades.
[F01D 5/046] Heating, heat insulation or cooling means -- This subclass covers heating, heat-insulating, or cooling devices applied to blades of non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines, including heating elements to prevent icing, insulating layers to retain temperature, and cooling passages to remove excess heat for efficient and safe operation.
[F01D 5/048] Form or construction -- This subclass deals with the shape, geometry, and structural features of turbine blades used in non-positive displacement engines such as steam and gas turbines, focusing on blade profile, airfoil shape, and mounting arrangement.
[F01D 5/06] Rotors for more than one axial stage, e.g. of drum or multiple disc type -- F01D 5/06 relates to rotors that incorporate more than one axial stage, such as drum-type or multiple-disc rotors, used in non-positive displacement machines like steam turbines.
[F01D 5/063] Welded rotors  welding  per se   B23K -- F01D 5/063 concerns the welding of turbine rotors themselves, describing welding processes applied directly to the rotor for producing blades of non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines. It addresses the manufacturing technique rather than the assembly of separate parts.
[F01D 5/066] Connecting means for joining rotor-discs or rotor-elements together, e.g. by a central bolt, by clamps -- F01D 5/066 concerns mechanical devices that fasten rotor-discs or other rotor elements in non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines. It covers joining methods like a central bolt, clamps or similar connectors that secure the discs to form a rigid rotor assembly.
[F01D 5/08] Heating, heat-insulating or cooling means -- F01D 5/08 covers the thermal management of turbine blades, including devices that heat, insulate or cool the blades to control temperature during operation. It addresses methods such as internal air passages, external jackets, thermal barrier coatings and steam or oil cooling circuits used to maintain blade integrity and performance.
[F01D 5/081] Cooling fluid being directed on the side of the rotor disc or at the roots of the blades  F01D5/087  takes precedence -- This subclass covers non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines where cooling fluid is directed onto the side of the rotor disc or at the blade roots. It belongs to the hierarchy Mechanical Engineering → Engines or Pumps → Non-positive Displacement Machines - Blades. The later subclass F01D5/087 takes precedence over this entry.
[F01D 5/082] on the side of the rotor disc -- The code F01D 5/082 covers blades that are positioned on the side of a rotor disc in non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines. It relates to the design, mounting or arrangement of side-mounted turbine blades on the disc rather than on its hub or outer rim. This subclass addresses technical features specific to side-located blade configurations.
[F01D 5/084] the fluid circulating at the periphery of a multistage rotor, e.g. of drum type -- F01D 5/084 relates to the fluid that circulates at the periphery of a multistage rotor, such as in drum-type machines, within non-positive displacement engines like steam turbines. The entry addresses how this peripheral flow interacts with blade arrangements in mechanical engineering.
[F01D 5/085] cooling fluid circulating inside the rotor -- The code F01D 5/085 covers arrangements where a cooling fluid is circulated through the rotor of a non-positive-displacement engine such as a steam turbine, providing heat removal from rotor components, especially blades, to maintain temperature and performance.
[F01D 5/087] in the radial passages of the rotor disc -- The code F01D 5/087 covers features that are located in the radial passages of the rotor disc of a non-positive-displacement machine such as a steam turbine. It relates to the design, shaping or arrangement of these passages for directing working fluid or cooling air through the disc.
[F01D 5/088] in a closed cavity -- F01D 5/088 covers blade arrangements located within a sealed enclosure of non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines. It pertains to the design, positioning or operation of blades that operate in a closed cavity, influencing flow and performance inside the turbine housing.
[F01D 5/10] Anti- vibration means -- This subclass deals with devices, structures or methods that reduce vibration of blades in non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines. It includes dampers, flexible mounts, balancing techniques and other means that limit blade oscillations and improve operational stability.
[F01D 5/12] Blades -- F01D 5/12 covers the individual blades and rotor-stage components of non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines and other turbomachinery, focusing on their design, geometry, material and mounting. This subclass is used for inventions that specifically treat the blade itself rather than the whole machine.
[F01D 5/14] Form or construction -- F01D 5/14 covers the shape, geometry and overall structural design of blades used in non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines. It includes specifications of blade profile, attachment method and aerodynamic or hydraulic features that influence performance.
[F01D 5/141] Shape, i.e. outer, aerodynamic form  F01D5/148  -  F01D5/20  take precedence; blade construction  F01D5/147
[F01D 5/142] of the blades of successive rotor or stator blade-rows -- F01D 5/142 covers the design and arrangement of blades that belong to successive rows of rotors or stators in non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines. It addresses the geometry, spacing and aerodynamic interaction of these blade-rows within the turbine flow path.
[F01D 5/143] Contour of the outer or inner working fluid flow path wall, i.e. shroud or hub contour -- The code F01D 5/143 relates to the shape of the shroud or hub that defines the outer or inner wall of the working-fluid flow path in non-positive-displacement machines such as steam turbines. It covers the contouring of these flow-path walls to influence fluid dynamics and turbine performance.
[F01D 5/145] Means for influencing boundary layers or secondary circulations  for compressors  F04D29/68
[F01D 5/146] of blades with tandem configuration, split blades or slotted blades -- The subclass F01D 5/146 concerns the design of turbine blades for non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines, specifically those that employ a tandem arrangement, are split into separate sections, or contain slots or openings to modify the flow of working fluid.
[F01D 5/147] Construction, i.e. structural features, e.g. of weight-saving hollow blades  F01D5/148 ,  F01D5/16  and  F01D5/20  take precedence; blade shape  F01D5/141 ; blades with cooling or heating channels or cavities  F01D5/18 ; heating, heat-insulating or cooling means on blades  F01D5/18 -- F01D 5/147 covers the construction of blades for non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines, focusing on structural features that reduce weight, for example hollow blades. It includes design aspects like internal voids, reinforcement, and integration of cooling or heating channels, with related sub-groups addressing blade shape and thermal management.
[F01D 5/148] Blades with variable camber, e.g. by ejection of fluid -- F01D 5/148 covers turbine or other engine blades whose camber can be varied during operation, for example by ejecting fluid to change their shape. This allows the blade pitch to be adjusted to optimise performance under different load conditions and falls within non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines.
[F01D 5/16] for counteracting blade vibration -- **Definition:** This place covers:  All aspects of blade vibration and countermeasures thereof if related to the blade itself and its construction
[F01D 5/18] Hollow blades, -- Hollow blades are turbine or pump blades that contain internal cavities or channels rather than being solid. The voids reduce mass and inertia, improve cooling or allow fluid flow, enhancing efficiency and dynamic response in steam turbines and similar non-positive displacement machines. This design is classified under F01D 5/18.
[F01D 5/181] Blades having a closed internal cavity containing a cooling medium, e.g. sodium -- The code F01D 5/181 designates blades for non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines that incorporate a closed internal cavity through which a cooling medium-commonly liquid sodium-is circulated to remove heat from the blade during operation.
[F01D 5/182] Transpiration cooling -- The F01D 5/182 class covers transpiration cooling of blades in non-positive-displacement machines such as steam turbines. It addresses methods where a coolant is passed through a porous blade surface or interior, creating a protective film that removes heat and reduces thermal stress during operation.
[F01D 5/183] Blade walls being porous -- F01D 5/183 relates to blade walls that are porous, allowing fluid to pass through the blade structure. This feature is employed in non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines to enhance cooling, reduce stresses, or improve aerodynamic performance.
[F01D 5/184] Blade walls being made of perforated sheet laminae -- F01D 5/184 pertains to turbine blades whose wall structure is formed from one or more layers of perforated sheet material, creating a laminated blade wall. The perforations reduce weight and allow fluid flow through the blade, while the laminae provide the required strength and stiffness for steam-turbine applications.
[F01D 5/185] Liquid cooling  F01D5/181  takes precedence -- F01D 5/185 pertains to liquid cooling methods applied to the blades of non-positive displacement machines such as steam turbines. It details systems that circulate coolant directly on or within turbine blades to manage operating temperatures and improve efficiency. This subclass follows the broader category of liquid cooling for such engines.
[F01D 5/186] Film cooling  F01D5/187  takes precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  All aspects of film cooled blades, arrangement and shape of film cooling holes  **Glossary:** - Shower head (cooling): ditto - Film cooling: expression used in order to describe the cooling technology used in order to cool a blade with an external layer of coolant
