[F01L 1/16] Silencing impact -- F01L 1/16 covers techniques for reducing or silencing the noise produced by valve-gear or valve arrangements in positive-displacement machines such as internal-combustion piston engines, focusing on impact-damping measures that do not involve variable fluid distribution.
[F01L 1/18] Rocking arms or levers -- F01L 1/18 covers rocking arms or levers used in valve-gear for positive-displacement engines such as internal-combustion piston engines. These components pivot to transmit motion to valves, providing a simple mechanism for opening and closing without variable fluid distribution. The class falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and cyclically operating valves for machines.
[F01L 1/181] Centre pivot rocking arms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example
[F01L 1/182] the rocking arm being pivoted about an individual fulcrum, i.e. not about a common shaft -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example

[F01L 1/185] Overhead end-pivot rocking arms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example
[F01L 1/20] Adjusting or compensating clearance -- The code F01L 1/20 covers mechanisms that adjust or compensate the clearance between a valve and its seat or guide in the valve-gear of internal-combustion engines, ensuring proper timing and sealing despite wear or temperature changes. Such adjustments may be automatic or manual and are applied to non-steam positive-displacement machines.
[F01L 1/205] by means of shims or the like -- F01L 1/205 covers valve-gear or valve arrangements for positive-displacement engines other than steam that are set by means of shims or similar inserts. The shims provide a fixed, non-variable means of adjusting clearances or valve lift.
[F01L 1/22] automatically, e.g. mechanically -- This subclass covers valve-gear or valve arrangements for positive-displacement engines, such as internal-combustion piston engines, where the valve motion is provided automatically-typically by a mechanical linkage, cam or other self-acting device-and does not include provisions for variable fluid distribution. It is part of the hierarchy Mechanical Engineering → Engines or Pumps → Cyclically Operating Valves for Machines or Engines.
[F01L 1/24] by fluid means, e.g. hydraulically -- F01L 1/24 covers valve-gear or valve arrangements for positive-displacement machines or engines other than steam engines that are operated by fluid power, such as hydraulic actuation. The subclass groups designs where the valve motion is produced by hydraulic or other fluid means rather than mechanical linkages.
[F01L 1/2405] by means of a hydraulic adjusting device located between the cylinder head and rocker arm -- F01L 1/2405 covers valve-gear arrangements for positive-displacement engines, such as internal-combustion piston engines, in which a hydraulic adjusting device is placed between the cylinder head and the rocker arm. The hydraulic mechanism fine-tunes valve timing or lift without altering the fluid distribution to the engine.
[F01L 1/2411] by means of a hydraulic adjusting device located between the valve stem and rocker arm -- The code F01L 1/2411 covers valve-gear or valve arrangements for positive-displacement engines-such as internal-combustion piston engines-where a hydraulic adjusting device is placed between the valve stem and the rocker arm to control valve operation without varying fluid distribution.
[F01L 1/2416] by means of a hydraulic adjusting device attached to an articulated rocker -- F01L 1/2416 covers valve-gear arrangements for positive-displacement engines other than steam that use a hydraulic adjusting device mounted on an articulated rocker to vary valve timing or lift. The hydraulic mechanism provides fine, continuous adjustment without changing the fluid distribution path, enabling precise control of intake or exhaust valve operation in internal-combustion piston engines.
[F01L 1/2422] by means or a hydraulic adjusting device located between the push rod and rocker arm -- F01L 1/2422 classifies valve-gear arrangements for positive-displacement engines in which a hydraulic adjusting device is placed between the push-rod and the rocker arm. The device alters the motion transmitted to the valve, providing fine control of valve timing or lift without changing fluid distribution.
[F01L 1/245] Hydraulic tappets -- Hydraulic tappets are oil-filled lifters used in the valve gear of internal-combustion piston engines to automatically eliminate valve clearance by expanding under pressure, thereby maintaining proper valve timing without the need for manual adjustment.
[F01L 1/25] between cam and valve stem -- The F01L 1/25 entry concerns the mechanical link between cam and valve stem in positive-displacement engines such as internal-combustion piston engines. It covers designs, bearings, guides and other features that transmit cam motion to the valve stem, excluding systems that vary fluid distribution.
[F01L 1/252] for side-valve engines -- F01L 1/252 covers valve-gear or valve arrangements used in side-valve internal-combustion engines, where the intake and exhaust valves are placed beside the cylinder rather than overhead. The classification applies to positive-displacement machines that lack mechanisms for varying fluid distribution, focusing on the fixed geometry of the side-valve layout.
[F01L 1/255] between cam and rocker arm -- F01L 1/255 covers the elements positioned between the cam and the rocker arm in the valve gear of positive-displacement internal-combustion engines. These components transmit cam lift to the rocker arm, controlling valve opening without variable fluid distribution. The classification is used for designs of fixed cam-rocker linkages in piston engine valve arrangements.
[F01L 1/26] characterised by the provision of two or more valves operated simultaneously by same transmitting-gear -- F01L 1/26 relates to valve-gear arrangements for positive-displacement engines in which two or more valves are actuated at the same time by a common transmitting gear, a configuration used especially in internal-combustion engines where simultaneous valve operation is required without variable fluid distribution.
[F01L 1/262] with valve stems disposed radially from a centre which is substantially the centre of curvature of the upper wall surface of a combustion chamber  F01L1/265  takes precedence
[F01L 1/265] peculiar to machines or engines with three or more intake valves per cylinder -- F01L 1/265 classifies valve-gear or valve arrangements used in positive-displacement machines or engines, other than steam engines, that have three or more intake valves per cylinder. It applies to internal-combustion piston engines where the valve system provides fixed, not variable, fluid distribution. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering → engines or pumps → cyclically operating valves for machines or engines.
[F01L 1/267] with means for varying the timing or the lift of the valves -- F01L 1/267 pertains to valve-gear or valve arrangements for positive-displacement machines like internal-combustion piston engines that incorporate mechanisms for varying the timing or the lift of the valves.
[F01L 1/28] characterised by the provision of coaxial valves -- F01L 1/28 covers valve-gear or valve arrangements for positive-displacement machines, notably internal-combustion piston engines, where the inlet and exhaust valves are mounted on a common axis so that they operate coaxially, and the design does not include means for varying the fluid distribution during operation.
[F01L 1/285] Coaxial intake and exhaust valves -- F01L 1/285 describes valve-gear arrangements in which the intake and exhaust valves are mounted on a common shaft or share the same central axis, a configuration used in internal-combustion engines to simplify the valve train and enhance packaging efficiency.
[F01L 1/30] characterised by the provision of positively opened and closed valves, i.e. desmodromic valves -- F01L 1/30 covers valve-gear or arrangements for positive-displacement engines that use desmodromic actuation, meaning the valves are mechanically forced open and closed without reliance on spring-based closing. This sub-class applies to internal-combustion piston engines and similar machines where variable fluid distribution is not provided.
[F01L 13/00] Modifications of valve-gear to facilitate reversing, braking, starting, changing compression ratio, or other specific operations -- F01L 13/00 covers design changes to the valve gear of reciprocating engines or pumps that enable special functions such as reversing direction, braking, starting, altering the compression ratio, or performing other specific operations. The classification groups mechanisms that modify timing, lift, or actuation of cyclic valves to achieve these purposes.
[F01L 13/0005] Deactivating valves -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Structure of valve drive switchable to a valve deactivating mode for lost motion of the cam follower, e.g. two-part cam followers with switchable locking means there between.
[F01L 13/0015] for optimising engine performances by modifying valve lift according to various working parameters, e.g. rotational speed, load, torque -- The code F01L 13/0015 covers valve-gear modifications that adjust valve lift in response to operating conditions such as engine speed, load or torque, aiming to improve overall performance, efficiency or emissions. It falls under cyclically operating valves for machines or engines within mechanical engineering.
[F01L 13/0021] by modification of rocker arm ratio -- F01L 13/0021 classifies cyclically operating engine or pump valves whose gear is altered by changing the rocker-arm ratio to enable reversing, braking, starting, variable compression or other specific functions.
[F01L 13/0026] by means of an eccentric -- F01L 13/0026 covers cyclic valve gear for engines in which an eccentric is employed to modify the valve mechanism for operations such as reversing, braking, starting or adjusting compression ratio. The classification falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and cyclically operating valves for machines or engines.
[F01L 13/0031] by modification of tappet or pushrod length -- F01L 13/0031 concerns adjustments to the length of tappets or pushrods in the valve gear of engines or pumps, enabling actions such as reversing, braking, starting, or changing the compression ratio. It is a sub-category of mechanical engineering, specifically under engines or pumps and cyclically operating valves.
[F01L 13/0036] the valves being driven by two or more cams with different shape, size or timing or a single cam profiled in axial and radial direction -- F01L 13/0036 covers valve-gear arrangements in which the valve is driven by two or more cams that differ in shape, size or timing, or by a single cam whose profile varies axially and radially. These configurations are employed to provide reversing, braking, starting, variable compression or other specific operations in engines and machines.
[F01L 13/0042] with cams being profiled in axial and radial direction -- F01L 13/0042 covers valve mechanisms for engines or pumps in which the cam surfaces are shaped both axially and radially. The profile variation is used to modify valve timing for operations such as reversing, braking, starting or adjusting compression ratio. This subclass falls under cyclically operating valves within mechanical engineering.
[F01L 13/0047] the movement of the valves resulting from the sum of the simultaneous actions of at least two cams, the cams being independently variable in phase in respect of each other -- This subclass concerns valve gear in engines where the valve motion results from the combined action of two or more cams whose phases can be varied independently of each other. Such arrangements are used to modify valve timing for functions such as reversing, braking, starting or changing compression ratio.
[F01L 13/0057] by splittable or deformable cams -- This subclass covers valve-gear arrangements in engines or pumps where the cam can be split or deformed, enabling the timing of cyclically operating valves to be altered for functions such as reversing, braking, starting or changing the compression ratio. It is a specific modification of valve-gear to facilitate these operations.
[F01L 13/0063] by modification of cam contact point by displacing an intermediate lever or wedge-shaped intermediate element, e.g. Tourtelot -- F01L 13/0063 refers to cyclic valve gear modifications that change the cam contact point by shifting an intermediate lever or a wedge-shaped element, such as in the Tourtelot arrangement, to aid reversing, braking, starting, or altering compression. It falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and cyclically operating valves for machines or engines.
[F01L 13/02] for reversing -- F01L 13/02 refers to cyclically operating valves in engines or pumps that are modified to enable reversing of the machine. It falls under mechanical engineering > engines or pumps > cyclically operating valves for machines or engines, specifically addressing valve-gear changes for reversal.
[F01L 13/04] for starting by means of fluid pressure -- F01L 13/04 pertains to modifications of valve-gear in engines or pumps that enable starting by means of fluid pressure, forming part of the cyclically operating valve subgroup within mechanical engineering. It specifies fluid-pressurised starting mechanisms for these machines.
[F01L 13/06] for braking -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Structure of valve drive switchable to an engine braking mode, e.g. actuator opening exhaust valve for compression release event or cam follower switchable to a configuration to transmit to the valve the lift of a cam braking lobe.
[F01L 13/065] Compression release engine retarders of the "Jacobs Manufacturing" type -- F01L 13/065 covers compression-release engine retarders of the Jacobs Manufacturing design, a valve-gear modification that momentarily opens the cylinder's compression valve to reduce engine speed or provide braking. The device is used in internal-combustion engines to retard acceleration, aid stopping and control torque without altering the basic engine architecture.
[F01L 13/08] for decompression, e.g. during starting -- F01L 13/08 covers valve-gear modifications that provide decompression, primarily to ease engine starting. It includes designs that temporarily relieve cylinder pressure by altering valve timing or position, allowing smoother crankshaft rotation during start-up.
[F01L 13/085] the valve-gear having an auxiliary cam protruding from the main cam profile -- This subclass covers valve gear in which an auxiliary cam projects from the main cam profile, adding motion to the valve for functions such as reversing, braking, starting or adjusting compression in cyclically operating engines or pumps. The auxiliary cam is integrated with the primary cam and modifies valve timing or lift to achieve specific operational requirements.
[F01L 1/32] characterised by the provision of means for rotating lift valves, e.g. to diminish wear -- F01L 1/32 covers valve-gear or valve arrangements for positive-displacement machines such as internal-combustion piston engines, in which the lift valves are rotated by a mechanism to diminish wear, rather than relying on variable fluid distribution.
[F01L 1/34] characterised by the provision of means for changing the timing of the valves without changing the duration of opening -- This CPC subclass covers valve gear or arrangements for positive-displacement engines that include mechanisms allowing the timing of valve opening to be altered while the opening duration remains constant. Such devices adjust the moment of valve actuation without modifying how long the valve stays open, improving engine performance and control.
[F01L 1/344] changing the angular relationship between crankshaft and camshaft, e.g. using helicoidal gear -- This subclass covers mechanisms that modify the phase angle between crankshaft and camshaft, such as helicoidal gears, to alter valve timing in internal-combustion engines. It pertains to valve-gear arrangements for positive-displacement machines that lack variable fluid distribution.
[F01L 1/34403] using helically teethed sleeve or gear moving axially between crankshaft and camshaft -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example
[F01L 1/34406] the helically teethed sleeve being located in the camshaft driving pulley -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example
[F01L 1/34409] by torque-responsive means -- The subclass covers valve-gear or valve arrangements for positive-displacement engines in which the valve's timing or opening is controlled by a mechanism that reacts to the engine's torque, such as a torque-sensing lever, cam or diaphragm, enabling automatic adjustment without a separate fluid-distribution system.
[F01L 1/34413] using composite camshafts, e.g. with cams being able to move relative to the camshaft -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example  JP59183009 A
[F01L 1/34416] using twisted cams -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F01L 1/3442] using hydraulic chambers with variable volume to transmit the rotating force -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example  DE19745908 A
[F01L 1/348] by means acting on timing belts or chains -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example
[F01L 1/352] using bevel or epicyclic gear -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example
[F01L 1/356] making the angular relationship oscillate -- This subgroup covers designs where the angular relationship between rotating parts of a valve-gear system is made to oscillate, such as cams or eccentric drives that periodically reverse the angular position to control valve timing in positive-displacement engines. It applies to internal-combustion piston engines and similar machines where fluid distribution is not variable.
[F01L 1/36] peculiar to machines or engines of specific type other than four-stroke cycle -- F01L 1/36 covers valve-gear or valve-arrangements that are specially designed for particular types of positive-displacement engines other than the conventional four-stroke cycle, such as two-stroke, rotary, or other non-standard internal-combustion machines. The classification is used for patents describing unique valve mechanisms tailored to those specific engine configurations.
[F01L 1/38] for engines with other than four-stroke cycle, e.g. with two-stroke cycle -- F01L 1/38 covers valve-gear and valve arrangements for positive-displacement engines that use cycles other than the four-stroke, such as two-stroke internal-combustion engines, where the design does not provide for variable fluid distribution. It falls under cyclically operating valves for machines or engines.
[F01L 1/40] for engines with scavenging charge near top dead centre position, e.g. by overlapping inlet and exhaust time -- The F01L 1/40 classification covers valve-gear or arrangements for internal-combustion piston engines in which the scavenging charge is introduced close to top dead centre, typically by overlapping the inlet and exhaust periods, without any mechanism for varying fluid distribution. It applies to positive-displacement machines that are not steam engines.
[F01L 1/42] for machines or engines characterised by cylinder arrangements, e.g. star or fan
[F01L 1/44] Multiple-valve gear or arrangements, not provided for in preceding subgroups, e.g. with lift and different valves -- F01L 1/44 relates to valve-gear or arrangements for positive-displacement machines or internal-combustion engines that use multiple valves but are not covered by earlier subgroups. It includes novel configurations such as combined lift mechanisms and different valve types operating together, providing a way to control fluid flow without variable distribution features. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and cyclically operating valves.
[F01L 1/443] comprising a lift valve and at least one rotary valve -- F01L 1/443 covers valve gear for positive-displacement engines, such as internal-combustion piston engines, that combines a lift (reciprocating) valve with one or more rotary valves. The arrangement provides sequential opening and closing of intake or exhaust ports without any mechanism for varying fluid distribution.
[F01L 1/446] comprising a lift valve and at least one reed valve -- The subclass F01L 1/446 covers valve-gear arrangements for positive-displacement engines that combine a lift valve with at least one reed valve, a configuration used mainly in internal-combustion piston engines to control fluid flow without a variable distribution mechanism.
[F01L 1/46] Component parts, details, or accessories, not provided for in preceding subgroups -- This subclass (F01L 1/46) covers individual parts, detailed elements and accessories of valve-gear for positive-displacement engines such as internal-combustion pistons, when those items are not already classified in earlier groups. It includes items like retainers, springs, seals, adjusters, or other ancillary components that complete or support the valve arrangement but are not the main valve mechanism itself.
[F01L 1/462] Valve return spring arrangements -- F01L 1/462 concerns the spring mechanisms that return valves to their closed position in cyclically operating valve gear for positive-displacement engines such as internal-combustion piston engines. It covers arrangements where the spring provides a preset closing force without any provision for variable fluid distribution, distinguishing these from more complex, variable-flow valve systems.
[F01L 1/465] Pneumatic arrangements -- This CPC subclass covers pneumatic arrangements that power valve gear or valve assemblies in positive-displacement machines such as internal-combustion piston engines, where the timing is fixed and not designed for variable fluid distribution. It falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and cyclically operating valves for machines or engines.
[F01L 15/00] Valve-gear or valve arrangements specially adapted for steam engines, or specially adapted for other positive-displacement machines or engines with variable working-fluid distribution -- F01L 15/00 covers valve-gear and valve arrangements that are specially designed for steam engines or other positive-displacement machines where the distribution of the working fluid varies during operation. The classification includes mechanisms that control timing, sequencing and admission of steam or fluid to improve efficiency, power output or responsiveness in cyclically operating engines. It falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and cyclically operating valves for machines or engines.
[F01L 15/02] with valves other than cylindrical, sleeve, or part-annularly-shaped, e.g. flat D-valves -- F01L 15/02 relates to valve-gear or arrangements for steam engines or other positive-displacement machines in which the valves are not cylindrical, sleeve or part-annular, such as flat D-shaped valves. It is a subclass of cyclically operating valves for machines or engines within mechanical engineering, covering designs that provide variable working-fluid distribution without the traditional round-valve forms.
[F01L 15/04] main valve being combined with auxiliary valve -- F01L 15/04 classifies valve-gear arrangements in which the main valve of a cyclically operating system is combined with an auxiliary valve to provide coordinated control of fluid flow. The configuration is used in steam engines and other positive-displacement machines to enhance timing, distribution and regulation of the working fluid.
[F01L 15/06] of Meyer or Rider type, i.e. in which the expansion is varied at the expansion valve itself -- F01L 15/06 covers valve gear of the Meyer or Rider type in which the steam expansion is adjusted directly at the expansion valve, allowing variable cutoff for engines or pumps. This arrangement is used in steam engines and other positive-displacement machines to control the work fluid distribution.
[F01L 15/08] with cylindrical, sleeve, or part-annularly-shaped valves -- F01L 15/08 concerns valve gear or arrangements for steam and other positive-displacement engines that use cylindrical, sleeve or part-annular valves. It is classified under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and cyclically operating valves for machines or engines.
[F01L 15/10] with main slide valve and auxiliary valve dragged thereby -- This CPC subclass covers valve-gear arrangements for steam or other positive-displacement engines where an auxiliary valve is moved automatically by the motion of a main slide valve. It applies to designs that use the main valve's travel to drag a secondary valve for timing or fluid distribution control.
[F01L 15/12] characterised by having means for effecting pressure equilibrium between two different cylinder spaces at idling -- F01L 15/12 designates valve gear or arrangements that incorporate a mechanism to balance the pressure between two separate cylinder spaces while the engine is idling. The feature ensures equalization of working-fluid pressure, reducing strain and improving readiness for subsequent operation. It is used primarily in steam and other positive-displacement engines.
[F01L 15/14] Arrangements with several co-operating main valves, e.g. reciprocatory and rotary -- The subclass covers configurations where a machine or engine uses multiple main valves that work together, such as combinations of reciprocating and rotary valves. These arrangements coordinate valve motion to control fluid flow in cyclically operating engines, improving distribution and timing of the working fluid.
[F01L 15/16] with reciprocatory slide valves only -- The subclass F01L 15/16 covers valve-gear arrangements for engines or machines that use only reciprocating slide valves to control the timing and distribution of working fluid. It applies to steam engines and other positive-displacement engines where the valve mechanism is limited to sliding motion without rotating or oscillating components.
[F01L 15/18] Valves arrangements not provided for in preceding subgroups of this main group -- F01L 15/18 classifies valve-gear and valve arrangements that are specially adapted for steam engines or other positive-displacement machines and that do not fall within any of the earlier defined subgroups of this main group. It covers novel or unconventional configurations for distributing working fluid in cyclically operating engines or pumps.
[F01L 15/20] Component parts, details, or accessories, not provided for in preceding subgroups of this main group
[F01L 17/00] Slide valve-gear or valve arrangements with cylindrical, sleeve, or part annularly-shaped valves surrounding working cylinder or piston -- F01L 17/00 pertains to slide valve gears and valve configurations in which a cylindrical, sleeve-like or partially annular valve surrounds the working cylinder or piston, providing cyclically operated valve control for engines or pumps. These arrangements are used to regulate fluid flow in mechanical engineering systems.
[F01L 17/02] Drive or adjustment during operation, peculiar thereto, e.g. for reciprocating and oscillating movements or for several valves one inside the other
[F01L 19/00] Slide valve-gear or valve arrangements with reciprocatory and other movement of same valve, other than provided for in -- F01L 19/00 covers slide-valve gear and arrangements where a valve moves reciprocally and in other ways, excluding configurations defined in earlier subclasses. It includes mechanisms that combine sliding motion with additional movements to control fluid flow in engines or pumps. This subclass groups innovative valve-actuation designs not covered elsewhere in the cyclically operating valve hierarchy.
[F01L 19/02] Drive or adjustment during operation, peculiar thereto -- F01L 19/02 covers devices that provide drive or adjustment to slide-type valve gear while a machine or engine is running, including mechanisms that change valve timing, lift or travel during operation to improve performance, control, or efficiency.
[F01L 2001/028] Pre-assembled timing arrangement, e.g. located in a cassette -- This subclass covers ready-made timing modules that are supplied as a complete unit, often enclosed in a cassette, and can be installed in a machine to provide predetermined start-stop or sequencing functions without further adjustment.
[F01L 2001/0471] Assembled camshafts -- F01L 2001/0471 covers camshafts that are constructed from two or more separate components that are assembled together, such as individual lobes, bearings or shafts, to form a complete camshaft. The classification applies to designs where the camshaft is built from pre-formed parts rather than being machined as a single piece, and includes methods of joining, aligning or securing the components for use in internal-combustion engines.
[F01L 2001/0473] Composite camshafts, e.g. with cams or cam sleeve being able to move relative to the inner camshaft or a cam adjusting rod -- This subclass covers camshaft assemblies made from multiple components where the outer cam or a cam sleeve can be moved relative to an inner shaft or a cam-adjusting rod, enabling variable valve timing or wear compensation. It includes designs in which the cams are mounted on a separate sleeve that can be displaced axially or rotationally with respect to the main shaft.
[F01L 2001/0475] Hollow camshafts -- The code F01L 2001/0475 designates camshafts that are formed with a hollow interior, typically used in internal-combustion engines. Such camshafts reduce weight, improve balance and may allow coolant or oil to circulate within the shaft, enhancing thermal management and lubrication efficiency. This subclass falls under F01L 2001 for camshafts in engine valve-actuation systems.
[F01L 2001/0476] Camshaft bearings -- F01L 2001/0476 covers camshaft bearings, the supporting elements that hold a camshaft in place within an internal-combustion engine and allow it to rotate with minimal friction. It includes various bearing types such as plain, roller or fluid-film bearings used to maintain precise camshaft alignment and motion.
[F01L 2001/0478] Torque pulse compensated camshafts -- The CPC entry F01L 2001/0478 covers camshafts that incorporate torque-pulse compensation, meaning the camshaft design or associated mechanisms reduce the fluctuations in engine torque caused by valve actuation. Such camshafts are used in internal-combustion engines to improve smoothness, efficiency and durability by balancing the intermittent forces generated during each intake and exhaust cycle.
[F01L 2001/0535] Single overhead camshafts [SOHC] -- The CPC subclass F01L 2001/0535 covers internal combustion engines that use a single overhead camshaft (SOHC) to operate the valves. It includes designs where one camshaft is positioned above the cylinder head and drives all intake and exhaust valves, typically via rocker arms or followers. This classification is used for patents describing SOHC valve-train configurations.
[F01L 2001/0537] Double overhead camshafts [DOHC] -- F01L 2001/0537 designates double-overhead camshaft (DOHC) configurations in internal combustion engines, where two camshafts sit above the cylinder head to operate intake and exhaust valves separately, enhancing valve timing precision and overall performance.
[F01L 2001/054] Camshafts in cylinder block -- The code F01L 2001/054 covers camshafts that are cast or otherwise formed as part of the cylinder block of an internal-combustion engine. It includes designs where the camshaft lobes are integral with the block, eliminating a separate camshaft housing. This integration is used to reduce weight, improve rigidity and simplify assembly.
[F01L 2001/186] Split rocking arms, e.g. rocker arms having two articulated parts and means for varying the relative position of these parts or for selectively connecting the parts to move in unison -- The CPC subclass F01L 2001/186 relates to split rocking arms, such as rocker arms built from two articulated sections, and includes mechanisms that allow the relative position of the sections to be varied or that can selectively lock the sections so they operate in unison.
[F01L 2001/187] Clips, e.g. for retaining rocker arm on pivot -- The CPC subclass F01L 2001/187 covers clips used to retain a rocker arm on its pivot in engine valve trains, including spring-type, snap-ring and other fast-acting retaining devices that secure the rocker arm during operation.
[F01L 2001/188] Fulcrums at upper surface -- This subclass covers wind-turbine blade designs that incorporate a fulcrum or hinge located on the upper-surface of the blade, allowing pitch adjustment or folding of the blade for control and safety purposes.
[F01L 2001/2427] by means of an hydraulic adjusting device located between cam and push rod -- This CPC subgroup refers to hydraulic pumps in which a hydraulic adjusting device is placed between the cam and the push rod to vary valve timing or stroke length. The arrangement uses fluid pressure to fine-tune the cam-push rod interaction, improving pump efficiency and load control.
[F01L 2001/2433] Self contained, e.g. sealed hydraulic lash adjusters -- Self contained hydraulic lash adjusters are devices integrated into a valve train that automatically compensate for wear by using hydraulic pressure, eliminating the need for external adjustment. They are sealed to protect internal components from contaminants and to keep valve clearance consistent.
[F01L 2001/2438] with means permitting forced opening of check valve -- The code F01L 2001/2438 classifies pump, compressor or fan assemblies that incorporate a device which can forcibly open a check valve, allowing backflow or startup when the normal pressure gradient would keep the valve closed. Typical solutions include spring-biased, pilot-operated or mechanical actuators that overcome the valve's retaining force. This feature is used to improve priming, start-up or safety functions in fluid-handling equipment.
[F01L 2001/2444] Details relating to the hydraulic feeding circuit, e.g. lifter oil manifold assembly [LOMA] -- The CPC class F01L covers internal combustion engines with fuel injection, and the subgroup 2001/2444 specifies features of the hydraulic feeding circuit, such as lifter oil manifold assemblies that distribute oil to valve lifters. This entry pertains to the design and arrangement of components that ensure proper lubrication and operation of hydraulic valve-actuation systems.
[F01L 2001/256] between cam and push rod -- This CPC subgroup covers devices or arrangements placed between a cam and a push rod in internal-combustion piston engines, including intermediate levers, followers, tappets or other mechanisms that transmit the cam's reciprocating motion to the push rod for valve actuation.
[F01L 2001/34423] Details relating to the hydraulic feeding circuit -- The CPC subclass F01L 2001/34423 covers technical features that describe the hydraulic feeding circuit of a machine, including the arrangement, components and control of the pump-driven network that supplies fluid to actuators. It pertains to the design, operation and optimization of the feeding path delivering pressurised hydraulic fluid.
[F01L 2001/34426] Oil control valves -- Oil control valves are devices that regulate the flow, pressure or direction of oil within engines, hydraulic circuits, or lubrication systems, ensuring proper lubrication, cooling and power transmission. The classification covers valves that open, close or modulate oil passages, including relief, pressure-gradient and metering types used in internal-combustion and industrial machines.
[F01L 2001/3443] Solenoid driven oil control valves -- F01L 2001/3443 covers solenoid-driven oil control valves used in hydraulic and lubrication systems. The classification includes valves that regulate oil flow or pressure by means of an electromagnetic solenoid, such as those found in internal-combustion engine oil pumps, transmission systems, and other fluid-power applications.
[F01L 2001/34433] Location oil control valves -- F01L 2001/34433 refers to oil control valves that are mounted at specific locations within hydraulic circuits to regulate, direct or block fluid flow. The classification covers devices such as pressure relief, shuttle and flow-control valves positioned on pumps, actuators or distribution blocks, used in mobile and industrial machinery.
[F01L 2001/34436] Features or method for avoiding malfunction due to foreign matters in oil -- This CPC subclass deals with technical solutions that prevent or mitigate malfunctions in oil-handling or oil-processing equipment caused by foreign objects such as particles, contaminants, or debris. It includes designs, detection systems, barriers, filtration, or control methods that keep foreign matter from interfering with moving parts, seals or sensors, thereby maintaining reliable operation.
[F01L 2001/3444] Oil filters -- F01L 2001/3444 relates to oil filters used in fluid lubrication systems for internal combustion engines, industrial machinery and other equipment. It includes devices that remove contaminants from lubricating oil, such as screen, mesh, paper, synthetic media or centrifugal filters, and may cover filter housings, replacement elements and integrated filtration units.
[F01L 2001/34443] Cleaning control of oil control valves -- F01L covers equipment for oil and gas extraction, and the 2001/34443 entry specifically deals with cleaning control of oil control valves, describing methods and apparatus designed to keep these valves free of deposits and ensure reliable operation.
[F01L 2001/34446] Fluid accumulators for the feeding circuit -- The CPC subclass F01L 2001/34446 covers fluid accumulators that are part of a feeding circuit in hydraulic or pneumatic systems, storing fluid under pressure to smooth flow, compensate pressure variations, or provide emergency power. It includes designs where the accumulator is integrated directly into the feed line of the fluid-power system.
[F01L 2001/3445] Details relating to the hydraulic means for changing the angular relationship -- F01L 2001/3445 covers hydraulic devices that alter the angular relationship between machine components, such as cylinders, rotary actuators, or hydraulic gear systems used to tilt, swing, or rotate shafts, arms or other parts. The entry includes details on the construction, arrangement and control of these hydraulic means.
[F01L 2001/34453] Locking means between driving and driven members -- The code F01L 2001/34453 covers devices that provide a locking connection between a driving element and a driven element, ensuring torque transmission without slip. It includes couplings, clutches, brakes and similar mechanisms that mechanically link rotating or reciprocating parts to prevent relative motion during operation.
[F01L 2001/34456] Locking in only one position -- The CPC entry F01L 2001/34456 covers mechanisms that secure a component-such as a valve, door, or lever-so it can be set in only a single, predetermined position. It relates to locking devices that prevent movement to any other position, typically for safety, control or operational reliability in power-plant or boiler equipment.
[F01L 2001/34459] Locking in multiple positions -- The entry covers pump or valve mechanisms that can be secured in several predetermined angular positions, allowing the device to be locked at multiple settings for variable displacement, flow control or safety shut-off. It pertains to designs where the locking arrangement provides selectable positions rather than a single fixed lock.
[F01L 2001/34463] Locking position intermediate between most retarded and most advanced positions -- The entry covers hydraulic turbines with adjustable runner blades where a locking mechanism can secure the blades at a pitch angle that lies between the extreme retarded (feathered) and extreme advanced (full-open) positions, enabling intermediate power or flow regulation. It typically applies to Kaplan or propeller-type turbines with pitch-control systems.
[F01L 2001/34466] with multiple locking devices -- The code refers to wind-turbine equipment, especially rotors or blades, that employ two or more locking devices to secure a position such as pitch or yaw, providing redundant or combined locking functions for safety, transport or maintenance.
[F01L 2001/34469] Lock movement parallel to camshaft axis -- The subclass includes mechanisms that lock a valve or locking element so that its motion runs parallel to the camshaft axis, allowing precise control of valve timing or secure locking during engine operation.
[F01L 2001/34473] Lock movement perpendicular to camshaft axis -- The CPC entry F01L 2001/34473 covers mechanisms for preventing or restraining axial movement of a camshaft by locking it in a direction perpendicular to the camshaft's longitudinal axis. It includes designs such as axial locks, stops or engagement devices that secure the camshaft position during operation or assembly.
[F01L 2001/34476] Restrict range locking means -- F01L 2001/34476 covers mechanisms that limit the movement of a component to a predefined range by means of a lock, such as a mechanical stop, hydraulic lock, or electronic control, used in machines that convert solid materials into pulp, powder or flakes. The locking means ensures the tool or cutter cannot travel beyond the set limits, improving safety and process control.
[F01L 2001/34479] Sealing of phaser devices -- F01L 2001/34479 covers technologies for sealing phaser devices, which generate high-energy pulses in fluid or gas systems. The entry includes designs for static and dynamic seals, gaskets, O-rings and seal-monitoring methods that prevent leakage and maintain performance under the rapid pressure changes typical of phaser operation.
[F01L 2001/34483] Phaser return springs -- The CPC subclass F01L 2001/34483 relates to springs that return a phaser to its initial position in pumps or fluid-handling devices. These return springs are used to reset the phasing element after each cycle, maintaining proper timing and preventing reverse flow.
[F01L 2001/34486] Location and number of the means for changing the angular relationship -- The CPC subclass F01L 2001/34486 pertains to thermal-power-plant or turbine devices and classifies inventions that disclose where and how many components are provided to alter the angular relationship between moving parts, such as adjustable guide vanes, cam levers, gear assemblies or other angle-changing mechanisms within the machine. It focuses on the spatial arrangement and count of these means for varying the relative angle of shafts, rotors or flow-directing elements.
[F01L 2001/34489] Two phasers on one camshaft -- This invention integrates two independent camshaft phasers onto a single camshaft, allowing separate adjustment of intake and exhaust valve timing. The dual-phaser system enhances engine performance, fuel economy and emissions control by providing more precise variable valve timing across different operating conditions.
[F01L 2001/34493] Dual independent phasing system [DIPS] -- The CPC entry F01L 2001/34493 covers dual-independent phasing systems, a control arrangement where two separate phasing mechanisms adjust the timing of turbine blade groups independently to improve efficiency and load balancing. Such systems are employed in steam, wind or hydraulic turbines to optimise performance under variable operating conditions.
[F01L 2001/34496] Two phasers on different camshafts -- The classification F01L 2001/34496 refers to engine valve-timing systems that employ two separate phasers mounted on different camshafts. It covers arrangements where each camshaft can be independently advanced or retarded to optimise valve opening timing, typically for multi-cylinder internal combustion engines.
[F01L 2001/3521] Harmonic drive of flexspline type -- Harmonic drive of flexspline type is a gear mechanism that uses a thin, flexible cylindrical spline that deforms to transmit motion with high reduction ratios and zero backlash, commonly used in precision positioning and aerospace applications.
[F01L 2001/3522] with electromagnetic brake -- The CPC subclass F01L 2001/3522 groups pumps, fans, blowers, compressors and similar fluid-moving machines that integrate an electromagnetic brake to arrest or hold the rotating part when power is cut, providing rapid stopping, safety or precise speed control. The brake may be based on eddy-current or energized coil action and is disclosed in patents concerning the drive or control of the device.
[F01L 2001/467] Lost motion springs -- F01L 2001/467 covers lost motion springs, which are spring devices that permit a pre-determined amount of relative movement between connected parts before the spring engages, providing shock absorption, delay or controlled release in mechanisms such as clutches, brakes and safety devices. They are used to prevent sudden load transmission and to allow gradual actuation.
[F01L 2003/11] Connecting valve members to rocker arm or tappet -- F01L 2003/11 relates to internal-combustion-engine valve-train components, specifically the methods for connecting valve members-such as valves, guides or retainers-to a rocker arm or tappet. The subclass covers mechanical linkages, fasteners and adapters that transmit cam-shaft motion to the valve.
[F01L 2003/25] Valve configurations in relation to engine -- The CPC subclass F01L 2003/25 covers the design and arrangement of valves within internal-combustion engines, including the placement, timing and type of intake and exhaust valves, valve actuation mechanisms and any special valve gear that influences engine performance.
[F01L 2003/251] Large number of valves, e.g. five or more -- F01L 2003/251 covers internal-combustion engines that incorporate a large number of valves, typically five or more, within their cylinder head or valve train. The classification is used for patents describing the design, arrangement, or control of multiple valves to improve airflow, combustion efficiency, or power output.
[F01L 2003/253] configured parallel to piston axis -- The entry F01L 2003/253 classifies pumps or hydraulic devices in which a component-typically a drive shaft, valve or rotating element-is arranged so that its longitudinal direction runs parallel to the piston axis, meaning it aligns with the line of piston movement rather than being angled or perpendicular. This orientation affects the layout, sealing and force transmission within reciprocating-type pump constructions.
[F01L 2003/255] configured other than parallel or symmetrical relative to piston axis -- This subclass covers piston arrangements that are not aligned parallel or symmetrical to the piston axis, including angled, offset, or otherwise tilted pistons used in reciprocating machines such as engines, compressors, and pumps.
[F01L 2003/256] configured other than perpendicular to camshaft axis -- This subclass covers camshaft arrangements in internal-combustion engines where the cam is set at an angle or offset rather than being perpendicular to the camshaft axis, affecting valve timing and actuation. It includes mechanisms, gearings or designs that position cams non-perpendicular to the axis to achieve specific performance, packaging or control advantages.
[F01L 2003/258] opening away from cylinder -- F01L 2003/258 covers pump designs in which the intake or discharge opening is positioned away from the cylinder, for example by using remote ports or extensions that distance the fluid flow path from the piston chamber. This configuration can simplify sealing and allow more flexible connection arrangements.
[F01L 2009/2103] comprising one coil -- F01L 2009/2103 covers wind turbine generators that include a single electrical coil as the primary element for energy conversion. The classification is used for designs where the turbine's generator employs one coil rather than multiple windings, typically in simple or compact turbine arrangements.
[F01L 2009/2105] comprising two or more coils -- This subgroup pertains to wind-turbine generators that incorporate two or more electromagnetic coils within the conversion assembly, such as multi-coil stator or rotor configurations intended to raise induced voltage, improve phase balance, or enhance overall power output.
[F01L 2009/2107] being disposed coaxially to the armature shaft -- F01L 2009/2107 covers arrangements in which a component or sub-assembly is positioned on the same axis as the armature shaft of an electrical machine, ensuring coaxial alignment for efficient torque transmission and balanced operation. This includes bearings, field windings, sensors or auxiliary devices that share the shaft's central line.
[F01L 2009/2109] The armature being articulated perpendicularly to the coils axes -- F01L 2009/2109 classifies electric machines whose armature is mounted so that it moves or is positioned at a right angle to the axes of the field coils, typically describing rotating or linear machines where the armature and coils are articulated perpendicularly to improve magnetic interaction and mechanical layout. This sub-group deals with the structural arrangement of the armature relative to the coil system in such devices.
[F01L 2009/2115] Moving coil actuators -- Moving-coil actuators are electromagnetic devices that convert electrical energy into precise linear or rotary motion by means of a coil suspended in a magnetic field; the interaction of current-induced magnetic forces moves the coil and attached load. The CPC entry F01L 2009/2115 groups patents concerning the design, construction, control and applications of such actuators, including their integration in positioning systems, feedback mechanisms and specialized machines.
[F01L 2009/2117] Floating actuators for varying the valve stroke -- F01L 2009/2117 covers floating actuators that adjust the stroke of hydraulic or pneumatic valves, allowing variable travel without changing the valve body. The actuators are mounted in a movable, buoyant or spring-loaded arrangement so that the stroke can be fine-tuned to match flow requirements, improving control precision and efficiency in fluid-power systems.
[F01L 2009/2125] Shaft and armature construction -- F01L 2009/2125 concerns the mechanical design of shafts and armatures in rotary electrical machines, detailing how the shaft supports, aligns and couples with the armature, the placement of bearings, and methods for transmitting torque while maintaining structural integrity. It addresses construction features that affect the machine's performance and reliability.
[F01L 2009/2126] Arrangements for amplifying the armature stroke -- F01L 2009/2126 covers arrangements that increase the mechanical travel of an electric machine's armature, such as levers, gears, hydraulic or magnetic amplifiers, which convert a small input motion into a larger stroke for improved performance in generators, motors or actuators.
[F01L 2009/2128] Core and coil construction -- This group covers the design and arrangement of magnetic cores and windings used in electromagnetic pumps, motor-driven pumps or related drive devices, including core shape, material, coil geometry and insulation aimed at optimizing magnetic flux, reducing losses and improving performance.
[F01L 2009/213] Casing construction -- F01L 2009/213 refers to the CPC subclass dealing with the design and manufacture of casings that contain pumps or pump units. It includes structural features, materials, sealing arrangements and protective measures for pump housings, as well as methods for forming or assembling such casings. The code is used to index patents that focus on improving durability, efficiency or ease of maintenance of pump enclosures.
[F01L 2009/2132] Biasing means -- The CPC subclass F01L 2009/2132 covers biasing means used in rotating machines, such as springs, elastic elements or other devices that apply a predetermined force to maintain or restore the position of components like rotors, shafts, or blades. It includes arrangements that counteract axial or radial loads, ensuring correct alignment and operational stability of turbines, compressors, pumps and similar equipment.
[F01L 2009/2134] Helical springs -- The CPC class F01L covers machines for working metal, especially spring-making equipment, and the subclass 2009/2134 is dedicated to helical springs. It includes the design, production, heat-treatment and testing of coiled spring elements used for load-bearing, energy-storage or vibration-damping applications.
[F01L 2009/2136] Two opposed springs for intermediate resting position of the armature -- The CPC entry F01L 2009/2136 covers a mechanism that uses two opposing springs to hold the armature of a hydraulic turbine or pump in an intermediate resting position, keeping the rotor centered during shutdown and enabling a stable, quick restart.
[F01L 2009/2138] Torsion springs -- F01L 2009/2138 designates patents concerning torsion springs, elastic devices that store energy by being twisted about an axis and release it when the torque is removed. The entry includes various forms such as helical, leaf and coil torsion springs used in mechanical, automotive and watch-making applications, and it falls under CPC class F01L for springs and elastic bodies.
[F01L 2009/214] Pneumatic springs -- The CPC class F01L 2009/214 covers pneumatic springs, devices that store energy in compressed gas to produce a restoring force and are used for suspension, load-support or actuation functions. It includes the design, construction and application of gas-filled cylinders, accumulators and related control mechanisms.
[F01L 2009/2142] Means for varying the spring bias -- This CPC subclass covers devices that allow the preload or stiffness of a spring to be adjusted, providing variable force in pumps, compressors, fans and related machinery. It includes mechanisms such as adjustable seats, shims, eccentric mounts or other means that enable the spring bias to be changed during set-up, operation or maintenance, thereby modifying performance characteristics of the equipment.
[F01L 2009/2144] Means for connecting springs to valve or anchor -- The subclass F01L 2009/2144 covers devices that attach springs to valves or anchors in hydraulic and pneumatic systems, such as fittings, brackets, clamps or similar means that secure the spring to the valve body or an anchoring point. It falls under the broader F01L group for fluid-power valve components.
[F01L 2009/2146] Latching means -- CPC entry F01L 2009/2146 covers latch mechanisms used in harvesting and related agricultural machines. It includes devices that secure, lock or release movable parts such as threshing drums, cutter bars or feed sections. The classification is applied to inventions that provide improved latching means for these machines.
[F01L 2009/2148] using permanent magnet -- F01L 2009/2148 classifies inventions that employ permanent magnets as the field source in electric machines such as motors, generators or actuators. It includes designs where the magnet replaces or supplements electromagnetic windings to produce the required magnetic flux.
[F01L 2009/2149] Means for varying the air gap -- The CPC entry F01L 2009/2149 covers devices that adjust the distance, or air gap, between components in a plant-typically between a furnace wall or heating surface and a downstream heat-exchange surface-to control heat transfer rates. It includes mechanisms such as actuators, springs, hydraulic or pneumatic systems that can vary the gap during operation.
[F01L 2009/2151] Damping means -- The CPC subclass F01L 2009/2151 covers any means used to damp or absorb vibrations, shocks, and impacts in mechanical systems. This includes devices such as springs, elastomeric elements, hydraulic, pneumatic, fluid or magnetic dampers, and any combined structures that convert kinetic energy into heat or another form for reduction of motion.
[F01L 2009/2153] Means for counteracting cylinder pressure -- A device or arrangement installed on a hydraulic or pneumatic cylinder that reduces or balances the pressure acting on the piston during operation, such as pressure-relief valves, counter-pressure chambers, or blow-off mechanisms, to improve control, prevent overload, and enhance safety.
[F01L 2009/2155] Lash adjusting means -- The entry covers a device that allows the position or tension of a flexible lash, such as a strap or cord, to be adjusted in agricultural implements. The means typically include screws, levers or hydraulic actuators that modify the lash length or angle to adapt cutting or gathering equipment to varying conditions.
[F01L 2009/2157] Actuator cooling means -- The CPC subclass F01L 2009/2157 covers cooling means for actuators, especially fluid-power devices such as hydraulic or pneumatic cylinders and motors. It includes structures and methods that dissipate heat generated during operation, for example heat exchangers, cooling jackets, fans, or coolant circulation systems, to maintain performance and avoid overheating.
[F01L 2009/2159] Means for facilitating assembly -- In CPC the code F01L covers steam turbines, and the 2009/2159 entry added under that group refers to means for facilitating assembly of turbine components, such as jigs, fixtures, alignment guides, quick-connect couplings and other devices that simplify or speed up construction, maintenance or repair of the turbine.
[F01L 2009/2161] Wiring -- The CPC entry F01L 2009/2161 "Wiring" covers the electrical wiring arrangements used in wind turbines, including the design, layout and installation of power and control cables, harnesses and interconnections between the generator, converters, control units and other turbine components.
[F01L 2009/2163] Connectors -- F01L 2009/2163 covers connectors used in wind-turbine installations, including electrical, hydraulic and mechanical couplings that join components such as blades, nacelles, towers, generators or power-cable assemblies. The entry applies to devices that facilitate the transfer of energy, signals or fluids between rotating or stationary parts within a wind-energy system. It is part of the CPC group for wind-turbine engineering.
[F01L 2009/2165] Harnesses -- CPC F01L 2009/2165 concerns harnesses that transmit mechanical power, such as rope, cable, belt or other flexible line assemblies used to connect rotating shafts or moving parts. The subclass includes devices for tensioning, guiding or supporting the line and is applicable to agricultural, construction and industrial machinery.
[F01L 2009/2167] Sensing means -- F01L 2009/2167 classifies the sensing means incorporated in agricultural or food-processing machines, covering sensors, transducers and detection devices that measure physical, chemical or biological parameters such as moisture, temperature, weight, composition or motion to control or monitor harvesting, handling, storage or processing operations. It is a sub-group of the broader F01L class for machines and appliances used in agriculture.
[F01L 2009/2169] Position sensors -- F01L 2009/2169 covers devices that sense the position, displacement or movement of a component, such as a piston, valve or lever, and convert it into an electrical or mechanical signal for control or monitoring. These position sensors are used primarily in hydraulic, pneumatic or fluid-power systems to provide feedback for automation, safety or performance optimization.
[F01L 2009/2171] Vibration sensors -- F01L 2009/2171 covers vibration sensors, devices that detect and measure mechanical oscillations in structures or machinery. The classification includes accelerometers, geophones, piezoelectric and capacitive sensors, and related signal-conditioning elements used for monitoring, diagnostics, or control of vibration phenomena.
[F01L 2009/2173] Temperature sensors -- F01L 2009/2173 refers to temperature sensors used in agricultural or horticultural installations, covering devices that detect and measure temperature for controlling climate, soil, or water conditions. The subclass includes sensors based on thermocouples, resistance, infrared, or semiconductor principles, as well as their integration into monitoring or automatic regulation systems.
[F01L 2009/2174] Flux sensors -- F01L 2009/2174 covers devices that detect and measure magnetic flux density or flux changes in electrical machines, such as Hall-effect, fluxgate, or transformer-based sensors, used for monitoring performance, control, or protection.
[F01L 2009/2176] Spring force sensors -- F01L 2009/2176 covers devices that measure force using a spring element whose deformation is correlated to the applied load. The sensors typically convert spring deflection into an electrical or mechanical signal for applications such as weighing, pressure monitoring, or force feedback. The classification falls under CPC section F (Mechanical engineering) and group F01L (load and pressure measuring devices).
[F01L 2009/25] Mixed arrangement with both mechanically and electromagnetically actuated valves -- F01L 2009/25 classifies fluid-handling systems in which the valve network combines mechanical actuation, such as levers or cams, with electromagnetic actuation like solenoids or voice-coils. The mixed arrangement allows coordinated control of flow, pressure or direction, improving response speed, reliability or energy efficiency in hydraulic, pneumatic or gas-distribution equipment.
[F01L 2009/408] Engine starting -- This classification covers devices and methods for initiating engine operation, such as starters, cranking mechanisms and control systems that supply energy to start internal combustion engines. It includes electrical, pneumatic, hydraulic and mechanical starter arrangements as well as start-stop control strategies.
[F01L 2009/4082] in normal conditions -- The CPC entry F01L 2009/4082 classifies information concerning internal-combustion engines that is presented for operation under normal atmospheric pressure, temperature and load conditions, distinguishing standard test or performance data from results obtained in extreme or simulated environments.
[F01L 2009/4084] Cold start -- The CPC entry F01L 2009/4084 concerns methods and devices for starting internal combustion engines in cold conditions. It addresses pre-heating of engine components, fuel-air preparation, ignition timing, and emission control during the initial start-up phase.
[F01L 2009/4086] Soft landing, e.g. applying braking current; Levitation of armature close to core surface -- The entry covers soft-landing techniques for electric machines, describing ways to decelerate or stop an armature without mechanical impact by applying a braking current or creating magnetic levitation that keeps the armature close to the core surface, thereby reducing wear and noise during shutdown.
[F01L 2009/4088] Fail safe, e.g. valve kept closed if not opening properly -- The CPC subclass F01L 2009/4088 covers safety devices that keep a valve closed unless it opens correctly, providing a fail-safe mechanism that prevents unintended flow. It pertains to pump and fluid-handling systems where a valve must remain shut if the opening action is defective or incomplete.
[F01L 2009/409] Determination of valve speed -- The CPC class F01L 2009/409 covers techniques for measuring the operating speed of valves used in fluid-power systems. It includes sensors, signal processing and diagnostic methods that determine how fast a valve opens, closes or moves between positions, enabling performance monitoring and control optimization.
[F01L 2009/4092] Determination of valve timing during particular working conditions, e.g. deceleration -- F01L 2009/4092 covers methods and apparatus for determining the timing of intake or exhaust valves in internal-combustion engines when the engine is operating under specific conditions such as deceleration or load changes. The focus is on detecting valve events to optimise performance or emissions during these transient states.
[F01L 2009/4094] Engine stopping; Engine stall -- F01L covers internal combustion engines, and the 2009/4094 subgroup addresses systems and methods for stopping an engine or handling a stall, including automatic shut-off devices, stall detection sensors, and control strategies that safely halt engine operation under various conditions.
[F01L 2009/4096] relating to sticking duration -- F01L 2009/4096 covers technical features that define or control the duration a substance remains adhered to a surface during processes such as gas or vapour handling, cleaning, separation or sorption, focusing on methods, apparatus or parameters that manage sticking time to improve efficiency or performance.
[F01L 2009/4098] relating to gap between armature shaft and valve stem end -- This subclass deals with hydraulic turbine designs that address the clearance between the armature's rotating shaft and the end of a valve stem, a gap that influences sealing, flow control and turbine efficiency. It includes features for adjusting, measuring or compensating that space to minimise leakage and optimise performance.
[F01L 2013/001] Deactivating cylinders -- The CPC group F01L 2013/001 pertains to pumps that incorporate means for deactivating one or more cylinders, allowing the machine to operate with fewer active cylinders to match reduced demand, improve efficiency, or facilitate maintenance.
[F01L 2013/0052] with cams provided on an axially slidable sleeve -- F01L 2013/0052 is a CPC sub-group for hydraulic or pneumatic actuators in which a cam mechanism is mounted on a sleeve that can slide axially relative to the cylinder body, allowing variable displacement or timing of the output force. This feature is used to adjust stroke or control valve actuation within the same device.
[F01L 2013/0068] with an oscillating cam acting on the valve of the "BMW-Valvetronic" type -- The code designates engine valve-train designs that use an oscillating cam to actuate a BMW-Valvetronic-type variable valve lift system, allowing direct and continuous control of intake valve opening without a conventional throttle valve.
[F01L 2013/0073] with an oscillating cam acting on the valve of the "Delphi" type -- The CPC entry F01L 2013/0073 covers hydraulic turbines that incorporate an oscillating cam which actuates a Delphi-type valve for regulating fluid flow. The cam's reciprocating motion adjusts the valve opening to control turbine output, improve efficiency and respond to load changes.
[F01L 2013/0078] by modification of cam contact point by axially displacing the camshaft -- The code F01L 2013/0078 covers methods of changing the camshaft contact point by shifting the camshaft along its axis, thereby adjusting valve timing or lift in internal combustion engines. This axial displacement allows precise control of valve events without redesigning the cam profile.
[F01L 2013/0084] by modification of cam contact point by radially displacing the camshaft -- F01L 2013/0084 covers camshaft designs where the point of contact between cam and follower is altered by shifting the camshaft outward or inward relative to its axis. This radial displacement changes valve timing or lift without changing the cam profile itself, allowing variable engine performance.
[F01L 2013/0089] with means for delaying valve closing -- This subclass covers hydraulic or pneumatic valves that incorporate a device or mechanism expressly intended to postpone the valve's closing action, such as timing elements, mechanical lags, accumulators or other means that create a delayed-closing effect to smooth flow, reduce pressure surges or control sequencing.
[F01L 2013/0094] with switchable clamp for keeping valve open -- The CPC subclass F01L 2013/0094 covers centrifugal fans, blowers or similar devices that incorporate a switchable clamp designed to hold a valve in the open position, allowing flow to be maintained or halted by actuating the clamp. This feature is used to control air or gas discharge without removing the valve entirely.
[F01L 2013/10] Auxiliary actuators for variable valve timing -- The CPC subclass F01L 2013/10 pertains to auxiliary actuators that support variable valve-timing mechanisms in internal-combustion engines. It covers devices such as hydraulic, electric or pneumatic actuators that work alongside the primary camshaft system to adjust valve opening and closing timing for improved performance, efficiency or emissions control.
[F01L 2013/101] Electromagnets -- F01L 2013/101 covers electromagnets, devices that generate a magnetic field when electric current passes through a coil wound around a ferromagnetic core. The classification includes designs for generating, controlling, and using the magnetic flux in applications such as lifting, actuating, and magnetic separation.
[F01L 2013/103] Electric motors -- F01L 2013/103 relates to electric motors, covering their design, construction and operation, including stator and rotor configurations, winding techniques, cooling arrangements and mounting. The subclass applies to both AC and DC machines used for power conversion and mechanical drive.
[F01L 2013/105] Hydraulic motors -- F01L 2013/105 Hydraulic motors covers inventions that convert pressurised fluid power into rotary mechanical motion, including axial-flow, radial-flow and mixed-flow motor designs, as well as their control systems and adaptations for variable speed or torque.
[F01L 2013/106] Pneumatic motors -- Pneumatic motors are devices that convert the energy of compressed air into rotary or linear mechanical motion, typically for driving machinery, tools or auxiliary equipment. The CPC entry F01L 2013/106 groups all types of pneumatic motor constructions, operating principles and applications within the broader class of fluid-driven machines.
[F01L 2013/108] Centrifugal force
[F01L 2013/11] Sensors for variable valve timing -- The CPC subclass F01L 2013/11 pertains to sensors that detect and monitor the position, speed, or pressure of valve-timing mechanisms in internal-combustion engines, enabling variable valve timing control. It includes devices such as cam-shaft, crank-shaft, and inlet/exhaust valve position sensors used to optimise engine performance.
[F01L 2013/111] Camshafts position or phase -- The CPC subclass F01L 2013/111 covers mechanisms that adjust the position or rotational phase of a camshaft relative to the crankshaft in internal-combustion engines. It includes cam-phasing gears, hydraulic or electronic actuation systems and any control device used to vary valve timing for performance or emission optimisation.
[F01L 2013/113] crankshafts position -- This CPC subgroup covers inventions concerning the spatial arrangement and mounting of a crankshaft within an engine, including its relative location to bearings, cylinder block, and other components, as well as methods for locating, aligning or adjusting the crankshaft position during assembly or operation.
[F01L 2013/115] Pressure -- This CPC subgroup covers inventions that generate, regulate, measure or use pressure in fluid processing, including pumps, compressors, pressure vessels, control systems and associated instrumentation. It applies to devices and methods where pressure is a primary functional parameter.
[F01L 2013/116] Temperature -- The CPC entry F01L 2013/116 covers features and methods related to the temperature of the fluid handled by pumps. It includes heating, cooling, temperature regulation, sensors, and control devices integrated with or associated with the pump to maintain a desired temperature range during operation. Applicable to liquid pumps in industrial, civil, or domestic settings.
[F01L 2013/118] Valve lift -- The CPC subclass F01L 2013/118 relates to devices that raise or lower a valve to control fluid flow in pumps, compressors or similar machinery. It covers mechanisms, actuators and arrangements that provide the lifting motion, including hydraulic, pneumatic or mechanical means, and the associated control elements that adjust valve position during operation.
[F01L 21/00] Use of working pistons or pistons-rods as fluid-distributing valves or as valve-supporting elements, e.g. in free-piston machines -- The F01L 21/00 subclass concerns the use of working pistons or piston-rods as fluid-distributing valves or as elements that support valves, a concept typically applied in free-piston machines. It includes designs where the piston's motion itself controls the flow of fluid, integrating valve functions directly into the engine or pump's moving components.
[F01L 21/02] Piston or piston-rod used as valve members -- The F01L 21/02 subclass covers the use of pistons or piston-rods as valve elements, where the working piston itself functions as a fluid-distributing valve or as a support for other valves, typical in cyclically operating engines or free-piston machines.
[F01L 21/04] Valves arranged in or on piston or piston-rod -- The subclass F01L 21/04 covers valves that are formed in or attached to a piston or piston-rod, where the moving piston itself acts as a fluid-distributing valve or supports a valve element. It pertains to cyclically operating valves in engines or pumps, such as those used in free-piston machines.
[F01L 2201/00] Electronic control systems -- The CPC entry F01L 2201/00 covers electronic control systems that operate cyclically working valves in engines or pumps, including hardware and software for monitoring, timing, and actuating valve movements to regulate fluid flow or combustion processes. It addresses integration of sensors, controllers, and electronic feedback loops that manage valve timing and position in mechanical power-train applications.
[F01L 2250/00] Camshaft drives characterised by their transmission means -- F01L 2250/00 pertains to camshaft drive systems whose defining characteristic is the means by which power is transmitted to the camshaft, including chains, belts, gears, hydraulic couplings or other transmission devices used to operate engine or pump valves.
[F01L 2250/02] the camshaft being driven by chains -- F01L 2250/02 covers camshaft drive arrangements in which the camshaft is powered by a chain, a common method for timing valve operation in internal-combustion engines. It falls under cyclically operating valves for machines or engines, focusing on the transmission means linking the crankshaft to the camshaft.
[F01L 2250/04] the camshaft being driven by belts -- This subclass covers camshaft drive arrangements where the camshaft is powered by a belt, typically a timing belt, used in internal combustion engines to synchronize valve operation. It falls under cyclically operating valves for machines or engines within mechanical engineering.
[F01L 2250/06] the camshaft being driven by gear wheels -- F01L 2250/06 pertains to camshaft drives in which the camshaft receives power directly from gear wheels. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and cyclically operating valves for machines or engines, and it specifically covers gear-wheel transmission arrangements that drive the camshaft to operate valve timing mechanisms.
[F01L 23/00] Valves controlled by impact by piston, e.g. in free-piston machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  valves actuated by interference with the stroke of the piston, valves ballistically driven
[F01L 2301/00] Using particular materials -- F01L 2301/00 covers the selection or application of specific materials for cyclically operating valves within engines or pumps, focusing on how material choice influences valve performance, durability, corrosion resistance, and thermal behavior. It addresses the use of metals, alloys, ceramics or composites tailored to the operating conditions of such valves.
[F01L 2301/02] Using ceramic materials -- F01L 2301/02 falls under Mechanical Engineering → Engines or Pumps → Cyclically operating valves for machines or engines → Using particular materials, and it pertains to the use of ceramic materials in those valves. The subclass addresses valves whose bodies, seats or other components are made of ceramics, emphasizing the material's properties and advantages in cyclic operation.
[F01L 2303/00] Manufacturing of components used in valve arrangements -- The F01L 2303/00 class covers processes and techniques for producing parts that make up cyclically operating valves in engines and other machines, such as valve bodies, seats, stems, guides and sealing elements. It includes methods for shaping, machining, joining, coating and finishing these valve components.
[F01L 2303/01] Tools for producing, mounting or adjusting, e.g. some part of the distribution -- The CPC class F01L 2303/01 covers tools used to manufacture, install or fine-tune parts of cyclically operating valve systems in engines and machines, such as equipment that shapes, fits or adjusts distribution components of valves. It includes devices for forming, mounting or adjusting valve bodies, seats, stems or related hardware.
[F01L 2303/02] Initial camshaft settings -- F01L 2303/02 covers techniques for establishing the initial camshaft position in engines during the manufacturing of valve-train components. It includes procedures for adjusting camshaft timing before final assembly or testing, ensuring proper valve opening and closing sequences.
[F01L 2305/00] Valve arrangements comprising rollers -- F01L 2305/00 relates to valve designs that incorporate rollers to guide or support moving valve parts in cyclically operating engines or pumps. The rollers reduce friction and wear while improving sealing performance compared with sliding or pivoting members, enhancing the efficiency and reliability of the valve train.
[F01L 2305/02] Mounting of rollers -- The entry covers the way rollers are attached to a valve arrangement that uses rollers to control fluid flow in cyclically operating engines or pumps. It specifies the mounting structures, such as brackets, shafts or housings, that secure the rollers in place to ensure reliable valve operation. This falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and cyclically operating valves.
[F01L 2307/00] Preventing the rotation of tappets -- The F01L 2307/00 group covers devices, mechanisms or control methods that keep tappets from rotating while they engage cyclically operating valves in engines or pumps. It includes stops, guides, locking features or hydraulic and pneumatic arrangements that maintain the correct angular position of the tappet during valve actuation.
[F01L 2309/00] Self-contained lash adjusters -- F01L 2309/00 covers self-contained lash adjusters, devices that automatically maintain correct valve clearance in internal-combustion engines. These adjusters incorporate a spring-loaded mechanism that compensates for wear, eliminating the need for manual adjustment. They are used in engines with cyclically operating valves to improve performance and reduce maintenance.
[F01L 2311/00] Differential gears located between crankshafts and camshafts for varying the timing of valves -- The CPC entry F01L 2311/00 pertains to differential gears installed between a crankshaft and a camshaft that allow the timing of engine valves to be varied. It falls under mechanical engineering, specifically engines or pumps and cyclically operating valves for machines or engines, and relates to mechanisms that adjust valve opening and closing intervals for improved performance.
