[F01M 2001/0292] Sealings -- This subclass covers sealing arrangements for fluid-handling equipment, including gaskets, packing, O-rings, lip seals and other devices that prevent leakages in pumps, compressors, fans and similar machines. It addresses sealing methods, materials and construction details for both static and dynamic applications.
[F01M 2001/062] Crankshaft with passageways
[F01M 2001/064] Camshaft with passageways -- The CPC subclass F01M 2001/064 covers camshafts that incorporate one or more passageways-such as internal channels, drilled holes or hollow stems-designed to convey oil, fuel, coolant or other fluids to the camshaft surfaces or to downstream components. These passageways may be integral to the camshaft body or added as separate conduits to improve lubrication, cooling or hydraulic actuation.
[F01M 2001/066] Connecting rod with passageways -- The CPC subclass F01M 2001/066 covers connecting rods for reciprocating internal-combustion engines that incorporate internal passageways, such as oil-circulation or cooling channels, to lubricate bearings or manage temperature within the rod. It includes designs where the passages are integral to the rod's geometry.
[F01M 2001/068] Bakance shaft with passageways -- F01M 2001/068 covers balance shafts equipped with passageways, typically used in internal-combustion engines to counteract secondary vibration. The passageways allow lubrication, cooling or venting of gases, improving shaft durability and engine performance while maintaining precise rotational timing.
[F01M 2001/083] for lubricating cylinders -- F01M 2001/083 relates to internal combustion piston engines and designates features, methods or devices that provide lubrication for the cylinders, such as oil delivery systems, channels or mechanisms that coat cylinder walls to reduce friction and wear. It is a sub-group of the broader CPC class for cylinder lubrication in liquid-fuel engines.
[F01M 2001/086] for lubricating gudgeon pins -- The CPC class F01M 2001/086 refers to internal-combustion engine technology that provides a means for lubricating the gudgeon (connecting-rod) pins. It covers designs, devices or methods that deliver oil or grease to these pins to reduce friction and wear during engine operation.
[F01M 2001/1007] characterised by the purification means combined with other functions -- F01M 2001/1007 pertains to fluid-treatment apparatus in which the purification stage-such as filtration, adsorption or disinfection-is integrated with additional functions like mixing, heating, cooling, dosing or aeration, allowing the same unit to perform multiple processing steps simultaneously. This subclass groups devices that combine a purification means with at least one other operative feature.
[F01M 2001/1014] comprising supply of additives -- This CPC subgroup defines machines or installations for the production or treatment of iron, steel or alloys that include a system for feeding additives into the process. The supply may be continuous or batchwise and is integral to the apparatus, enabling control of composition, reaction conditions or product properties.
[F01M 2001/1021] comprising self cleaning systems -- F01M 2001/1021 classifies water-supply or water-treatment apparatus that is equipped with self-cleaning systems, meaning the device automatically removes fouling, deposits or blockages to maintain its operation without manual cleaning. This includes pumps, filters, membranes or other components that integrate mechanisms such as back-flushing, scrapers or ultrasonic cleaning.
[F01M 2001/1028] characterised by the type of purification -- F01M 2001/1028 is a CPC subgroup for machines or devices for the treatment of liquids, specifically specifying that the invention is characterised by the type of purification employed. It covers arrangements where the purification method-such as filtration, adsorption, ion exchange or membrane separation-is the defining technical feature of the apparatus. This helps differentiate the claim from other liquid-treatment technologies that use alternative purification steps.
[F01M 2001/1035] comprising centrifugal filters -- F01M 2001/1035 covers apparatus that integrate centrifugal action with filter elements to separate solids from liquids, such as centrifuge units that retain particles on a filter while spinning. The classification is used for devices that combine centrifugation and filtration in chemical, pharmaceutical or food-processing operations.
[F01M 2001/1042] comprising magnetic parts -- This subgroup covers machines or devices that incorporate magnetic components, such as magnetic bearings, couplings, drives or stators, as part of their construction or operation, indicating that the invention includes magnetic parts that play a functional role in energy conversion or transmission.
[F01M 2001/105] characterised by the layout of the purification arrangements -- F01M 2001/105 covers inventions where the specific layout of purification arrangements is a defining feature, such as the organization of filters, separators and related equipment within a plant or processing system to achieve desired separation or cleaning functions.
[F01M 2001/1057] comprising a plurality of filters, parallel or serial -- This CPC subgroup designates filter apparatus that incorporates a plurality of filter elements arranged either in parallel or in series within a single installation, enabling simultaneous or staged treatment of a fluid stream. It covers devices where multiple filters are combined to increase capacity, achieve progressive impurity removal, or provide redundancy.
[F01M 2001/1064] comprising drains for oil to the carter, e.g. to recover spilled oil during change of filters -- The CPC subgroup F01M 2001/1064 relates to engine lubrication systems that incorporate drains directing oil back to the crankcase, specifically designed to capture oil that spills when filters are changed. It covers arrangements that prevent loss of lubricating oil and facilitate its recovery during maintenance operations.
[F01M 2001/1071] comprising oil tanks -- The CPC subclass F01M 2001/1071 covers hydraulic or oil-lubricated pump systems that incorporate one or more oil tanks as part of the apparatus. It includes designs where the oil reservoir is integral to the pump's operation, providing fluid storage, cooling, and pressure stabilization for the pumping mechanism. This entry is used for inventions that feature oil tanks as essential components of pump assemblies.
[F01M 2001/1078] comprising an oil pick-up tube to oil pump, e.g. strainer -- The subclass covers oil-pump arrangements that incorporate a pick-up tube directly attached to the pump, usually combined with a screen or strainer to remove debris before the oil enters the pump. It pertains to hydraulic or lubrication systems where the pick-up tube and filtering element are integral to the pump's suction side.
[F01M 2001/1085] comprising non-return valves -- The CPC subgroup F01M 2001/1085 covers pump arrangements that incorporate non-return (check) valves, which permit fluid flow in only one direction and prevent backflow. It includes designs where the check valve is integral to the pump housing, mounted on the pump outlet, or otherwise combined with the pump to form a single functional unit.
[F01M 2001/1092] comprising valves bypassing the filter -- F01M 2001/1092 concerns fluid-handling installations that incorporate valves enabling the fluid to bypass a filter. The bypass arrangement is used to maintain flow when the filter is clogged, being cleaned or isolated, and appears in pumps, compressors and similar hydraulic systems.
[F01M 2001/123] using two or more pumps -- The CPC subclass F01M 2001/123 covers devices or arrangements in which two or more pumps are employed together, typically to increase flow rate, pressure, or redundancy. It applies to hydraulic, pneumatic or other fluid-power systems where multiple pumps operate in series or parallel to achieve the desired performance.
[F01M 2001/126] Dry-sumps -- F01M 2001/126 is a CPC subgroup within class F01M for machinery that handles liquids, covering dry-sump lubrication systems. It pertains to engines where the lubricating oil is collected in a separate external reservoir rather than being stored in the crankcase, a design common in high-performance, racing and aircraft engines to improve cooling and oil control.
[F01M 2001/165] according to fuel dilution in oil -- F01M 2001/165 covers internal-combustion engines or related systems that detect, monitor or control the extent of fuel mixing into the lubricating oil, using the degree of fuel dilution as a parameter for maintenance, performance optimization or emission reduction. This subgroup includes methods and devices for measuring fuel-oil dilution and adjusting operating conditions accordingly.
[F01M 2005/004] Oil-cooled engines -- The CPC subclass F01M 2005/004 covers engines that use oil as the principal means of cooling, including internal-combustion and rotary types where oil circulation removes heat from the combustion chamber, cylinders, bearings and other hot components, and may involve oil-cooling jackets, pumps or heat exchangers.
[F01M 2005/008] Lubrication means facilitating engine starting -- F01M 2005/008 covers devices or methods that supply lubrication to an internal combustion engine to reduce friction and aid starting, including pre-lubrication systems, spray nozzles or oil injection mechanisms that operate before or during ignition.
[F01M 2005/023] Oil sump with partition for facilitating heating of oil during starting -- The F01M 2005/023 subgroup covers engine oil sumps that incorporate a partition or chamber designed to aid oil heating during start-up. The partition isolates a portion of the oil, allowing it to be warmed more quickly so that adequate lubrication is achieved in cold-start conditions, improving engine protection and performance.
[F01M 2005/026] with an auxiliary pump -- F01M 2005/026 covers agricultural or horticultural machines that incorporate an auxiliary pump to assist the main operation, such as providing additional hydraulic pressure for seed or fertilizer delivery. The auxiliary pump is used to boost fluid flow or pressure beyond what the primary system supplies, improving performance in planting or sowing equipment. This subclass refines the broader category of devices with auxiliary pumps in the F01M section.
[F01M 2005/028] with a reservoir under pressure -- F01M 2005/028 covers hydraulic or fluid-power machines that incorporate a pressure-maintained reservoir, such as accumulators or closed-loop tanks, which store fluid under pressure to supply energy, dampen pulsations, or improve system response during operation. This subclass is used for devices where the pressurised reservoir is integral to the machine's function, for example in hydraulic presses, pumps, or actuators that rely on stored fluid energy.
[F01M 2011/0008] with means for reducing vibrations -- F01M 2011/0008 classifies rotary pumps equipped with vibration-reducing means. It covers pumps that incorporate devices such as flexible mounts, dampers, counter-weights or fluid-filled chambers to minimise operational vibration, noise and wear, thereby improving stability and service life.
[F01M 2011/0012] with acoustic insulation -- F01M 2011/0012 covers centrifugal pumps that incorporate acoustic insulation to minimise noise emission, including sound-absorbing housings, liners, baffling or other noise-dampening measures integrated with the pump structure. It applies to designs where the insulation is an essential part of the pump's operation or mounting, aimed at reducing vibration-generated sound in industrial or residential settings.
[F01M 2011/0016] with thermic insulation -- The subclass refers to compressors or pump units that include thermal insulation on the housing, casing or internal components, intended to reduce heat loss, protect surrounding parts from temperature extremes, and improve overall energy efficiency.
[F01M 2011/002] with means for improving the stiffness -- F01M 2011/002 relates to pumps with variable-volume chambers that incorporate features designed to increase the rigidity of the chamber or its supporting structure. Such means may include reinforcement ribs, stiffening plates, or other structural elements that reduce deformation under pressure, thereby improving performance and reliability.
[F01M 2011/0025] with heat exchangers -- F01M 2011/0025 covers agricultural or horticultural machines that incorporate a heat-exchanger as an integral part of the equipment, used to heat, cool or otherwise control the temperature of crop-related material during processing, handling, storage or conditioning. The scope includes devices such as grain dryers, seed-conditioning units, or climate-control installations where the heat-exchanger is essential to the machine's operation.
[F01M 2011/0029] with oil filters -- F01M 2011/0029 covers internal-combustion engines that incorporate oil filtration devices. The entry includes designs, arrangements and methods for integrating oil filters into the engine's lubrication system to remove contaminants and protect moving parts.
[F01M 2011/0033] with special means for guiding the return of oil into the sump -- The CPC subclass F01M 2011/0033 pertains to hydraulic or oil-based power units that incorporate dedicated components or arrangements to direct the returning oil back into the sump. It covers designs such as guides, channels, valves or ducts that facilitate oil flow from the system's working parts to the reservoir, ensuring efficient recirculation and proper lubrication. This subclass is used for devices where the oil return path is specially engineered rather than relying on ordinary piping.
[F01M 2011/0037] with different oil compartments -- The CPC class F01M 2011/0037 covers pumps or pump assemblies that incorporate separate oil compartments, allowing distinct oil supplies for different functions such as lubrication, cooling or hydraulic actuation within the same unit. This arrangement improves efficiency and reliability by isolating oil streams that may have differing pressure, temperature or contamination requirements. It is typically applied in engine oil-pump systems, hydraulic circuits and other fluid-handling equipment.
[F01M 2011/0041] for accommodating movement or position of engines -- This CPC subgroup covers devices and arrangements that allow an engine to be moved, tilted or otherwise positioned relative to its supporting structure, such as adjustable mounts, pivots, gimbals or brackets. It includes mechanisms for angular or linear adjustment to accommodate installation, vibration isolation, steering or other positional requirements in automotive, marine, aerospace and similar applications.
[F01M 2011/0045] for controlling the oil temperature -- F01M covers machines and apparatus for heating, cooling, mixing or otherwise treating liquids, especially oil. The 2011/0045 entry specifies devices that monitor and regulate oil temperature, such as thermostatic controls, temperature sensors and feedback systems used in engines, hydraulic circuits or industrial lubricating equipment. It addresses both the detection of temperature and the automatic adjustment of heating or cooling elements to maintain a set oil temperature.
[F01M 2011/005] with special anti-turbulence means, e.g. anti-foaming means or intermediate plates -- F01M 2011/005 covers centrifuge or separator devices that incorporate features designed to suppress turbulence in the working fluid, such as anti-foaming inserts or intermediate plates positioned to break up eddies. These anti-turbulence means improve separation efficiency and reduce mixing.
[F01M 2011/0054] Fastening to the cylinder block -- The CPC subclass F01M 2011/0054 covers devices, methods or arrangements for securing components directly to an engine's cylinder block, including bolts, screws, clamps, threaded inserts and other fastening elements that attach parts such as cylinder heads, oil pumps or accessory brackets to the block. It focuses on the mechanical connection and the features that ensure reliable attachment under engine operating conditions.
[F01M 2011/0058] Fastening to the transmission -- The entry covers devices and methods for securing a transmission to a machine or vehicle, including bolts, clamps, brackets, and other fastening mechanisms that attach gear sets, shafts or housings to the transmission. It includes both permanent and removable solutions used in automotive, industrial and aerospace applications.
[F01M 2011/0062] Gaskets -- F01M 2011/0062 is a CPC subgroup in the mechanical engineering section that covers gaskets employed in pumps, compressors, fans and turbines. It includes sealing elements, their design, material composition and mounting arrangements used to prevent leakage between moving or stationary parts of these fluid-handling devices.
[F01M 2011/0066] with passages in the wall, e.g. for axles or fluid passages -- Rolls or cylindrical components that incorporate internal channels through their walls, enabling axles, coolant or lubricating fluid to pass through the element during operation. The built-in passages facilitate mechanical support or fluid distribution without external fittings.
[F01M 2011/007] Oil pickup tube to oil pump, e.g. strainer -- The CPC class F01M 2011/007 covers oil-pickup tubes that feed oil to a pump, typically incorporating a strainer to remove contaminants before the oil enters the pump housing. It includes designs for the tube, attachment means, and integrated filtering elements that ensure clean lubrication flow.
[F01M 2011/0075] with a plurality of tubes -- F01M 2011/0075 designates machinery for preparing animal food that incorporates a plurality of tubes, such as multiple feed-conveying, mixing or extruding tubes integrated into the equipment. This sub-group belongs to the broader CPC class for animal-feed processing machines and is differentiated by the presence of several tubes as a functional element.
[F01M 2011/0079] with the oil pump integrated or fixed to sump -- F01M 2011/0079 covers agricultural machines whose lubrication system incorporates the oil pump directly into, or permanently fixed to, the oil sump. This integration eliminates a separate pump unit and is typical of tractors and harvesting equipment where compact, low-maintenance engine designs are required.
[F01M 2011/0083] Dry sumps -- The CPC entry F01M 2011/0083 covers dry-sump systems in which a pump draws fluid from a separate, essentially empty reservoir rather than from a filled oil pan, a design used to improve lubrication, reduce oil aeration and maintain a constant supply in high-performance engines and other machinery.
[F01M 2011/0087] Sump being made of different parts -- The code refers to pump or liquid-handling equipment whose sump - the basin that collects fluid - is built from separate components rather than as one solid piece, enabling modular assembly, easier repair and the use of interchangeable parts in the overall pump system.
[F01M 2011/0091] characterised by used materials -- This CPC subgroup covers plant-related apparatuses or processes whose distinguishing feature is the use of previously used, recycled or waste materials, such as spent biomass, residues or reclaimed substrates, influencing how the material is treated, transformed or recovered. It is applied to inventions that exploit such used materials in cultivation, harvesting or downstream processing.
[F01M 2011/0095] Supplementary oil tank -- F01M 2011/0095 covers engines equipped with a supplementary oil tank that provides an additional reservoir of lubricating oil, separate from the primary oil circuit, to ensure continuous lubrication of moving parts during operation or under extreme conditions.
[F01M 2011/021] for lubricating auxiliaries, e.g. pumps or turbo chargers -- The CPC subclass F01M 2011/021 covers auxiliary components that supply or circulate lubricant in internal combustion engines, such as oil pumps, metering devices, and related fluid-handling units. It also includes lubricated accessories like turbochargers where the lubrication function is integral to the component's operation.
[F01M 2011/022] for lubricating cylinders -- The CPC code F01M 2011/022 covers pump designs that include provisions for lubricating the pump cylinders, such as lubricant supply systems, lubrication chambers or delivery mechanisms that ensure proper cylinder lubrication during operation. This subclass groups inventions that address lubrication of cylinders within pump constructions.
[F01M 2011/023] between oil sump and cylinder head -- This CPC subclass covers devices and methods that convey oil from the oil sump (oil pan) to the cylinder head of an internal-combustion engine, including oil pumps, passages, galleries and related control mechanisms that ensure proper lubrication of the valve train and head components.
[F01M 2011/025] for lubricating gudgeon pins -- F01M 2011/025 falls under the CPC group for internal-combustion engines, specifically the sub-class dealing with lubrication. This entry covers devices, methods or systems for lubricating gudgeon pins-the pins that join the piston to the connecting rod-aimed at reducing friction and wear during engine operation.
[F01M 2011/026] for lubricating crankshaft bearings -- The CPC subclass F01M 2011/026 covers lubrication arrangements that are specifically intended for crankshaft bearings in internal-combustion engines or similar machinery. It includes methods, devices and oil-delivery systems that ensure a continuous film of lubricant on the bearing surfaces to reduce friction and wear during operation.
[F01M 2011/027] for lubricating connecting rod bearings -- The CPC subclass F01M 2011/027 covers devices or methods that supply lubrication specifically to the connecting-rod bearings of reciprocating engines. It includes arrangements such as oil galleries, pumps, channels and control mechanisms designed to maintain a film of oil on those bearing surfaces during operation. This subclass is part of the broader group dealing with engine lubrication and bearing protection.
[F01M 2011/028] for lubricating balance shafts -- F01M 2011/028 falls under the CPC class for internal combustion engines that include provisions for lubricating balance shafts. The entry covers designs where oil is supplied to the shafts, such as through dedicated passages, reservoirs or spray systems, to reduce wear and maintain proper engine balance.
[F01M 2011/031] characterised by mounting means -- The CPC subgroup F01M 2011/031 includes liquid-application devices - such as sprays, nozzles or coating units - that are distinguished by the presence of mounting means. These mounting means comprise brackets, clamps, rails or other support structures that secure the apparatus to a frame, machine or workpiece, providing stability, adjustability or ease of installation as an essential feature of the device.
[F01M 2011/033] comprising coolers or heat exchangers -- F01M 2011/033 covers machines, apparatus or installations for producing, liquefying or treating gases that incorporate coolers or heat exchangers as part of the process. The inclusion of these thermal-exchange components is considered an essential feature of the claimed invention.
[F01M 2011/035] comprising oil pumps -- F01M 2011/035 refers to inventions that incorporate oil pumps as a functional element, typically within systems for lubrication, hydraulic power transmission or cooling. It is a sub-group of the rotating-shaft pump class (F01M) and is used to index patents where an oil pump is part of the disclosed apparatus or method.
[F01M 2011/036] comprising pumps for the cooling circuit -- F01M 2011/036 refers to inventions that include pumps as part of a cooling circuit, typically used to circulate coolant in systems such as engines, industrial machinery, or power plants. The classification covers designs where the pump is integrated within the circuit to maintain temperature control.
[F01M 2011/038] comprising lubricant-air separators -- F01M 2011/038 covers pumps that incorporate lubricant-air separators, which are devices that remove air from the lubricating oil to prevent cavitation, reduce wear and improve efficiency, especially in hydraulic and gear-type pumps.
[F01M 2011/0416] Plugs -- F01M 2011/0416 covers plugs used in pump, compressor or fan assemblies to seal or close openings in housings, casings, inlet or outlet ports, and related fluid passages. The class includes removable or permanently installed plugs that prevent flow, protect internal components, or allow maintenance access. It pertains to any plug designed for use with rotating or static fluid-handling machinery.
[F01M 2011/0425] with a device facilitating the change of oil -- This CPC subclass covers oil circulation or lubrication systems for engines that include a built-in means to facilitate oil removal, such as drain valves, quick-disconnect fittings or automated draining devices, enabling an oil change without disassembly.
[F01M 2011/0433] with a device defining the lubricant level during filling -- This subclass covers lubricating systems that include a device for indicating or measuring the lubricant level while the system is being filled, such as sight glasses, float sensors or electronic level detectors integrated with the filler opening.
[F01M 2011/0441] for measuring the lubricant level -- F01M 2011/0441 pertains to devices or methods that detect or indicate the amount of lubricant present in a machine or component. It includes sensors, gauges, electronic circuits and visual indicators that monitor oil level in engines, gearboxes, hydraulic systems or other lubricated equipment. The subclass is used to classify inventions that provide means for measuring lubricant quantity.
[F01M 2011/0466] Filling or draining during running -- The code F01M 2011/0466 covers machines or installations that supply or remove fluid while the system is in operation, such as pumps, dispensers, or drainage devices that fill containers, reservoirs or pipelines during normal running conditions. It includes mechanisms that control flow to maintain pressure or level without stopping the plant.
[F01M 2011/0475] with combustion of used lubricant in the engine -- F01M 2011/0475 covers internal-combustion engines or methods in which used lubricating oil is introduced into the combustion chamber for disposal, energy recovery, or emission control. It includes devices and processes for feeding, controlling and handling the lubricant during engine operation.
[F01M 2011/0483] with a lubricant cartridge for facilitating the change -- The CPC entry F01M 2011/0483 covers lubricating devices that incorporate a self-contained lubricant cartridge designed to make replacement or replenishment of the lubricant quick and easy. It applies to systems where the cartridge can be removed and a fresh one inserted without disassembly of the main mechanism, commonly used in bearings, gearboxes and other rotating or sliding assemblies.
[F01M 2011/0491] Filing cap with special features -- F01M is the CPC section for pumps, compressors and related equipment, focusing on their structural and functional elements. The subgroup 2011/0491 covers caps or closures fitted to pump housings that incorporate special features such as enhanced sealing, pressure-relief devices or monitoring elements. It is used to classify inventions that improve the safety, serviceability or performance of pump casings through specially designed caps.
[F01M 2011/068] with internal reservoir -- In CPC F01M 2011/068 the focus is on electric machines that contain an internal reservoir, such as a built-in tank for coolant, oil, or other fluid used for cooling, lubrication or pressure management. The classification groups patents describing the integration of that reservoir within the machine's structure to improve performance or simplify ancillary systems.
[F01M 2011/14] for indicating the necessity to change the oil -- The CPC subclass F01M 2011/14 covers devices that indicate when engine oil needs to be replaced, such as sensors, dipsticks or dashboard warning lights that monitor oil condition, level or usage and alert the operator to change the oil.
[F01M 2011/1406] by considering acidity -- F01M 2011/1406 defines a sub-group of machines for processing agricultural or food products in which the operation is performed while taking the material's acidity into account, such as controlling pH during mixing, kneading, or other treatment steps. This limitation applies to equipment whose functional features are dependent on the acidity of the substance being handled.
[F01M 2011/1413] by considering dielectric properties -- F01M 2011/1413 covers rotating electrical machines where the design, analysis or operation explicitly takes dielectric properties into account, such as insulation strength, material permittivity or loss factors, to optimise performance, safety or reliability. It includes methods and apparatus that assess or incorporate these electrical characteristics in machine construction or monitoring.
[F01M 2011/142] by considering speed, e.g. revolutions per minute [RPM] -- This CPC entry F01M 2011/142 deals with the definition or specification of speed for rotating equipment, expressed as revolutions per minute (RPM). It is applied to patents that include speed-based performance criteria for fans, pumps, compressors or other rotary machines.
[F01M 2011/1426] by considering distance -- F01M 2011/1426 covers devices or methods that control fans, blowers or ventilation equipment by using distance measurements, such as proximity sensors, to adjust airflow, speed or direction according to the distance between the system and a target or occupied area.
[F01M 2011/1433] by considering load
[F01M 2011/144] by considering magnetic properties of the oil -- F01M 2011/144 covers lubricating or metalworking oils whose magnetic properties are taken into account in their formulation, selection or monitoring. It includes oils containing magnetic particles, ferro-fluids or methods that use magnetic response to assess oil condition or improve performance.
[F01M 2011/1446] by considering pressure -- This CPC group (F01M 2011/1446) covers inventions that control, regulate or monitor internal-combustion engines by using pressure information from the cylinder, intake, exhaust or related system to adjust timing, fuel delivery, boost or other operating parameters. It includes methods and devices that base decisions on measured pressure to improve efficiency, emissions or performance.
[F01M 2011/1453] by considering oil quantity -- F01M 2011/1453 pertains to harvesting or threshing machines that modify their operation based on the oil quantity of the material being processed, such as oil-seed harvesters that adjust threshing speed, separation or cleaning parameters according to measured oil content.
[F01M 2011/146] by considering moisture level -- F01M 2011/146 concerns steam turbine designs or control methods that take the moisture level of the working steam into account, such as moisture-separator arrangements, moisture-sensing devices, or operating strategies that adjust turbine parameters based on steam dryness. It addresses ways to improve efficiency and prevent blade erosion caused by water droplets in the steam flow.
[F01M 2011/1466] by considering quantity of soot -- F01M 2011/1466 covers furnace or boiler technologies that regulate combustion based on the amount of soot produced. It includes methods and control systems that monitor soot concentration and adjust fuel, air or operating parameters to limit deposits and improve efficiency.
[F01M 2011/1473] by considering temperature -- F01M 2011/1473 covers drilling or well-tool techniques that incorporate temperature considerations, such as monitoring, controlling or compensating for temperature variations during drilling operations. It applies to methods or apparatus that adjust drilling parameters based on temperature measurements to improve efficiency and safety. The classification is used for technologies where temperature effects on the drill string, formation or equipment are explicitly taken into account.
[F01M 2011/148] by considering viscosity -- This subclass covers pumps whose design, selection or operation explicitly takes the viscosity of the fluid into account, including methods for measuring, adjusting or optimizing performance based on fluid viscosity.
[F01M 2011/1486] by considering duration of operation -- This CPC subgroup covers pumps and related fluid-handling devices whose design, control or performance is defined by the length of time they are operated, including continuous, intermittent or seasonal use. It includes methods for selecting, monitoring or adapting pump operation based on duration.
[F01M 2011/1493] by considering total base number [TBN] -- This subclass groups patents that disclose methods or devices which evaluate a material by calculating its total base number, a quantitative measure of alkaline content used to monitor the condition of oils, fuels or related lubricants.
[F01M 2013/0005] with systems regulating the pressure in the carter -- This subclass covers inventions that include mechanisms for monitoring and adjusting the pressure within an engine's crankcase (carter), such as blow-by gas removal, positive crankcase ventilation, oil-pressure regulation and associated sensors or control units.
[F01M 2013/0016] with a membrane -- F01M 2013/0016 classifies pumps that employ a flexible membrane as the primary moving element to displace fluid, such as diaphragm or membrane pumps, where the membrane separates the drive mechanism from the pumped medium and provides a sealed, pulsation-free flow.
[F01M 2013/0022] electromagnetic -- F01M 2013/0022 classifies pumps that use electromagnetic fields to move conductive fluids, employing Lorentz forces rather than mechanical moving parts. These electromagnetic pumps are commonly applied to liquid-metal handling, cooling loops and other processes where contact-free fluid transport is desired.
[F01M 2013/0027] with a de-icing or defrosting system -- F01M 2013/0027 covers machines used for handling, storing or transporting agricultural produce that incorporate a de-icing or defrosting system. The system may use heating elements, hot air, heated fluid or similar means to melt ice or prevent frost buildup, ensuring reliable operation in cold conditions. It applies when ice removal is an integral part of the equipment's function.
[F01M 2013/0038] Layout of crankcase breathing systems -- F01M 2013/0038 pertains to the arrangement of crankcase breathing systems that control the movement of gases and vapours between a crankcase and the external environment, including vents, filters, and pressure-regulating components incorporated into the engine layout.
[F01M 2013/0044] with one or more valves -- Covers kinetic energy storage devices such as flywheel systems that incorporate one or more valves to control the flow of a working fluid, adjust pressure, or provide safety functions within the storage apparatus.
[F01M 2013/005] having one or more deoilers -- F01M 2013/005 designates machines or processing lines that incorporate at least one deoiler, a unit used to separate or remove oil from solid or liquid feedstock such as oilseeds, sludge or waste water. The subclass applies to equipment in agricultural, food-processing or waste-treatment sectors where oil removal is an integrated step.
[F01M 2013/0055] with a by-pass -- F01M 2013/0055 covers water turbines that incorporate a bypass passage allowing a portion of the working fluid to circumvent the runner. The bypass is used to regulate load, facilitate start-up, or control pressure in the turbine system.
[F01M 2013/0061] having a plurality of deoilers -- The CPC code F01M 2013/0061 covers apparatus that incorporates several deoiling units, each designed to remove oil from a liquid or slurry. It applies to machines where multiple deoilers are arranged in series or parallel to increase throughput or efficiency in processes such as oil recovery, waste-water treatment, or food-product de-oiling.
[F01M 2013/0066] in parallel -- F01M 2013/0066 refers to fluid-power machines or systems in which two or more functional elements such as pumps, motors, cylinders or valves are arranged to operate in parallel, sharing the load or flow to increase capacity, redundancy or efficiency. This subclass groups inventions that specifically describe parallel configuration of such components.
[F01M 2013/0072] in series -- The CPC entry F01M 2013/0072 covers devices, such as fans, blowers or air-moving units, that are arranged in series. It pertains to configurations where the output of one unit feeds directly into the inlet of the next, creating a combined system that delivers higher pressure or specific flow characteristics than a single unit alone.
[F01M 2013/0077] Engine parameters used for crankcase breather systems -- This subclass covers methods and devices that monitor or control engine parameters such as pressure, temperature, flow rate or vacuum to operate crankcase breather systems, ensuring proper ventilation and emission control. It includes sensors, control algorithms and feedback mechanisms used in internal combustion engines.
[F01M 2013/0083] Crankcase pressure -- The code refers to devices, methods and control systems that monitor, regulate or relieve pressure inside an internal-combustion-engine crankcase, such as pressure-sensing elements, relief or vent valves and associated circuitry designed to manage blow-by gases and prevent oil leakage.
[F01M 2013/0088] Rotation speed -- The CPC subgroup F01M 2013/0088 covers features related to the rotation speed of rotating shafts or machines, including the specification, measurement, control, adjustment and monitoring of speed values, variable-speed operation and speed-range limits.
[F01M 2013/0094] Engine load -- F01M 2013/0094 relates to devices, methods or control strategies for determining or managing the load applied to an engine, typically an internal-combustion engine. It covers techniques for measuring torque, power demand or operating conditions and for adjusting fuel, air or ignition parameters to optimise performance, efficiency or emissions under varying load levels.
[F01M 2013/026] with pumps sucking air or blow-by gases from the crankcase -- This CPC subgroup covers internal-combustion-engine lubrication systems that incorporate a pump to draw air or blow-by gases out of the crankcase, typically to reduce emissions, prevent oil aeration and improve crankcase ventilation. It applies to designs where the pump is integral to the engine's oil-circulation or ventilation circuit.
[F01M 2013/027] with a turbo charger or compressor -- The CPC subclass F01M 2013/027 covers power-plant or engine designs that incorporate a turbocharger or compressor to raise the pressure of the working fluid before it enters the combustion chamber or turbine. It includes arrangements where the forced-induction device is integral to the engine's operation or performance enhancement.
[F01M 2013/0411] using cooling means -- F01M 2013/0411 pertains to rotary or other types of pumps that incorporate cooling means, such as internal or external heat exchangers, water, oil or air circulation, or other devices that remove heat from the pump or the conveyed fluid. The classification covers the design, arrangement and operation of these cooling systems to prevent overheating, maintain efficiency and protect pump components.
[F01M 2013/0422] Separating oil and gas with a centrifuge device -- F01M 2013/0422 is a CPC subgroup covering centrifuge apparatus and processes for separating liquid and gaseous phases in petroleum streams. It specifically addresses devices that use centrifugal force to separate oil from gas, typically in offshore or on-shore production facilities. The classification includes design features, rotor configurations and control methods aimed at improving separation efficiency and reducing emulsion formation.
[F01M 2013/0427] the centrifuge device having no rotating part, e.g. cyclone -- The code F01M 2013/0427 classifies centrifuge devices that operate without any rotating component, such as cyclone separators. It covers apparatuses that generate centrifugal forces by directing fluid flow in a vortex to separate particles from gases or liquids, rather than using a mechanically driven rotor.
[F01M 2013/0433] with a deflection device, e.g. screen -- A subclass of agricultural machines that includes a deflection device, such as a screen, used to separate or redirect material during operation. It applies to harvesters, threshers or similar equipment where a screen guides unwanted particles away from the processing flow.
[F01M 2013/0438] with a filter -- F01M 2013/0438 covers heat-exchanger or steam-generator designs that incorporate a filter element to trap solids or contaminants in the fluid stream. The filter may be integral to the exchanger or positioned in the flow path, allowing simultaneous heat transfer and filtration.
[F01M 2013/0444] with means for accommodating movement or position of engines -- This CPC subgroup covers engine installations that incorporate devices allowing the engine to be shifted, rotated or otherwise repositioned relative to its supporting structure. Examples include pivoting mounts, sliding brackets and hydraulic-or-pneumatic actuators designed for vibration control, accessibility or optimal alignment.
[F01M 2013/045] using compression or decompression of the gas -- CPC class F01M relates to compressors, fans, pumps and related equipment. Subgroup 2013/045 is assigned to inventions that use compression or decompression of a gas, including methods for pressure boosting, gas recirculation or vacuum generation in industrial and mechanical processes.
[F01M 2013/0455] with a de-icing or defrosting system -- F01M 2013/0455 designates pump arrangements that include a de-icing or defrosting system to remove or prevent ice formation on the pump or its components, a feature common in cold-climate installations such as marine, aviation or heating systems.
[F01M 2013/0461] with a labyrinth -- F01M 2013/0461 covers hydraulic or water-jet turbines that incorporate a labyrinth structure, typically used as a sealing or flow-guiding element within the runner, draft tube or housing. The classification is applied to designs where the labyrinth reduces leakage, improves efficiency or controls fluid passage through the turbine.
[F01M 2013/0466] with electrostatic means -- The CPC subgroup F01M 2013/0466 covers pumps that incorporate electrostatic devices for fluid handling, such as electrostatic charging, induction or discharge elements that assist in moving, directing or separating liquids or gases within the pump system. This classification is used for technologies where electrostatic forces are employed as part of the pumping or fluid-control mechanism.
[F01M 2013/0472] using heating means -- The CPC subclass F01M 2013/0472 groups inventions that employ heating means to raise the temperature of a fluid, material or space, covering the heating element itself, its power source, control arrangements and methods of application in industrial or domestic processes.
[F01M 2013/0477] by separating water or moisture -- F01M 2013/0477 covers devices or processes that remove water or moisture from a material stream by separation. It includes dehumidifiers, condensers, drying chambers and similar equipment that use physical, mechanical or chemical means to extract liquid water from gases, liquids or solids.
[F01M 2013/0483] using catalysis -- F01M 2013/0483 relates to machines and apparatus for processing agricultural, horticultural or food products where the operation relies on catalytic action. The classification is applied to devices that incorporate a catalyst to accelerate chemical reactions during treatment, extraction, conversion or other chemical transformations of the material.
[F01M 2013/0488] with oil trap in the return conduit to the crankcase -- The entry relates to crankcase ventilation arrangements for internal-combustion engines in which an oil-collecting trap is positioned within the conduit that returns gases or oil mist from the crankcase, allowing separation of oil before the flow proceeds onward.
[F01M 2013/0494] using check valves -- F01M 2013/0494 covers inventions that incorporate check valves in fluid-handling installations to permit flow in a single direction while blocking reverse flow, often for protecting pumps, compressors or pipelines and improving system reliability. The subclass includes designs, configurations and methods that employ such valves for pressure, flow or temperature control in mechanical plants.
[F01M 2250/00] Measuring -- The F01M 2250/00 entry covers methods, devices and apparatus used to measure aspects of lubrication in engines or pumps, such as quantity, pressure, temperature, flow rate or condition of the lubricant. It relates to monitoring and control of lubricating systems to ensure proper operation and prevent wear.
[F01M 2250/60] Operating parameters -- F01M 2250/60 pertains to the measurement of operating parameters in the lubrication of machines or engines, covering the monitoring of temperature, pressure, flow rate, viscosity and similar conditions of lubricants during engine or pump operation to ensure proper performance and wear control.
[F01M 2250/62] Load -- The subclass F01M 2250/62 concerns the measurement of load in lubricated machines or engines. It covers devices and methods for determining the mechanical load or pressure that the lubrication system must support, such as torque, hydraulic pressure or bearing load, to monitor performance and ensure proper lubrication.
[F01M 2250/64] Number of revolutions -- The subclass F01M 2250/64 covers techniques for measuring the rotational speed of machines such as engines or pumps, including the counting of revolutions and determination of revolutions-per-minute using tachometers, optical or magnetic sensors, or other speed-sensing devices. It is applied to monitor performance, control operation and support lubrication regimes in mechanical engineering systems.
[F01M 2250/66] Vehicle speed -- The CPC subclass F01M 2250/66 falls under mechanical engineering, specifically engines or pumps, lubricating of machines or engines in general, and the measuring group. It covers inventions and methods related to determining the speed of a vehicle, typically as part of a system that monitors or controls engine lubrication. This classification is used by the EPO and USPTO to index technologies that measure vehicle speed for performance, safety, or efficiency purposes.
[F01M 3/00] Lubrication specially adapted for engines with crankcase compression of fuel-air mixture or for other engines in which lubricant is contained in fuel, combustion air, or fuel-air mixture -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Lubrication specially adapted for engines with crankcase compression of fuel-air mixture or for other engines in which lubricant is contained in fuel, combustion air, or fuel-air mixture
[F01M 3/02] with variable proportion of lubricant to fuel, lubricant to air, or lubricant to fuel-air-mixture -- This subclass covers engines whose lubrication system introduces oil into the fuel, combustion air, or the combined fuel-air mixture, allowing the ratio of lubricant to these media to be varied during operation. It applies to crankcase-compression engines and other types where the lubricant is mixed with the charge.
[F01M 3/04] for upper cylinder lubrication only -- F01M 3/04 covers lubrication methods that are applied only to the upper part of a cylinder in engines where the lubricant is mixed with the fuel-air charge or present in the combustion air. It is a subcategory of lubricating systems for engines with crankcase compression, focusing on upper-cylinder lubrication as the sole means of oil delivery.
[F01M 5/00] Heating, cooling, or controlling temperature of lubricant -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Heating, cooling, or controlling temperature of lubricant; lubrication means facilitating engine starting
[F01M 5/001] Heating -- The F01M 5/001 subclass covers methods and apparatus for heating lubricants used in engines or pumps, ensuring the oil reaches an optimal temperature for effective lubrication. This includes devices that raise lubricant temperature before or during operation to improve flow, reduce viscosity, and protect moving parts from wear. It falls under lubricating of machines or engines in general within mechanical engineering.
[F01M 5/002] Cooling -- F01M 5/002 (Cooling) covers methods and devices for lowering the temperature of lubricants used in engines or pumps, including heat-exchangers, cooling circuits, and temperature-control systems that maintain optimal lubricant performance and protect moving parts from overheating. It falls within the broader category of lubricating of machines or engines in general.
[F01M 5/005] Controlling temperature of lubricant -- The F01M 5/005 entry covers devices or processes that heat, cool or otherwise regulate the temperature of lubricants used in engines or pumps. It includes control systems, thermostats, heating elements and cooling circuits designed to maintain optimal lubricant viscosity and performance.
[F01M 5/007] Thermostatic control -- F01M 5/007 covers systems that regulate the temperature of lubricants in engines or pumps by using thermostatic devices. It includes methods and apparatus that heat, cool, or otherwise maintain lubricant temperature within a desired range to ensure proper lubrication performance. The classification falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, lubricating of machines or engines in general.
[F01M 5/02] Conditioning lubricant for aiding engine starting, e.g. heating -- This classification relates to methods and devices that condition engine lubricant to improve start-up, chiefly by heating the oil or otherwise adjusting its temperature or properties before ignition. It includes systems that warm the lubricant or treat it to ensure adequate flow in cold conditions, reducing start resistance and wear.
[F01M 5/021] by heating -- F01M 5/021 covers methods or devices that raise the temperature of lubricating oil or other lubricants to improve performance, reduce viscosity, or aid flow, typically by applying external heat sources, heated passages, or circulation loops that pre-heat the lubricant before it reaches the engine or pump.
[F01M 5/025] by prelubricating, e.g. using an accumulator -- This sub-group covers methods of pre-lubricating engines or pumps, where lubricant is supplied to moving parts before operation, typically by using an accumulator that stores pressurised oil. It falls under the broader category of controlling temperature of lubricants in mechanical engineering.
[F01M 5/04] Diluting, e.g. with fuel -- F01M 5/04 covers techniques for diluting lubricants, for example by mixing fuel or other liquids with the oil to modify its viscosity, temperature or cooling properties in engines or pumps. It includes systems that automatically add diluents to the lubricant to improve performance or protect components.
[F01M 7/00] Lubrication means specially adapted for machine or engine running-in -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Lubrication means specially adapted for machine or engine running-in
[F01M 9/00] Lubrication means having pertinent characteristics not provided for in, or of interest apart from, groups -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Having means for introducing additives to lubricant;  Use of fuel as lubricant;  Dip or splash lubrication;  Drip lubrication;  Lubrication of valve gear or auxiliaries;  Non-pressurised lubrication, or non-closed-circuit lubrication, not otherwise provided for.
[F01M 9/02] having means for introducing additives to lubricant -- The class refers to engines or pumps whose lubrication system incorporates a device that doses or mixes additives into the oil, such as a dosing pump, injection point or mixing chamber, allowing the lubricant's properties-viscosity, anti-wear, corrosion protection, or temperature control-to be adjusted during operation.
[F01M 9/04] Use of fuel as lubricant -- F01M 9/04 covers machines or engines where the fuel itself also functions as the lubricant, such as fuel oil that provides a boundary film for moving parts while being combusted for power. It includes designs that use the same fluid for both lubrication and energy, reducing the need for separate oil circuits.
[F01M 9/06] Dip or splash lubrication -- F01M 9/06 refers to dip or splash lubrication, a method where parts of a machine are immersed in oil or periodically splashed with oil to create a lubricating film. This technique is applied in engines and pumps, allowing moving components to pick up oil from a reservoir or splash it onto surfaces, providing continuous lubrication without a dedicated pump.
[F01M 9/08] Drip lubrication -- Drip lubrication refers to supplying lubricant to a machine or engine by means of drops or a continuous stream that falls onto moving parts, usually using a reservoir, wick or valve to control the flow. It is applied to bearings, gears and other components where precise, low-volume oil delivery is needed to reduce friction and wear.
[F01M 9/10] Lubrication of valve gear or auxiliaries -- F01M 9/10 relates to the lubrication of valve gear or auxiliary components in engines and pumps. It covers arrangements, devices or methods that supply, distribute or control lubricants to valve mechanisms, timing gear, camshafts, feed pumps and other supporting parts to ensure proper operation and wear reduction.
[F01M 9/101] of cam surfaces -- F01M 9/101 covers the lubrication of cam surfaces in engines and other mechanical devices, including the selection of lubricants and methods for reducing wear on cam lobes and followers. It falls under the broader category of lubricating machines or engines in general.
[F01M 9/102] of camshaft bearings -- F01M 9/102 classifies lubrication measures that specifically relate to camshaft bearings, covering the selection, application and maintenance of oils or greases for these components. It is situated within mechanical engineering, under engines or pumps and the broader category of lubricating machines or engines in general.
[F01M 9/103] of valve stem and guide -- F01M 9/103 covers lubricating techniques or devices specifically applied to the valve stem and its guide in engines or pumps, aiming to reduce friction and wear on these moving parts. The entry includes any system, material or method that provides adequate lubrication to ensure reliable valve operation.
[F01M 9/104] of tappets -- F01M 9/104 classifies lubrication features that specifically relate to tappets in engines or pumps, covering the selection of lubricants, delivery methods and design aspects aimed at ensuring proper oil film formation on tappet surfaces. This subclass falls under the broader category of lubricating machines or engines in general, focusing on the functional requirements and performance of tappet lubrication.
[F01M 9/105] using distribution conduits -- F01M 9/105 pertains to lubricating engines or machines by means of distribution conduits such as pipes, tubes or channels that convey oil, grease or other lubricants to moving parts. It covers systems and methods that use these conduits to deliver lubricant throughout the equipment, ensuring continuous and controlled lubrication during operation.
[F01M 9/106] Oil reservoirs -- F01M 9/106 pertains to oil reservoirs used in the lubrication of machines or engines. It covers the design, arrangement and features of oil tanks, sumps or storage chambers that hold lubricating oil for supply to moving parts, including provisions for filling, venting, cooling or pressure regulation.
[F01M 9/107] of rocker shaft bearings -- F01M 9/107 covers methods and devices for lubricating the bearings of a rocker-shaft in engines or other machines. It falls under mechanical engineering, specifically the lubricating of engines or pumps, and addresses bearing lubrication practices not covered by other groups.
[F01M 9/108] of auxiliaries -- F01M 9/108 covers auxiliary elements used in the lubrication of machines or engines, such as additives, auxiliary pumps, reservoirs, filters or other supporting components that aid the main lubrication system. These items are not primary lubricants themselves but facilitate, enhance or control the distribution and effectiveness of the lubricant.
[F01M 9/109] of rotary slide or sleeve valves -- The F01M 9/109 subclass concerns lubricating machines or engines that use rotary slide or sleeve valves, focusing on the specific lubrication characteristics and requirements of these valve types rather than broader lubrication methods. It falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and general machine lubrication.
[F01M 9/12] Non-pressurised lubrication, or non-closed-circuit lubrication, not otherwise provided for -- F01M 9/12 covers lubrication systems for machines or engines that operate without a pressure source or a closed-circuit, where the method of delivering lubricant is not covered by any other subclass. It includes simple drip, splash or gravity-fed arrangements used for general lubrication of mechanical parts. This subclass resides under Mechanical Engineering → Engines or Pumps → Lubricating of Machines or Engines in General.
[F01N] GAS-FLOW SILENCERS OR EXHAUST APPARATUS FOR MACHINES OR ENGINES IN GENERAL -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Silencing apparatus in exhaust systems for reducing the noise emitted by machines or engines in general, but more particularly internal combustion engines.  Exhaust or silencing apparatus having means for purifying, rendering innocuous or otherwise treating exhaust. The main apparatus includes particulate filters, absorbents and other catalytic reactors. The means can also be an electric discharge or the addition of air or liquids.  Exhaust or silencing apparatus combined or associated with devices profiting by exhaust energy, mainly the devices using exhaust heat.  Electrical control, monitoring or diagnostic of exhaust gas treating apparatus.  Other constructional features common to the above apparatus or the exhaust conduits including manufacture, assembly, disassembly or material selection.  **Glossary:** - Absorbent: Could also be found under the wording: trap, adsorbent or storage device, e.g. NOx-trap. It is an element which can absorb at least one compound and release/adsorb it again. The reduction or oxidation of the compound in the absorbent may or may not occur, depending of the presence of a suitable catalyst therefore.
[F01N 1/00] Silencing apparatus characterised by method of silencing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Silencing apparatus for reducing or controlling the noise in exhaust emitted by internal combustion engines.
[F01N 1/003] by using dead chambers communicating with exhaust gas flow passages -- F01N 1/003 refers to exhaust-gas silencers that achieve noise reduction by means of dead chambers linked to the exhaust flow passages. The chambers act as pressure-pulse absorbers, dampening sound waves generated by the engine or pump before they exit the system. This method falls under the broader group of gas-flow silencers for mechanical engines.
[F01N 1/006] comprising at least one perforated tube extending from inlet to outlet of the silencer -- This subclass covers silencing devices for engines or machines that incorporate at least one perforated tube extending continuously from the inlet to the outlet of the silencer, allowing gas to pass through the perforations while attenuating noise. The design is used in exhaust or gas-flow silencers to improve acoustic performance without restricting flow.
[F01N 1/02] by using resonance -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Exhaust gas silencers with physical dimensions intended to alter, reduce or cancel sound at particular frequencies, often with little or no impedance to the passage of exhaust gas.  Resonance is achieved by placing a chamber or a channel in communication with the exhaust flow without the exhaust flow passing therethrough.
[F01N 1/023] Helmholtz resonators -- F01N 1/023 designates Helmholtz resonators, devices that employ a cavity with a narrow neck to create acoustic resonance that attenuates targeted sound frequencies in gas-flow or exhaust systems. They are used as silencing apparatus for engines or pumps, characterized by the specific method of sound reduction.
[F01N 1/026] Annular resonance chambers arranged concentrically to an exhaust passage and communicating with it, e.g. via at least one opening in the exhaust passage
[F01N 1/04] having sound-absorbing materials in resonance chambers -- F01N 1/04 covers silencing devices for engines that incorporate sound-absorbing material positioned in resonance chambers to dampen acoustic vibrations. The feature is used to reduce noise emitted by gas-flow silencers or exhaust systems in mechanical engineering applications.
[F01N 1/06] by using interference effect -- F01N 1/06 refers to silencing devices for engines or pumps that reduce noise by creating destructive interference of sound waves. The method uses geometrical or acoustic features that cause sound waves to cancel each other, improving exhaust or gas-flow noise attenuation. It falls under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and gas-flow silencers.
[F01N 1/065] by using an active noise source, e.g. speakers -- F01N 1/065 covers exhaust silencing devices for engines that employ active noise control, such as speakers that generate an anti-phase sound to cancel the engine's exhaust noise. This method is classified under mechanical engineering, engines or pumps, and gas-flow silencers.
[F01N 1/08] by reducing exhaust energy by throttling or whirling -- F01N 1/08 covers silencing devices for engines or pumps that lower exhaust energy by using throttling or whirling mechanisms. It is a subcategory of gas-flow silencers or exhaust apparatus within the broader mechanical-engineering field of engines or pumps.
