[F03B 13/12] characterised by using wave or tide energy -- F03B 13/12 covers mechanical devices that convert the kinetic or potential energy of ocean waves or tides into useful work, representing adaptations of liquid-driven machines for renewable energy applications.
[F03B 13/14] using wave energy -- F03B 13/14 classifies adaptations of liquid-handling machines or engines that harness wave energy, such as wave-driven pumps, turbines or generators that convert ocean-surface motion into hydraulic or electrical power for specialized applications. It falls under mechanical engineering, specifically machines or engines for liquids, and focuses on special-use adaptations.
[F03B 13/141] with a static energy collector -- F03B 13/141 covers liquid-driven machines or engines that incorporate a static energy collector, such as a stationary solar-thermal or photovoltaic device, to supply power to the pump or engine. The classification is used for adaptations where the collector is fixed in place and provides the energy needed for the liquid-handling operation.
[F03B 13/142] which creates an oscillating water column -- This subclass covers machines or engines for liquids that are adapted to produce an oscillating water column, a chamber in which water level moves up and down with wave action to drive an air turbine for power generation. It lies within mechanical engineering, machines or engines for liquids, and adaptations for special use.
[F03B 13/144] which lifts water above sea level -- F03B 13/144 classifies machines and engines adapted to lift water to a height above sea level, including pumps, hydraulic lifts and similar devices used for irrigation, flood control, water supply and other applications that require elevating water to higher elevations. It falls under mechanical engineering, specifically machines or engines for liquids and their special-use adaptations.
[F03B 13/145] for immediate use in an energy converter -- This subclass covers adaptations of liquid-driven machines that are intended to be integrated directly into an energy conversion system, such as turbines or pumps that feed immediately into generators or other converters. It includes modifications that allow immediate power transfer without intermediate storage.
[F03B 13/147] for later use -- The F03B 13/147 subclass concerns adaptations of liquid-handling machines that allow the processed fluid to be stored for later use, such as integrated holding tanks, buffer chambers or delayed-discharge features that keep the liquid within the equipment until needed. This falls under mechanical engineering provisions for specialized modifications of pumps, compressors or other liquid engines.
[F03B 13/148] using the static pressure increase due to the wave -- The code F03B 13/148 relates to liquid-handling machines that harness the static pressure rise generated by a water wave, for example wave-driven pumps or hydraulic devices that convert wave motion into useful pressure for pumping, lifting or processing fluids.
[F03B 13/16] using the relative movement between a wave-operated member, and another member, -- **Definition:** This place covers:  For a mechanical energy collection there must be a relative movement between a member directly acted upon by the waves (the Wave Operated Member or "wom" for short) and some other reaction structure or member relative to which the "wom" moves. This reaction structure is called REaction Member or "rem" (for short).
[F03B 13/18] where the other member, is fixed, at least at one point, with respect to the sea bed or shore -- F03B 13/18 relates to adaptations of liquid-handling machines or engines in which one component is anchored, at least at one point, to the sea-bed or to a shore structure, such as fixed pontoons, anchored floating platforms or wave-energy devices that remain restrained while the other part moves relative to the water.

[F03B 13/181] for limited rotation -- F03B 13/181 covers adaptations of liquid-driven machines or engines that limit the rotary motion of the shaft or rotor to a restricted range rather than continuous rotation, such as oscillating pumps, reciprocating stirrers or valves that turn only a few degrees for a specific function.
[F03B 13/1815] with an up-and-down movement -- F03B 13/1815 designates liquid-handling machines that are specially adapted to produce an up-and-down, or reciprocating, motion of the working element such as pistons or diaphragms for pumping, mixing or stirring fluids.
[F03B 13/182] with a to-and-fro movement -- F03B 13/182 covers machines or engines for liquids that employ a reciprocating (to-and-fro) motion, such as pumps, compressors or similar devices where the working element moves back and forth to transfer fluid, typically adapted for special or non-standard applications.
[F03B 13/1825] for 360° rotation -- F03B 13/1825 covers adaptations of liquid-handling machines that provide continuous 360° rotation, allowing components to turn fully around during operation. It falls under mechanical engineering, machines or engines for liquids, and deals with special-use modifications.
[F03B 13/183] of a turbine-like wom -- F03B 13/183 covers turbine-like wind machines adapted for special uses in liquid handling, such as wind-driven water pumps or power-generation devices whose rotors operate like turbines.
[F03B 13/1835] of an endless-belt type wom -- This subclass covers liquid-moving machines that employ an endless-belt arrangement to drive a worm or similar transmission for moving the fluid, a special adaptation of belt-driven pump mechanisms.
[F03B 13/184] of a water-wheel type wom -- F03B 13/184 covers adaptations of water-wheel type water turbines for special applications, such as modifications to the wheel shape, runner configuration, mounting or flow-control features that tailor performance to particular operating conditions or uses, extending the basic water-wheel concept.

This sub-category falls under adaptations of hydraulic or pneumatic machinery for specialized applications.
[F03B 13/1865] where the connection between wom and conversion system takes tension only  F03B13/187 ,  F03B13/1875  take precedence -- This CPC sub-group covers adaptations of liquid-handling machines or engines where the link between a wind-driven unit and its conversion system is designed to carry only tensile forces, taking precedence over the more specific sub-classes F03B 13/187 and F03B 13/1875.
[F03B 13/187] and the wom directly actuates the piston of a pump -- The CPC subclass F03B 13/187 pertains to liquid-handling machines where a wom element directly drives the pump piston, providing a dedicated actuation mechanism for specialized pump applications. [F03B 13/189] acting directly on the piston of a pump -- The CPC subclass F03B 13/189 covers mechanisms that act directly on a pump piston, such as cams, levers, rods or other devices that transfer motion straight to the piston to create suction or discharge. It is used for adaptations of liquid-pumping machines where the piston is driven without intermediate hydraulic or pneumatic stages.
[F03B 13/1895] where the tie is a tension/compression member -- F03B 13/1895 pertains to liquid-handling machines in which a structural tie functions as a tension or compression element, acting as a brace or load-bearing member rather than a moving part. The classification is used for adaptations where such ties support the machine's frame or components under specific loading conditions.
[F03B 13/20] wherein both members are movable relative to the sea bed or shore -- F03B 13/20 covers machines or engines for liquids in which both components can move relative to the seabed or shore, such as floating or movable marine pumps, anchoring systems, or vessels whose drive units and supporting structures are not fixed. The classification is used for adaptations that allow relative motion between the machine and its foundation in marine environments.
[F03B 13/22] using the flow of water resulting from wave movements to drive a motor or turbine -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The waves indirectly cause a flow of liquid somewhere, which then drives a motor or turbine.  DE102008016839
[F03B 13/24] to produce a flow of air, e.g. to drive an air turbine -- The subclass F03B 13/24 covers apparatus that creates a flow of air, such as blowers, fans or compressors, specifically intended to drive an air turbine. [F03B 13/26] using tide energy -- F03B 13/26 covers devices that harness tidal movements to generate power, describing adaptations of machines or engines for liquids that are specially designed to extract energy from tides.

[F03B 13/268] making use of a dam -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The tide is collected in a basin closed by dams with sluices, which are open when the tide is rising.
