It includes pumps and similar devices whose specific piston-type features fall outside the more detailed group definitions.
[F04B 19/24] Pumping by heat expansion of pumped fluid -- F04B 19/24 covers pumps that operate by heating the fluid to be moved, causing it to expand and generate flow, a subclass of positive-displacement machines for liquids. It includes devices where thermal expansion of the pumped fluid is the primary driving mechanism.
[F04B 2015/026] with a priming plunger or piston ahead of the pumping piston and connected on the same piston rod -- The entry relates to reciprocating pumps that incorporate a priming plunger or piston positioned ahead of the main pumping piston on the same piston rod, used to create suction and pre-fill the pump chamber before the principal pumping stroke.
[F04B 2015/081] Liquefied gases -- F04B 2015/081 covers machines and apparatus for producing, handling or storing liquefied gases, including compressors, expansion devices and refrigeration cycles used to liquefy gases such as oxygen, nitrogen or hydrogen. It also includes related control and safety systems for maintaining gases in a liquid state.
[F04B 2015/0812] Air -- CPC F04B 2015/0812 covers inventions relating to the use of air for plant protection or pest control, including the generation, distribution or application of air streams to treat crops, forests or stored products.
[F04B 2015/0814] Argon -- F04B 2015/0814 relates to the use of argon, an inert gas, in steam turbine or turbo-generator systems, such as for cooling, gas-filled bearings or sealing to enhance performance and corrosion resistance.
[F04B 2015/0816] Carbon monoxide -- The CPC class F04B 2015/0816 pertains to steam-generator and boiler technology that involves carbon monoxide. It covers inventions related to the production, monitoring, control or utilization of CO in boiler systems, including methods to limit emissions, detect concentrations and manage safety or efficiency aspects of CO as a by-product or fuel.
[F04B 2015/0818] Carbon dioxide -- This CPC sub-group covers pumps, compressors, fans or other fluid-handling equipment specifically designed for the use, circulation, pressurisation, recovery or disposal of carbon dioxide as a working or process fluid, including systems that exploit CO2 in refrigeration, heat-pumping or power-generation cycles.
[F04B 2015/082] Helium -- F04B 2015/082 classifies pumps, compressors or related devices that are specifically adapted for handling helium, including methods of pressurising, circulating or delivering helium in industrial, scientific or cryogenic applications. It covers designs that address helium's low density, high diffusivity and inertness, and any associated sealing, lubrication or cooling features.
[F04B 2015/0822] Hydrogen -- The CPC subclass F04B 2015/0822 covers the use of hydrogen in steam generators, superheaters and other devices for heating gases, including methods of producing, handling or burning hydrogen as an energy carrier.
[F04B 2015/0824] Nitrogen -- F04B 2015/0824 relates to internal-combustion or gas-turbine engines that employ nitrogen as a working fluid or fuel, focusing on power-generation or propulsion systems that exploit nitrogen's thermodynamic characteristics. It includes designs, control strategies and cycle modifications specifically adapted for nitrogen-based operation.
[F04B 2015/0826] Oxygen -- The CPC subclass F04B 2015/0826 covers inventions that involve the generation, supply, handling or use of oxygen in pump-type or compression devices. It includes oxygen generators, oxygen-enriched compressors and systems that deliver or manage oxygen for industrial, medical or other applications within the broader category of pumps and lift devices.
[F04B 2027/1809] Controlled pressure -- F04B 2027/1809 relates to devices and methods that automatically regulate fluid pressure in hydraulic or pneumatic systems, including pressure regulators, relief valves, and control circuits that maintain a set pressure level, ensuring safe and efficient operation of pumps, machines, or process equipment.
[F04B 2027/1813] Crankcase pressure -- The CPC subclass F04B 2027/1813 relates to devices or methods that control, measure, or utilize pressure within the crankcase of internal-combustion engines. It covers systems such as pressure relief valves, monitoring sensors, and ventilation arrangements designed to manage crankcase gases and maintain engine performance and durability.
[F04B 2027/1818] Suction pressure -- F04B 2027/1818 concerns the control, measurement or regulation of suction pressure in pumps, compressors or similar fluid-moving devices, including methods to prevent cavitation, maintain flow stability or optimize inlet conditions.
[F04B 2027/1822] Valve-controlled fluid connection -- The CPC subclass F04B 2027/1822 relates to fluid-handling assemblies where a valve governs the connection between a conduit and a component such as a pump, engine or hydraulic circuit, allowing start-stop or directional control of flow. It covers mechanisms that open, close or modulate the passage in response to pressure, control signals or mechanical actuation.
[F04B 2027/1827] between crankcase and discharge chamber -- This subclass covers devices, passages or coupling elements located between the crankcase, where a crank-driven pump or compressor resides, and the discharge chamber where the fluid is expelled. It includes ducts, manifolds, valves or other structures that manage the flow path and pressure transition between these two sections of a turbomachine.
[F04B 2027/1831] between crankcase and suction chamber -- The classification concerns structural or functional elements that connect a crankcase to a suction chamber in an internal-combustion engine, such as passages, valves, ducts or sealing arrangements that control the flow of air-fuel mixture or gases between these two volumes.
[F04B 2027/1836] between crankcase and working chamber -- The CPC entry F04B 2027/1836 pertains to internal-combustion piston engines and specifically to components or arrangements that lie between the crankcase and the working (combustion) chamber, such as seals, partitions or other means that isolate the two volumes and prevent leakage. It covers designs that connect or separate these spaces while allowing the engine to function safely and efficiently.
[F04B 2027/184] Valve controlling parameter -- The CPC subclass F04B 2027/184 covers valves equipped with mechanisms that adjust or regulate operating parameters such as flow rate, pressure, temperature or speed. It includes designs where the valve itself incorporates control elements-like throttles, pilots or feedback devices-to maintain desired conditions in hydraulic, pneumatic or fluid power systems. This subclass groups inventions that focus on the parameter-control function rather than the basic valve structure.
[F04B 2027/1845] Crankcase pressure -- The CPC class F04B 2027/1845 covers arrangements and components that manage the pressure inside the crankcase of internal-combustion engines. It includes pressure-relief valves, venting passages, filtering devices and sensors designed to prevent excess pressure, oil leakage or contamination. These measures help maintain engine performance and reduce emissions.
[F04B 2027/185] Discharge pressure -- F04B 2027/185 covers aspects of pump operation related to the pressure at the pump outlet. It includes devices, methods or control systems that generate, regulate, monitor or adjust the discharge pressure of hydraulic or pneumatic pumps, and associated pressure-sensing or relief mechanisms.
[F04B 2027/1854] External parameters -- The F04B 2027/1854 subclass covers inventions that detect, measure, process or display external environmental data of a watercraft-such as wind, waves, currents, temperature, sea state or other surrounding conditions-for use in navigation, propulsion control, safety or other ship-board functions. It includes sensors, data-handling units and display arrangements that manage these external parameters.
[F04B 2027/1859] Suction pressure -- F04B 2027/1859 relates to the measurement, control or regulation of suction pressure in fluid-moving devices such as pumps, compressors and hydraulic systems, covering methods for detecting, maintaining or adjusting the inlet pressure to ensure proper flow and prevent cavitation.
[F04B 2027/1863] with an auxiliary valve, controlled by -- The subclass F04B 2027/1863 pertains to pump assemblies that incorporate an auxiliary valve whose operation is governed by a control mechanism, such as a pressure, flow or temperature sensor or an electronic controller. This arrangement allows the valve to open or close automatically in response to system conditions, improving pump performance, safety or efficiency.
[F04B 2027/1868] Crankcase pressure -- The CPC code F04B 2027/1868 designates technology for managing pressure inside a crankcase of internal-combustion engines. It includes ventilation systems, pressure sensors and relief valves that control crankcase pressure to prevent oil leakage, reduce emissions and improve engine performance.
[F04B 2027/1872] Discharge pressure -- The CPC subgroup F04B 2027/1872 covers hydraulic or pneumatic machines such as pumps and compressors with respect to the pressure at which the working fluid is expelled. It includes designs, control strategies and measuring arrangements that define or regulate discharge pressure to meet performance, safety or efficiency requirements.
[F04B 2027/1877] External parameters -- The entry covers pumps or pump-control arrangements that sense and respond to external conditions such as pressure, temperature, flow rate, viscosity, fluid level or similar parameters, using sensors or control logic to adjust operation. It includes methods and devices that modify speed, displacement or power based on those measured external inputs.
[F04B 2027/1881] Suction pressure -- The CPC subclass F04B 2027/1881 relates to devices or methods that generate or control the suction side pressure in pumps, compressors or similar fluid-moving machines. It covers arrangements for creating a pressure drop before the impeller, suction chambers, inlet ducts, and any mechanisms for regulating that suction pressure to improve flow stability or prevent cavitation.
[F04B 2027/1886] Open (not controlling) fluid passage -- The CPC subclass F04B 2027/1886 relates to fluid passages that are open and do not provide flow control, such as ducts, channels or conduits that allow fluid to move freely without valves, throttles or other regulating elements. It applies to designs where the passage itself does not influence pressure or flow rate.
[F04B 2027/189] between crankcase and discharge chamber -- F04B 2027/189 covers arrangements that link the crankcase of an internal-combustion engine to its discharge chamber or exhaust system. It includes devices such as crankcase ventilation ducts, pressure-relief passages and combined crankcase-exhaust passages that allow gases to flow from the crankcase to the exhaust for cleaning, pressure equalisation or emission control. This subgroup pertains to the mechanical connection and related sealing or filtering features between these two engine sections.
[F04B 2027/1895] between crankcase and suction chamber -- This CPC subgroup covers provisions that link or separate the engine crankcase from the suction (intake) chamber in internal-combustion engines. It includes breather valves, pressure-equalising passages and sealing arrangements that control the flow of gases between the two volumes.
[F04B 2201/00] Pump parameters -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Pump control parameters for pumps for liquids and elastic fluids.
[F04B 2201/02] Piston parameters -- The CPC class F04B 2201/02, titled Piston parameters, falls under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids, pump parameters. It covers the technical characteristics of pistons in positive-displacement pumps, such as dimensions, stroke length, speed, clearances, material composition and surface treatments that influence pump performance.
[F04B 2201/0201] Position of the piston -- The CPC code F04B 2201/0201 relates to the position of the piston in positive-displacement liquid pumps. It covers features that define, control, measure or adjust where the piston is within its stroke, including sensors, stops and mechanisms that indicate piston location for performance or safety.
[F04B 2201/02011] Angular position of a piston rotating around its own axis -- This subclass covers features related to the angular orientation of a piston that rotates about its own axis within a positive-displacement pump, used to define operating characteristics like timing and flow. It falls under CPC section F04B dealing with pump parameters.
[F04B 2201/0202] Linear speed of the piston -- The code F04B 2201/0202 classifies technical information concerning the linear speed of the piston in positive-displacement pumps for liquids. It falls under pump-parameter specifications within the broader category of mechanical engineering for displacement machines, detailing how piston travel rate influences pump performance.
[F04B 2201/0203] Acceleration of the piston -- This entry addresses the rate of change of piston speed in positive-displacement liquid pumps, covering how piston acceleration affects flow, pressure, mechanical stresses and overall pump performance, and includes design methods for controlling acceleration to improve efficiency and reduce wear.
[F04B 2201/0204] Power on the piston -- F04B 2201/0204 covers the power supplied to the piston in positive-displacement liquid pumps, focusing on the energy source and its transmission to the moving piston. It is applied to patents describing how mechanical power is delivered to pistons that drive pump operation.
[F04B 2201/0205] Piston ring wear -- F04B 2201/0205 pertains to the wear of piston rings in positive-displacement liquid pumps. It covers the degradation of sealing surfaces that can affect pump efficiency and includes methods for assessing, preventing, and managing such wear.
[F04B 2201/0206] Length of piston stroke -- The CPC entry F04B 2201/0206 designates the specification of piston-stroke length in positive-displacement liquid pumps. It covers how the distance traveled by the pump piston is defined, measured and related to pump performance parameters such as flow rate, pressure and efficiency.
[F04B 2201/0207] Number of pumping strokes in unit time -- The CPC subclass F04B 2201/0207 relates to pump parameters that specify the number of pumping strokes performed in a given time interval. It describes the operating frequency of positive-displacement liquid pumps, influencing flow rate, power consumption and overall performance characteristics.
[F04B 2201/02071] Total number of pumping strokes -- The entry F04B 2201/02071 covers the specification or measurement of the total number of strokes performed by a positive-displacement pump. It pertains to pump parameters that indicate how many pumping cycles have occurred, which is useful for monitoring performance, maintenance schedules and operational control.
[F04B 2201/0208] Leakage across the piston -- F04B 2201/0208 concerns leakage across the piston in positive-displacement liquid pumps, addressing fluid loss through the piston seal and its impact on pump efficiency and performance.
[F04B 2201/0209] Duration of piston stroke -- F04B 2201/0209 covers the duration of piston stroke in positive-displacement liquid pumps, defining the time the piston moves from one end of its travel to the other and how this parameter affects flow rate and pump performance. It is a detailed sub-category of pump parameters within the mechanical engineering hierarchy for positive-displacement machines.
[F04B 2201/021] Rotational speed of a piston rotating around its own axis -- The CPC subclass F04B 2201/021 covers specifications of the rotational speed of a piston that spins about its own axis, as a parameter influencing the performance of positive-displacement liquid pumps. It includes methods for measuring, controlling or optimizing that speed to affect flow rate, pressure or efficiency.
[F04B 2201/04] Carter parameters -- F04B 2201/04 is a CPC subclass that defines the Carter parameters used to characterise positive-displacement pumps for liquids. These parameters include the pump's displacement per revolution, volumetric efficiency, leakage, and hydraulic losses, providing a standard way to describe pump performance in mechanical-engineering specifications.
[F04B 2201/0401] Carter pressure -- CPC code F04B 2201/0401 refers to the Carter pressure parameter used in positive-displacement liquid pumps, describing the pressure drop that occurs across flow-restricting components such as valves or throttles within the pump. It is a key design and performance metric for assessing suction characteristics and overall pump efficiency.
[F04B 2201/0402] Lubricating oil temperature -- F04B 2201/0402 refers to specifications dealing with the temperature of lubricating oil in positive-displacement pumps for liquids, covering how oil temperature is measured, controlled, or managed to maintain pump performance and reliability. It falls under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids, and focuses on pump parameters.
[F04B 2201/0403] Carter housing temperature -- The CPC subclass F04B 2201/0403 covers features that monitor or control the temperature of the pump's housing-often called the carter-within positive-displacement liquid pumps. It includes designs, sensors, heating or cooling arrangements, and related methods aimed at maintaining optimal operating temperature of the pump casing.
[F04B 2201/0404] Lubricating oil condition -- F04B 2201/0404 classifies pump parameters that relate to the condition of lubricating oil, such as temperature, viscosity, contamination level, and wear indicators. It is applied to inventions that monitor, control, or maintain oil quality in positive-displacement liquid pumps.
[F04B 2201/0405] Leakage -- In the CPC, code F04B 2201/0405 designates the parameter of leakage in positive-displacement liquid pumps, covering the amount of fluid that escapes the pump during operation, whether through clearances, seals or wear. It is used to classify inventions that measure, reduce, or describe such unintended fluid loss.
[F04B 2201/0406] Pressure change across an oil filter -- F04B 2201/0406 concerns the pressure drop that occurs when oil flows through a filter in a positive-displacement pump system, focusing on how the pressure change is measured or specified to evaluate filter performance and ensure proper operation of hydraulic or lubrication circuits.
[F04B 2201/06] Valve parameters -- F04B 2201/06 concerns the technical characteristics of valves used in positive-displacement pumps, including dimensions, flow-rate coefficients, opening and closing pressures, travel limits, sealing performance and response to fluid properties, as they affect pump operation and efficiency.
[F04B 2201/0601] Opening times -- F04B 2201/0601 covers the specification and control of opening times in positive-displacement liquid pumps, including the duration and timing of valve or chamber opening during each cycle and related adjustment mechanisms.
[F04B 2201/06011] of the inlet valve only -- The CPC subclass F04B 2201/06011 covers pump parameters that are defined solely for the inlet valve, such as its flow-rate, pressure drop, timing or geometry, without reference to other pump components. It falls under positive-displacement machines for liquids in mechanical engineering.
[F04B 2201/06012] of the outlet valve only -- The CPC entry F04B 2201/06012 covers pump-parameter specifications that pertain solely to the outlet valve, such as its opening pressure, flow-control characteristics, and sealing performance. It is used to classify documents that discuss valve-only aspects of positive-displacement liquid pumps without reference to other pump components.
[F04B 2201/0602] Valve acceleration -- F04B 2201/0602 - Valve acceleration covers the rate at which a valve in a positive-displacement liquid pump moves from closed to open, affecting flow and pressure dynamics. It is used to classify inventions that control or improve the timing and speed of valve operation in pump mechanisms.
[F04B 2201/0603] Valve wear -- F04B 2201/0603 concerns the deterioration of valves used in positive-displacement pumps for liquids, including erosion, corrosion, abrasion and other forms of material wear that affect valve performance and lifespan. It covers methods for detecting, preventing or mitigating such wear in pump systems.
[F04B 2201/0604] Valve noise -- The code F04B 2201/0604 covers acoustic phenomena associated with valves in positive-displacement pumps, including the generation, measurement and analysis of valve-induced noise. It relates to how valve design, operation speed and fluid properties affect sound levels, and to methods for reducing or mitigating that noise.
[F04B 2201/0605] Leakage over a valve -- F04B 2201/0605 deals with the assessment and control of fluid leakage that occurs across a valve in positive-displacement pumps, covering methods for detecting, quantifying, and minimizing such leakage to maintain pump efficiency and performance.
[F04B 2201/0606] Opening width or height -- F04B 2201/0606 covers the dimensional characteristics of openings in positive-displacement liquid pumps, specifically the width or height of ports, chambers or valves that affect fluid flow. It is used to classify patents that describe how these opening dimensions are defined, measured or optimized to influence pump performance.
[F04B 2201/06061] of the inlet valve -- This subclass covers specifications and design features of the inlet valve in positive-displacement liquid pumps, including its geometry, opening and closing timing, flow-control and sealing arrangements, and related performance parameters.
[F04B 2201/06062] of the outlet valve -- The subclass F04B 2201/06062 concerns the outlet valve of positive-displacement liquid pumps, covering its design, operation and related parameters such as flow control, sealing and actuation. It addresses how the valve is integrated into pump systems to regulate the discharge of fluid.
[F04B 2201/08] Cylinder or housing parameters -- The CPC subclass F04B 2201/08 addresses the dimensional and material characteristics of cylinders or housings used in positive-displacement liquid pumps, including size, shape, bore, wall thickness and construction material. It falls under mechanical engineering and positive-displacement machines for liquids, covering pump-parameter specifications.
[F04B 2201/0801] Temperature -- The code addresses temperature as a functional parameter of positive-displacement liquid pumps, covering how temperature is measured, monitored, or controlled in pump operation. It includes temperature-dependent performance data, thermal protection, and regulation features that influence pump design and efficiency.
[F04B 2201/0802] Vibration -- This subclass concerns the vibration characteristics of positive-displacement liquid pumps, covering the generation, measurement, analysis and control of oscillatory motions that affect pump performance, reliability and noise. It includes studies of resonant frequencies, vibration-induced wear and methods for damping or isolation.
[F04B 2201/0803] Leakage -- The CPC subclass F04B 2201/0803 covers the measurement, control, and reduction of leakage in positive-displacement liquid pumps. It includes any features, designs, or methods intended to detect, quantify, or minimise fluid loss through seals, clearances or valve gaps during operation.
[F04B 2201/0804] Noise -- The code F04B 2201/0804 covers the study and control of acoustic emissions generated by positive-displacement liquid pumps. It includes methods for measuring, reducing or otherwise managing noise produced during pump operation, encompassing design features, materials and auxiliary devices aimed at improving acoustic performance.
[F04B 2201/0805] Rotational speed of a rotating cylinder block -- The CPC subclass F04B 2201/0805 covers specifications of the rotational speed of a rotating cylinder block in positive-displacement liquid pumps. It falls under pump parameters for mechanical engineering devices that move fluids by positive displacement, focusing on how fast the cylinder block turns during operation.
[F04B 2201/0806] Resonant frequency -- F04B 2201/0806 refers to the resonant frequency of positive-displacement liquid pumps, the natural vibration frequency of the pump and its drive components. This parameter is used to evaluate dynamic stability, prevent harmful resonance, and guide design for efficient operation. It is classified under pump parameters within the positive-displacement machines for liquids sub-category of mechanical engineering.
[F04B 2201/0807] Number of working cylinders -- In the CPC, code F04B 2201/0807 classifies specifications that address how many working cylinders a positive-displacement pump for liquids contains. It falls under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines, and the sub-group dealing with pump parameters.
[F04B 2201/0808] Size of the dead volume -- F04B 2201/0808 refers to the measurement or specification of the dead volume in positive-displacement liquid pumps. The dead volume is the internal space that retains fluid when the pump is at rest, influencing start-up flow, efficiency and pulsation. This subclass groups inventions that address or describe the size of that residual volume.
[F04B 2201/12] Parameters of driving or driven means -- The classification addresses the specifications and performance characteristics of the power source and driven components in positive-displacement liquid pumps. It includes aspects such as speed, torque, power consumption and efficiency of motors, engines, gearboxes, couplings and other means that drive or are driven by the pump.
[F04B 2201/1201] Rotational speed of the axis -- The CPC subclass F04B 2201/1201 pertains to positive-displacement liquid pumps and defines the rotational speed of the pump's axis or shaft. It covers specifications, measurement methods, control techniques and speed limits that influence pump performance, efficiency and operating characteristics.
[F04B 2201/1202] Torque on the axis -- F04B 2201/1202 relates to the torque acting on the drive shaft of positive-displacement pumps, covering methods for measuring, monitoring, or controlling the axial torque that develops during pumping operation. It includes specifications of torque limits, performance curves, and torque-related design features of the pump mechanism.
[F04B 2201/1203] Power on the axis -- The CPC entry F04B 2201/1203 covers the specification of the power transmitted to the rotating shaft of a liquid-displacement pump, including the nominal power rating, input or output power, and related performance limits. It relates to pump parameters that define how much mechanical energy is supplied to or extracted from the pump's drive axis. This information is used to assess suitability, efficiency and sizing of positive-displacement pumps.
[F04B 2201/1204] Position of a rotating inclined plate -- The CPC subclass F04B 2201/1204 covers specifications of the location of a rotating inclined plate within positive-displacement pumps for liquids. It addresses how the plate's position relative to the pump housing or drive shaft influences flow characteristics, sealing, and efficiency. This subclass is part of the broader category for mechanical engineering pump parameters.
[F04B 2201/12041] Angular position -- The F04B 2201/12041 subclass governs the definition, measurement or control of angular position in positive-displacement liquid pumps, covering how the rotational orientation of shafts, pistons, rotors or other moving elements is specified, monitored or adjusted for proper pump operation. It is used to classify documents dealing with angular-position sensors, encoders, feedback systems or related design parameters.
[F04B 2201/1205] Position of a non-rotating inclined plate -- The subclass F04B 2201/1205 covers the location and orientation of a stationary inclined plate used inside positive-displacement liquid pumps, detailing how its position influences pump geometry, flow path, seal formation or performance characteristics. It relates to design parameters that affect the pump's capacity, efficiency or pressure handling by specifying where the plate is placed relative to other pump components.
[F04B 2201/12051] Angular position -- The code F04B 2201/12051 defines a pump parameter concerning the angular position of moving parts such as impellers, rotors or vanes within a positive-displacement liquid pump. It covers specifications, adjustments and measurements of the component's orientation during operation. This parameter is used to describe performance, control and monitoring of the pump's mechanical action.
[F04B 2201/1206] Rotational speed of a rotating inclined plate -- This subclass covers the definition and control of the rotational speed of a rotating inclined plate in positive-displacement liquid pumps. It relates to pump parameters where the plate's angular velocity is specified for performance, efficiency or design calculations.
[F04B 2201/1207] Wear of the bearings -- The CPC subclass F04B 2201/1207 addresses wear of bearings in positive-displacement liquid pumps, covering degradation mechanisms, material loss, and their effect on pump performance and maintenance. It falls under pump parameters within mechanical engineering.
[F04B 2201/1208] Angular position of the shaft -- The code F04B 2201/1208 covers features that measure, detect or control the angular position of the rotating shaft in positive-displacement liquid pumps. It relates to pump-parameter technologies used to monitor shaft angle for performance, safety or regulation purposes.
[F04B 2201/1209] Radial force on the bearings -- The code F04B 2201/1209 covers specifications of the radial forces acting on pump bearings in positive-displacement machines for liquids, focusing on how these forces affect bearing performance and pump reliability. It is used to classify technical information on bearing load calculations, design limits and operating conditions within pump parameter documentation.
[F04B 2201/121] Load on the sucker rod -- F04B 2201/121 designates the load on the sucker-rod in positive-displacement machines for liquids, specifically the mechanical stresses and forces acting on the rod that transmits power from the prime mover to the pump plunger. It covers parameters such as tensile, compressive and cyclic loads that affect rod performance and durability.
[F04B 2201/1211] Position of the walking beam -- The code F04B 2201/1211 designates the specification of where the walking beam is situated in a positive-displacement liquid pump. It relates to the geometric placement of the beam within the pump housing, linkages and drive system, influencing stroke and flow characteristics.
[F04B 2201/1212] Oil pressure in the bearings -- F04B 2201/1212 covers the monitoring, regulation or specification of oil pressure supplied to the bearings of positive-displacement liquid pumps. It pertains to pump parameter considerations such as lubrication system performance and bearing protection.
[F04B 2201/1213] Eccentricity of an outer annular cam -- The code F04B 2201/1213 covers specifications of the eccentric offset of an outer annular cam within positive-displacement liquid pumps. It relates to how the cam's non-central positioning influences pump parameters such as displacement volume, flow rate and pressure development. This classification is used to identify design features that affect the performance and efficiency of cam-driven pumps.
[F04B 2201/124] Coupling parameters -- F04B 2201/124 covers the specification of coupling parameters for positive-displacement liquid pumps, including the dimensions, stiffness, speed limits and alignment tolerances of shafts, gears, belts or other mechanical links that transmit power between the motor and the pump.
[F04B 2201/1241] Engagement -- F04B 2201/1241 Engagement covers the way pump components are coupled or linked to transmit motion or power, such as shafts, gears, belts or other drive connections, and how this interaction influences pump performance parameters. It includes specifications of mechanical fit, timing and synchronization between driving and driven parts within positive-displacement liquid pumps.
[F04B 2201/127] Braking parameters -- F04B 2201/127 covers the specification and measurement of braking parameters for positive-displacement liquid pumps. It includes characteristics such as brake torque, stopping time, deceleration rate and control methods used to regulate pump shutdown, ensuring safe and efficient operation.
[F04B 2203/00] Motor parameters -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Motor control parameters of pumps for liquids and elastic fluids.
[F04B 2203/02] of rotating electric motors -- This subclass covers rotating electric motors used to power positive-displacement liquid machines, focusing on motor parameters such as power rating, speed, torque, voltage and control characteristics that affect the operation of the pump or hydraulic device.
[F04B 2203/0201] Current -- F04B 2203/0201 covers specifications of the electrical current supplied to motors that drive positive-displacement liquid pumps or compressors. It includes rating, measurement, limits and performance characteristics of the current required for operation of such machines.
[F04B 2203/0202] Voltage -- F04B 2203/0202 pertains to the voltage characteristics of the motor driving positive-displacement machines for liquids, covering the specification, control, measurement and limits of electrical voltage supplied to such pumps or compressors.
[F04B 2203/0203] Magnetic flux -- F04B 2203/0203 addresses the magnetic flux characteristics of the electric motors used in positive-displacement machines for liquids, covering flux density, distribution and its influence on motor performance. It concerns the generation, measurement and control of magnetic fields within these pumps.
[F04B 2203/0204] Frequency of the electric current -- This CPC subclass F04B 2203/0204 covers the frequency of the electric current supplied to motors of positive-displacement machines for liquids. It is used to classify patents that describe or claim particular current frequencies as a motor parameter influencing pump operation within mechanical engineering.
[F04B 2203/0205] Temperature -- F04B 2203/0205 is a CPC subclass that concerns temperature aspects of positive-displacement machines for liquids. It addresses the measurement, control and effects of temperature on the motor, pump housing, fluid or associated components in such devices. The class is used for patent disclosures that treat heating, cooling, thermal protection or temperature-dependent performance of liquid-handling pumps.
[F04B 2203/0206] Vibration -- The subclass deals with vibration aspects of motors that drive positive-displacement machines for liquids, covering the measurement, analysis and control of oscillatory forces that affect performance and durability.
[F04B 2203/0207] Torque -- This CPC subclass covers torque specifications and characteristics of motors used in positive-displacement liquid machines such as pumps and compressors. It relates to the mechanical parameters that define the rotational force produced by the motor driving the displacement device.
[F04B 2203/0208] Power -- F04B 2203/0208 pertains to the specification of motor power for positive-displacement liquid machines, covering the rating, supply, and output characteristics of the driving motor that operates pumps, compressors or similar devices. It includes limits on horsepower or kilowatt values, voltage requirements, and related performance criteria.
[F04B 2203/0209] Rotational speed -- F04B 2203/0209 covers the specification of rotational speed for motors that drive positive-displacement machines for liquids. It relates to how fast the motor turns, which directly influences pump performance, flow rate and power requirements.
[F04B 2203/021] Lubricating-oil temperature -- The F04B 2203/021 subclass covers the temperature of lubricating oil in positive-displacement liquid machines, such as pumps and compressors, focusing on how oil temperature is monitored, regulated or used as a motor-parameter for performance and reliability.
[F04B 2203/0211] Noise -- F04B 2203/0211 covers the assessment and control of noise generated by motor parameters in positive-displacement liquid pumps and compressors. It relates to methods, devices and specifications aimed at reducing acoustic emissions and vibrations of the driving motor.
[F04B 2203/0212] Amplitude of the electric current -- The CPC subclass F04B 2203/0212 covers the amplitude of the electric current supplied to motors that drive positive-displacement machines for liquids. It specifies how current magnitude is defined, measured or limited as a motor parameter within the broader category of mechanical engineering and fluid-moving equipment.
[F04B 2203/0213] Pulses per unit of time (pulse motor) -- The CPC subclass F04B 2203/0213 classifies technical information that specifies the number of pulses or strokes a positive-displacement liquid pump produces per unit of time, i.e., the pulse frequency of the motor driving the pump. It is used for documents describing timing, rate or frequency characteristics of pulse motors in liquid displacement machines.
[F04B 2203/0214] Number of working motor-pump units -- The subclass records the quantity of motor-pump units employed in a liquid positive-displacement system, classifying inventions that specify single, dual, or multiple working motor-pump assemblies and related performance or control features. It is used to differentiate designs based on how many such units operate together.
[F04B 2203/04] of linear electric motors -- F04B 2203/04 refers to the definition of motor parameters for linear electric motors, which are positive-displacement machines used to move liquids. The subclass addresses characteristics such as force, speed, power, efficiency and control aspects of these linear electric drives within the broader mechanical-engineering hierarchy.

It includes current rating, consumption, and related performance parameters relevant to the operation of such pump-driven machines.

It covers specifications of supply voltage, voltage rating, regulation and any control aspects that affect motor performance in these hydraulic applications.

[F04B 2205/00] Fluid parameters -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid parameters relating to pumps for liquids and elastic fluids.
[F04B 2205/01] Pressure before the pump inlet

It falls under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids, and the fluid-parameters group.

[F04B 2205/05] Pressure after the pump outlet -- In the CPC, code F04B 2205/05 covers the pressure that exists downstream of a positive-displacement liquid pump. [F04B 2205/06] Pressure in a (hydraulic) circuit -- This CPC subclass concerns the pressure occurring within hydraulic circuits of positive-displacement machines for liquids. [F04B 2205/063] in a reservoir linked to the pump outlet -- This subclass covers designs where a liquid reservoir is directly linked to the outlet of a positive-displacement pump, used to regulate pressure, flow rate or to provide a buffer volume. [F04B 2205/065] between two stages in a multi-stage pump -- This subgroup addresses the section situated between successive stages of a multi-stage liquid pump, covering the intermediate cavity, inter-stage piping, and any bearings, seals or connections that link one pump stage to the next.

[F04B 2205/08] Pressure difference over a throttle -- F04B 2205/08 covers the measurement or specification of the pressure difference across a throttle within positive-displacement liquid machines. [F04B 2205/10] Inlet temperature -- The CPC subclass F04B 2205/10 deals with the inlet temperature of the fluid entering positive-displacement machines such as pumps or compressors. [F04B 2207/045] of the resonant frequency of the unit motor-pump -- This CPC subclass covers specifications related to the resonant frequency of a combined motor-pump unit, focusing on how the natural frequency of the assembly influences operation and design. [F04B 2207/046] of length of piston stroke -- F04B 2207/046 covers the external parameter of piston-stroke length in positive-displacement machines for liquids, such as pumps and compressors. [F04B 2207/047] of the nominal power of the driving motor -- This CPC subgroup classifies positive-displacement liquid machines according to the nominal power of their driving motor, treating motor power as an external parameter of the pump or compressor. [F04B 2207/048] of a reference voltage of the driving motor -- The code F04B 2207/048 covers positive-displacement liquid machines in which the reference voltage supplied to the driving motor is specified or controlled as an external parameter. [F04B 2207/70] Warnings -- This subclass covers warning or alarm provisions associated with positive-displacement machines for liquids such as pumps or compressors. [F04B 2207/701] Sound -- The CPC subclass F04B 2207/701 covers aspects of sound associated with positive-displacement machines for liquids, such as noise generation, acoustic measurement, vibration control and sound-reduction measures. [F04B 2207/702] Light -- F04B 2207/702 covers positive-displacement machines for liquids where light is an external operating parameter, such as illumination-based control, light-driven actuation or photonic sensing of pump performance. [F04B 2207/703] Stopping -- F04B 2207/703 relates to means for stopping or halting the motion of positive-displacement liquid machines, such as pumps or compressors, by using external control parameters like stop valves, brakes or control signals, thereby interrupting the displacement cycle to regulate flow or pressure.
[F04B 2207/704] Idling -- This subclass covers the idle or no-load condition of positive-displacement pumps and similar devices, focusing on external parameters such as speed, torque, and pressure when the machine is operating without delivering fluid. It includes methods for measuring, controlling, or optimizing idling behavior.
[F04B 23/00] Pumping installations or systems -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Pumps for liquids combined with reservoirs or with other fluid pumps.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Pumps characterised by combination with, or adaptation to, specific driving engines or motors -> F04B17/00
[F04B 23/02] having reservoirs -- A pumping installation or system that incorporates one or more reservoirs for fluid storage, allowing accumulation, smoothing of flow, or pressure stabilization in positive-displacement liquid machines. This sub-category defines designs where reservoirs are integral to the pump's operation.
[F04B 23/021] the pump being immersed in the reservoir -- F04B 23/021 covers positive-displacement pumping installations where the pump is placed directly inside the liquid reservoir, allowing the pump mechanism to operate while submerged. This arrangement is used in systems that require compact integration of the pump with the fluid source, often to simplify piping and improve reliability.
[F04B 23/023] only the pump-part being immersed, the driving-part being outside the reservoir -- F04B 23/023 classifies pumps in which the pumping element is placed inside the liquid while the power-transmission or driving unit remains outside the reservoir, such as external-drive submersible pumps. This arrangement allows the moving parts to operate in the fluid without exposing the motor to it.
[F04B 23/025] the pump being located directly adjacent the reservoir -- The subgroup F04B 23/025 covers pumping installations in which the pump is positioned directly next to the liquid reservoir, eliminating intervening piping and reducing suction head. This arrangement is used to simplify the system, improve priming reliability and enhance overall efficiency.
[F04B 23/026] a pump-side forming a wall of the reservoir -- F04B 23/026 covers pump-side mechanisms that create or shape the wall of a fluid reservoir in positive-displacement pumping installations, such as pistons or diaphragms that form the chamber walls during operation.
[F04B 23/028] the pump being mounted on top of the reservoir -- F04B 23/028 concerns positive-displacement pumping installations in which the pump is mounted on top of the reservoir, enabling gravity-assisted feed and easing priming of the system. This arrangement is used in mechanical engineering applications where the pump sits above the liquid source.
[F04B 23/04] Combinations of two or more pumps -- The subgroup F04B 23/04 covers installations that employ two or more pumps working together, either in series, parallel, or mixed configurations, to achieve higher pressure, increased flow, redundancy, or variable performance. It includes arrangements for staging, load-sharing, backup operation, and other combined-pump solutions within a single pumping system.
[F04B 23/06] the pumps being all of reciprocating positive-displacement type -- F04B 23/06 covers pumping installations or systems where every pump employed is a reciprocating positive-displacement type, such as piston, plunger or diaphragm pumps. The classification is used for designs in which the fluid is moved by a series of back-and-forth strokes rather than rotary action.
[F04B 23/08] the pumps being of different types -- This CPC subgroup covers pumping installations that incorporate two or more distinct types of positive-displacement pumps within the same system, such as mixed gear, diaphragm, or piston pumps operating together to achieve desired flow or pressure characteristics.
[F04B 23/10] at least one pump being of the reciprocating positive-displacement type -- This subclass covers pumping installations or systems that incorporate at least one reciprocating positive-displacement pump, such as piston or plunger pumps, which move fluid by repeatedly displacing a fixed volume per stroke.
[F04B 23/103] being a radial piston pump -- F04B 23/103 designates radial piston pumps, a type of positive-displacement pump where pistons are arranged radially around a drive shaft and move fluid from the inlet to the outlet by reciprocating action. These pumps are used in hydraulic and industrial systems for high-pressure fluid transport and are classified under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids, and pumping installations or systems.
[F04B 23/106] being an axial piston pump -- F04B 23/106 covers axial piston pumps, a type of positive-displacement liquid pump where a rotor with multiple pistons moves parallel to a drive shaft, delivering fluid through ports as pistons reciprocate. These pumps are used in hydraulic and automotive systems for high-pressure fluid flow.
[F04B 23/12] at least one pump being of the rotary-piston positive-displacement type -- A rotary-piston positive-displacement pump is a type of pump in which one or more pistons rotate within a cylindrical chamber, creating a sealing action that moves fluid in fixed volumes per rotation. It is used in pumping installations where precise, pulse-free flow of liquids is required.
[F04B 23/14] at least one pump being of the non-positive-displacement type -- This subclass covers pumping installations or systems that incorporate at least one pump that operates on a non-positive-displacement principle, i.e., flow is generated by kinetic energy rather than volume displacement per cycle. It is used to classify devices where the pump type is not a positive-displacement type.
[F04B 25/00] Pumps specially adapted for elastic fluids -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Pumps for elastic fluids, e.g. gas or steam, having more than one pumping stage, e.g. with two or more cylinders or with stepped pistons.
[F04B 25/005] with two cylinders -- F04B 25/005 refers to positive-displacement pumps specially adapted for elastic fluids that employ two cylinders. The two-cylinder arrangement provides reciprocal motion to draw in and expel the fluid, typical of twin-piston or twin-plunger pump designs. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering for positive-displacement liquid machines.
[F04B 25/02] of stepped piston type -- This subclass relates to positive-displacement pumps designed for elastic or highly viscous liquids that employ a stepped piston mechanism, where the piston has multiple diameter sections to provide successive increments of fluid displacement during each stroke. The design is used in hydraulic and pneumatic systems requiring controlled, repeatable flow of non-Newtonian fluids.
[F04B 25/04] having cylinders coaxial with, or parallel or inclined to, main shaft axis -- F04B 25/04 pertains to pumps designed for elastic fluids, such as hydraulic or pneumatic systems, in which the pumping cylinders are arranged coaxially with, parallel to, or inclined relative to the main shaft axis, allowing the driving shaft to directly actuate the cylinders.
[F04B 27/00] Multi-cylinder pumps specially adapted for elastic fluids and characterised by number or arrangement of cylinders -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Pumps for elastic fluids, e.g. gas or steam, defined by the number of cylinders, e.g. having two or more cylinders, or by the arrangement of the cylinders, e.g. radial piston pumps with cylinders in star or fan arrangement, or axial piston pumps with cylinders parallel or inclined with respect to a main shaft.  Details relating to control systems and methods for pumps for elastic fluids , e.g. gas or steam, defined by having cylinders coaxial with, or parallel or inclined to, main shaft axis.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Multi-stage pumps specially adapted for elastic fluids -> F04B25/00
[F04B 27/005] with two cylinders -- F04B 27/005 covers positive-displacement pumps designed for elastic fluids that use exactly two cylinders, defining the pump's operation and performance by the number and arrangement of the two working chambers. This subclass includes multi-cylinder designs where the two cylinders are the key distinguishing feature.
[F04B 27/02] having cylinders arranged oppositely relative to main shaft -- F04B 27/02 covers multi-cylinder positive-displacement pumps designed for elastic fluids in which the cylinders are positioned opposite one another around the main driving shaft. This arrangement distinguishes the pump from other configurations by having paired cylinders that rotate in opposite directions relative to the shaft, improving balance and flow characteristics. It falls under the broader class of mechanical engineering devices for liquid displacement.
[F04B 27/04] having cylinders in star- or fan-arrangement -- F04B 27/04 covers multi-cylinder positive-displacement pumps designed for elastic liquids that feature cylinders arranged in a star or fan configuration. The classification is applied when the number or layout of cylinders is a defining technical characteristic.
[F04B 27/0404] Details, component parts specially adapted for such pumps -- The code F04B 27/0404 covers the specific details and component parts that are specially adapted for multi-cylinder pumps handling elastic fluids, where the design is defined by the number of cylinders or their arrangement. It pertains to positive-displacement machines for liquids within the broader mechanical-engineering category.
[F04B 27/0409] Pistons -- This subclass covers multi-cylinder positive-displacement pumps designed for elastic liquids, distinguished by the number or arrangement of pistons within the cylinders. It falls under mechanical engineering for positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04B 27/0414] Cams -- The subclass F04B 27/0414 concerns cams used in multi-cylinder positive-displacement pumps that handle elastic fluids, where the cam design is defined by the number or arrangement of the cylinders. It classifies the mechanisms that control piston timing and motion within such specialised pump configurations.
[F04B 27/0418] consisting of several cylindrical elements, e.g. rollers -- F04B 27/0418 covers positive-displacement pumps for liquids that use several cylindrical components, such as rollers, arranged in a multi-cylinder configuration and designed for elastic fluids. The classification distinguishes these pumps by the number and layout of the cylindrical elements that convey the fluid.
[F04B 27/0423] Cylinders -- The subclass F04B 27/0423 covers multi-cylinder pumps that are specially designed for elastic fluids, where the defining feature is the number of cylinders or the way they are arranged within the pump. It includes pumps in which the cylinder configuration-such as parallel, series, or staggered layouts-affects performance and is used as a primary classification criterion.
[F04B 27/0428] Arrangements for pressing or connecting the pistons against the actuated cam -- The CPC subclass F04B 27/0428 addresses multi-cylinder positive-displacement pumps for elastic fluids that use particular arrangements to press or connect the pistons to an actuated cam, such as spring-loaded, lever-linked, or other mechanisms that ensure the pistons follow the cam's motion.
[F04B 27/0432] hydraulically -- F04B 27/0432 covers hydraulic multi-cylinder positive-displacement pumps specially designed for elastic fluids, such as oils, where the distinguishing feature is the number or spatial arrangement of the cylinders. It classifies pumps that use hydraulic power to move liquid by the coordinated action of several cylinders. This entry falls under mechanical engineering's positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04B 27/0437] Disconnecting the pistons from the actuated cam -- F04B 27/0437 relates to multi-cylinder positive-displacement pumps for elastic fluids in which the pistons are mechanically separated from the actuated cam, allowing independent piston movement and reducing wear.
[F04B 27/0442] Supporting and guiding means for the pistons -- A supporting and guiding means for the pistons is a component that keeps piston rods aligned and limits lateral movement within multi-cylinder pumps designed for elastic fluids, improving reliability and reducing wear. Such devices may include sleeves, bushings, guides or bearing arrangements that synchronize piston motion and maintain seal integrity throughout the pump's displacement cycle.
[F04B 27/0446] Draining of the engine housing; Arrangements dealing with leakage fluid -- The subclass F04B 27/0446 covers provisions for draining the engine housing of multi-cylinder pumps that handle elastic fluids, focusing on designs that manage or collect leakage fluid. It addresses mechanisms that remove unwanted fluid from the pump casing to maintain proper operation.
[F04B 27/0451] Particularities relating to the distribution members  F04B27/0472 ,  F04B27/0531  and  F04B27/0535  take precedence -- F04B 27/0451 covers multi-cylinder pumps that are specially adapted for elastic fluids, such as hydraulic oil, and where the design is defined by the number or arrangement of cylinders. The entry deals with particular features of the distribution members that allocate fluid to the cylinders, and it notes that the rules in the related groups F04B 27/0472, F04B 27/0531 and F04B 27/0535 take precedence.
[F04B 27/0456] to cylindrical distribution members -- The code F04B 27/0456 covers cylindrical distribution members used in multi-cylinder positive-displacement pumps that are specially adapted for elastic fluids. It pertains to the design and arrangement of the cylinders that distribute the fluid within the pump. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids, and multi-cylinder pumps.

[F04B 27/0465] to plate like distribution members -- This subclass covers multi-cylinder positive-displacement pumps for elastic liquids where the fluid is routed between cylinders by plate-type distribution members. [F04B 27/047] with an actuating element at the outer ends of the cylinders -- This subclass concerns multi-cylinder pumps for elastic fluids in which the driving or actuating element is positioned at the outer ends of the cylinders rather than inside them. It defines the arrangement of the cylinders and the external placement of the actuation mechanism for such positive-displacement pumps.
[F04B 27/0472] with cam-actuated distribution members -- Multi-cylinder pumps designed for elastic fluids that are distinguished by the number or layout of their cylinders and employ cam-actuated distribution members to control fluid flow between stages. This arrangement enables precise timing and sequencing of the pump's pistons, improving efficiency in applications such as hydraulic and lubrication systems.

[F04B 27/0476] directly located side-by-side -- F04B 27/0476 covers multi-cylinder positive-displacement pumps intended for elastic fluids, defined by the cylinders being positioned directly side-by-side in the pump assembly.

[F04B 27/22] by varying the relative positions of a swash plate and a cylinder block -- This subclass concerns multi-cylinder positive-displacement pumps for elastic fluids in which the pump's displacement is controlled by changing the relative position of the swash plate and the cylinder block. By altering the angle or axial offset between these components, the volume swept by each cylinder is varied, allowing adjustable flow rates.
[F04B 27/24] Control not provided for in a single group of groups -- This subclass covers multi-cylinder pumps specially adapted for elastic fluids where the control arrangement is not provided for within a single standard group of control groups, focusing on the number or layout of the cylinders. It addresses pumps whose control scheme is distributed across multiple groups or otherwise unconventional.
[F04B 29/00] Other pumps with movable, e.g. rotatable cylinders -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Pumps for elastic fluids, e.g. gas or steam, being characterised by rotatable cylinders.
[F04B 3/00] Machines or pumps with pistons coacting within one cylinder, e.g. multi-stage -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Pumps for liquids, wherein two or more pistons reciprocate within one cylinder, or wherein one piston forms the cylinder of a further piston.
[F04B 3/003] with two or more pistons reciprocating one within another, e.g. one piston forning cylinder of the other -- F04B 3/003 covers positive-displacement liquid pumps that use two or more pistons arranged so one piston reciprocates inside the other, the inner piston acting as the cylinder for the outer piston. Such configurations are typical of multi-stage piston pumps where one piston forms the working chamber of the second.
[F04B 3/006] with rotating cylinder block -- F04B 3/006 covers positive-displacement pumps in which the cylinder block rotates relative to the pistons, typically applied to multi-stage piston pumps that move fluid by the rotating cylinder assembly.
[F04B 31/00] Free-piston pumps specially adapted for elastic fluids -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Pumps for elastic fluids, e.g. gas or steam, being of free-piston type.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Muscle-driven pumps in which the stroke is not defined by gearing -> F04B33/00
[F04B 33/00] Pumps actuated by muscle power, e.g. for inflating -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Pumps for elastic fluids specially adapted for manual operation and for inflating tyres.
[F04B 33/005] specially adapted for inflating tyres of non-motorised vehicles, e.g. cycles, tricycles -- This subclass covers manually powered positive-displacement pumps that are specifically designed for inflating the tyres of non-motorised vehicles such as bicycles and tricycles.
[F04B 33/02] with intermediate gearing -- F04B 33/02 designates mechanically powered positive-displacement liquid pumps that are driven by human or animal muscle effort and incorporate an intermediate gearing mechanism to increase torque or speed. Such pumps are used for applications like manual inflators, water lifting, or other low-pressure fluid transfer where direct muscle power is mediated through a gear train.
[F04B 35/00] Piston pumps specially adapted for elastic fluids and characterised by the driving means to their working members, or by combination with, or adaptation to, specific driving engines or motors, not otherwise provided for -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Pumps for elastic fluids, e.g. gas or steam, driven by specific engines or other driving means, such as e.g. electric motors, floating elements, steam engines, or combustion engines; Mobile pumping systems, e.g. compressors on vehicles.
[F04B 35/002] driven by internal combustion engines -- F04B 35/002 designates piston pumps for elastic fluids that are directly powered by internal-combustion engines, detailing the mechanical coupling, drive arrangement and integration of the engine with the pump's working members. It falls under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids, and covers configurations not otherwise specified in related subclasses.
[F04B 35/004] driven by floating elements -- F04B 35/004 addresses piston pumps designed for elastic fluids that are driven by floating elements such as hydraulic or pneumatic actuators. The classification includes arrangements where the pump's driving means are integrated with or adapted to particular engines or motors not otherwise specified.
[F04B 35/006] driven by steam engines -- F04B 35/006 covers piston pumps that handle elastic fluids, such as oil or grease, where the pump's driving mechanism is a steam engine. The classification applies to designs where the pump is specifically adapted to operate with steam power rather than other drive types.
[F04B 35/008] the means being a fluid transmission link -- A CPC subclass for piston pumps handling elastic fluids where the driving power is transmitted via a fluid-based link, such as a hydraulic motor or fluid coupling, instead of a direct mechanical shaft. It falls under positive-displacement machines for liquids in the mechanical-engineering hierarchy.
[F04B 35/01] the means being mechanical -- F04B 35/01 covers piston pumps designed for elastic fluids where the drive is defined by a specific mechanical means, such as a particular engine or motor, or by a unique combination with those drives that is not covered elsewhere. It falls under positive-displacement liquid pumps in mechanical engineering.
[F04B 35/04] the means being electric -- F04B 35/04 covers piston pumps designed for elastic fluids whose distinguishing feature is an electric driving means, such as an electric motor or generator, used to power the working members. The subclass groups these electrically driven positive-displacement liquid pumps within the broader category of mechanical engineering and positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04B 35/045] using solenoids -- F04B 35/045 covers piston pumps that handle elastic fluids and are powered by solenoid devices. The classification addresses designs where solenoids directly drive the pump's working members or are integrated with specific motors, providing a distinct means of actuation not covered elsewhere.
[F04B 35/06] Mobile combinations -- F04B 35/06 covers piston pumps designed for elastic fluids that are integrated with their own driving mechanism or combined with a specific engine or motor, forming a self-contained, mobile unit. The classification applies to arrangements where the pump and the drive are packaged together for transport or field use, and where the drive configuration is not covered by other, more specific subclasses.
[F04B 37/00] Pumps having pertinent characteristics not provided for in, or of interest apart from, groups -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Various types of pumps for elastic fluids, e.g. gas or steam, not provided for in other groups, such as e.g. vacuum pumps evacuating by absorption means, vacuum pumps evacuating by thermal means, pumps for very high or very low pressure, or pumps for wet gases.
[F04B 37/02] for evacuating by absorption or adsorption -- F04B 37/02 covers pumps that create a vacuum or remove fluid by means of absorption or adsorption, such as those employing sorbent materials to capture gas or liquid. It is classified under positive-displacement machines for liquids and includes designs not specified in other groups.
[F04B 37/04] Selection of specific absorption or adsorption materials
[F04B 37/06] for evacuating by thermal means -- F04B 37/06 covers positive-displacement pumps that remove liquids by applying heat, such as steam-ejector or thermally driven pumps that evacuate a fluid through vaporisation or heating-induced expansion. It falls under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids, and specifically pumps with characteristics not covered in other groups.
[F04B 37/08] by condensing or freezing, e.g. cryogenic pumps -- This subclass includes positive-displacement pumps that move fluid by condensing or freezing it, for example cryogenic pumps used to transfer liquefied gases. It is applied when the pump's operation relies on phase change rather than mechanical displacement mechanisms covered in other groups.
[F04B 37/085] Regeneration of cryo-pumps -- The entry F04B 37/085 concerns techniques for restoring cryogenic (cryo-) vacuum pumps to service, including methods for removing adsorbed gases, thawing, and reheating the cold-trap elements to regain pumping performance. It applies to regeneration procedures distinct from those used for conventional liquid-displacement pumps.
[F04B 37/10] for special use -- F04B 37/10 is a CPC subclass for positive-displacement pumps that are designed for special or atypical applications not covered by the standard groups. It includes pumps with unique operating conditions, materials, or configurations intended for niche industrial, scientific, or environmental uses. This entry falls under mechanical engineering, specifically under positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04B 37/12] to obtain high pressure -- F04B 37/12 covers positive-displacement liquid pumps that are specifically designed to generate high discharge pressures, often by employing specialized configurations or operating principles not addressed in other CPC groups. The code is used for pump designs where achieving elevated pressure is the primary technical objective.
[F04B 37/14] to obtain high vacuum -- F04B 37/14 covers positive-displacement pumps specifically designed to achieve high vacuum, typically operating by repeatedly expanding a sealed volume to draw out gas and reduce pressure to well below atmospheric levels. These devices are used where extremely low pressures are required, such as in scientific instrumentation, electron microscopy, and certain manufacturing processes.
[F04B 37/16] Means for nullifying unswept space -- F04B 37/16 refers to devices incorporated in positive-displacement liquid pumps that eliminate or reduce the unswept volume within the pump chamber, such as fillers, expansion chambers or compensating mechanisms that prevent dead space during operation.
[F04B 37/18] for specific elastic fluids -- The subclass F04B 37/18 covers positive-displacement pumps that are specially adapted for handling elastic fluids, such as rubbery, polymeric or viscoelastic liquids. It includes pump designs, components or operating methods that address the particular flow, sealing or pressure characteristics of these non-Newtonian fluids.
[F04B 37/20] for wet gases, e.g. wet air -- F04B 37/20 covers positive-displacement pumps designed to handle wet gases, such as humid or moist air, where the gas contains a significant liquid fraction. These devices move the combined gas-liquid mixture without relying on centrifugal action, typically using reciprocating or rotary chambers that ensure a steady flow despite the presence of liquid droplets.
[F04B 39/00] Component parts, details, or accessories, of pumps or pumping systems specially adapted for elastic fluids, not otherwise provided for in, or of interest apart from, groups -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Various components adapted for pumps for elastic fluids, e.g. gas or steam, such as e.g. piston means, pulsation damping means, lubrication means, cooling means, filtering means, compressor valves, compressor casings.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Of machines or engines in general -> F01M
[F04B 39/0005] adaptations of pistons -- F04B 39/0005 covers specialized adaptations of pistons used in positive-displacement pumps that handle elastic fluids such as oils or polymers. It includes design features or modifications that improve sealing, flexibility, or performance of pistons within pump systems not covered by other subclasses.
[F04B 39/0011] liquid pistons -- Liquid pistons are the moving elements that directly contact and displace elastic liquids within positive-displacement pumps, forming part of the pump's sealing and pressure-generation system. They may be solid or flexible and are designed to fit the pump chamber to create a variable volume for fluid movement.
[F04B 39/0016] with valve arranged in the piston -- A sub-group of positive-displacement liquid pumps that deals with components, details or accessories specifically designed for elastic fluids, where the pump incorporates a valve built into the piston. This covers designs not covered elsewhere in the pump classification.
[F04B 39/0022] piston rods -- Piston rods are cylindrical components that connect a pump's moving piston to the driving mechanism, transmitting reciprocating force while containing high-pressure elastic fluids. They are typically made of hardened steel, feature seals or coatings to prevent leakage, and are sized to match the pump's stroke and pressure requirements.
[F04B 39/0027] Pulsation and noise damping means -- F04B 39/0027 pertains to components that dampen pulsation and noise in pumps or pumping systems designed for elastic fluids, such as hydraulic oil or water. It includes devices like dampers, silencers and cushioning elements that are fitted to or incorporated into the pump to smooth flow and reduce acoustic emissions.
[F04B 39/0033] with encapsulations -- F04B 39/0033 covers components, details or accessories for pumps or pumping systems that handle elastic fluids and are specially adapted with encapsulation. It includes items such as sealed housings, protective sleeves or other encapsulating elements designed to isolate the moving parts from the fluid. This subclass is used when the encapsulated feature is not addressed by any other more specific classification.
[F04B 39/0038] of inlet or outlet channels -- The subclass pertains to inlet or outlet channels of pumps or pumping systems designed for elastic fluids such as flexible or compressible liquids. It includes specially shaped passages, manifolds or conduit sections that direct fluid into or out of the pump, distinct from other pump components.
[F04B 39/0044] with vibration damping supports -- The code F04B 39/0044 covers components, details or accessories of positive-displacement pumps designed for elastic fluids that incorporate vibration-damping supports. These features are intended to reduce transmitted vibration and protect the pumping system, and are classified separately from other pump parts not specifically adapted for such damping.
[F04B 39/005] with direct action on the fluid flow using absorptive materials -- This subgroup comprises components, details or accessories for positive-displacement pumps handling elastic fluids where the fluid flow is directly influenced by absorptive materials such as porous inserts, wicks or filters. The absorptive elements act on the moving fluid to retain, release or modify it, and are not covered by other sections of the classification.
[F04B 39/0055] with a special shape of fluid passage, e.g. bends, throttles, diameter changes, pipes -- A sub-category of positive-displacement liquid pumps that covers components, details or accessories specially adapted for elastic fluids where the fluid passage is shaped in a particular way, such as bends, throttles, changes in diameter or other specially formed pipe sections.
[F04B 39/0061] using muffler volumes -- This subclass covers pump or pumping-system components for elastic fluids that employ muffler volumes, i.e., chambers or void spaces designed to absorb pressure pulsations and reduce noise. The feature is used to dampen hydraulic transients and improve the smooth operation of positive-displacement pumps.
[F04B 39/0066] using sidebranch resonators, e.g. Helmholtz resonators -- The subclass covers components, details or accessories of pumps or pumping systems for elastic fluids that incorporate side-branch resonators, such as Helmholtz resonators, to modify flow or vibration characteristics and improve performance.
[F04B 39/0072] characterised by assembly or mounting -- This subclass covers component parts, details or accessories for positive-displacement pumps handling elastic fluids that are specifically designed for the way they are assembled or mounted to the pump or system, where such features are not covered elsewhere in the classification. It includes fittings, brackets, housings or other elements whose primary function is to secure, align or integrate the pump components during installation.
[F04B 39/0077] by generating oil foam -- F04B 39/0077 covers pump components or accessories that handle elastic fluids by generating an oil-foam within the pumping system. The foam reduces friction and improves sealing, enabling more efficient displacement of viscous or compressible liquids. This subclass is used for inventions where foam generation is the primary means of adapting the pump for such fluids.
[F04B 39/0083] using blow off silencers -- The CPC subclass F04B 39/0083 covers the use of blow-off silencers on components, details or accessories of positive-displacement pumps designed for elastic fluids, where such silencers are not otherwise specified in the classification. It addresses noise-reduction devices that vent or discharge fluid while limiting sound emission, typically applied to pump systems handling liquids such as oil, water or polymer solutions.
[F04B 39/0088] using mechanical tuned resonators
[F04B 39/0094] crankshaft -- F04B 39/0094 covers crankshafts that form part of positive-displacement pump assemblies designed for elastic fluids. The classification applies to crankshaft components, details or accessories used in pumping systems where the fluid's compressibility is a key consideration.
[F04B 39/02] Lubrication -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Lubrication of pumps for liquids and elastic fluids.
[F04B 39/0207] with lubrication control systems -- This subclass pertains to components, details or accessories for positive-displacement pumps handling elastic fluids that incorporate systems for controlling lubrication, such as oil reservoirs, metered supply units or automatic lubricating devices. It covers any pump element specially adapted to manage lubricant flow and pressure within the pumping system.
[F04B 39/0215] characterised by the use of a special lubricant -- The CPC subgroup F04B 39/0215 covers component parts, details or accessories of pumps or pumping systems designed for elastic fluids that are specifically adapted by the use of a special lubricant, such as to improve sealing, reduce wear or enhance efficiency, and which are not otherwise classified in other groups.
[F04B 39/0223] characterised by the compressor type  swash-plate compressors  F04B27/109 -- This subclass (F04B 39/0223) pertains to components, details or accessories of pumps or pumping systems designed for elastic fluids that are not covered elsewhere, specifically where the pump is a positive-displacement machine employing a swash-plate compressor. It falls under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04B 39/023] Hermetic compressors -- F04B 39/023 covers hermetic compressors, a type of positive-displacement machine where the motor and pump are sealed together in a single, airtight housing for moving elastic fluids such as refrigerants. The entry includes component parts, details, and accessories specially adapted for these sealed compressors that are not classified elsewhere.
[F04B 39/0238] with oil distribution channels -- This subclass covers specialized components of positive-displacement pumps that handle elastic fluids, specifically parts designed to route oil through internal distribution channels such as oil passages, lubricating ducts, or similar conduits not covered elsewhere.
[F04B 39/0246] in the rotating shaft -- The classification F04B 39/0246 covers components, details or accessories of pumps or pumping systems designed for elastic fluids, focusing on the rotating shaft that transmits power to the pump. It applies to shafts specially adapted for handling liquids such as oils, polymers or other non-water fluids, where the shaft design is not covered by other more specific groups.
[F04B 39/0253] using centrifugal force for transporting the oil -- This CPC subclass covers components, details or accessories of pump systems specially adapted for elastic fluids that employ centrifugal force to move oil. It is part of the positive-displacement machines for liquids hierarchy within mechanical engineering.
[F04B 39/0261] with an auxiliary oil pump -- This subclass covers pumps or pump systems for elastic fluids that incorporate a secondary oil pump to provide lubrication, cooling or auxiliary power functions. It relates to component parts, details or accessories specially adapted for positive-displacement liquid machines and not otherwise classified. The auxiliary oil pump is described as an additional element integrated with the main pump.
[F04B 39/0269] with device for spraying lubricant or with mist lubrication -- A subcategory of positive-displacement liquid machines dealing with components, details or accessories of pumps for elastic fluids that incorporate a device for spraying lubricant or providing mist lubrication. It specifies such pump parts when the lubrication system is integral to the component rather than a separate element.
[F04B 39/0276] the pump being of the reciprocating piston type, e.g. oscillating, free-piston compressors -- F04B 39/0276 covers component parts, details or accessories of pumps or pumping systems designed for elastic fluids, specifically when the pump is of the reciprocating piston type such as oscillating or free-piston compressors.
[F04B 39/0284] Constructional details, e.g. reservoirs in the casing  swash-plate compressors  F04B27/0878 ,  F04B27/109 -- It concerns constructional details of positive-displacement pumps for elastic fluids, specifically internal reservoirs or similar cavities built into the housing of swash-plate compressors. These features are intended to manage fluid compressibility or provide auxiliary storage.
[F04B 39/0292] Lubrication of pistons or cylinders -- This subclass deals with methods, devices and accessories for lubricating pistons or cylinders in positive-displacement pumps that handle elastic fluids, covering oil supply, distribution and retention systems intended to reduce wear and improve pump performance.
