[F04B 39/04] Measures to avoid lubricant contaminating the pumped fluid -- F04B 39/04 concerns measures that prevent the lubricant used in pumps from contaminating the fluid being pumped. It covers design features, seals, barriers or drainage systems that isolate or remove lubricant in positive-displacement machines handling elastic fluids.
[F04B 39/041] sealing for a reciprocating rod  sealing in general  F16J -- The subclass F04B 39/041 relates to component parts, details or accessories of pumps or pumping systems that are specially adapted for elastic fluids, focusing on sealing arrangements for reciprocating rods. It covers general sealing concepts and devices used to prevent leakage in positive-displacement machines for liquids, particularly where the seal must accommodate rod motion.
[F04B 39/042] sealing being provided on the piston -- This subclass covers the provision of seals on pistons in positive-displacement pumps that handle elastic fluids such as oil or hydraulic fluid. It includes designs, materials and mounting methods for piston sealing elements that are not covered elsewhere in the pump classification.
[F04B 39/044] sealing with a rolling diaphragm between piston and cylinder -- F04B 39/044 covers sealing arrangements that use a rolling diaphragm positioned between a piston and the cylinder wall, primarily in positive-displacement pumps handling elastic fluids such as rubbers or gels. The rolling diaphragm provides a flexible seal that moves with the piston, reducing wear and preventing leakage while accommodating fluid compressibility.
[F04B 39/045] Labyrinth-sealing between piston and cylinder -- This CPC subgroup deals with labyrinth-type seals fitted between a piston and its cylinder in positive-displacement pumps for liquids, especially elastic fluids. It covers component parts, details or accessories specially adapted for such sealing arrangements that are not classified elsewhere.
[F04B 39/047] Sealing between piston and carter being provided by a bellow -- The CPC entry F04B 39/047 covers components for positive-displacement pumps handling elastic fluids where the seal between the piston and the pump housing (carter) is achieved with a flexible bellow. It relates to specialized sealing accessories not otherwise classified, allowing accommodation of fluid compressibility and maintaining pressure integrity.
[F04B 39/048] Sealing between piston and carter being provided by a diaphragm -- This classification covers pump components in which a diaphragm is used to seal the gap between the piston and the carter, a solution applied in positive-displacement machines that handle elastic fluids.
[F04B 39/06] Cooling -- The F04B 39/06 subclass addresses cooling arrangements incorporated in pump components or accessories that handle elastic fluids, such as hydraulic oil. It includes heat-exchangers, cooling jackets, and other devices designed to dissipate heat from pumps or pumping systems where such features are not covered elsewhere in the classification. This falls under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04B 39/062] Cooling by injecting a liquid in the gas to be compressed -- A method for cooling the gas in a positive-displacement pump for elastic fluids by injecting a liquid directly into the gas as it is compressed, thereby controlling temperature and improving efficiency. It includes components or accessories of pump systems that are specifically adapted for this liquid-injection cooling technique.
[F04B 39/064] Cooling by a cooling jacket in the pump casing -- F04B 39/064 covers pump or pumping-system components that provide cooling of the pump casing by means of a cooling jacket built around it. The classification applies to designs where the jacket circulates a coolant to remove heat generated during operation of pumps handling elastic fluids, and is not covered by other more specific subclasses.
[F04B 39/066] Cooling by ventilation -- F04B 39/066 relates to components, details or accessories of positive-displacement liquid pumps that are specially adapted for handling elastic fluids, with the particular function of providing cooling by ventilation of the pump or system.
[F04B 39/068] prevention of freezing -- This subclass covers devices and accessories used with pumps or pumping systems for elastic fluids that provide measures to stop the fluid from freezing, such as heaters, thermal insulation or anti-freeze additives, focusing on preventing ice formation within the pump or its associated components.
[F04B 39/08] Actuation of distribution members -- The F04B 39/08 subclass covers components that drive or control the distribution elements-such as valves, jets, nozzles or other flow-directing parts-of positive-displacement pumps designed for elastic fluids. It includes specialised actuation mechanisms and accessories that are not covered by other pump-specific subclasses, focusing on how the fluid-handling system is regulated.
[F04B 39/10] Adaptations or arrangements of distribution members -- F04B 39/10 covers the design and integration of distribution members such as manifolds, channels or ducts within positive-displacement pumping systems that handle elastic fluids, where the parts are specifically adapted or arranged in ways not covered by other subclasses. It includes special accessories and component details for such pumps.
[F04B 39/1006] the members being ball valves -- This subclass concerns positive-displacement machines for liquids, specifically pump or pumping-system components in which the functional members are ball valves, adapted for handling elastic fluids such as oils or polymers and not classified elsewhere.
[F04B 39/1013] the members being of the poppet valve type -- F04B 39/1013 designates component parts, details or accessories of pumps or pumping systems for elastic fluids that incorporate poppet-type valves. The classification is used for elements not otherwise classified and relates to positive-displacement liquid machines.
[F04B 39/102] the members being disc valves -- F04B 39/102 classifies disc valves that serve as components, details or accessories of positive-displacement pumps handling elastic fluids such as water or oil, where the valves are not covered by other more specific groups.
[F04B 39/1026] without spring  F04B39/1033  takes precedence -- F04B 39/1026 covers component parts, details or accessories of positive-displacement pumps for elastic fluids that are specially adapted without a spring, and it takes precedence over the broader subclass F04B 39/1033.
[F04B 39/1033] annular disc valves -- Annular disc valves are circular, disc-shaped flow control devices used as components or accessories in positive-displacement pumps handling elastic fluids. The disc is mounted in an annular seat and rotates or retracts to open or close the passage, allowing precise regulation of liquid flow and pressure within the pumping system.
[F04B 39/104] the members being parallel flexible strips -- F04B 39/104 covers component parts, details or accessories for pumps or pumping systems that handle elastic fluids, specifically those whose working members consist of parallel flexible strips. It falls under the mechanical-engineering hierarchy for positive-displacement machines for liquids, addressing designs not covered elsewhere.
[F04B 39/1046] Combination of in- and outlet valve -- The subclass deals with a single unit that incorporates both the inlet (suction) and outlet (discharge) valves for pumps handling elastic fluids such as oils, rubbers or polymers. By merging the two valve functions into one assembly, the design simplifies the pumping system, enhances sealing, and better accommodates fluids that expand under pressure.
[F04B 39/1053] the members being Hoerbigen valves -- The CPC entry F04B 39/1053 covers component parts, details or accessories of pumps or pumping systems designed for elastic fluids that are not otherwise classified, specifically including Hoerbigen valves. It falls under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids, and pertains to specialised pump components.
[F04B 39/106] the members being parallel non-flexible strips -- F04B 39/106 covers components of positive-displacement pumps for elastic fluids where the pumping members are parallel non-flexible strips. The strips act as rigid moving elements that displace fluid, and the class is used for such strip-type pump parts not otherwise classified.
[F04B 39/1066] Valve plates -- F04B 39/1066 covers valve plates that are component parts, details or accessories specially adapted for positive-displacement pumps handling elastic fluids such as oils or polymers. These plates regulate flow and pressure within pump or pumping system assemblies.
[F04B 39/1073] the members being reed valves -- F04B 39/1073 relates to pump or pumping-system components in which the moving members are reed valves, a type of flexible one-way valve used in positive-displacement machines handling elastic fluids. The subclass covers the design, arrangement and integration of these reed-valve members as parts of pumps that are not covered elsewhere in the classification.
[F04B 39/108] circular reed valves
[F04B 39/1086] flat annular reed valves -- Flat annular reed valves are flexible, ring-shaped components used in pumps or pumping systems that handle elastic fluids such as rubber-based lubricants or polymer solutions. They function as one-way flow elements, opening under pressure to allow fluid passage and closing when pressure reverses, thereby preventing backflow and protecting delicate pump parts. This design is catalogued under the CPC subgroup for specialized accessories of positive-displacement liquid machines.
[F04B 39/1093] the members being low-resistance valves allowing free streaming -- This subclass covers valves or valve members designed with minimal flow resistance so that fluid can pass freely, used as components or accessories in positive-displacement pumps handling elastic liquids. It applies to specialized low-pressure-drop valves not covered elsewhere.
[F04B 39/12] Casings -- F04B 39/12 covers casings for positive-displacement pumps that handle elastic fluids, including housings, jackets, liners and similar enclosures that contain moving parts and maintain pressure, but excludes components classified elsewhere.
[F04B 39/121] Casings -- F04B 39/121 pertains to casings and related components of positive-displacement pumps designed for elastic fluids, covering parts that are not classified elsewhere. It includes accessories specially adapted for handling rubber, polymer or similar deformable liquids.
[F04B 39/122] Cylinder block -- F04B 39/122 - Cylinder block refers to the main housing component of a positive-displacement pump designed for elastic fluids such as oil or water. It forms the structural body that contains the moving parts of the pump and is classified as a component part or accessory of pumping systems not covered elsewhere.
[F04B 39/123] Fluid connections -- F04B 39/123 covers fluid connections and associated components, details or accessories for pumps or pumping systems that handle elastic liquids such as water. The class addresses parts specially adapted for these fluids that are not covered elsewhere in the classification.
[F04B 39/125] Cylinder heads -- F04B 39/125 covers cylinder heads that are component parts of positive-displacement pumps or pumping systems designed for elastic fluids. The classification includes the design, construction, sealing and cooling features of the head that houses the pump cylinder and connects to the drive mechanism, when these elements are not covered elsewhere.
[F04B 39/126] Cylinder liners -- Cylinder liners are wear-resistant sleeves that line the cylinders of positive-displacement pumps handling elastic fluids, providing sealing, reducing friction and protecting the pump body from abrasion and corrosion.
[F04B 39/127] Mounting of a cylinder block in a casing -- The code F04B 39/127 covers the mounting of a cylinder block inside a pump casing, describing brackets, bolts, sleeves, or other attachment methods that secure the block, maintain alignment, and provide sealing for positive-displacement pumps handling elastic fluids. It focuses on how the cylinder assembly is fixed to ensure mechanical stability and proper operation.
[F04B 39/128] Crankcases -- F04B 39/128 covers crankcases, the housings that contain and support the moving parts of positive-displacement pumps designed for elastic fluids. It includes component parts, details or accessories specific to pump or pumping system crankcases that are not covered elsewhere. The classification falls under mechanical engineering for positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04B 39/14] Provisions for readily assembling or disassembling -- The CPC subclass F04B 39/14 covers design features that facilitate quick assembly or disassembly of pump components and accessories used with elastic fluids. It includes quick-connect couplings, removable housings, modular mounts and other mechanisms that allow rapid removal, replacement or servicing without special tools.
[F04B 39/16] Filtration -- F04B 39/16 pertains to filtration components that are part of, or accessories to, positive-displacement pumps or pumping systems specifically designed for elastic fluids. It includes filters, strainers, screens and similar devices used to remove contaminants from the fluid, when such parts are not covered by other CPC groups. This subclass addresses specialized filtration solutions integrated with pump assemblies.
[F04B 41/00] Pumping installations or systems specially adapted for elastic fluids -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Pumps for elastic fluids, e.g. gas or steam, combined with reservoirs or with other fluid pumps.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Free-piston pumps specially adapted for elastic fluids or systems incorporating such pumps -> F04B31/00 - Piston pumps specially adapted for elastic fluids and characterised by the driving means to their working members, or by combination with, or adaptation to, specific driving engines or motors, not otherwise provided for -> F04B35/00
[F04B 41/02] having reservoirs -- F04B 41/02 pertains to pumping installations or systems specially adapted for elastic fluids that incorporate reservoirs, i.e., devices that store the fluid either before it is displaced by the pump or after discharge, to aid in continuous or controlled operation.
[F04B 41/04] Conversion of internal-combustion engine cylinder units to pumps -- The entry concerns methods and apparatus for repurposing internal-combustion engine cylinder units as positive-displacement pumps for elastic fluids. It details the modification of pistons, combustion chambers, valve gear and related components so the former engine hardware can generate fluid flow in pumping installations. This adaptation falls within mechanical engineering of pump systems designed for liquids such as hydraulic oil or other compressible fluids.
[F04B 41/06] Combinations of two or more pumps -- F04B 41/06 classifies pumping installations or systems designed specifically for elastic fluids that incorporate two or more pumps combined in a single arrangement. The combination may be in series or parallel to achieve higher pressure, flow rate, or reliability.
[F04B 43/00] Machines or pumps having flexible working members -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Pumps for liquids and for elastic fluids having flexible working members, such as diaphragms, tubular flexible members or pumps with peristaltic action; Micropumps having flexible working members.
[F04B 43/0009] Special features -- This subclass covers positive-displacement pumps that employ flexible working members such as diaphragms or bellows and focuses on any special design elements, modifications or auxiliary devices that improve performance, sealing, control or safety, including variable-stroke mechanisms, integrated check valves or unique sealing arrangements not dealt with in broader groups.
[F04B 43/0018] the periphery of the flexible member being not fixed to the pump-casing, but acting as a valve -- Within the CPC class for positive-displacement liquid pumps with flexible members, code F04B 43/0018 pertains to designs where the outer edge of the flexible component is not rigidly attached to the pump casing but serves as a valve, using its movement to regulate fluid flow.
[F04B 43/0027] without valves -- This subclass concerns positive-displacement liquid machines that use flexible working members and function without any valves, relying on the deformation of those components to move fluid. It covers pumps that achieve fluid transfer solely through the flexible elements, eliminating valve mechanisms.
[F04B 43/0036] the flexible member being formed as an O-ring -- This subclass covers positive-displacement machines for liquids that employ a flexible working member shaped as an O-ring, which forms sealing chambers or valves during operation. It applies to pumps where the O-ring serves as the primary moving element that creates the required fluid displacement.
[F04B 43/0045] with a number of independent working chambers which are actuated successively by one mechanism -- This subclass covers positive-displacement liquid machines that use flexible working members and contain several independent chambers, each of which is driven in turn by a single actuation mechanism to move fluid. The design enables sequential chamber operation for continuous pumping.
[F04B 43/0054] particularities of the flexible members -- The code F04B 43/0054 relates to the specific features of flexible working members-such as diaphragms, bellows or flexible pistons-used in positive-displacement machines for liquids. It covers their design, material selection, geometry and operating behaviour within pumps that rely on elastic movement to transport fluid.
[F04B 43/0063] bell-shaped flexible members -- F04B 43/0063 covers pumps that employ bell-shaped flexible members as the working element to displace liquid. The bell shape expands and contracts during operation, creating a positive displacement that moves fluid through the machine.
[F04B 43/0072] of tubular flexible members -- F04B 43/0072 concerns positive-displacement pumps that employ tubular flexible members as the working elements. The classification covers machines in which fluid is moved by the deformation or motion of flexible tubes, such as peristaltic, diaphragm or similar pumps that use tubular components to create displacement.
[F04B 43/0081] systems, control, safety measures -- The code F04B 43/0081 covers the arrangement and implementation of control and safety features for positive-displacement liquid machines that use flexible moving parts, such as monitoring devices, pressure-relief valves, automatic shut-off, and other protective measures that ensure reliable operation.
[F04B 43/009] leakage control; pump systems with two flexible members; between the actuating element and the pumped fluid -- This subclass covers pump arrangements that use two flexible elements to control leakage, with the flexible members positioned between the actuating element and the pumped fluid. The design emphasizes sealing and leakage mitigation in positive-displacement liquid machines.
[F04B 43/02] having plate-like flexible members, e.g. diaphragms -- The CPC subclass F04B 43/02 defines pumps or other positive-displacement machines for liquids that use plate-like flexible members, such as diaphragms, to transfer fluid. It is situated within mechanical engineering under positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04B 43/021] the plate-like flexible member is pressed against a wall by a number of elements, each having an alternating movement in a direction perpendicular to the plane of the plate-like flexible member and each having its own driving mechanism -- This sub-group of positive-displacement liquid machines covers devices in which a plate-like flexible element is pressed against a wall by several actuators that move alternately in a direction perpendicular to the plate, each actuator having its own drive mechanism.
[F04B 43/023] double acting plate-like flexible member -- The code F04B 43/023 designates a positive-displacement pump that uses a plate-like flexible member which bends in two opposite directions during operation, acting alternately as a suction and a discharge element to move liquid. The member is typically made of elastomeric or metal material and provides reciprocating motion within the pump chamber.
[F04B 43/025] two or more plate-like pumping members in parallel -- F04B 43/025 covers positive-displacement pumps that use two or more plate-like members arranged in parallel to move liquid. The flexible plates act as working members that create chambers which expand and contract as the plates flex, producing a continuous flow. This design falls within mechanical engineering under machines or pumps having flexible working members.
[F04B 43/026] each plate-like pumping flexible member working in its own pumping chamber -- This CPC subclass relates to positive-displacement liquid machines where each plate-like flexible member functions within its own pumping chamber, providing a separate volume of fluid that is moved by the flexing of the member during each cycle.
[F04B 43/028] with in- or outlet valve arranged in the plate-like flexible member  valve arranged in the piston  F04B53/12 -- F04B 43/028 covers positive-displacement liquid pumps that incorporate an inlet or outlet valve formed directly in a plate-like flexible component, such as a diaphragm or the pump piston itself, allowing flow control integrated with the moving membrane. This arrangement eliminates separate valve hardware, simplifying the pump design and improving reliability.
[F04B 43/04] Pumps having electric drive -- The subclass F04B 43/04 covers positive-displacement liquid pumps that use flexible working elements and are powered by an electric motor. It falls under mechanical engineering, specifically the group for positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04B 43/043] Micropumps -- Micropumps are small positive-displacement devices that move liquids by repeatedly flexing or deforming a thin, elastic component such as a diaphragm, membrane or chamber wall. Their flexible working members create a sealed volume change that draws fluid in and forces it out with precise, low-flow rates.
[F04B 43/046] with piezoelectric drive -- F04B 43/046 covers positive-displacement machines for liquids that use a piezoelectric drive to actuate flexible working members such as membranes or diaphragms. These devices convert electrical energy into mechanical motion via piezoelectric elements to pump or move fluids.
[F04B 43/06] Pumps having fluid drive -- This subclass includes positive-displacement pumps whose moving elements are powered by a fluid-driven mechanism rather than direct mechanical or electrical input. The fluid drive transmits pressure energy to flexible working members such as pistons, diaphragms or membranes, causing the pump to displace liquid in a controlled, incremental fashion.
[F04B 43/067] the fluid being actuated directly by a piston -- F04B 43/067 covers piston-driven positive-displacement pumps in which the fluid is acted on directly by the piston's stroke, without intermediate valves or diaphragms. The classification applies to machines where the piston itself creates the pressure change that moves the liquid, typical of reciprocating pump designs used in hydraulic and petroleum systems.
[F04B 43/073] the actuating fluid being controlled by at least one valve -- F04B 43/073 covers positive-displacement machines for liquids that use flexible working members, such as diaphragms or membranes, where the fluid that drives the motion is regulated by at least one valve. This sub-class defines the valve-controlled actuation of the pump's operating fluid.
[F04B 43/0733] with fluid-actuated pump inlet or outlet valves; with two or more pumping chambers in series -- This subclass covers positive-displacement machines for liquids that use flexible components and incorporate inlet or outlet valves operated by the fluid itself. It specifically applies to designs that contain two or more pumping chambers arranged in series.
[F04B 43/0736] with two or more pumping chambers in parallel -- This subgroup covers positive-displacement liquid pumps that use flexible components and contain two or more chambers that operate simultaneously in parallel to deliver flow. It is part of the F04B 43/07 series.
[F04B 43/08] having tubular flexible members -- This subclass covers positive-displacement pumps that employ flexible tubular members to move liquids. The tube is repeatedly deformed by rollers or cams, as in peristaltic or hose pumps, allowing fluid to be transferred while keeping the pumped medium isolated from the pump's moving parts.
[F04B 43/082] the tubular flexible member being pressed against a wall by a number of elements, each having an alternating movement in a direction perpendicular to the axes of the tubular member and each having its own driving mechanism -- F04B 43/082 pertains to positive-displacement liquid pumps that use a flexible tubular member which is forced against a surrounding wall by several actuators that move alternately perpendicular to the tube axis, each actuator being driven independently.
[F04B 43/084] the tubular member being deformed by stretching or distortion -- This subgroup covers pumps where the moving part is a tube that changes shape by being stretched or otherwise distorted to move liquid. It includes designs such as peristaltic or haematocrit pumps where the tube's elongation creates a volume change that displaces fluid.
[F04B 43/086] with two or more tubular flexible members in parallel  F04B43/1136  takes precedence -- F04B 43/086 covers positive-displacement pumps that use two or more flexible tubular members arranged in parallel to move liquid. It applies to machines where the flexible tubes act as the working members, providing simultaneous flow paths, and is superseded by the more specific subclass F04B 43/1136.
[F04B 43/088] with two or more tubular flexible members in series  F04B43/1133  takes precedence -- F04B 43/088 relates to positive-displacement liquid pumps that employ two or more tubular flexible members arranged in series as the working elements. The configuration uses successive flexible hoses or bellows to convey the fluid throughout the pump cycle.
[F04B 43/09] Pumps having electric drive -- The CPC subclass F04B 43/09 designates pumps that employ flexible working members and are powered by an electric motor. It is situated within mechanical engineering, under positive-displacement machines for liquids, specifically machines or pumps having flexible working members.
[F04B 43/095] Piezoelectric drive -- F04B 43/095 refers to pumps or other positive-displacement machines for liquids that are driven by a piezoelectric actuator. The piezoelectric element expands or contracts under an electric field, providing a flexible working member that creates fluid displacement with high precision.
[F04B 43/10] Pumps having fluid drive -- This subclass covers pumps that are powered by a fluid - typically hydraulic or pneumatic - where the driving force is transmitted through flexible components such as diaphragms, bellows, or elastic membranes to displace the pumped liquid.
[F04B 43/107] the fluid being actuated directly by a piston -- F04B 43/107 covers pumps where the liquid is moved directly by the motion of a piston, without intermediate valves or diaphragms. The piston's reciprocating action creates a positive displacement of the fluid, typical of piston pumps used in hydraulic or water systems.
[F04B 43/113] the actuating fluid being controlled by at least one valve -- This subclass covers positive-displacement liquid pumps or other machines with flexible working members in which the driving (actuating) fluid is regulated by at least one valve. The valve may control pressure, flow rate or direction of the actuating medium to achieve the desired pumping action.
[F04B 43/1133] with fluid-actuated pump inlet or outlet valves; with two or more pumping chambers in series -- The subclass F04B 43/1133 covers positive-displacement pumps that use flexible working members and incorporate fluid-actuated inlet or outlet valves, with at least two pumping chambers connected in series to increase pressure or flow.
[F04B 43/1136] with two or more pumping chambers in parallel -- This subclass defines positive-displacement machines for liquids that employ flexible working members and contain two or more pumping chambers operating in parallel. It is situated under mechanical engineering, within the group of positive-displacement liquid machines, and is identified by the code F04B 43/1136.
[F04B 43/12] having peristaltic action -- This subclass covers pumps that move fluid by compressing a flexible tube or hose in a peristaltic manner, where rotating rollers or shoes create a wave that pushes the liquid forward without the fluid contacting moving parts. Such devices are used when contamination must be avoided or the fluid is abrasive.
[F04B 43/1207] the actuating element being a swash plate -- This CPC entry F04B 43/1207 pertains to positive-displacement pumps that use a swash plate as the actuating element. It lies within mechanical engineering, under positive-displacement machines for liquids, specifically machines or pumps having flexible working members.
[F04B 43/1215] having no backing plate (deforming of the tube only by rollers) -- This subclass covers positive-displacement liquid pumps that deform a flexible tube solely with rollers, omitting any backing plate. The tube is compressed between rotating rollers to create flow, a design used in certain peristaltic-type pumps.
[F04B 43/1223] the actuating elements, e.g. rollers, moving in a straight line during squeezing -- F04B 43/1223 covers the actuating components of flexible-member displacement pumps, such as rollers that travel linearly while the pump chamber is squeezed to move fluid. These elements provide the motion that drives the squeezing action in positive-displacement liquid machines.
[F04B 43/123] using an excenter as the squeezing element -- F04B 43/123 relates to positive-displacement liquid machines that employ flexible working members, where the squeezing element is an excenter - an eccentric cam that deforms the flexible chamber or tube to displace fluid. This subclass covers pumps and similar devices that use the excentric motion to create the pumping action.
[F04B 43/1238] using only one roller as the squeezing element, the roller moving on an arc of a circle during squeezing -- A positive-displacement liquid pump that uses a single roller as the squeezing element, the roller traveling along a circular-arc path to compress a flexible working member and move fluid. This arrangement is classified under mechanical engineering for machines or pumps having flexible working members.
[F04B 43/1246] the roller being placed at the outside of the tubular flexible member -- The subclass concerns positive-displacement liquid pumps that use a flexible tubular member, specifying designs where a roller is positioned on the outside of the tube to drive or compress it, as found in peristaltic-type or roller-pump mechanisms.
[F04B 43/1253] by using two or more rollers as squeezing elements, the rollers moving on an arc of a circle during squeezing -- F04B 43/1253 covers positive-displacement liquid pumps that employ flexible working members where two or more rollers act as squeezing elements, and the rollers travel along an arc of a circle while compressing the fluid. This design falls under mechanical engineering sub-categories for flexible-member displacement machines.
[F04B 43/1261] the rollers being placed at the outside of the tubular flexible member -- The subclass covers liquid-pumping devices in which rollers are positioned on the outside of a tubular flexible member, compressing the tube to create peristaltic motion that displaces the fluid. It falls under positive-displacement machines that use flexible working members.
[F04B 43/1269] the rotary axes of the rollers lying in a plane perpendicular to the rotary axis of the driving motor -- F04B 43/1269 relates to positive-displacement liquid pumps that use flexible rollers whose rotation axes are arranged in a plane that is perpendicular to the motor's drive shaft. This geometry is typical of peristaltic or roller-type pumps where the rollers encircle a flexible tube and advance the fluid as they turn.
[F04B 43/1276] Means for pushing the rollers against the tubular flexible member -- A device that forces rollers onto a flexible tubular element in a positive-displacement pump, adjusting roller pressure to maintain sealing and drive the tube. The means for pushing the rollers ensures consistent contact, improving pumping efficiency and preventing slippage of the flexible member.
[F04B 43/1284] Means for pushing the backing-plate against the tubular flexible member -- This subclass covers devices that press a backing-plate against a tubular flexible member in positive-displacement liquid pumps, providing the force needed to deform the element and move fluid. It relates to mechanisms that ensure sealing and fluid displacement by applying pressure to the flexible pumping tube.
[F04B 43/1292] Pumps specially adapted for several tubular flexible members -- This CPC subclass concerns positive-displacement pumps that are designed to work with multiple flexible tubing elements. Such pumps use flexible working members, typically corrugated or elastic tubes, to move liquid, allowing the machine to adapt to several tubes simultaneously.
[F04B 43/14] having plate-like flexible members -- F04B 43/14 designates positive-displacement liquid machines that employ plate-like flexible members, such as flexible plates or membranes, as the working elements to move fluid. These devices fall under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement pumps with flexible components.
[F04B 45/00] Pumps or pumping installations having flexible working members and specially adapted for elastic fluids -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Pumps having flexible working members and which are specifically adapted for elastic fluids. The flexible working members may be bellows, diaphragms or tubular flexible members.
[F04B 45/02] having bellows -- F04B 45/02 covers pumps or pumping installations that use bellows as flexible working members, designed specifically for handling elastic fluids such as oils or polymers. The bellows provide displacement by expanding and contracting, allowing the pump to move fluid without rigid moving parts. This subclass falls under positive-displacement machines for liquids in mechanical engineering.
[F04B 45/022] with two or more bellows in parallel -- A pump or pumping installation that uses flexible working members, such as bellows, designed for handling elastic fluids and incorporates two or more bellows arranged in parallel to provide combined displacement or improved flow characteristics. This configuration is used to increase capacity, reduce pressure pulsation, or enhance reliability in positive-displacement liquid handling systems.
[F04B 45/024] with two or more bellows in series -- This CPC subgroup covers positive-displacement pumps that use flexible working members for elastic fluids, specifically designs that employ two or more bellows arranged in series to achieve the pumping action.
[F04B 45/027] having electric drive -- F04B 45/027 designates pumps or pumping installations with flexible working members specially adapted for elastic fluids that are powered by an electric drive. It falls under the mechanical-engineering group for positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04B 45/033] having fluid drive -- This subclass covers pumps or pumping installations that employ flexible working members such as diaphragms or bellows, specially adapted for elastic fluids, and are driven by a fluid power source such as hydraulic or pneumatic pressure.
[F04B 45/0333] the fluid being actuated directly by a piston -- F04B 45/0333 covers pumping installations that use flexible components, such as bellows or diaphragms, designed for elastic liquids, where the fluid is moved directly by the linear action of a piston. This subclass falls under positive-displacement pumps for liquids within mechanical engineering. It specifies the direct piston actuation of the fluid in flexible-member pumps.
[F04B 45/0336] the actuating fluid being controlled by one or more valves -- F04B 45/0336 pertains to pumps or pumping installations that use flexible working members for elastic fluids, in which the actuating fluid is regulated by one or more valves.
[F04B 45/04] having plate-like flexible members, e.g. diaphragms -- This CPC subclass F04B 45/04 covers pumps or pumping installations that use plate-like flexible members, such as diaphragms, to handle elastic fluids.
[F04B 45/041] double acting plate-like flexible pumping member -- The class covers pumps that use a double-acting, plate-like flexible element to move fluid. The member flexes in both directions to create a positive displacement of elastic liquids, suitable for applications requiring smooth, low-shear pumping.
[F04B 45/043] two or more plate-like pumping flexible members in parallel -- F04B 45/043 covers pumps or pumping installations that use two or more plate-like flexible members arranged in parallel to move elastic fluids. The flexible members are designed to deform under pressure, providing a positive-displacement action suitable for handling liquids such as oils, greases or polymers. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering for positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04B 45/045] with in- or outlet valve arranged in the plate-like pumping flexible members -- This subclass covers pumps or pumping installations for elastic fluids that use flexible, plate-like pumping members such as diaphragms, where the inlet or outlet valve is incorporated directly into those flexible plates, allowing the valve to open and close as the plate deforms.
[F04B 45/047] Pumps having electric drive -- F04B 45/047 covers pumps that are driven by an electric motor, belonging to positive-displacement machines for liquids with flexible working elements specially adapted for elastic fluids.
[F04B 45/053] Pumps having fluid drive -- F04B 45/053 pertains to positive-displacement pumps that are powered directly by a fluid, using flexible working members such as diaphragms or membranes to transfer energy. These designs are adapted for elastic or compressible liquids and are commonly employed where a fluid-driven actuation is preferred over mechanical shafts.
[F04B 45/0533] the fluid being actuated directly by a piston -- F04B 45/0533 covers pumps or pumping installations for elastic fluids in which the fluid is moved directly by the stroke of a piston, without intermediate hydraulic transmission. The classification applies to positive-displacement machines that use flexible working members such as diaphragms or bellows and are designed especially for liquids like oil or rubber suspensions.
[F04B 45/0536] the actuating fluid being controlled by one or more valves -- The subclass F04B 45/0536 covers pumps or pumping installations that use flexible working members for elastic fluids in which the actuation medium is regulated by one or more valves, allowing controlled delivery of the fluid to the pump. This arrangement is typical of hydraulic or pneumatic positive-displacement machines where valve-controlled flow governs pump performance.
[F04B 45/06] having tubular flexible members -- CPC F04B 45/06 classifies pumps or pumping installations that employ tubular flexible members, such as hoses or bellows, to move elastic fluids. The subclass covers designs where the flexible tube itself acts as the displacement element, enabling efficient handling of liquids with appreciable elasticity.
[F04B 45/061] with fluid drive -- The subclass F04B 45/061 covers positive-displacement pumps that are powered by a fluid drive instead of a direct mechanical linkage, employing flexible members such as diaphragms, bellows or sleeves to move elastic liquids. These pumps are designed for applications where the pumping action is integrated into a hydraulic circuit and the fluid being handled is compressible or otherwise elastic.
[F04B 45/062] the fluid being actuated directly by a piston -- The classification F04B 45/062 covers positive-displacement pumps that move elastic fluids by having the fluid act directly on a piston, without intermediate hydraulic transmission, often with flexible seals or diaphragms to accommodate the fluid's compressibility.
[F04B 45/064] the actuating fluid being controlled by one or more valves -- This subgroup pertains to positive-displacement pumps that employ flexible working members such as diaphragms or bellows, in which the fluid that drives the pump is regulated by one or more control valves. The valving arrangement allows the actuating fluid to be started, stopped or throttled, providing precise control of pump output especially for elastic liquids.
[F04B 45/065] with electric drive -- F04B 45/065 covers positive-displacement pumps or pumping installations that use flexible working members to handle elastic fluids and are driven by an electric motor. It belongs to the mechanical-engineering class for positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04B 45/067] Pumps having electric drive -- F04B 45/067 covers pumps that use an electric motor as their source of power. These are positive-displacement machines with flexible working members, designed for handling elastic fluids, and fall within the broader category of mechanical engineering for liquid displacement.
[F04B 45/073] Pumps having fluid drive -- F04B 45/073 covers pumps that are powered by a fluid source, such as hydraulic or pneumatic pressure, rather than by a direct mechanical drive. These fluid-driven pumps use the energy of the driving fluid to move the working fluid, and are suited for handling elastic or otherwise flexible liquids in positive-displacement pumping installations.
[F04B 45/0733] the fluid being actuated directly by a piston -- The CPC class F04B 45/0733 covers pumps or pumping installations that employ flexible working members for elastic fluids, where the fluid is moved directly by the action of a piston. This subclass falls under positive-displacement machines for liquids within mechanical engineering.
[F04B 45/0736] the actuating fluid being controlled by one or more valves -- F04B 45/0736 classifies pumps or pumping installations that employ flexible working members for elastic fluids and where the actuating fluid is regulated by one or more valves. It is a sub-category of positive-displacement machines for liquids within mechanical engineering.
[F04B 45/08] having peristaltic action -- This subclass covers pumps that move fluid by compressing a flexible tube or hose in a peristaltic manner, where the tube itself forms the moving element and fluid is displaced by a travelling wave of squeezing. Such devices are suited for handling elastic or shear-sensitive liquids.
[F04B 45/085] the actuating element being a swash plate -- The subclass F04B 45/085 covers pumps or pumping installations for elastic fluids in which the driving element is a swash plate, a slanted disc that converts rotary motion into the reciprocating motion of pistons or rollers. It applies to positive-displacement machines with flexible working members such as axial-piston or vane pumps that use this swash-plate actuation.
[F04B 45/10] having plate-like flexible members -- A pump or pumping installation that moves elastic fluids by means of plate-shaped flexible members such as diaphragms or membranes, where the plates deform to create displacement and flow. This includes plate-type positive-displacement pumps designed for liquids with high viscosity or compressibility.
[F04B 47/00] Pumps or pumping installations specially adapted for raising fluids from great depths, e.g. well pumps -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Pumps specifically adapted for raising fluids from great depths, such as wellbore pumps.
[F04B 47/005] Sand trap arrangements -- F04B 47/005 covers sand-trap arrangements incorporated in positive-displacement pumps or pumping installations designed for extracting fluids from great depths, such as well pumps. The classification addresses devices and methods for collecting and removing sand or solid particles that could be drawn into the pump, thereby protecting moving parts and maintaining performance. This falls under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04B 47/02] the driving mechanisms being situated at ground level -- F04B 47/02 covers pumping installations for deep-well fluids where the power source, such as a motor or engine, is positioned at ground level rather than downhole. This classification applies to systems that transmit drive energy to the pump via rods, shafts or other surface-mounted mechanisms.
[F04B 47/022] driving of the walking beam -- This CPC subclass concerns the means of powering the walking-beam mechanism used in deep-well positive-displacement pumps. It falls under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids, and covers driving arrangements that transmit motion to the pump piston.
[F04B 47/024] actuated by muscle power -- The subclass F04B 47/024 covers positive-displacement pumps or pumping installations that are specially adapted for lifting fluids from deep wells and are powered manually by human muscle, such as hand-operated or foot-operated well pumps. It pertains to mechanical devices that use direct human effort rather than external power sources to raise liquids from great depths.
[F04B 47/026] Pull rods, full rod component parts -- F04B 47/026 classifies the pull rods and complete rod assemblies employed in deep-well pumping installations, such as sucker-rod pumps, that transmit the reciprocating motion of the surface drive to the downhole pump. The entry covers the design, construction and material of these rods used to raise fluids from great depths.
[F04B 47/028] details of the walking beam -- F04B 47/028 covers the design and construction details of the walking-beam mechanism used in positive-displacement pumps for lifting fluids from great depths, such as well pumps. It includes features like beam geometry, pivot arrangements, linkage connections, guiding devices and motion-transmission elements that affect the operation and efficiency of the pump.
[F04B 47/04] the driving means incorporating fluid means -- This subclass covers pump drive systems that use a fluid, such as hydraulic or pneumatic power, to transmit motion to the pump shaft or other moving parts. It includes arrangements where the driving device itself contains a fluid passage or cylinder that generates the required force, typically employed in deep-well pumping installations.
[F04B 47/06] having motor-pump units situated at great depth -- F04B 47/06 covers pump installations in which the motor and pump are placed at the bottom of a deep well or borehole, allowing the pump to be driven directly at the fluid source. This arrangement reduces transmission losses, handles high pressures, and is used for extracting fluids from great depths.
[F04B 47/08] the motors being actuated by fluid -- F04B 47/08 relates to positive-displacement pumps, especially deep-well or other high-lift installations, in which the driving motor is powered directly by the fluid being handled. It covers designs where fluid flow energizes a hydraulic or fluid-driven motor that in turn powers the pumping mechanism, integrating actuation and fluid movement in a single system.
[F04B 47/10] the units or parts thereof being liftable to ground level by fluid pressure -- F04B 47/10 covers components or whole units of deep-well positive-displacement pump systems that are designed to be raised to the surface by using fluid pressure, enabling the pump or its parts to be lifted from great depths. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering for pumps specifically adapted for well applications.
[F04B 47/12] having free plunger lifting the fluid to the surface -- F04B 47/12 covers positive-displacement pumps that use a free-moving plunger to lift fluid from deep sources such as wells, enabling the fluid to be raised to the surface. This subclass is part of the mechanical-engineering group for pumps specially adapted for great depths.
[F04B 47/14] Counterbalancing -- The subclass F04B 47/14 deals with counterbalancing devices incorporated in deep-well pumps or pumping installations that raise fluids from great depths. It includes mechanisms such as springs, gas-loaded pistons or hydraulic accumulators that offset the fluid-column weight to reduce the load on the drive and improve efficiency.
[F04B 47/145] with fluid means -- F04B 47/145 covers positive-displacement pumps or pumping installations specifically adapted to raise fluids from great depths, such as well pumps, where the lifting action is achieved by fluid means rather than direct mechanical linkage. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04B 49/00] Control of, or safety measures for, machines, pumps, or pumping installations, not otherwise provided for, or of interest apart from, groups -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Control systems and methods for pumps for liquids and for elastic fluids.
[F04B 49/002] Hydraulic systems to change the pump delivery -- F04B 49/002 covers hydraulic arrangements that modify the delivery rate of a positive-displacement pump, providing control or safety functions for pump or pumping installations that are not treated elsewhere. It falls under mechanical engineering, specifically positive-displacement machines for liquids, and addresses systems that adjust pump output to meet operational or safety requirements.
[F04B 49/005] changing the phase relationship of two working pistons in one working chamber or the phase-relationship of a piston and a driven distribution member -- F04B 49/005 covers devices that alter the timing between two pistons operating in the same chamber, or between a piston and a driven distribution element, to adjust their phase relationship during operation. This includes mechanisms that shift the relative motion to improve efficiency, control flow, or adapt to varying load conditions.
[F04B 49/007] Installations or systems with two or more pumps or pump cylinders, wherein the flow-path through the stages can be changed, e.g. from series to parallel  centrifugal pumps  F04D15/0072 -- The entry covers installations or systems that use two or more pumps or pump cylinders and allow the hydraulic flow path to be reconfigured, such as switching between series and parallel arrangements, particularly for centrifugal pumps. It relates to control or safety features not otherwise specified.
[F04B 49/02] Stopping, starting, unloading or idling control -- F04B 49/02 deals with control measures for starting, stopping, unloading or idling of positive-displacement machines for liquids such as pumps, focusing on safety or operational features not covered elsewhere. It specifies how these machines are managed to ensure proper and secure operation.
[F04B 49/022] by means of pressure -- F04B 49/022 pertains to control or safety devices for positive-displacement liquid machines that operate by means of pressure, such as pressure-actuated valves, regulators or safety shut-offs, when these measures are not covered by other specific subclasses.
[F04B 49/025] by means of floats -- F04B 49/025 covers safety or control measures for positive-displacement liquid machines that operate by means of floats, such as float-controlled pumps or pumping installations, where the float acts as a level-sensing element to start, stop or regulate the equipment.
[F04B 49/03] by means of valves -- This subclass deals with control or safety features for positive-displacement liquid machines that are implemented by means of valves. It covers valve-based regulation of flow, pressure, direction or start-stop functions in pumps and pumping installations where no other specific provision exists.
[F04B 49/035] Bypassing -- F04B 49/035 deals with bypass arrangements used to control or protect positive-displacement liquid pumps and pumping installations when normal safety functions are unavailable or insufficient. The subclass covers devices that divert flow around the pump, provide alternate pathways, or isolate the machine to prevent damage or hazardous conditions.
[F04B 49/04] Regulating by means of floats -- This subclass covers devices that regulate the operation of positive-displacement pumps or other liquid-handling machines by using floats that respond to fluid level or pressure changes. It includes float-controlled valves, level-sensing switches, and safety shut-off mechanisms that adjust flow or stop the machine when a predetermined level is reached.
[F04B 49/06] Control using electricity -- F04B 49/06 covers the electrical control of positive-displacement machines for liquids, such as pumps and pumping installations, where the control system is not covered elsewhere. It includes electric circuits, switches, sensors and safety devices that regulate speed, start-stop operation, pressure or flow, ensuring reliable and safe functioning.
[F04B 49/065] and making use of computers -- This CPC subclass relates to the integration of computers in the control and safety systems of positive-displacement liquid pumps and related installations that are not otherwise covered. It includes computer-based control algorithms, digital monitoring, and software-driven safety interlocks for pump operation.
[F04B 49/08] Regulating by delivery pressure -- This subclass covers control or safety devices that regulate the operation of positive-displacement liquid pumps by monitoring the pressure of the fluid at the delivery point. It includes mechanisms that adjust flow, speed or activation of the pump based on delivery-pressure feedback, distinct from other control schemes.
[F04B 49/10] Other safety measures -- F04B 49/10 covers safety devices and control mechanisms applied to positive-displacement liquid pumps that are not covered by more specific subclasses. It includes general or auxiliary safety features such as overload protection, emergency shut-off, or monitoring systems designed to prevent damage or hazards during pump operation. This subclass is used for measures that are not otherwise specifically classified.
[F04B 49/103] Responsive to speed -- F04B 49/103 covers control or safety features for positive-displacement liquid pumps that adjust operation based on pump speed, such as speed-sensitive regulators, governors and shut-off devices that activate or modify flow when a preset speed threshold is reached.
[F04B 49/106] Responsive to pumped volume -- The classification covers control or safety features for positive-displacement liquid machines that adjust operation based on the volume actually pumped, such as devices that modify speed, stroke or valve settings in response to measured flow to maintain desired performance or protect the system.
[F04B 49/12] by varying the length of stroke of the working members -- This subclass covers control or safety measures for positive-displacement liquid machines that adjust the pump's performance by varying the stroke length of the working members, such as pistons or diaphragms, to regulate flow, pressure or speed.
[F04B 49/121] Lost-motion device in the driving mechanism -- A lost-motion device in the driving mechanism is a safety or control feature for positive-displacement liquid machines such as pumps. It allows a temporary disengagement or slip between the motor and the pump shaft, preventing damage or over-pressurisation when the pump encounters resistance or an abnormal load.
[F04B 49/123] by changing the eccentricity of one element relative to another element -- The subclass concerns control or safety devices for positive-displacement liquid pumps that work by varying the eccentricity between two moving parts, such as shafts, cams or rotors. Changing this offset modifies the pump's displacement, flow or pressure for regulation or protection.
[F04B 49/125] by changing the eccentricity of the actuation means, e.g. cams or cranks, relative to the driving means, e.g. driving shafts  F04B49/128  takes precedence -- This subclass relates to control or safety devices for positive-displacement liquid machines that vary pump operation by altering the eccentricity between actuation elements such as cams or cranks and the driving shaft.
[F04B 49/126] with a double eccenter mechanism -- This subclass covers control or safety devices for positive-displacement liquid pumps that employ a double-eccenter arrangement to drive pistons or gears. The double-eccenter mechanism provides two offset crank throws for smoother motion or pressure regulation, and the entry addresses features such as overload protection, speed regulation or automatic shut-off specific to that design.
[F04B 49/128] by changing the eccentricity of the cylinders, e.g. by moving a cylinder block -- This subclass covers safety or control features for positive-displacement liquid pumps that work by changing the eccentricity of the cylinders, for example by moving a cylinder block to adjust pump operation.
[F04B 49/14] Adjusting abutments located in the path of reciprocation -- The code F04B 49/14 relates to devices that adjust abutments positioned in the reciprocal travel path of positive-displacement pumps or similar machinery. It covers mechanisms for setting, fine-tuning or automatically shifting these abutments to control stroke limits, reduce wear or improve safety, when such adjustments are not covered by more specific provisions. This falls under the broader class of control and safety measures for liquid-pumping equipment.
[F04B 49/16] by adjusting the capacity of dead spaces of working chambers -- The subclass F04B 49/16 covers arrangements that modify the effective volume of dead spaces within the working chambers of positive-displacement liquid machines, such as pumps, by altering chamber geometry or internal components. This adjustment is used to control flow, efficiency or safety characteristics of the device.
[F04B 49/18] by changing the effective cross-section of the working surface of the piston -- F04B 49/18 covers pumping or displacement machines in which the piston's working surface area is varied by altering its effective cross-section, thereby adjusting the volume of fluid moved per stroke. This includes mechanisms such as adjustable-area pistons, variable-geometry chambers, or movable collars that change the piston's displacement characteristics for flow control or safety purposes.
[F04B 49/20] by changing the driving speed -- This subclass concerns control or safety arrangements for positive-displacement liquid pumps that operate by varying the driving speed, using speed changes to regulate flow, pressure or to protect the machine.
[F04B 49/22] by means of valves -- This CPC subclass deals with control or safety measures for positive-displacement liquid machines that use valves to regulate flow, pressure, or direction. It includes devices where valves are employed to start, stop, or modify operation of pumps and related installations, not covered elsewhere.
[F04B 49/225] with throttling valves or valves varying the pump inlet opening or the outlet opening -- F04B 49/225 covers positive-displacement liquid pumps that incorporate throttling or adjustable-opening valves at the inlet or outlet to regulate flow or pressure. These valves may be used for control, safety or performance optimisation, and are not covered by more specific valve-related subclasses.
[F04B 49/24] Bypassing -- The F04B 49/24 classification covers bypass arrangements used in positive-displacement liquid machines, such as pumps, to route flow around the device for control or safety purposes when not otherwise specified. It addresses systems that divert fluid to protect equipment, manage pressure, or enable maintenance without stopping the overall installation.
[F04B 49/243] by keeping open the inlet valve -- The CPC entry F04B 49/243 covers safety or control measures for positive-displacement liquid machines that involve keeping the inlet valve open, thereby preventing vacuum formation or back-flow during operation. This measure is applied to pumps or pumping installations when other protective provisions are not specified.
[F04B 49/246] by keeping open the outlet valve -- The CPC entry F04B 49/246 concerns safety or control devices for positive-displacement liquid machines that maintain the outlet valve in an open position, thereby preventing pressure buildup, backflow or suction hazards in pumps and pumping installations. It applies when such measures are not covered by other specific provisions.
[F04B 5/00] Machines or pumps with differential-surface pistons -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Pumps for liquids, with stepped pistons or with double-acting pistons.
[F04B 5/02] with double-acting pistons -- F04B 5/02 covers positive-displacement machines for liquids that use pistons acting alternately on both sides of the stroke, i.e. double-acting pistons. These devices include pumps where each piston delivers fluid during both forward and return movements, improving flow continuity and efficiency.
[F04B 51/00] Testing machines, pumps, or pumping installations -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Systems and methods for testing pumps for liquids and for elastic fluids.
[F04B 53/00] Component parts, details or accessories not provided for in, or of interest apart from, groups -- **Definition:** This place covers:  In this group are classified various components adapted for pumps for liquids and for elastic fluids, e.g. piston means, pulsation and noise damping means, lubrication means, cooling means, filtering means, compressor valves, housings or casings.
[F04B 53/001] Noise damping -- F04B 53/001 covers noise-damping measures applied to component parts, details or accessories of positive-displacement liquid machines such as pumps and compressors. The subclass addresses designs, materials or devices that reduce acoustic emissions generated during operation, including vibration isolators, sound-absorbing housings and acoustic liners. It is used for innovations that improve quietness without altering the primary pumping function.
[F04B 53/002] by encapsulation -- The CPC subclass F04B 53/002 pertains to positive-displacement liquid pumps whose moving elements are enclosed within a sealed capsule or housing. It covers designs where the pump mechanism is encapsulated to protect against leakage, contamination or external damage, often used in sanitary or high-pressure applications. This subclass falls under component parts, details or accessories of positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04B 53/003] by damping supports -- F04B 53/003 covers damping supports used in positive-displacement liquid machines such as hydraulic pumps or compressors, where the supports reduce vibrations or shock loads on moving parts. It pertains to component parts, details or accessories not classified elsewhere in the positive-displacement machine group.
[F04B 53/004] by mechanical resonators -- F04B 53/004 covers component parts, details or accessories of positive-displacement liquid machines that employ mechanical resonators, such as oscillating springs, diaphragms or other resonant elements that aid fluid displacement. It pertains to devices where the resonator's vibratory action is used to enhance pumping or metering functions, without describing the complete machine itself.
[F04B 53/005] Adaptations or arrangements of valves used as foot valves, of suction strainers, or of mud-boxes -- F04B 53/005 covers adaptations or arrangements of valves that serve as foot valves, suction strainers or mud-boxes in positive-displacement machines for liquids. It pertains to component parts, details or accessories not otherwise classified within the pump family.
[F04B 53/006] Crankshafts -- Crankshafts are the rotating shafts that convert reciprocating motion into rotary motion in positive-displacement liquid machines such as pumps. The classification covers their design, geometry, bearing arrangements, connection to pistons or cams, and any accessories that enable torque transmission or speed regulation within these hydraulic systems.
[F04B 53/007] Cylinder heads -- Cylinder heads are components that close and seal the cylinder of positive-displacement liquid pumps, forming the chamber where the piston moves. They often incorporate valve seats, cooling passages or mounting features, and are essential for containing pressure and directing flow within the pump assembly.
[F04B 53/008] Spacing or clearance between cylinder and piston -- F04B 53/008 relates to the spacing or clearance between a cylinder and its piston in positive-displacement machines for liquids such as pumps and hydraulic cylinders. The code classifies component parts, details or accessories that are not otherwise covered, focusing on dimensional tolerances, clearance mechanisms and related adjustments.
[F04B 53/02] Packing the free space between cylinders and pistons -- F04B 53/02 relates to the sealing or packing of the clearance between pistons and cylinder walls in positive-displacement liquid pumps or compressors. It includes rings, gaskets, sleeves and other materials that fill the free space to prevent fluid leakage while permitting piston motion. The code is applied to component parts that are not otherwise classified in the CPC.
[F04B 53/04] Draining -- F04B 53/04 covers the draining of liquids from machines or systems, including outlets, drainage passages, and associated fittings used with positive-displacement pumps. It pertains to component parts, details or accessories that are not covered elsewhere in the classification of positive-displacement liquid-handling equipment.
[F04B 53/06] Venting -- F04B 53/06 covers venting features for positive-displacement liquid machines, such as pumps, that relieve trapped air or excess pressure. The classification applies to components, details or accessories specifically designed to provide or improve venting functions.
[F04B 53/08] Cooling -- F04B 53/08 covers cooling provisions for positive-displacement liquid machines, such as pumps, including the design and integration of cooling systems, heat-exchange elements, and related accessories that manage temperature of the machine's moving parts or fluid path. It addresses methods and devices that prevent overheating and maintain operational efficiency of the pump components.
[F04B 53/10] Valves -- F04B 53/10 covers valves used in positive displacement machines for liquids, including component parts and accessories of such machines. The classification pertains to devices that control the flow, pressure, or direction of liquid in pumps, compressors, or similar equipment.
[F04B 53/1002] Ball valves -- F04B 53/1002 pertains to ball valves, rotary flow-control components that use a hollow, perforated sphere to start, stop, or throttle liquid flow in positive-displacement machines. Classified as component parts or accessories, they are treated separately from other valve groups within hydraulic and fluid-handling systems.
[F04B 53/1005] being formed by two closure members working in series -- A positive-displacement pump element in which the closure or valve is created by two closure members placed one after the other, so that the fluid path is sealed sequentially. This arrangement is used as a component part or accessory in machines that move liquids by displacing a fixed volume with each cycle.
[F04B 53/1007] having means for guiding the closure member -- This subclass pertains to components that provide guiding mechanisms for a closure member, such as a valve or pump door, ensuring proper alignment and movement within positive-displacement liquid machines. It is used for parts, details or accessories not otherwise classified in the main groups.
[F04B 53/101] having means for limiting the opening height -- F04B 53/101 relates to component parts, details or accessories of positive-displacement liquid machines that incorporate a device for limiting the opening height of a moving element such as a piston, cylinder or valve. Typical features include stops, collars, adjustable limiters or locking mechanisms that restrict the maximum stroke, thereby controlling the volume displaced per cycle. This subclass is used for describing the means that prevent excessive opening in pumps or compressors.
[F04B 53/1012] and means for controlling the opening height -- F04B 53/1012 belongs to the CPC group for positive-displacement liquid machines and designates components, details or accessories specifically intended to control the opening height of moving parts such as valves, pistons or chambers in pumps and compressors. It covers mechanisms that set or adjust lift, travel or clearance to regulate fluid displacement.
[F04B 53/1015] Combinations of ball valves working in parallel -- The CPC subclass F04B 53/1015 covers hydraulic or pneumatic systems that employ two or more ball valves arranged to operate side-by-side, allowing flow to be divided or combined for redundancy, capacity increase, or balanced control. It pertains to parallel valve configurations within positive-displacement liquid machines.
[F04B 53/1017] Semi-spherical ball valves -- F04B 53/1017 covers semi-spherical ball valves, which are valve components shaped like a half-sphere and used to control the flow of liquids in positive-displacement machines. The entry is applied to component parts, details or accessories that are not otherwise provided for in the main groups of positive-displacement liquid machinery.
[F04B 53/102] Disc valves -- F04B 53/102 covers disc valves, a type of rotary valve that uses a rotating disc to open or close a passage for liquid flow in positive-displacement machines such as pumps and compressors. The classification pertains to the component parts, details or accessories of these machines that are not otherwise specified. It falls under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04B 53/1022] having means for guiding the closure member axially -- This CPC subclass pertains to positive-displacement machines for liquids, specifically component parts not otherwise listed, covering designs that include a mechanism to guide the closure member along the axis of motion. It falls under F04B 53/1022 and addresses axial guiding means for the closure member in pumps or similar equipment.
[F04B 53/1025] the guiding means being provided within the valve opening -- The classification F04B 53/1025 pertains to positive-displacement liquid machines where guiding elements are incorporated inside the valve opening, controlling the motion of the valve or its sealing parts to direct flow and reduce wear.
[F04B 53/1027] the guiding means being provided at both sides of the disc -- This CPC subclass deals with positive-displacement liquid machines that use a rotating disc, where guide elements such as shafts, bearings or sleeves are mounted on both faces of the disc to provide axial and radial alignment during operation.
[F04B 53/103] Flat-annular type disc valves -- This subclass pertains to flat-annular disc valves used as component parts, details or accessories in positive-displacement machines for liquids, such as pumps or compressors. It covers the design of annular disc elements that provide sealing or flow control between stationary and moving components.
[F04B 53/1032] Spring-actuated disc valves  F04B53/1022 ,  F04B53/103  take precedence -- Spring-actuated disc valves are valve assemblies in which a spring provides the closing force for a disc that regulates fluid flow, typically used in positive displacement machines for liquids. The spring holds the disc against a seat and releases it when pressure overcomes the spring preload, allowing rapid opening and automatic reseating. This element is classified under CPC F04B 53/1032 as a component part distinct from complete valve groups.
[F04B 53/1035] with means for limiting the opening height -- The classification covers components or accessories of positive-displacement liquid machines that incorporate devices to restrict the maximum opening or travel of a pump chamber, such as travel limiters, stops, or adjustable height-limiting mechanisms. It applies to any element that limits the opening height of pistons, diaphragms, or other moving parts within the pump.
[F04B 53/1037] Flap valves -- F04B 53/1037 relates to flap valves, components used in positive-displacement machines for liquids to control flow direction. These valves consist of a hinged or pivoted plate that opens or closes a passage, functioning as check or shut-off devices in pumps, compressors and similar equipment. They fall under mechanical engineering for liquid displacement.
[F04B 53/104] the closure member being a rigid element oscillating around a fixed point -- This CPC subclass falls under positive-displacement liquid pumps and covers components in which the valve or closure member is a rigid element that oscillates about a fixed pivot point. Such oscillating plates, flaps or levers seal and open the pump chamber during each stroke, providing flow control without flexible seals.
[F04B 53/1042] by means of a flexible connection -- The F04B 53/1042 subgroup covers component parts, details or accessories for positive-displacement liquid machines that are connected by a flexible means such as hoses, flexible couplings or bellows. It includes any flexible connection used to transmit motion or fluid between the machine's moving parts.
[F04B 53/1045] the valve being formed by two elements -- F04B 53/1045 refers to components of positive-displacement liquid machines, specifically valves constructed from two elements, covering the parts, details or accessories of such valves that are not classified elsewhere. This falls under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04B 53/1047] the valve being formed by one or more flexible elements -- This sub-group covers valves used in positive-displacement machines for liquids where the sealing or flow-control element is formed from one or more flexible components such as membranes, diaphragms or bellows, including designs that rely on the flexibility of these elements to open, close or modulate fluid flow.
[F04B 53/105] one flexible element oscillating around a fixed point -- The code F04B 53/105 designates a flexible element that swings about a fixed point and is used as a component or accessory in positive-displacement machines for liquids. It covers parts such as oscillating diaphragms or flexible screws that are not otherwise classified within the main groups.
[F04B 53/1052] two flexible elements oscillating around a fixed point -- F04B 53/1052 covers positive-displacement liquid machines that use a pair of flexible members that swing about a common pivot to pump fluid. The two flexible elements act like oscillating diaphragms or bellows, creating a sealed chamber whose volume changes during each oscillation, thereby moving liquid. This subclass includes components and accessories specifically designed for such dual-flexible-element pumps.
[F04B 53/1055] more than two flexible elements oscillating around a fixed point -- F04B 53/1055 covers liquid-moving devices that employ three or more flexible members-such as diaphragms, bellows or membranes-that oscillate about a fixed point to create displacement. The elements work together in a coordinated motion, each moving back and forth while anchored at a common location, to pump or transfer fluid. This subclass is part of the positive-displacement machines for liquids hierarchy.
[F04B 53/1057] the valve being a tube, e.g. normally closed at one end -- This subclass concerns tube-type valves used in positive-displacement machines for liquids, where the tube itself acts as the valve element and is normally closed at one end. It relates to component parts, details or accessories that are not classified elsewhere in the F04B series.
[F04B 53/106] the valve being a membrane -- This subclass deals with membrane-type valves used as component parts in positive-displacement liquid pumps or compressors. It covers design, structure and operation of flexible membrane valves that control fluid flow, separate chambers, or act as check or control elements in such machines.
[F04B 53/1062] fixed at two or more points at its periphery -- The F04B 53/1062 subclass refers to components of positive-displacement liquid machines whose moving elements are secured at two or more points around their periphery. It covers designs where the pump or rotor is fixed at multiple peripheral locations to provide stability, precise positioning or improved sealing within the overall pump assembly. This classification falls under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids, and component parts not otherwise specified.
[F04B 53/1065] fixed at its centre -- The class F04B 53/1065 covers components of positive-displacement liquid machines that are fixed at their centre, such as centrally mounted pistons, rotors or other moving elements that remain stationary relative to the machine housing. It applies to pumps, compressors or similar devices where the central part is anchored rather than rotating or sliding.
[F04B 53/1067] fixed at its whole periphery and with an opening at its centre -- A stationary component of a positive-displacement liquid pump that is secured around its entire outer edge and features a central opening, typically serving as a housing or guide for the moving piston or rotor within the machine.
[F04B 53/107] the opening normally being closed by a fixed element -- This CPC subclass F04B 53/107 concerns component parts of positive-displacement machines for liquids that are not otherwise listed, specifically openings that are normally sealed by a fixed element such as a valve seat or a permanently positioned cover.
[F04B 53/1072] the valve being an elastic body, the length thereof changing in the opening direction -- F04B 53/1072 relates to valves constructed from an elastic body whose length varies in the direction of opening, a feature used in positive-displacement machines for liquids to control flow.
[F04B 53/1075] the valve being a flexible annular ring -- The CPC entry F04B 53/1075 covers valves that consist of a flexible annular ring and are used as component parts, details or accessories in positive-displacement machines for liquids. It classifies such ring-type valves when they are not otherwise specified in the main groups of the pump or compressor architecture. This provision captures flexible ring valves employed for sealing, throttling or flow control in liquid-handling equipment.
[F04B 53/1077] Flow resistance valves, e.g. without moving parts -- Flow resistance valves are passive devices that restrict or regulate fluid flow without any moving components, relying on fixed geometry or fluid-dynamic principles to create a pressure drop. They are used in hydraulic and pneumatic systems where simplicity, reliability and low maintenance are required, often in applications such as pressure limiting, metering or safety shut-off.
