[F04C 15/068] of the elastic type, e.g. reed valves -- F04C 15/068 covers elastic-type components such as reed valves used in rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive displacement pumps for liquids. It includes details or accessories that regulate flow by flexing, typically mounted in the pumping chamber to control suction and discharge.
[F04C 18/00] Rotary-piston pumps specially adapted for elastic fluids -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Rotary-piston pumps for liquids or both liquids and elastic fluids -> F04C2/00 - Rotary-piston pumps with fluid ring or the like -> F04C19/00 - Rotary-piston pumps in which the working-fluid is exclusively displaced by one or more reciprocating pistons -> F04B
[F04C 18/02] of arcuate-engagement type, i.e. with circular translatory movement of co-operating members, each member having the same number of teeth or tooth-equivalents -- F04C 18/02 covers rotary-piston pumps designed for elastic fluids that use an arcuate-engagement mechanism, where the pistons and the housing move in a circular translatory motion and each cooperating member has an equal number of teeth or tooth-equivalents. This subclass falls under positive-displacement machines for liquids, specifically rotary-piston or oscillating-piston types.
[F04C 18/0207] both members having co-operating elements in spiral form -- F04C 18/0207 concerns rotary-piston pumps designed for elastic fluids in which the rotor and the corresponding stator each have spiral-shaped cooperating elements that mesh to displace the fluid. The classification is part of the positive-displacement machines for liquids hierarchy under mechanical engineering.
[F04C 18/0215] where only one member is moving -- F04C 18/0215 covers rotary-piston pumps designed for elastic fluids in which the pump operates with a single moving member, such as one rotating piston while the other components remain stationary. The classification is used for positive-displacement liquid machines that employ a rotary-piston mechanism adapted to handle compressible or elastically deformable liquids. It falls under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids, and the subgroup for rotary-piston pumps for elastic fluids.
[F04C 18/0223] with symmetrical double wraps -- F04C 18/0223 classifies rotary-piston pumps that are specially designed for elastic fluids and feature a pair of symmetrical double wraps around the rotor. The double-wrap arrangement provides balanced sealing and efficient displacement of compressible liquids.
[F04C 18/023] where both members are moving -- This subclass covers rotary-piston pump designs in which both cooperating members move relative to each other, intended for pumping elastic (compressible) fluids. It belongs to the positive-displacement machines for liquids group within mechanical engineering.
[F04C 18/0238] with symmetrical double wraps -- The subclass F04C 18/0238 pertains to rotary-piston pumps designed for elastic fluids that incorporate a symmetrical double-wrap configuration, meaning the pumping elements are arranged in two mirrored wraps around the rotor to ensure balanced displacement and uniform flow.
[F04C 18/0246] Details concerning the involute wraps or their base, e.g. geometry -- The subclass F04C 18/0246 covers technical details of the involute wraps-or their base-in rotary-piston pumps that are specially adapted for handling elastic fluids, describing the geometry and related features of these wrap components.
[F04C 18/0253] Details concerning the base -- F04C 18/0253 covers technical features of the base or housing of rotary-piston pumps that are designed for compressible or elastic liquids. It falls under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines, specifically rotary-piston pumps adapted to elastic fluids.
[F04C 18/0261] Details of the ports, e.g. location, number, geometry -- The subclass addresses the design of inlet and outlet ports in rotary-piston pumps intended for elastic fluids, describing their position, number and geometric shape. These port characteristics influence fluid intake, discharge and sealing behavior within the pump.
[F04C 18/0269] Details concerning the involute wraps -- This subclass deals with the design features of the involute-shaped wraps that guide the rotating pistons in rotary-piston pumps used with elastic fluids such as hydraulic oil or rubber-based liquids. It covers geometrical parameters, material choices, and manufacturing details that affect sealing, efficiency, and durability of the pump.
[F04C 18/0276] Different wall heights -- The code F04C 18/0276 covers rotary-piston pumps designed for elastic liquids that incorporate chambers with walls of varying heights. Different wall heights affect the pump's volumetric displacement and pressure characteristics, enabling tailored performance for specific fluid properties.
[F04C 18/0284] Details of the wrap tips -- F04C 18/0284 pertains to the specific design of wrap-tips, the flexible sealing elements that surround the rotor in rotary-piston pumps built for elastic fluids. The entry covers their geometry, material choice and mounting features that ensure reliable sealing and durability when pumping highly compressible or rubbery liquids.
[F04C 18/0292] Ports or channels located in the wrap -- F04C 18/0292 covers the design of ports or channels that are integrated into the wrap-the flexible outer casing-of rotary-piston pumps specifically intended for handling elastic or compressible fluids such as oils and hydraulic liquids.
[F04C 18/04] of internal-axis type -- F04C 18/04 covers rotary-piston pumps that are designed for handling elastic fluids such as oil or plastic, where the piston moves around an internal axis rather than an external one. These internal-axis type pumps are a subclass of positive-displacement machines used in mechanical engineering for liquid transfer.
[F04C 18/045] having a C-shaped piston -- This subclass relates to rotary-piston positive-displacement pumps for elastic fluids that use a piston shaped like the letter C, which slides within a cylindrical chamber to create flow. It falls under the broader category of rotary-piston machines for liquids in mechanical engineering.
[F04C 18/06] of other than internal-axis type -- F04C 18/06 covers rotary-piston positive-displacement pumps that are specially adapted for elastic (compressible) liquids and are not of the internal-axis type. The group includes designs where the rotating piston is mounted on an external or off-center axis, allowing the pump to handle fluids such as hydraulic oil or other compressible media.
[F04C 18/063] with coaxially-mounted members having continuously-changing circumferential spacing between them -- This subclass covers rotary-piston pumps for elastic (compressible) liquids in which the coaxially arranged components, such as the rotor and stator, are designed with a continuously varying circumferential gap between them, allowing the spacing to change continuously around the axis.
[F04C 18/067] having cam-and-follower type drive -- F04C 18/067 classifies rotary-piston pumps that are specifically adapted for elastic liquids and employ a cam-and-follower drive to control the piston motion. The cam-follower arrangement provides a timed, positive-displacement action suitable for pumping compressible fluids such as hydraulic oil.
[F04C 18/07] having crankshaft-and-connecting-rod type drive -- F04C 18/07 designates rotary-piston pumps specifically for elastic fluids that are powered by a crankshaft-and-connecting-rod drive. These machines belong to the positive-displacement, rotary-piston subgroup of liquid handling equipment in mechanical engineering.
[F04C 18/073] having pawl-and-ratchet type drive -- F04C 18/073 covers rotary-piston pumps designed for elastic or compressible liquids that use a pawl-and-ratchet type drive to impart intermittent rotational motion to the piston, enabling controlled fluid displacement.
[F04C 18/077] having toothed-gearing type drive -- This CPC subclass deals with rotary-piston pumps adapted for elastic fluids that use a toothed-gear type drive to transmit power to the pumping chamber. The gear mechanism provides positive displacement and precise flow control, making it suitable for hydraulic or pneumatic applications.
[F04C 18/08] of intermeshing-engagement type, i.e. with engagement of co-operating members similar to that of toothed gearing -- F04C 18/08 covers rotary-piston pumps designed for elastic fluids that use an intermeshing-engagement mechanism, where the cooperating piston elements mesh like toothed gears to transmit motion and seal the chambers.
[F04C 18/082] Details specially related to intermeshing engagement type pumps -- F04C 18/082 covers rotary-piston pumps that use intermeshing gears or lobes to move elastic liquids such as oils or polymers. The subclass details design features, sealing arrangements and timing mechanisms specific to the intermeshing engagement of the rotors. It is part of the positive-displacement machine group for fluids.
[F04C 18/084] Toothed wheels -- F04C 18/084 covers rotary-piston pumps adapted for elastic fluids, where a toothed wheel meshes with a cylindrical rotor to create the positive-displacement action. The entry is part of the mechanical-engineering hierarchy for positive-displacement machines for liquids, specifically rotary-piston or oscillating-piston types.

[F04C 18/088] Elements in the toothed wheels or the carter for relieving the pressure of fluid imprisoned in the zones of engagement -- This subclass covers design features incorporated in the teeth of rotary-piston pump wheels or in the surrounding carter that provide a path for fluid trapped in the tooth-meshing zones to escape, thereby relieving pressure, preventing hydraulic lock and reducing wear. It applies to positive-displacement pumps handling elastic liquids such as hydraulic oil.
[F04C 18/10] of internal-axis type with the outer member having more teeth or tooth equivalents, e.g. rollers, than the inner member -- F04C 18/10 covers internally-axis rotary-piston pumps designed for elastic fluids where the outer rotating member carries more teeth, rollers or equivalent projections than the inner member. This configuration provides a higher number of contact points to improve sealing and efficiency in pumping viscous or compressible liquids. It is a sub-class of positive-displacement pumps for liquids within mechanical engineering.
[F04C 18/103] with a crescent shaped filler element, located between the inner and outer intermeshing elements -- The F04C 18/103 subclass covers rotary-piston pumps designed for elastic fluids that incorporate a crescent-shaped filler element positioned between the inner and outer intermeshing components. This element improves sealing and reduces leakage in positive-displacement pumps where the pistons rotate within a housing.
[F04C 18/107] with helical teeth
[F04C 18/1075] the inner and outer member having a different number of threads and one of the two being made of elastic material, e.g. Moineau type -- F04C 18/1075 covers rotary-piston pumps designed for elastic fluids in which the inner and outer members have different numbers of threads, with at least one member made of an elastic material, exemplified by Moineau-type designs. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering positive-displacement rotary-piston machines for liquids.
[F04C 18/113] the inner member carrying rollers intermeshing with the outer member -- F04C 18/113 covers rotary-piston pumps in which the inner member carries rollers that intermesh with rollers on the outer member, forming sealed chambers that move elastic fluids. This arrangement provides a positive-displacement mechanism especially suited for handling compressible liquids.
[F04C 18/12] of other than internal-axis type -- F04C 18/12 covers rotary-piston pumps that are not of the internal-axis type and are specially adapted for elastic liquids such as hydraulic fluids. These positive-displacement machines use a rotor with pistons that move on a circular path to displace fluid, distinguishing them from conventional axial-axis pump designs.
[F04C 18/123] with radially or approximately radially from the rotor body extending tooth-like elements, co-operating with recesses in the other rotor, e.g. one tooth -- This subclass pertains to rotary-piston pumps for elastic fluids in which the rotor is equipped with radially extending tooth-like elements that engage recesses on the opposing rotor to create positive displacement. The configuration allows efficient pumping of viscous or rubbery liquids by converting rotary motion into linear displacement.
[F04C 18/126] with radially from the rotor body extending elements, not necessarily co-operating with corresponding recesses in the other rotor, e.g. lobes, Roots type -- This CPC subclass (F04C 18/126) concerns rotary-piston positive-displacement pumps for elastic fluids that have radially extending elements on the rotor body, such as lobes or Roots-type projections, which are not required to match corresponding recesses in the opposite rotor.

[F04C 18/321] with vanes hinged to the inner member and reciprocating with respect to the inner member -- F04C 18/321 covers rotary-piston (or rotary-vane) pumps that are specially adapted for handling elastic, compressible fluids. [F04C 2/073] having pawl-and-ratchet type drive -- Rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids that are driven by a pawl-and-ratchet mechanism, which converts reciprocating motion into rotation by means of a toothed wheel and a locking lever that permits motion in only one direction. This arrangement provides a simple, self-locking drive for pumps and compressors.
[F04C 2/077] having toothed-gearing type drive -- Rotary-piston positive-displacement pumps in which the driving motor is coupled to the rotor by a toothed-gearing transmission; the gear train transfers rotational power and synchronises the pistons' motion.
[F04C 2/08] of intermeshing-engagement type, i.e. with engagement of co-operating members similar to that of toothed gearing -- F04C 2/08 covers rotary-piston positive-displacement machines in which the cooperating members intermesh like toothed gears, the pistons engaging each other through gear-like teeth rather than sliding or rolling contacts. This intermeshing-engagement type is used in gear pumps, gerotor and lobed-rotor pumps, providing smooth volumetric flow and high sealing efficiency.

[F04C 2/1073] where one member is stationary while the other member rotates and orbits -- This subclass covers rotary-piston pumps or engines in which the piston or a part of the moving assembly remains fixed while another component rotates around it or follows an orbital path, converting rotary motion into fluid displacement. [F04C 2/1075] Construction of the stationary member -- F04C 2/1075 pertains to the construction of the stationary member in rotary-piston machines or pumps, covering the design, materials, mounting and sealing of the housing or casing that remains fixed while the piston rotates, including methods for supporting, cooling and integrating these components.
[F04C 2/1076] where one member orbits or wobbles relative to the other member which rotates around a fixed axis -- F04C 2/1076 covers rotary-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids in which one component orbits or wobbles while a second component rotates about a fixed axis, creating a pumping action through the relative motion of the two members. This subclass is used for pumps where the moving piston follows an eccentric or wobbling path rather than a purely circular one.
[F04C 2/1078] where one member rotates and both members are allowed to orbit or wobble -- F04C 2/1078 covers rotary-piston liquid pumps in which the rotating member turns while the complementary member is free to orbit or wobble, allowing a combined rotary and orbital motion. This arrangement improves sealing and flow characteristics by letting both components move relative to each other during operation.
[F04C 2/113] the inner member carrying rollers intermeshing with the outer member -- The sub-group F04C 2/113 relates to rotary-piston positive-displacement machines in which the inner rotor carries rollers that intermesh with the outer rotor, providing sealing and power transmission between the two rotating members.
[F04C 2/12] of other than internal-axis type -- F04C 2/12 relates to rotary-piston fluid machines that are not of the internal-axis type, describing pumps and compressors where the piston rotates about an axis situated outside the working chamber. This subclass covers designs in which the piston's motion is generated by an external rotating shaft rather than by an axis passing through the piston itself.
[F04C 2/123] with radially or approximately radially from the rotor body extending tooth-like elements, co-operating with recesses in the other rotor, e.g. one tooth -- This CPC subclass F04C 2/123 covers rotary-piston positive-displacement pumps in which one rotor carries radially extending tooth-like elements that engage matching recesses on the opposite rotor. The arrangement provides a sealing and transfer action for moving liquid through the pump.
[F04C 2/126] with radially from the rotor body extending elements, not necessarily co-operating with corresponding recesses in the other rotor, e.g. lobes, Roots type -- It covers rotary-piston positive-displacement pumps that use radial protrusions from the rotor, such as lobes or Roots-type elements, which need not match recesses on the opposing rotor. The design relies on intermeshing rotors to trap and move fluid without requiring complementary cuts.
[F04C 2/14] with toothed rotary pistons -- F04C 2/14 covers rotary-piston pumps in which the pistons are provided with teeth that engage each other or a gear-like housing to transmit motion and maintain sealing. The toothed design improves synchronization and torque transmission in positive-displacement liquid machines.
[F04C 2/16] with helical teeth, e.g. chevron-shaped, screw type -- Rotary-piston positive-displacement pumps that move liquid using a screw-type rotor with helical or chevron-shaped teeth. The helical teeth engage the pump housing, creating sealed chambers that transport fluid as the rotor turns, providing smooth, pulsation-free flow. This design is used in applications where precise metering and high efficiency are required.
[F04C 2/165] having more than two rotary pistons with parallel axes -- This CPC subclass defines rotary-piston machines that employ more than two pistons whose axes are parallel to each other. It belongs to the mechanical-engineering group for positive-displacement rotary-piston pumps, specifically under F04C 2/165. The entry covers designs where multiple parallel pistons work together to move liquid.
[F04C 2/18] with similar tooth forms -- F04C 2/18 covers rotary-piston positive-displacement pumps in which the rotor and housing teeth have matching or similar profiles, allowing the moving pistons to maintain a continuous seal as they rotate. This geometry improves volumetric efficiency and reduces leakage, making the design suitable for high-pressure liquid transfer in hydraulic and lubrication systems.
[F04C 2/20] with dissimilar tooth forms -- F04C 2/20 covers rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids in which the teeth on the rotating and stationary elements have different shapes or profiles. The classification applies to pumps where the rotor and housing or other interacting surfaces use non-identical tooth forms to achieve sealing or fluid transfer.
[F04C 2210/00] Fluid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Additional information regarding the function, the nature and the properties of fluids inside the machine.
[F04C 2210/10] working -- The CPC subclass F04C 2210/10 covers the working of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement pumps for fluids, describing how the pump converts rotational motion into fluid displacement. It includes the internal mechanisms that move the pistons and the resulting fluid flow.
[F04C 2210/1005] Air -- This CPC subclass covers rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines that handle air as the fluid. It includes pumps, compressors and related devices designed to move or pressurise air using the described piston mechanisms. The code is F04C 2210/1005.
[F04C 2210/1011] Amine -- The subclass F04C 2210/1011 covers positive-displacement liquid machines such as rotary or oscillating-piston pumps in which amines are used as the processed fluid or as additives, for example as corrosion inhibitors, lubricants or cleaning agents within the pump system.
[F04C 2210/1016] Blood -- The entry F04C2210/1016 refers to positive-displacement machines that handle blood as the working fluid, such as medical pumps used in extracorporeal circulation or heart-lung support systems. It falls under rotary-piston or oscillating-piston devices for liquids within mechanical engineering.

[F04C 2210/228] Vapour -- The CPC subclass F04C 2210/228 refers to positive-displacement machines that move or compress vapour, such as vapour pumps, condensers and vapour-driven rotary- or oscillating-piston devices used in fluid handling systems. [F04C 2210/40] Properties -- F04C 2210/40 covers the study and specification of fluid properties that influence the performance of rotary-piston and oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines, such as viscosity, density, compressibility, temperature and surface tension, and how these characteristics affect pumping efficiency and wear.
[F04C 2210/42] magnetic or ferromagnetic -- F04C 2210/42 classifies positive-displacement liquid machines-specifically rotary-piston or oscillating-piston pumps-that use magnetic or ferromagnetic elements to transmit torque, provide sealing, or enable drive separation.
[F04C 2210/44] Viscosity -- The CPC code F04C 2210/44 pertains to the viscosity of fluids handled by rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids. It covers aspects such as how fluid viscosity influences pump performance, design considerations, and methods for measuring or controlling viscosity in these devices.
[F04C 2210/60] Condition -- F04C 2210/60 pertains to the operating conditions of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids, including factors such as temperature, pressure, viscosity and flow-rate constraints that affect their performance.
[F04C 2210/62] Purity -- F04C 2210/62 covers aspects of fluid purity in rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids, including measures to keep the pumped fluid clean, remove contaminants, or maintain its chemical integrity during operation. This subclass is used for inventions that focus on ensuring the quality of the fluid moved by such pumps.
[F04C 2/22] of internal-axis type with equidirectional movement of co-operating members at the points of engagement, or with one of the co-operating members being stationary, the inner member having more teeth or tooth-equivalents than the outer member -- F04C 2/22 designates internal-axis rotary-piston pumps in which the cooperating members engage and move in the same direction, or where one member remains stationary, and the inner member carries a greater number of teeth or tooth-equivalents than the outer member. This configuration is used to achieve positive displacement of liquids.
[F04C 2220/00] Application -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Additional information regarding the use of the machine.
[F04C 2220/10] Vacuum -- The F04C 2220/10 subclass covers rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines that generate a vacuum to draw or move liquids, such as suction pumps and vacuum-assist devices used in hydraulic or lubrication systems. It falls under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04C 2220/12] Dry running -- F04C 2220/12 classifies positive-displacement pumps with rotary or oscillating pistons that are designed to operate without liquid lubrication, i.e., in a dry-running condition. The entry covers features that prevent damage when the pump runs empty or with insufficient fluid, such as protective clearances, bearings or monitoring systems.
[F04C 2220/20] Pumps with means for separating and evacuating the gaseous phase -- This CPC subclass covers pumps that incorporate devices for removing gas bubbles from the liquid being moved, typically by separating the gaseous phase and evacuating it from the pump chamber. Such arrangements are found in rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines.
[F04C 2220/22] for very low temperatures, i.e. cryogenic -- This subclass concerns rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement pumps that are specially adapted for handling liquids at cryogenic temperatures. It includes design features such as insulated chambers, low-temperature lubricants and materials capable of withstanding extreme cold.
[F04C 2220/24] for metering throughflow -- F04C 2220/24 covers positive-displacement machines that meter the throughflow of liquids, typically using rotary or oscillating pistons to deliver a measured volume per cycle. These devices are used where precise dosage or flow control is required, such as in pumps for chemicals or fuel.
[F04C 2220/26] for step-by-step output movement -- The CPC subclass F04C 2220/26 covers positive-displacement rotary-piston or oscillating-piston machines that deliver fluid by means of a step-by-step or incremental output movement, such as stepped rotary mechanisms or devices that produce discrete strokes rather than continuous rotation. It includes designs where the working volume is advanced in distinct increments to pump or compress liquids.
[F04C 2220/28] for pulsed fluid flow -- This group covers rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines that produce a pulsed fluid flow, meaning the liquid is delivered in periodic bursts rather than a continuous stream. It includes designs where the piston motion or valve arrangement creates regular pressure variations to generate the pulse.
[F04C 2220/30] Use in a chemical vapor deposition [CVD] process or in a similar process -- F04C 2220/30 refers to rotating- or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids that are used to feed liquid precursors into a chemical vapor deposition (CVD) chamber or a comparable vapor-generation process, enabling the controlled deposition of thin films or coatings.
[F04C 2220/40] Pumps with means for venting areas other than the working chamber, e.g. bearings, gear chambers, shaft seals -- F04C 2220/40 classifies positive-displacement liquid pumps that incorporate venting provisions for ancillary spaces such as bearing chambers, gear enclosures or shaft-seal cavities, rather than for the main working chamber. The code falls under mechanical engineering, specifically rotary-piston or oscillating-piston displacement machines. It covers designs that prevent pressure build-up in these secondary volumes.
[F04C 2220/50] Pumps with means for introducing gas under pressure for ballasting -- F04C 2220/50 covers pumps that incorporate a system for injecting pressurised gas into the pumping chamber or discharge line to provide ballast. The gas-filled space reduces the effective weight of the moving parts, improves sealing and can assist in priming, especially in positive-displacement rotary or oscillating piston pumps handling liquids. This design is used to enhance performance and protect the pump during start-up or low-load conditions.
[F04C 2230/00] Manufacture -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Additional information regarding the manufacturing process of the machine, including surface treatments, repair, assembly and disassembly methods.
[F04C 2230/10] by removing material -- F04C 2230/10 is a subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification for positive-displacement machines for liquids, specifically rotary-piston or oscillating-piston types, that covers manufacturing operations in which material is removed from component parts, such as machining, grinding or cutting. It relates to the subtractive production processes used to shape or finish these machines.
[F04C 2230/101] by electrochemical methods -- This CPC subclass F04C 2230/101 covers the manufacture of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement liquid machines using electrochemical techniques, such as electrodeposition or electrolytic machining, to form or modify moving parts. It falls under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids, and specifically addresses production methods that rely on electrochemical processes.
[F04C 2230/102] by spark erosion methods -- F04C 2230/102 covers the manufacturing of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement liquid machines by spark-erosion (electrical discharge machining) techniques, detailing how the components are shaped or finished using controlled electrical sparks.
[F04C 2230/103] using lasers -- This subclass covers the manufacture of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement liquid machines in which laser technology is employed for machining, cutting, welding or surface treatment of components.
[F04C 2230/20] essentially without removing material -- A subclass of positive-displacement machines for liquids, specifically rotary-piston or oscillating-piston pumps, that move fluid while the working parts remain intact, i.e., the process is carried out essentially without removing material from the liquid.
[F04C 2230/21] by casting -- F04C 2230/21 pertains to the manufacture of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids by means of casting. It covers the production step where pump housings or components are formed through a casting process.
[F04C 2230/22] by sintering -- F04C 2230/22 covers the manufacture of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement liquid machines by sintering, a powder-metallurgy process that bonds metal particles through heat without melting. The classification applies to components such as pistons, cylinders or housings produced via sintering to achieve desired strength, wear resistance or porosity, enabling efficient fluid displacement in pumps and compressors.
[F04C 2230/23] by permanently joining parts together -- F04C 2230/23 classifies the manufacture of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids when the components are permanently joined, such as by welding, brazing, soldering, adhesive bonding or similar techniques. It is a subcategory of rotary-piston positive-displacement pumps within the mechanical-engineering hierarchy. This code captures methods that create a fixed, inseparable assembly of the pump's moving and stationary parts.
[F04C 2230/231] by welding -- F04C 2230/231 covers manufacturing techniques that join components of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement liquid pumps by welding. The code applies to the fabrication of pump housings, rotors, pistons and related parts where welding is used to assemble the machine's pressure-tight structure.
[F04C 2230/24] by extrusion -- F04C 2230/24 designates rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement liquid pumps that are produced using an extrusion process. The code falls under the mechanical-engineering hierarchy for positive-displacement machines and specifies manufacturing by shaping pump components through extrusion.
[F04C 2230/25] by forging -- The CPC subclass F04C 2230/25 concerns the manufacturing of rotary- or oscillating-piston positive-displacement liquid machines by forging. It includes forging processes used to shape pump housings, pistons or related components to achieve the required strength and geometry for liquid displacement equipment.
[F04C 2230/26] by rolling -- F04C 2230/26 covers the manufacture of rotary- or oscillating-piston positive-displacement liquid machines using rolling processes. It includes methods where pistons, cylinder sleeves or other working parts are formed by rolling metal sheets or plates into the required shape, enabling efficient production of pumps and compressors in the mechanical-engineering sector.
[F04C 2230/27] by hydroforming -- F04C 2230/27 relates to the manufacture of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids by hydroforming, a process that forms metal parts through high-pressure fluid to achieve the required shape and strength. It falls under mechanical engineering, specifically positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04C 2230/40] Heat treatment -- F04C 2230/40 relates to heat-treatment operations applied in the manufacture of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids, covering processes such as heating, annealing, quenching or tempering of components and assemblies.
[F04C 2230/41] Hardening -- This subclass covers processes that increase the hardness of components of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston liquid pumps, such as heat treatment, case hardening, surface carburizing, nitriding, induction hardening, or laser hardening, aimed at improving wear resistance and durability of moving parts.
[F04C 2230/60] Assembly methods -- F04C 2230/60 pertains to the manufacturing stage of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids, specifically covering the methods used to assemble their components. It includes techniques for joining, fitting and integrating parts such as pistons, cylinders, shafts and casings to create a functional machine. This subclass groups all assembly-related processes applied during production.
[F04C 2230/601] Adjustment -- F04C 2230/601 concerns the manufacturing adjustments of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids, covering the setting, regulation or modification of clearances, stroke, timing, or other operational parameters to optimise performance, efficiency or reliability of the device.
[F04C 2230/602] Gap -- The CPC entry F04C 2230/602 "Gap" relates to rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids, covering features such as clearances, spacing or sealing gaps between moving and stationary components within the pump or compressor design. It is used to classify inventions that address the geometry, adjustment or control of these gaps to affect performance, leakage or durability.
[F04C 2230/603] Centering -- The CPC subclass F04C 2230/603 "Centering" covers devices or methods that align or maintain the geometric centre of pistons, rotors or other moving parts in rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids, including centering supports, bearings, guides and adjustment mechanisms used during manufacture and operation.
[F04C 2230/604] Mounting devices for pumps or compressors -- The F04C 2230/604 subclass includes mounting devices for pumps or compressors, such as brackets, bases, clamps and other fittings that secure the machine to a frame or structure, ensure proper alignment, and transmit operational forces. It falls under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids, specifically rotary-piston or oscillating-piston types.
[F04C 2230/605] Balancing -- This subclass covers methods and apparatus for balancing the reciprocating or rotating components of positive-displacement liquid pumps such as rotary-piston or oscillating-piston machines. It includes counterweights, dynamic or static balancing arrangements, and control systems that equalise forces to minimise vibration and improve efficiency during manufacture and operation.
[F04C 2230/70] Disassembly methods -- F04C 2230/70 covers methods for disassembling rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement liquid machines, such as reciprocating pumps, facilitating maintenance, repair, or refurbishment. The classification addresses procedures for separating components, cleaning, and preparing parts for reassembly or reuse.
[F04C 2230/80] Repairing methods -- F04C 2230/80 covers methods for repairing rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines that move liquids, such as pumps. The classification applies to procedures, techniques, and processes used to restore or maintain the functional performance of these devices.
[F04C 2230/85] Methods for improvement by repair or exchange of parts -- The entry covers procedures for enhancing rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement liquid machines by repairing or swapping components. It includes methods aimed at restoring or improving performance, reliability, or efficiency through part replacement, refurbishment, or corrective modifications during manufacture or servicing.
[F04C 2230/90] Improving properties of machine parts -- This subclass covers methods for improving the material or surface characteristics of components used in positive-displacement liquid machines such as rotary-or oscillating-piston pumps. It includes heat-treatment, alloying, coating, surface-hardening and other processes that enhance wear resistance, strength, corrosion resistance or dimensional stability of parts.
[F04C 2230/91] Coating -- F04C 2230/91 relates to the manufacture of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement liquid machines in which a coating is applied to the machine or its components. The coating may be protective, wear-resistant, corrosion-preventive or functional, and is intended to enhance the performance or durability of the pump or compressor during production.
[F04C 2230/92] Surface treatment -- F04C 2230/92 relates to surface treatment processes applied to the pistons, cylinders or other components of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids, including coating, hardening, cleaning or corrosion-resistant treatments performed during manufacture.
[F04C 2/24] of counter-engagement type, i.e. the movement of co-operating members at the points of engagement being in opposite directions -- The subclass F04C 2/24 covers rotary-piston positive-displacement machines of the counter-engagement type, where the cooperating members contact each other and move in opposite directions at their points of engagement. This arrangement is employed to improve sealing, torque transmission or flow control in liquid-handling pumps.
[F04C 2240/00] Components -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Additional information regarding machine parts.
[F04C 2240/10] Stators -- F04C 2240/10 classifies the stationary parts of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines that move liquids, such as the housing, liners, or sleeves that encase the moving pistons and form the fluid chambers. These stators provide structural support and define the working volume while remaining fixed during operation.
[F04C 2240/102] with means for discharging condensate or liquid separated from the gas pumped -- The CPC subclass F04C 2240/102 covers components of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement liquid pumps that incorporate a system for removing condensate or any liquid separated from the gas being pumped. It pertains to mechanical-engineering designs that provide discharge means for such separated fluids.
[F04C 2240/20] Rotors -- F04C 2240/20 covers rotors used in rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement liquid pumps. The classification includes the design, geometry and functional elements of rotors that create chambers for moving fluid within the pump. It is applied to components of machines that convert rotational motion into reciprocating displacement of liquids.
[F04C 2240/30] Casings or housings -- The CPC entry F04C 2240/30 relates to casings or housings used in rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids. It covers structural components that enclose, protect and provide support and sealing for the moving parts of these machines.
[F04C 2240/40] Electric motor -- F04C 2240/40 designates electric motors that serve as power sources for rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines handling liquids. The code covers motor types and configurations specifically integrated into these hydraulic pump or compressor systems.
[F04C 2240/401] Linear motor -- F04C 2240/401 is a component subclass within the mechanical-engineering group for positive-displacement liquid machines, specifically rotary- or oscillating-piston devices. It covers linear motors that directly drive pistons or other moving parts by producing a straight-line electromagnetic force, used as actuators in pumps and similar fluid-handling equipment.
[F04C 2240/402] Plurality of electronically synchronised motors -- A plurality of electric motors that are electronically synchronised to drive a rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement pump for liquids. The coordinated operation allows balanced torque, improved efficiency and redundancy in pump components, ensuring smooth, reliable movement of the piston assembly.
[F04C 2240/403] with inverter for speed control -- F04C 2240/403 covers components of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement liquid machines that incorporate an inverter to regulate the motor speed, enabling precise control of pump output and efficiency. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering for displacement pumps.
[F04C 2240/45] Hybrid prime mover -- A hybrid prime mover is a power unit that combines two or more energy sources-typically an internal-combustion engine with an electric motor or another drive-to operate a rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machine for moving liquids. This arrangement improves efficiency, offers variable speed control, and can reduce emissions.
[F04C 2240/50] Bearings -- F04C 2240/50 covers the bearing assemblies that support rotating or oscillating pistons in positive-displacement liquid pumps. The classification includes journal, sleeve, ball, roller and magnetic bearings designed to reduce friction and maintain alignment of moving pump components. It is applied to mechanical engineering designs of rotary-piston and oscillating-piston pump mechanisms.
[F04C 2240/51] for cantilever assemblies -- This CPC subclass concerns positive-displacement machines for liquids that employ rotary-piston or oscillating-piston mechanisms, focusing on components used in cantilever assemblies. It covers the design, mounting and structural features of cantilevered parts integrated into such machines.
[F04C 2240/52] for assemblies with supports on both sides -- This CPC subclass F04C 2240/52 covers rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement liquid machines whose piston assemblies are supported on both sides, giving symmetric backing and better alignment. Dual-side support structures are used in pump designs where axial and radial loads need to be balanced.
[F04C 2240/54] Hydrostatic or hydrodynamic bearing assemblies specially adapted for rotary positive displacement pumps or compressors -- The CPC subclass F04C 2240/54 pertains to hydrostatic or hydrodynamic bearing assemblies that are specially adapted for use in rotary positive-displacement pumps or compressors. These fluid-film bearings support rotating pistons or rotors, reducing friction and wear in mechanical-engineering applications.
[F04C 2240/56] Bearing bushings or details thereof -- F04C 2240/56 covers bearing bushings and related details used in rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for moving liquids. The bushings provide support and reduce friction between moving parts such as shafts or pistons, often being removable liners or sleeves. They are integral components of the machine's displacement mechanism.
[F04C 2240/60] Shafts -- F04C 2240/60 pertains to shafts that form part of the rotating or oscillating pistons in positive-displacement liquid pumps. It covers the design, mounting, coupling and bearing arrangements of these shafts as essential components of the pump mechanism.
[F04C 2240/601] Shaft flexion -- F04C 2240/601 covers the design and treatment of shaft flexion in rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids. It addresses bending, torsional deflection and related support or alignment features of the rotating shaft to ensure reliable operation of pumps or compressors.
[F04C 2240/603] with internal channels for fluid distribution, e.g. hollow shaft -- The code F04C 2240/603 covers components of rotary or oscillating piston positive-displacement pumps that incorporate internal passages for distributing fluid, such as a hollow shaft through which the liquid flows. It pertains to designs where the shaft itself serves as a conduit, enabling fluid to be delivered directly within the rotating or reciprocating element.
[F04C 2240/605] Shaft sleeves or details thereof -- Shaft sleeves or related details are components used in rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement pumps for liquids. They provide sealing, support and guidance for the moving shafts within the pump housing, helping to maintain pressure and reduce wear. This entry covers the design and function of such sleeves and their associated parts.
[F04C 2240/70] Use of multiplicity of similar components -- This CPC group covers rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement liquid pumps that employ several identical components such as multiple pistons, cylinders or valves. The use of a multiplicity of similar parts is intended to balance forces, increase flow or improve efficiency. It includes arrangements where the components are repeated symmetrically or in parallel within the same machine.
[F04C 2240/80] Other components -- F04C 2240/80 relates to miscellaneous parts used in rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids, covering components that are not classified elsewhere. It includes items such as mounts, couplings, seals and auxiliary devices that support the main pump or compressor mechanism.
[F04C 2240/801] Wear plates -- F04C 2240/801 relates to wear plates employed in rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids. The plates are component parts that protect moving surfaces from abrasion, preserve clearances and prolong the service life of the pump.
[F04C 2240/802] Liners -- F04C 2240/802 - Liners refer to removable or integral sleeves placed inside a cylinder or pump housing to provide a wear-resistant surface for the moving piston or rotor, facilitating sealing and reducing friction in rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04C 2240/803] Electric connectors or cables -- A CPC subgroup for electric connectors or cables employed in rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids, such as pumps. It covers the design, arrangement and integration of power and signal conductors that link the machine's motor or control electronics to external sources or other components.
[F04C 2240/804] Accumulators for refrigerant circuits -- Accumulators for refrigerant circuits are sealed containers that store a volume of refrigerant and release it to even out pressure spikes, maintain system stability and provide a backup supply during transient operation. They usually contain a flexible bladder or a piston that separates the refrigerant from a compressible gas, and are employed in heat-pump, air-conditioning and refrigeration equipment to smooth the flow of liquid refrigerant.
[F04C 2240/805] Fastening means, e.g. bolts -- F04C 2240/805 concerns fastening means such as bolts that secure parts of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids, i.e. components used to hold together pump or engine elements.
[F04C 2240/806] Pipes for fluids -- This CPC subclass F04C 2240/806 concerns pipes for fluids used in rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines, covering their design, material selection and arrangement within the hydraulic circuit. It falls under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04C 2240/807] Balance weight, counterweight -- A balance weight or counterweight is a mass attached to moving parts of a rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement pump to offset inertial forces, smooth operation and reduce vibration during rotation. By counterbalancing the reciprocating masses, it helps maintain steady flow and protects the machine from mechanical stress.
[F04C 2240/808] Electronic circuits (e.g. inverters) installed inside the machine -- F04C 2240/808 covers the integration of electronic circuits, such as inverters, within positive-displacement liquid machines that use rotary or oscillating pistons. The classification pertains to on-board power-conversion and control electronics that drive the pump's motor or regulate its operation, rather than external control units. It is used for patents describing built-in circuitry that directly manages the hydraulic or pneumatic function of the machine.
[F04C 2240/809] Lubricant sump -- F04C 2240/809 designates the lubricant sump, the reservoir that collects and stores oil used to lubricate the moving parts of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement liquid machines. It includes the structure, mounting and fluid-sealing features that keep the oil within the machine and supply it to bearings and gears.
[F04C 2240/81] Sensor, e.g. electronic sensor for control or monitoring -- F04C 2240/81 classifies electronic or other sensors that are installed in rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement liquid machines to monitor or control operating variables such as pressure, flow, temperature or piston position, providing feedback to the machine's control system.
[F04C 2240/811] Actuator for control, e.g. pneumatic, hydraulic, electric -- This subclass covers actuators used to regulate the operation of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement liquid machines, such as pneumatic, hydraulic or electric devices that control valve timing, stroke or speed.
[F04C 2250/00] Geometry -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Additional information regarding forms and dimensions of machine parts.
[F04C 2250/10] of the inlet or outlet -- The F04C 2250/10 entry classifies inventions that define the geometry of the inlet or outlet in rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement liquid pumps, covering shapes, dimensions, and positioning that influence fluid flow and pump performance.
[F04C 2250/101] of the inlet -- The F04C 2250/101 subclass covers the geometry of the inlet in rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids. It addresses design features such as shape, size, orientation and placement of the fluid entry to optimise flow into the compression chamber.
[F04C 2250/102] of the outlet -- The F04C 2250/102 entry covers the geometric design of the outlet in rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement pumps and compressors for liquids. It addresses the shape, size, positioning and flow-path characteristics of the discharge opening that influences fluid ejection and overall machine performance.

[F04C 2250/301] compression chamber profile defined by a mathematical expression or by parameters -- This CPC subclass covers designs of the compression-chamber shape in rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement liquid machines where the profile is defined by a mathematical expression or a set of parameters rather than a fixed drawing, allowing the chamber geometry to be described analytically for performance optimisation.
[F04C 2/26] of internal-axis type -- F04C 2/26 covers rotary-piston machines of the internal-axis type, a subclass of positive-displacement devices for moving liquids. These are rotary-piston pumps where the pistons rotate around a central shaft, generating flow by displacing fluid within the cylinder. The group falls under mechanical engineering and positive-displacement machinery for liquids.

This control area is classified under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines, and specifically under timing control.
[F04C 2270/24] Level of liquid, e.g. lubricant or cooling liquid -- This CPC subclass covers mechanisms for controlling the level of a liquid such as lubricant or cooling fluid in positive-displacement rotary-piston or oscillating-piston machines.
[F04C 2270/40] Conditions across a pump or machine -- F04C 2270/40 covers the definition and monitoring of operating conditions applied to or measured across a pump or positive-displacement machine, such as inlet and outlet pressures, temperatures, flow rates, and required torque or power. It relates to the control aspects of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston liquid displacement devices.
[F04C 2270/42] Conditions at the inlet of a pump or machine -- F04C 2270/42 pertains to the control of inlet conditions for rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines. It covers specifications of pressure, temperature, flow rate, suction head and other parameters at the pump's inlet, influencing performance, cavitation risk and efficiency.
[F04C 2270/44] Conditions at the outlet of a pump or machine -- The entry covers the characteristics of the fluid as it leaves a positive-displacement pump or rotary-piston machine, such as pressure, temperature, flow rate and any required control of these parameters. It is used to define outlet conditions for proper system performance and safety.
[F04C 2270/46] Conditions in the working chamber -- F04C 2270/46 designates the specifications for the conditions inside the working chamber of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids. It encompasses parameters such as pressure, temperature, and fluid characteristics that influence pump performance, wear, and safety. This subclass is part of the broader mechanical-engineering group dealing with positive-displacement pumps for liquids.
[F04C 2270/48] Conditions of a reservoir linked to a pump or machine -- The code F04C 2270/48 categorises specifications of the fluid reservoir that is coupled to a pump or other positive-displacement machine, including its pressure, level, volume, temperature or other hydraulic conditions that influence the machine's performance, control or efficiency.
[F04C 2270/50] Conditions before a throttle -- The entry covers the parameters and operating conditions that exist upstream of a throttle in rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids, such as pressure, temperature, flow rate and fluid properties. It relates to how these conditions are measured, monitored or prepared before the throttling element to ensure proper control and performance of the pump or compressor.
[F04C 2270/52] Conditions after a throttle -- F04C 2270/52 refers to the analysis and control of the fluid conditions that exist downstream of a throttle in rotary- or oscillating-piston positive-displacement pumps, such as pressure, flow rate, temperature, cavitation or vapor formation. It covers methods for measuring, regulating or optimizing these post-throttle parameters to ensure efficient pump operation.
[F04C 2270/54] Conditions in a control cylinder/piston unit -- F04C 2270/54 covers the design and regulation of the control cylinder or piston unit that governs the motion of a rotary or oscillating piston in positive-displacement liquid machines. It includes specifications such as pressure, temperature and adjustment mechanisms that determine the operating conditions of the control cylinder.
[F04C 2270/56] Number of pump/machine units in operation -- F04C 2270/56 relates to the determination or control of how many pump or machine units are operating at the same time. It classifies inventions that monitor, adjust or limit the number of active positive-displacement pump or piston units to achieve desired performance, safety or efficiency.
[F04C 2270/58] Valve parameters -- F04C 2270/58 relates to the definition and adjustment of valve parameters in rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids, addressing control features such as flow rate, pressure settings, opening characteristics and timing.
[F04C 2270/585] Controlled or regulated -- The CPC subclass F04C 2270/585 covers positive-displacement machines for liquids, such as rotary-piston or oscillating-piston pumps and compressors, that include control or regulation features to adjust flow, pressure or speed, for example variable-geometry pistons, throttling devices or electronic control systems.
[F04C 2270/60] Prime mover parameters -- F04C 2270/60 covers the parameters of the prime mover used in rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement liquid machines. It includes specifications such as power, speed, torque, efficiency and other performance characteristics that define the driving source for these pumps.
[F04C 2270/605] Controlled or regulated -- F04C 2270/605 covers positive-displacement liquid machines-rotary-piston or oscillating-piston pumps-in which the output flow is actively controlled or regulated, such as by variable-speed drives, throttling devices, or feedback systems that adjust displacement to maintain a set pressure or volume.
[F04C 2270/70] Safety, emergency conditions or requirements -- This sub-group covers safety and emergency features for rotary or oscillating piston positive-displacement liquid pumps, such as overload protection, emergency shut-off, pressure-relief devices, and monitoring systems that prevent damage or hazardous operation.
[F04C 2270/701] Cold start -- The CPC code F04C 2270/701 "Cold start" covers features and methods for initiating rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement liquid machines when the fluid or the machine is cold. It includes arrangements that aid lubrication, reduce wear, and ensure reliable start-up under low-temperature conditions.
[F04C 2270/72] preventing reverse rotation -- F04C 2270/72 covers control measures that stop a rotary or oscillating piston pump from turning backward, such as anti-back-driving gears, clutches or hydraulic locks. These features are intended to prevent reverse rotation of the machine during operation.
[F04C 2270/78] Warnings -- The F04C 2270/78 entry covers warning devices and systems used to alert operators of abnormal or unsafe conditions in rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids. It includes alarms, visual indicators, audible signals and other control mechanisms that monitor pressure, temperature, flow or mechanical failure and provide timely warnings to prevent damage or injury.
[F04C 2270/782] Sound -- F04C 2270/782 covers measures for controlling or reducing sound emitted by rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement liquid machines. It includes acoustic shielding, dampening structures, noise-absorbing materials and other techniques aimed at limiting noise generation or propagation from these pumps and compressors.
[F04C 2270/784] Light -- F04C 2270/784 covers features that use light to control or monitor the operation of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement liquid pumps. This includes illumination of pump components, light-based sensors, and optical actuation for flow regulation or safety. The classification is applied to inventions that integrate photonic elements into pump control systems.
[F04C 2270/80] Diagnostics -- F04C 2270/80 covers diagnostic features for rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement liquid machines, including methods for monitoring operation, detecting faults, and assessing performance. It addresses the control systems and techniques used to evaluate the condition of these pumps and to ensure efficient, reliable functioning.
[F04C 2270/86] Detection -- F04C 2270/86 Detection covers devices or methods for monitoring the operation of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement liquid pumps, such as sensors that detect pressure, flow, speed, or malfunctions, and the associated control systems.
[F04C 2270/90] Remote control, e.g. wireless, via LAN, by radio, or by a wired connection from a central computer -- The F04C 2270/90 subclass classifies remote-control systems for positive-displacement liquid machines, such as rotary or oscillating piston pumps, that operate by wireless, LAN, radio or wired links from a central computer. It covers the means of command and signal transmission used to start, stop or adjust the pump's performance remotely.
[F04C 2/28] of other than internal-axis type -- F04C 2/28 covers rotary-piston positive-displacement machines in which the piston's axis of rotation lies outside the piston itself, distinguishing them from internal-axis designs. This includes external-axis rotary pumps and similar devices that move fluids by the eccentric rotation of pistons mounted on a peripheral shaft.
[F04C 2280/00] Arrangements for preventing or removing deposits or corrosion -- The CPC entry F04C 2280/00 covers designs and methods that prevent or remove deposits, scaling or corrosion in rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids. It includes protective coatings, cleaning devices, corrosion-inhibiting systems and other arrangements that keep the pump's working parts free of harmful build-up.
[F04C 2280/02] Preventing solid deposits in pumps, e.g. in vacuum pumps with chemical vapour deposition [CVD] processes -- F04C 2280/02 pertains to arrangements that prevent or remove solid deposits and corrosion in positive-displacement liquid pumps, especially vacuum pumps employed in chemical vapour deposition processes. It addresses cleaning, coating or other methods to keep pump interiors free of buildup that could impair operation.
[F04C 2280/04] Preventing corrosion -- A preventive system for rotary- or oscillating-piston positive-displacement liquid machines that inhibits or eliminates corrosive attack and deposit buildup on components. It covers arrangements such as protective coatings, sacrificial anodes, inhibitors, and cleaning mechanisms designed to maintain the integrity and performance of the pump.
[F04C 2/30] having the characteristics covered by two or more groups -- F04C 2/30 is a subclass in the Cooperative Patent Classification for mechanical engineering that pertains to rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids which combine the features defined in two or more other groups.
[F04C 23/00] Combinations of two or more pumps, each being of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston type, specially adapted for elastic fluids -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Combinations of two or more pumps, each being of rotary-piston or oscillating-piston type, specially adapted for elastic fluids; Pumping installations specially adapted for elastic fluids; Multi-stage pumps operating in parallel or in series specially adapted for elastic fluids.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Combination of engines with pumps or compressors driven thereby -> F01C13/04
[F04C 23/001] of similar working principle -- This subgroup covers combined positive-displacement pumps that use two or more rotary-piston or oscillating-piston units operating on the same working principle, specially adapted for elastic fluids such as oils or hydraulic liquids. The units may be arranged in series or parallel to modify flow or pressure while retaining the basic pump cycle.
[F04C 23/003] having complementary function -- F04C 23/003 covers assemblies that combine two or more rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement pumps, each designed for elastic fluids, where the pumps are arranged to perform complementary functions such as alternating pressure stages, flow smoothing, or staged pressure increase within a single system.
[F04C 23/005] of dissimilar working principle -- F04C 23/005 covers devices that combine two or more rotary-piston or oscillating-piston pumps that operate on different working principles, designed specifically for pumping elastic fluids such as oils or polymers. The classification applies to arrangements where the pumps are mechanically linked but employ distinct displacement mechanisms to handle the fluid's elastic properties.
[F04C 23/006] having complementary function -- F04C 23/006 covers pump assemblies that combine two or more rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement pumps, each adapted for elastic (compressible) fluids, where the individual pumps perform complementary functions within the system.
[F04C 23/008] Hermetic pumps -- F04C 23/008 covers hermetic pumps that combine two or more rotary-piston or oscillating-piston units designed for use with elastic fluids. The pumps are sealed in a single enclosure to prevent leakage, offering compact, reliable operation for high-pressure liquid systems.
[F04C 23/02] Pumps characterised by combination with, or adaptation to, specific driving engines or motors -- F04C 23/02 covers pumps that are integrated with or specially adapted to particular driving engines or motors, especially combinations of two or more rotary-piston or oscillating-piston pumps designed for elastic liquids. The classification is used for positive-displacement machines that employ such combined drive systems.
[F04C 2/32] having both the movement defined in groups -- F04C 2/32 is a subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification for positive-displacement machines for liquids, specifically rotary-piston or oscillating-piston pumps that combine two types of motion defined in earlier groups. It covers devices where the piston motion includes both rotary and reciprocating components within the same pump.
[F04C 2/321] with vanes hinged to the inner member and reciprocating with respect to the inner member -- F04C 2/321 covers rotary-piston pumps in which the vanes are hinged to the inner rotating member and can move reciprocally relative to it, allowing the vanes to stay aligned with the pump chambers as the rotor turns. This arrangement improves sealing and accommodates variations in chamber geometry, making it suitable for efficient liquid displacement in various industrial applications.
[F04C 2/322] with vanes hinged to the outer member and reciprocating with respect to the outer member -- F04C 2/322 classifies rotary-piston pumps in which the vanes are hinged to the outer housing and move back and forth relative to that housing. The design lets the vanes pivot as the rotor turns, improving sealing and flow control in positive-displacement liquid machines.
[F04C 2/324] with vanes hinged to the inner member and reciprocating with respect to the outer member -- This subclass covers rotary-piston pumps in which the vanes are hinged to the rotating inner member and move reciprocally against the outer stationary housing, creating variable-volume chambers that pump liquid.
[F04C 2/328] and hinged to the outer member -- F04C 2/328 covers rotary-piston positive-displacement liquid machines in which a part-such as a valve, piston or rotor element-is hinged to the outer housing, allowing it to pivot relative to the machine case. The hinge arrangement is used to accommodate movement, sealing or actuation functions while the device operates as a pump or compressor. This subclass refines the rotary-piston category by specifying the hinged-to-outer-member configuration.
[F04C 2/332] with vanes hinged to the outer member and reciprocating with respect to the inner member -- This subclass covers rotary-piston pumps in which the vanes are hinged to the outer housing and move back-and-forth relative to a central rotor. The hinged vanes allow the pumping chambers to change volume as the rotor turns, providing positive displacement of liquids. It is part of the hierarchy for mechanical engineering positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04C 2/336] and hinged to the inner member -- The code F04C 2/336 covers rotary-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids in which a component, such as a piston or sealing element, is hinged to the inner moving member, allowing it to pivot or swing relative to that member during operation. This arrangement improves sealing or allows clearance compensation within the pump or compressor.
[F04C 2/34] having the movement defined in groups -- This subclass covers rotary-piston pumps whose reciprocating motion is organized into groups rather than continuous rotation, defining the displacement movement in grouped sequences. It pertains to positive-displacement liquid machines that use a rotary piston arrangement with grouped movement patterns.
[F04C 2/344] with vanes reciprocating with respect to the inner member -- This subgroup of the CPC deals with rotary-piston positive-displacement machines where the vanes move back and forth against the inner rotor or cylinder, typically found in vane pumps and compressors. The configuration involves reciprocating vanes that slide within a rotating housing to create chambers for liquid displacement.
[F04C 2/3441] the inner and outer member being in contact along one line or continuous surface substantially parallel to the axis of rotation -- This subgroup covers rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines in which the inner and outer members are in contact along a line or continuous surface that runs substantially parallel to the axis of rotation, providing a coaxial sealing interface.
[F04C 2/3442] the surfaces of the inner and outer member, forming the working space, being surfaces of revolution -- The CPC entry F04C 2/3442 covers rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines in which both the inner and outer members that define the working chamber have surfaces that are generated by revolving a profile around an axis. It relates to the geometric design of these revolving surfaces, which shape the fluid-tight space where the working fluid is displaced during each cycle.
[F04C 2/3443] with a separation element located between the inlet and outlet opening -- F04C 2/3443 refers to rotary-piston positive-displacement pumps that include a separation element positioned between the inlet and outlet openings. The element-such as a plate, sleeve or screen-creates a physical barrier that isolates the incoming fluid stream from the discharge, enabling phase separation or preventing back-mixing within the pump.
[F04C 2/3445] the vanes having the form of rollers, slippers or the like -- F04C 2/3445 pertains to rotary-piston positive-displacement liquid pumps in which the vanes are shaped as rollers, slippers or comparable forms that roll or slide against the cylinder wall, forming the pumping chambers and improving sealing and wear characteristics. This subclass groups such roller-type vane configurations within rotary-piston machines.
[F04C 2/3446] the inner and outer member being in contact along more than one line or surface -- F04C 2/3446 covers rotary-piston positive-displacement machines in which the moving inner component and the surrounding outer component touch each other along several lines or extended surface areas, providing continuous sealing or power transmission rather than a single point of contact. This feature improves fluid handling efficiency and reduces leakage.
[F04C 2/3447] the vanes having the form of rollers, slippers or the like -- In rotary-piston positive-displacement pumps, the moving vanes are shaped as rollers, slippers or similar forms that roll or slide along the cylinder wall, reducing friction and improving sealing. This design is used to transfer liquids efficiently in devices such as rotary-piston pumps and engines.
[F04C 2/3448] with axially movable vanes -- The subclass F04C 2/3448 covers rotary-piston liquid displacement machines in which the vanes move axially within the rotor. These machines use axially-movable vanes to create chambers that pump fluid as the rotor turns. It falls under positive-displacement pumps in mechanical engineering.
[F04C 2/348] the vanes positively engaging, with circumferential play, an outer rotatable member -- This subclass relates to rotary-piston or vane pumps where the vanes are positively engaged with an outer rotating member yet permitted limited circumferential play. The design creates a sliding seal as the rotor turns, allowing positive-displacement of liquid through the machine.
[F04C 2/352] the vanes being pivoted on the axis of the outer member -- This subclass concerns rotary-piston pumps in which the vanes are hinged to the rotor and swing about the axis of the outer housing. The pivoted vanes follow the eccentric motion of the rotor, maintaining sealing against the casing while allowing variable chamber volume. The arrangement improves efficiency and reduces wear in positive-displacement liquid machines.
[F04C 2/356] with vanes reciprocating with respect to the outer member -- The code covers positive-displacement rotary-piston pumps in which internal vanes move back and forth against the housing to produce fluid flow. It includes designs where the vanes slide or oscillate relative to the outer rotating member, maintaining sealing and changing volume.
[F04C 2/3562] the inner and outer member being in contact along one line or continuous surface substantially parallel to the axis of rotation -- This subclass of rotary-piston (positive-displacement) machines for liquids defines designs in which the inner rotor and the surrounding outer member touch each other along a single line or across a continuous surface that runs substantially parallel to the axis of rotation, typically forming a sealing or bearing interface.
[F04C 2/3564] the surfaces of the inner and outer member, forming the working space, being surfaces of revolution -- This CPC subgroup F04C 2/3564 concerns rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids, specifically the geometry where the inner and outer members that define the working chamber are shaped as surfaces of revolution. The classification covers design features of these curved surfaces that guide fluid flow and piston motion within the pump.
[F04C 2/3566] the inner and outer member being in contact along more than one line or surface -- F04C 2/3566 covers rotary-piston liquid pumps where the inner rotor and outer stator are in continuous contact along multiple lines or surfaces rather than a single line. This design provides improved sealing and torque transmission in positive-displacement machines.
[F04C 2/3568] with axially movable vanes -- F04C 2/3568 classifies rotary-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids that use vanes able to slide along the rotor axis. The axially movable vanes adjust to changes in chamber volume, enhancing sealing, reducing leakage and improving pump efficiency.
[F04C 2/36] having both the movements defined in groups -- Rotary-piston pumps that incorporate both of the motion types defined in the preceding groups, typically a combination of rotational and reciprocating or oscillating movement to transfer liquid. The design allows simultaneous axial and circumferential motion of the piston, improving flow characteristics.
[F04C 2/38] having the movement defined in group -- The CPC subclass F04C 2/38 covers rotary-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids where the type of piston movement-such as rotation, oscillation, or combined motion-is defined in a higher-level group. It includes pumps and compressors that use a rotating or oscillating piston whose specific kinematic principle is specified elsewhere in the classification hierarchy.
[F04C 2/39] with vanes hinged to the inner as well as to the outer member -- This subgroup of the CPC covers rotary-piston positive-displacement pumps in which the vanes are hinged both to the inner rotor and to the surrounding outer member, allowing the blades to pivot as they move between the two surfaces to maintain a seal during fluid displacement.
[F04C 2/40] having the movement defined in group -- F04C 2/40 covers rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids whose motion is defined in the preceding group. The class includes devices where the piston follows the specified movement pattern, such as rotating or reciprocating actions within the pump housing.
[F04C 2/44] with vanes hinged to the inner member -- F04C 2/44 covers rotary-piston positive-displacement pumps in which the vanes are hinged to the inner rotating member, allowing each vane to swing outward as the rotor turns to maintain contact with the housing. The hinged design improves sealing and accommodates variations in clearance, making it suitable for pumping liquids under varying pressure.
[F04C 2/46] with vanes hinged to the outer member -- F04C 2/46 classifies rotary-piston positive-displacement machines for liquids that employ vanes hinged to the outer housing. The hinged vanes pivot as the rotor turns, improving sealing and flow control in pumps and similar devices.
[F04C 25/00] Adaptations of pumps for special use of pumps for elastic fluids -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Adaptations of pumps for special use of pumps for elastic fluids.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Combination of engines with pumps or compressors driven thereby -> F01C13/04
[F04C 25/02] for producing high vacuum -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Pumps adapted for working at pressures below atmospheric pressure.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Sealing arrangements -> F04C27/00 - Silencing -> F04C29/06
[F04C 27/00] Sealing arrangements in rotary-piston pumps specially adapted for elastic fluids -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Any seal construction or mode not provided by the groups, e.g. labyrinth.
[F04C 27/001] Radial sealings for working fluid -- F04C 27/001 covers radial sealing arrangements in rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement pumps designed for elastic fluids such as hydraulic oil. The classification deals with seals that are positioned radially to contain the working fluid within the pump chamber.
[F04C 27/002] of rigid material -- This subclass covers sealing solutions used in rotary-piston or oscillating-piston positive-displacement pumps that handle elastic liquids such as oils or hydraulic fluids, where the sealing element is made of a rigid material rather than elastomers. The rigid seals are designed to withstand high pressures and wear while maintaining a leak-tight connection between the rotating piston and pump housing.
[F04C 27/003] of resilient material -- The CPC subclass F04C 27/003 covers sealing arrangements in rotary-piston positive-displacement pumps that are specially designed for elastic fluids such as oils or polymers. It focuses on seals made of resilient or elastomeric material to accommodate deformation and maintain tightness.
[F04C 27/004] Radial sealing elements specially adapted for intermeshing-engagement type pumps, e.g. gear pumps -- The classification covers radial sealing components designed for intermeshing-engagement pumps such as gear pumps, where the seals are fitted around the gear teeth to prevent leakage of elastic fluids. These seals are adapted to the geometry of the meshing gears and provide a tight, wear-resistant barrier in rotary-piston pump assemblies.
