It includes configurations where aerodynamic fans provide airflow for cooling, deaeration or to boost pump performance within mechanical-engineering applications.
[F04D 27/00] Control, e.g. regulation, of pumps, pumping installations or pumping systems specially adapted for elastic fluids -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Systems and schemes for controlling a pump for elastic fluids, either matching the pump to the requirements of the system or avoiding harmful conditions for the pump, e.g. surge or stall conditions.
[F04D 27/001] Testing thereof; Determination or simulation of flow characteristics; Stall or surge detection, e.g. condition monitoring -- The CPC subclass F04D 27/001 relates to testing non-positive-displacement pumps used with elastic fluids, covering methods and devices for measuring or simulating flow characteristics and for detecting stall or surge, including condition-monitoring techniques.
[F04D 27/002] by varying geometry within the pumps, e.g. by adjusting vanes -- F04D 27/002 covers methods of controlling pumps that handle elastic fluids by changing the pump's internal geometry, such as adjusting the position or shape of vanes, to regulate flow or pressure.
[F04D 27/003] by throttling  F04D27/002  takes precedence
[F04D 27/004] by varying driving speed -- F04D 27/004 covers control methods for non-positive-displacement pumps handling elastic fluids that adjust the pump's output by varying the driving speed, such as changing motor rpm to regulate flow or pressure. This approach is used in systems where the fluid's compressibility requires precise speed-based modulation of the pumping operation.
[F04D 27/005] by changing flow path between different stages or between a plurality of compressors; Load distribution between compressors
[F04D 27/006] by influencing fluid temperatures -- This subclass concerns control of non-positive-displacement pumps for elastic fluids by modifying the temperature of the fluid. It includes methods, devices or system arrangements that deliberately heat or cool the pumped liquid to influence its viscosity, density or flow behaviour, thereby achieving regulated pump performance.
[F04D 27/007] Conjoint control of two or more different functions -- F04D 27/007 covers systems that jointly control two or more distinct functions of non-positive-displacement pumps used for moving elastic fluids, such as coordinated regulation of flow rate, pressure, or speed within a pumping installation. The classification applies to integrated control schemes that manage multiple pump parameters simultaneously.
[F04D 27/008] Stop safety or alarm devices, e.g. stop-and-go control; Disposition of check-valves -- The CPC class F04D 27/008 concerns safety or alarm mechanisms that halt or regulate pump operation, especially in systems handling elastic fluids. It includes stop-and-go controls and the arrangement or disposition of check-valves used to protect or signal pump installations.
[F04D 27/009] by bleeding, by passing or recycling fluid -- F04D 27/009 relates to control methods for non-positive-displacement pumps that handle elastic fluids, specifically by bleeding, passing or recycling fluid within the system. These techniques regulate pump operation or pressure by deliberately diverting part of the flow back into the pump or through a bleed line.
[F04D 27/02] Surge control -- F04D 27/02 covers devices and methods for controlling pressure surges in pumping systems that handle elastic fluids. It includes regulation techniques for non-positive-displacement pumps and related installations to prevent or mitigate surge phenomena.
[F04D 27/0207] by bleeding, bypassing or recycling fluids  influencing the boundary layer by an uncontrolled bleeding of the working fluid  F04D29/681 -- This subclass deals with methods for controlling pumps or pumping systems handling elastic fluids by bleeding, bypassing or recycling the working fluid, especially when uncontrolled bleeding alters the boundary layer of the fluid flow.
[F04D 27/0215] Arrangements therefor, e.g. bleed or by-pass valves -- This subclass covers the design of control devices for non-positive-displacement pumps handling elastic fluids, such as arrangements for bleed or by-pass valves that regulate the pump or pumping system.
[F04D 27/0223] Control schemes therefor -- A control scheme for non-positive-displacement pumps that handle elastic fluids, encompassing methods and devices that regulate flow, pressure, speed or power of the pump or its system, often using sensors, feedback loops or automatic adjustments to maintain desired operating conditions.
[F04D 27/023] Details or means for fluid extraction -- F04D 27/023 concerns devices or methods that control or regulate the extraction of fluid from pumping systems that handle elastic liquids such as hydraulic fluids in non-positive-displacement pump arrangements. It includes details of control means, sensors, actuators and other features that manage fluid withdrawal and system performance.
[F04D 27/0238] Details or means for fluid reinjection -- F04D 27/0238 covers components and methods used to re-inject fluid back into a system, such as valves, pipelines or control devices that manage the return flow of elastic liquids in pumping installations. It falls under the non-positive-displacement pump category within mechanical engineering, focusing on regulation of pump operation for elastic fluids.
[F04D 27/0246] by varying geometry within the pumps, e.g. by adjusting vanes -- This CPC subclass covers control of pumps handling elastic fluids by varying pump geometry, such as adjusting vanes, within non-positive displacement pumps. It falls under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids, non-positive displacement pumps.
[F04D 27/0253] by throttling  F04D27/0246  takes precedence -- This subgroup deals with controlling non-positive-displacement pumps that handle elastic fluids by using throttling, i.e., adjusting a restriction valve or similar device in the pump circuit to regulate flow or pressure. The prior subgroup F04D27/0246 takes precedence over this entry.
[F04D 27/0261] by varying driving speed -- This subclass covers methods for controlling pumps or pumping systems designed for elastic fluids by adjusting the speed of the drive motor, thereby influencing flow or pressure. It falls under non-positive-displacement pumps in the mechanical engineering field.
[F04D 27/0269] by changing flow path between different stages or between a plurality of compressors; load distribution between compressors -- The F04D 27/0269 subclass covers methods for regulating pumping systems handling elastic fluids by altering the flow path among different stages or among multiple compressors, thereby distributing load across the compressors to achieve desired performance. It falls under non-positive-displacement pumps within mechanical engineering.
[F04D 27/0276] by influencing fluid temperature -- F04D 27/0276 covers control of non-positive-displacement pumps and pumping installations for elastic fluids by influencing the fluid's temperature, such as heating or cooling mechanisms that adjust pump performance.
[F04D 27/0284] Conjoint control of two or more different functions -- F04D 27/0284 concerns the integrated control of two or more distinct functions in pump installations that handle elastic fluids, such as simultaneous regulation of flow, pressure, temperature or speed, using coordinated control mechanisms to improve performance and safety.
[F04D 27/0292] Stop safety or alarm devices, e.g. stop-and-go control; Disposition of check-valves -- This subclass covers safety and alarm devices used to control pumps or pumping systems handling elastic fluids, such as stop-and-go control mechanisms and devices that manage the operation or positioning of check-valves. It falls under non-positive-displacement pump control in mechanical engineering.
[F04D 29/00] Details, component parts, or accessories -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Details, component parts, or accessories of any type of pump for elastic or non elastic fluids.
[F04D 29/002] especially adapted for elastic fluid pumps -- F04D 29/002 is a subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification for mechanical engineering, covering non-positive-displacement pumps. It pertains to details, component parts or accessories that are especially adapted for pumps handling elastic fluids such as water, oil, or other compressible liquids. This includes specialized seals, housings, or fittings designed to accommodate the elasticity of the fluid during pumping.
[F04D 29/005] Decorative aspects, i.e. features which have no effect on the functioning of the pump -- F04D 29/005 pertains to decorative features of non-positive-displacement pumps, covering any ornamental or aesthetic elements that do not influence the pump's operation. It falls under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids, non-positive-displacement pumps, and specifically details, component parts, or accessories.
[F04D 29/007] especially adapted for liquid pumps -- F04D 29/007 covers component parts or accessories that are specially adapted for liquid pumps, particularly for non-positive-displacement types. It includes items such as specialized housings, seals, bearings or couplings designed to improve pump performance, installation or maintenance.
[F04D 29/02] Selection of particular materials -- F04D 29/02 pertains to the choice of specific materials for components or accessories of non-positive-displacement pumps used in positive-displacement liquid machines. It addresses material selection criteria such as corrosion resistance, durability, and performance for pump parts.
[F04D 29/023] especially adapted for elastic fluid pumps -- F04D 29/023 covers the details, component parts and accessories that are specially designed for elastic-fluid pumps, a type of non-positive-displacement pump used to move liquids without mechanical displacement. The entry falls under mechanical engineering, specifically positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04D 29/026] especially adapted for liquid pumps -- This subclass (F04D 29/026) deals with components, accessories and detailed parts that are specially adapted for liquid pumps, focusing on non-positive displacement pump types. It covers modifications that enhance installation, operation or maintenance of such pumps.
[F04D 29/04] Shafts or bearings, or assemblies thereof -- F04D 29/04 pertains to the shafts, bearings and their assemblies used in non-positive-displacement pumps, a subgroup of positive-displacement machines for liquids within mechanical engineering. The code covers detailed component parts and accessories related to these rotating and supporting elements.
[F04D 29/0405] joining shafts, e.g. rigid couplings, quill shafts -- The group F04D29/0405 is no longer used for the classification of new documents as from July 1st, 2007. The backlog of this group is being continuously reclassified to F04D29/044 and F04D29/054
[F04D 29/041] Axial thrust balancing -- Axial thrust balancing comprises design features that counteract the axial forces produced by a pump's rotating impeller, using thrust plates, bearing arrangements or hydraulic balancing mechanisms to minimise wear, reduce vibration and improve efficiency in non-positive-displacement pumps.
[F04D 29/0413] hydrostatic; hydrodynamic thrust bearings -- F04D 29/0413 relates to thrust bearings that generate axial load support by hydrostatic or hydrodynamic film pressure, used in pumps and other fluid-handling equipment. The entry falls under mechanical engineering, non-positive-displacement pump components and accessories.
[F04D 29/0416] balancing pistons -- F04D 29/0416 covers balancing pistons used in non-positive-displacement pumps, i.e., devices that equalise forces on pistons to reduce vibration and improve efficiency. It relates to components and accessories for these pump types.
[F04D 29/042] Axially shiftable rotors -- Axially shiftable rotors are rotor components that can be moved longitudinally to vary the clearance or volume between rotor and housing, allowing adjustment of fluid displacement in non-positive-displacement pump assemblies. The classification covers their design, operation and related accessories within mechanical engineering pump technology.
[F04D 29/043] Shafts -- The subclass F04D 29/043 covers shafts that are part of non-positive-displacement pump assemblies, detailing their design, construction, and related accessories. It includes rotating shafts used to transmit power or motion within these pumps, focusing on the component itself rather than the overall pump mechanism.
[F04D 29/044] Arrangements for joining or assembling shafts -- This subclass covers designs and methods for connecting shafts in pump assemblies, such as couplings, keying, splines and other mechanical means to transmit torque, used in non-positive-displacement pump units.
[F04D 29/046] Bearings -- This CPC subclass covers bearings that are incorporated in non-positive-displacement pump assemblies, including the design, types and accessories that support pump shafts and rotating elements. It falls under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids, non-positive-displacement pumps, details, component parts, or accessories.
[F04D 29/0462] Bearing cartridges -- Bearing cartridges are self-contained bearing assemblies that support rotating shafts in pumps, providing lubrication, alignment and easy replacement. In the CPC they fall under F04D 29/0462, covering components and accessories for non-positive-displacement pumps within the broader class of positive-displacement machines for liquids.

[F04D 29/048] magnetic -- F04D 29/048 covers magnetic elements that are part of non-positive-displacement pump accessories, such as magnetic couplings, seals, or drive devices used to transmit torque to the pump without direct mechanical contact. [F04D 29/05] Shafts or bearings, or assemblies thereof, specially adapted for elastic fluid pumps -- F04D 29/05 covers shafts, bearings, and related assemblies that are specifically designed for elastic fluid pumps such as diaphragm or rubber-bellows pumps. The code pertains to component-level details for non-positive displacement pump designs within the mechanical engineering domain.
[F04D 29/051] Axial thrust balancing -- This subgroup covers designs and components that counteract axial forces generated by impellers in non-positive-displacement pumps, such as thrust bearings, balancing pads or adjustable shrouds, aimed at reducing wear and improving efficiency.
[F04D 29/0513] hydrostatic; hydrodynamic thrust bearings -- Hydrostatic and hydrodynamic thrust bearings are specialized bearing assemblies that support axial loads in non-positive-displacement pumps by creating a thin film of liquid between moving surfaces. In hydrostatic versions the film is maintained by external pressure, while hydrodynamic types generate the film through relative motion of the surfaces, reducing wear and improving pump efficiency.
[F04D 29/0516] balancing pistons -- Balancing pistons are auxiliary components used in non-positive-displacement pumps to equalize forces on the pump pistons, reducing vibration and wear. They are installed within the pump housing and help maintain smooth operation of the displacement mechanism.
[F04D 29/052] Axially shiftable rotors -- F04D 29/052 covers axially shiftable rotors used in non-positive displacement pumps, describing rotor designs that can move along the pump axis to convey liquids. It pertains to details of component parts or accessories related to these rotors.
[F04D 29/053] Shafts -- F04D 29/053 covers shafts that transmit torque within non-positive displacement pumps, including the rotating axles that drive impellers or other moving elements. The entry also includes related bearings and couplings that are integral to the pump's operation but not the housing itself.
[F04D 29/054] Arrangements for joining or assembling shafts -- This subclass concerns the mechanical solutions used to connect or assemble shafts in non-positive-displacement pumps. It includes couplings, splines, keyways, adhesives, and other joining methods that secure the rotating shafts of pump components. The focus is on the structural arrangements that enable reliable torque transmission within the pump system.
[F04D 29/056] Bearings -- Bearings for non-positive displacement pumps are component parts that support the rotating shafts of centrifugal, axial-flow or mixed-flow pumps, handling radial and axial loads while allowing high-speed rotation. They include journal, sleeve, ball, roller or magnetic types designed for continuous liquid-handling operation.
[F04D 29/0563] Bearings cartridges -- F04D 29/0563 covers cartridge-type bearings used in pumps and other hydraulic equipment, describing designs that integrate bearing elements into a replaceable cartridge for ease of maintenance and sealing. It applies to components of non-positive-displacement pumps and related accessories where the bearing cartridge can be serviced without dismantling the entire pump.
[F04D 29/0566] Ceramic bearing designs -- F04D 29/0566 covers the design of ceramic bearings used in non-positive-displacement pump components and accessories. The classification addresses material selection, geometry and lubrication considerations that improve wear resistance and performance in fluid-moving machinery.
[F04D 29/057] hydrostatic -- F04D 29/057 refers to hydrostatic systems, covering the design and construction of hydrostatic pumps and related components or accessories that operate without positive displacement. [F04D 29/102] especially adapted for elastic fluid pumps -- This subclass covers components or accessories specially adapted for elastic-fluid pumps, a type of non-positive-displacement pump that moves liquid by flexing a diaphragm or bladder. [F04D 29/104] the sealing fluid being other than the working fluid or being the working fluid treated -- The CPC subclass F04D 29/104 covers non-positive-displacement pumps in which the sealing fluid differs from the liquid being pumped, or the sealing fluid is the same liquid after it has been treated. It relates to the design of seals, barriers or auxiliary circuits that isolate the working fluid from the pump housing. [F04D 29/168] of an axial flow wheel -- The entry covers axial-flow wheels used as components or accessories in non-positive-displacement pumps, which move fluid by imparting kinetic energy with a rotating impeller that directs flow parallel to the shaft. It falls under CPC section F04D (positive-displacement machines for liquids) and subclass 29/168 specifying details of axial-flow wheels.
[F04D 29/18] Rotors -- Rotors are rotating components used in non-positive-displacement pumps to move liquid by generating pressure differences as they turn. They form part of the pump's internal mechanism, converting mechanical energy into fluid flow, and are classified under CPC code F04D 29/18 as accessories or detailed parts of such pumps.
[F04D 29/181] Axial flow rotors  F04D29/185  take precedence -- The CPC subclass F04D 29/181 covers axial flow rotors that are details, component parts, or accessories for non-positive-displacement pumps. These rotors impart fluid motion primarily in the axial direction and are used in pump designs that move liquid without creating a sealed volume. More specific aspects of such rotors are treated in the narrower subclass F04D 29/185, which takes precedence over this entry.
[F04D 29/183] Semi axial flow rotors -- Semi axial flow rotors are component parts of non-positive-displacement pumps in which the rotor imparts fluid motion both axially along the shaft and radially outward, creating a mixed-flow pattern that improves pressure rise and efficiency in applications such as marine and industrial pump systems.
[F04D 29/185] Rotors consisting of a plurality of wheels -- This CPC subclass covers rotors that consist of multiple wheel elements used in non-positive-displacement pumps such as centrifugal pumps. It details the design of rotor assemblies formed from a plurality of wheels.
[F04D 29/186] Shaftless rotors  F04D13/024  takes precedence -- Shaftless rotors are rotor assemblies for non-positive-displacement pumps that operate without a central shaft, covering their design, construction and accessory components. The classification addresses details such as bearing arrangements, housing integration and drive mechanisms specific to these rotors, and is subordinate to the broader F04D13/024 category.
[F04D 29/188] specially for regenerative pumps -- F04D 29/188 covers components, parts and accessories specially designed for regenerative pumps, a type of non-positive-displacement pump used to move liquids. The subclass is nested under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids, and non-positive-displacement pumps.
[F04D 29/20] Mounting rotors on shafts -- The CPC subclass F04D 29/20 concerns the mounting of rotors on shafts in non-positive-displacement pump assemblies. It covers designs, fittings and accessories that secure a rotor to a rotating shaft, ensuring proper alignment and torque transmission in liquid displacement machines.
[F04D 29/22] specially for centrifugal pumps -- F04D 29/22 designates items that are details, component parts or accessories specially intended for centrifugal pumps, which are non-positive-displacement machines used to move liquids. It falls under the hierarchy Mechanical Engineering → Positive-Displacement Machines for Liquids → Non-Positive-Displacement Pumps, and covers any specialized features or fittings that improve pump performance or integration.
[F04D 29/2205] Conventional flow pattern  F04D29/18  takes precedence -- The CPC subclass F04D 29/2205 pertains to details, components, or accessories of non-positive-displacement pumps that employ a conventional flow pattern for moving liquids. It covers design features that guide fluid in the usual manner typical of such pumps, without involving positive displacement mechanisms.
[F04D 29/2211] More than one set of flow passages -- Covers pump designs that employ two-or-more distinct flow passages within the pump body, such as parallel channels, split volutes or manifold systems, allowing separate or simultaneous fluid streams. The classification includes the arrangement of these passages and related components that direct flow, improve efficiency or enable handling of different liquids.
[F04D 29/2216] Shape, geometry  F04D29/2211  takes precedence -- F04D 29/2216 covers the shape and geometric features of non-positive-displacement pump components and accessories. It pertains to design aspects such as cavity profile, rotor contours, and inlet/outlet geometry that affect fluid handling without relying on positive displacement. The subclass follows F04D 29/2211 which deals with related dimensional aspects.
[F04D 29/2222] Construction and assembly  F04D29/2211  takes precedence -- F04D 29/2222 designates the construction and assembly of non-positive-displacement pumps, including their component parts and accessories. It falls under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids, and non-positive-displacement pumps. This subclass is subordinated to F04D 29/2211, which takes precedence.
[F04D 29/2227] for special materials -- The subclass F04D 29/2227 covers details, component parts or accessories of non-positive-displacement pumps that are made from or designed for use with special materials, such as corrosion-resistant alloys, ceramics or polymers, where the material properties are essential to the pump's performance or durability. It is part of the mechanical engineering group for positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04D 29/2233] entirely open or stamped from one sheet -- F04D 29/2233 covers components for non-positive-displacement pumps that are manufactured as a single, uninterrupted sheet, either left completely open or produced by stamping the sheet into shape. Such parts include housings, covers, or impeller shells formed without separate assembled pieces.
[F04D 29/2238] Special flow patterns  F04D11/005  takes precedence -- F04D 29/2238 is a CPC subclass that covers details, component parts or accessories of non-positive-displacement pumps that create special flow patterns in the liquid being moved. It focuses on design features that produce unusual or engineered fluid motion, such as swirl, pulsation or vortex generation, and is subordinate to the more specific subclass F04D 11/005, which takes precedence.
[F04D 29/2244] Free vortex -- F04D 29/2244 covers pumps that create a free-vortex flow, where the fluid circulates around a central low-pressure region without solid boundaries shaping the vortex. It relates to non-positive-displacement pumps and includes design details, component parts and accessories for generating this vortex motion.
[F04D 29/225] Channel wheels, e.g. one blade or one flow channel -- F04D 29/225 covers channel wheels, such as a single blade or a single flow channel, used in non-positive-displacement pumps for moving liquids. These components direct fluid through a rotating wheel to create flow without trapping a fixed volume.
[F04D 29/2255] flow-channels with a special cross-section contour, e.g. ejecting, throttling or diffusing effect -- F04D 29/2255 classifies flow channels whose cross-sectional shape is designed to produce ejecting, throttling or diffusing effects in non-positive-displacement pumps. The code applies to detailed components or accessories that modify fluid dynamics through special contouring of the conduit.
[F04D 29/2261] with special measures -- The CPC subclass F04D 29/2261 covers components, parts or accessories of non-positive-displacement pumps that incorporate special measures such as safety devices, control or monitoring features, or other specific functional adaptations.
[F04D 29/2266] for sealing or thrust balance  F04D29/04  and  F04D29/16  take precedence -- F04D 29/2266 covers component parts or accessories of non-positive displacement pumps that provide sealing or thrust-balancing functions, and it takes precedence over the broader subclasses F04D 29/04 and F04D 29/16. This subclass falls under the hierarchy Mechanical Engineering, Positive-displacement machines for liquids, Non-positive-displacement pumps, Details, component parts, or accessories.
[F04D 29/2272] for influencing flow or boundary layer -- The CPC subclass F04D 29/2272 refers to components for non-positive-displacement pumps that are intended to influence the fluid flow or the boundary layer, such as flow straighteners, diffusers, vortex generators or surface-treating devices. It is a detailed part or accessory within the mechanical engineering group for positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04D 29/2277] for increasing NPSH or dealing with liquids near boiling-point -- This subgroup covers devices or accessories for non-positive-displacement pumps that raise net positive suction head (NPSH) or cope with liquids close to their boiling point, such as cavitation-preventing boosters, pre-heaters, pressure-raising fittings or other components that improve suction conditions and avoid vaporisation in the pump.
[F04D 29/2283] for reverse pumping action -- The CPC subclass F04D 29/2283 relates to parts, components or accessories of non-positive-displacement pumps that are specifically designed for reverse pumping action, enabling the pump to move fluid in the opposite direction of its normal flow. It covers features such as reversible impellers, reverse-flow valves and other adaptations that allow the pump to operate effectively when driven backwards.
[F04D 29/2288] for comminuting, mixing or separating -- F04D 29/2288 covers details, component parts, or accessories of non-positive-displacement pumps that are designed for comminuting, mixing or separating liquids. It is classified under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids, non-positive-displacement pumps.
[F04D 29/2294] for protection, e.g. against abrasion -- This subclass deals with protective measures for components of non-positive-displacement pumps, including wear-resistant linings, coatings or shields that guard pump parts against abrasion and similar damage.
[F04D 29/24] Vanes -- The code covers vanes that serve as component parts or accessories in non-positive-displacement pumps, such as centrifugal or axial-flow machines. It pertains to the design, shaping, mounting or material of the vanes that impart kinetic energy to the liquid.
[F04D 29/242] Geometry, shape -- The F04D 29/242 subclass covers the geometry and shape of parts and accessories used in non-positive-displacement pumps for liquids, such as housings, impellers, volutes and related components. It specifies design features that affect fluid flow and pump performance within the broader category of mechanical engineering pump technology.
[F04D 29/245] for special effects -- F04D 29/245 designates accessories, components or details of non-positive-displacement pumps that are used to generate special hydraulic effects, such as pulsating flow, pressure surges, fluid mixing or other purposeful variations in the pump's performance. It covers devices integrated with or added to pumps to achieve these specific functional outcomes.
[F04D 29/247] elastic or self-adjusting -- F04D 29/247 covers components and accessories for non-positive-displacement pumps that are elastic or self-adjusting, such as flexible diaphragms, membranes or spring-loaded elements that adapt to pressure changes and maintain sealing. These parts enable the pump to accommodate variations in flow or volume without relying on fixed-geometry mechanisms.
[F04D 29/26] Rotors specially for elastic fluids -- F04D 29/26 covers rotors that are specifically adapted for use with elastic or compressible fluids in non-positive-displacement pumps. It includes designs where rotor geometry or material selection addresses the fluid's elasticity, such as in hydraulic or pneumatic rotary pumps, and relates to component-level details rather than complete pump assemblies.
[F04D 29/263] mounting fan or blower rotors on shafts -- The F04D 29/263 subgroup covers the methods and devices for attaching fan or blower rotors to rotating shafts, including hubs, keys, splines, brackets and other fastening arrangements. It pertains to component-level solutions for non-positive-displacement pump systems.
[F04D 29/266] mounting compressor rotors on shafts -- F04D 29/266 addresses the mounting of compressor rotors onto shafts, covering the design of fittings, couplings and other attachment components that secure rotor assemblies in non-positive-displacement pump systems.
[F04D 29/28] for centrifugal or helico-centrifugal pumps -- F04D 29/28 covers the details, component parts and accessories of centrifugal and helico-centrifugal pumps, which are non-positive-displacement machines that move liquids by rotation. The classification falls under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids, and non-positive-displacement pumps.
[F04D 29/281] for fans or blowers -- The code F04D 29/281 covers accessories or component parts that are used specifically with fans or blowers. It falls under the non-positive-displacement pump category of mechanical engineering, pertaining to details and auxiliary elements for these air-moving devices.
[F04D 29/282] the leading edge of each vane being substantially parallel to the rotation axis -- A non-positive-displacement pump element in which each vane's leading edge is oriented substantially parallel to the pump's rotation axis, defining a specific detail of the vane geometry used to guide fluid flow in the pump.
[F04D 29/283] rotors of the squirrel-cage type -- This subclass pertains to rotors built in the squirrel-cage form and used as components of non-positive-displacement pumps, such as centrifugal or axial-flow pumps. The rotor consists of conductive bars short-circuited by end rings, forming a simple, rugged rotor that interacts with a stator's magnetic field to drive the pump.
[F04D 29/284] for compressors -- F04D 29/284 classifies the details, component parts and accessories that are specifically used in compressors, which are non-positive-displacement machines for moving fluids. It covers items such as valves, seals, bearings, drive couplings and control devices that are integral to the construction or operation of a compressor.
[F04D 29/285] the compressor wheel comprising a pair of rotatable bladed hub portions axially aligned and clamped together -- This classification covers a compressor wheel made up of two axially aligned rotatable bladed hub portions that are clamped together, forming a detailed component of a non-positive-displacement pump. It is listed under F04D 29/285 in the Cooperative Patent Classification for mechanical engineering.
[F04D 29/286] multi-stage rotors -- Multi-stage rotors are rotor assemblies arranged in series within a pump to successively raise fluid pressure. Each stage adds head by transferring kinetic energy to the liquid, allowing compact designs that achieve high discharge pressures. They are used as components or accessories in non-positive-displacement pumping machines.

[F04D 29/30] Vanes -- F04D 29/30 covers vanes used as components or accessories in non-positive-displacement pumps for moving liquids. It relates to mechanical engineering applications where vanes form part of the pump's internal geometry, providing flow guidance or sealing functions.
[F04D 29/305] Flexible vanes -- Flexible vanes are bendable blade elements used as part of non-positive-displacement pump designs, serving as moving or stationary components that shape the pumping chamber and guide fluid flow. They are typically mounted on a rotor or stator and can deform to maintain contact with the housing, improving seal and efficiency in detail-level pump assemblies.
[F04D 29/32] for axial flow pumps -- F04D 29/32 covers axial flow pumps, a subclass of non-positive-displacement pumps that move liquids by directing flow parallel to the shaft axis. It includes the details, component parts, and accessories specific to these axial flow machines.
[F04D 29/321] for axial flow compressors -- F04D 29/321 covers the details, component parts and accessories of axial-flow compressors, which are non-positive-displacement pumps that move liquids or gases by directing flow parallel to the shaft. The subclass includes blades, diffusers, casings, sealing and mounting elements specifically designed for axial-type compression machinery.
[F04D 29/322] Blade mountings -- F04D 29/322 covers the design and attachment of blades in non-positive-displacement pumps, detailing components such as brackets, shafts and fastening devices that secure impeller or propeller blades within the pump housing.
[F04D 29/323] adjustable -- F04D 29/323 classifies adjustable details, component parts or accessories for non-positive-displacement pumps, which fall under mechanical engineering's positive-displacement machines for liquids. The subclass covers any adjustable elements that modify pump performance, mounting or operation.
[F04D 29/324] Blades -- The class F04D 29/324 covers blades that are part of the detailed components or accessories of non-positive-displacement pumps, such as the blades forming an impeller or other rotating elements that move liquid without a fixed volume per revolution.
[F04D 29/325] for axial flow fans  blade mountings  F04D29/34 , blades  F04D29/38 -- F04D 29/325 relates to the detailed design of blade mountings and the blades themselves for axial-flow fans used in fluid-moving equipment, specifically within non-positive-displacement pump systems. It falls under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids, and the subgroup covering details, component parts, or accessories.
[F04D 29/326] comprising a rotating shroud -- F04D 29/326 covers components or accessories of non-positive-displacement liquid pumps that include a rotating shroud, used to guide fluid flow or protect moving parts. It falls under the mechanical engineering subclass for positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04D 29/327] with non identical blades -- This subclass relates to non-positive-displacement pumps and covers details, component parts or accessories where the impeller blades are not identical, such as mixed-size, staggered or otherwise varied blade designs that influence the pump's flow and performance.
[F04D 29/328] with unequal distribution of blades around the hub -- This subclass covers non-positive-displacement pumps and their components in which the impeller or rotor blades are not evenly spaced around the hub, resulting in an unequal angular distribution of blades. The arrangement is defined as a detail or accessory of the pump that influences flow characteristics, efficiency or balancing, and is grouped under mechanical-engineering positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04D 29/329] Details of the hub -- F04D 29/329 covers the design and construction details of a pump hub, including its shape, attachment, bearing arrangements, sealing features and related accessories that affect the hub's function within non-positive-displacement liquid pumps. It addresses the specific geometries and mounting methods used to connect the hub to impellers or other rotating elements.
[F04D 29/34] Blade mountings -- F04D 29/34 relates to the design and securing of blades in liquid-handling machines, covering mounting elements that attach, support or adjust impeller or rotor blades in pumps and similar equipment. It includes brackets, hubs, clamping devices and other accessories that hold blades in place during operation.
[F04D 29/36] adjustable -- F04D 29/36 covers adjustable details, component parts, or accessories for non-positive-displacement pumps, enabling the pump's output or characteristics to be varied during operation. Examples include adjustable impellers, variable-stroke mechanisms, or interchangeable vanes that modify flow rate or pressure.
[F04D 29/362] during rotation -- F04D 29/362 covers the design and construction of components or accessories of non-positive displacement pumps that operate or are subjected to forces while the pump is rotating, such as rotating shafts, impellers, balance mechanisms, seals and bearing arrangements.
[F04D 29/364] The blades having only a predetermined number of possible positions -- F04D 29/364 covers blades in non-positive-displacement pumps that can be set in only a limited, predefined number of positions. The classification applies to the design, adjustment mechanisms, and accessories that restrict blade movement to these fixed settings, ensuring consistent flow characteristics in such pumps.
[F04D 29/366] Adjustment by interaction of inertion and lift -- The F04D 29/366 code refers to pumps with adjustment by interaction of inertia and lift. This classification is for non-positive-displacement pumps. It is a subset of mechanical engineering.
[F04D 29/368] Adjustment by differences of temperature -- This class concerns components or accessories for non-positive-displacement pumps in which the pump's output is regulated by temperature-induced changes. It includes designs that use thermal expansion of parts or variations in fluid viscosity to adjust flow, allowing pump performance to be controlled through differences of temperature.
[F04D 29/38] Blades -- This CPC subclass F04D 29/38 relates to the blades that form part of non-positive-displacement liquid pumps, such as impeller or runner blades. It covers the design, shape, material and other details of these blade components used to move fluid in pump assemblies.
[F04D 29/382] Flexible blades -- The CPC class F04D 29/382 covers flexible blades used as component parts or accessories in non-positive-displacement pumps, such as impellers or rotor blades that can deform under load to move fluid. These blades are typically made of elastomeric or composite materials and are designed to adapt shape for efficient fluid handling.
[F04D 29/384] characterised by form -- F04D 29/384 is a CPC subclass for non-positive-displacement pumps that focuses on details, component parts, or accessories whose design is defined primarily by their form or shape. It groups pumps whose functional classification depends on geometric characteristics rather than operating principles.
[F04D 29/386] Skewed blades -- F04D 29/386 classifies skewed or twisted blades used as components or accessories in non-positive-displacement liquid pumps, a sub-group of mechanical engineering for positive-displacement machines. The code covers blade designs that are angled to modify flow direction, improve efficiency, and reduce pulsation.
[F04D 29/388] characterised by construction -- F04D 29/388 covers the structural design of components and accessories for non-positive-displacement pumps such as gear, screw or vane types. It includes details of the construction of housings, rotors, shafts and related parts that affect the pump's operation.
[F04D 29/40] Casings -- F04D 29/40 concerns the casings of non-positive-displacement pumps, encompassing the housings and surrounding structures that contain the pump's working parts. The classification covers design, materials, sealing, and mounting features of these pump enclosures.
[F04D 29/403] especially adapted for elastic fluid pumps -- This subclass covers components or accessories specially designed for elastic fluid pumps, a type of non-positive-displacement pump that uses the elastic deformation of a fluid chamber to move liquids. It includes adaptations such as seals, valves, or mounting features tailored to these pumps.
[F04D 29/406] especially adapted for liquid pumps -- F04D 29/406 designates devices, component parts or accessories that are specially adapted for liquid pumps, in particular for non-positive-displacement pump systems. It includes modifications or fittings that improve performance, sealing, drive coupling or control of such pumps. The subgroup falls under the broader class of mechanical engineering for positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04D 29/42] for radial or helico-centrifugal pumps -- This subclass covers radial or helico-centrifugal pumps, a type of non-positive-displacement pump that moves liquids by imparting kinetic energy with a rotating impeller and a radial or helical flow path. It includes details, component parts and accessories related to these pumps.
[F04D 29/4206] especially adapted for elastic fluid pumps -- F04D 29/4206 covers component parts or accessories specifically designed for elastic fluid pumps, a type of non-positive-displacement pump used to move liquids. The classification pertains to details of these pumps within the broader category of mechanical engineering positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04D 29/4213] suction ports -- The classification F04D 29/4213 covers suction ports, the openings that admit fluid into a pump, detailing their geometry, sizing, placement and any features intended to influence flow, pressure recovery or cavitation resistance. It is applied to patents describing the design or improvement of these inlet components in non-positive-displacement pumps.
[F04D 29/422] Discharge tongues  F04D17/04  takes precedence -- Discharge tongues are component parts or accessories of non-positive-displacement pumps used for moving liquids, classified under the mechanical-engineering group for positive-displacement machines. The element directs fluid flow from the pump chamber to the outlet and is detailed in CPC entry F04D 29/422, which follows the broader heading F04D 17/04.
[F04D 29/4226] Fan casings -- Fan casings are protective housings that enclose the fan or impeller of a pump, guiding airflow and shielding internal components from contaminants. They are used as accessories for non-positive displacement pumps to improve cooling, reduce noise, and maintain structural integrity.
[F04D 29/4233] with volutes extending mainly in axial or radially inward direction -- The CPC subclass F04D 29/4233 covers non-positive-displacement pumps and their component parts or accessories that feature volutes extending primarily in an axial direction or radially inward, detailing design variations where the volute shape directs flow along the pump axis or toward the centre.
[F04D 29/424] Double entry casings -- F04D 29/424 covers double entry casings, which are pump housings provided with two fluid inlet ports. The design allows fluid to be drawn from two sources or to provide a bypass, improving flow flexibility and reliability in non-positive-displacement pump assemblies.
[F04D 29/4246] comprising more than one outlet -- The CPC subclass F04D 29/4246 falls under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids, non-positive-displacement pumps and concerns details, component parts or accessories of pumps that comprise more than one outlet.
[F04D 29/4253] with axial entry and discharge -- This subgroup covers non-positive-displacement pumps and their component parts that draw fluid into the machine along the axis and expel it along the same axis, describing axial entry and discharge flow paths.
[F04D 29/426] especially adapted for liquid pumps -- F04D 29/426 concerns components, parts or accessories that are specially designed for use with liquid pumps, particularly within the non-positive-displacement pump family. The classification groups items adapted to improve or support pump operation, such as seals, bearings, housings or control devices. It falls under mechanical engineering of positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04D 29/4266] made of sheet metal -- F04D 29/4266 pertains to sheet-metal components used in non-positive-displacement pumps, such as housings, casings or internal parts fabricated from sheet metal. It falls under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids, specifically the details, component parts or accessories of non-positive-displacement pumps.
[F04D 29/4273] suction eyes -- Suction eyes are the inlet openings or nozzles that guide fluid into non-positive-displacement pumps such as gear, vane or screw types. They are designed to create a smooth, low-velocity entry, reduce cavitation risk and improve pump efficiency. These components are integral to the pump's suction system.
[F04D 29/428] Discharge tongues  F04D17/04  takes precedence -- F04D 29/428 defines discharge tongues, the shaped protrusions or guide elements that direct fluid flow as it exits a non-positive-displacement pump. They are considered detailed component parts or accessories of such pumps and are classified under mechanical engineering, positive-displacement machines for liquids, non-positive-displacement pumps. This subclass takes precedence over the broader F04D 17/04 category.
[F04D 29/4286] inside lining, e.g. rubber -- The subclass F04D 29/4286 covers interior linings of non-positive-displacement pumps, such as rubber sleeves or coatings applied to pump housings, chambers or volutes. These linings are intended to protect the pump's internal surfaces from wear, corrosion or leakage and are treated as component parts or accessories rather than the pump's main structure.
[F04D 29/4293] Details of fluid inlet or outlet -- F04D 29/4293 covers the design and configuration of fluid inlets and outlets for non-positive-displacement pump systems. It includes features such as ports, nozzles, manifolds, seals and other accessories that control the entry or discharge of liquids. The subclass is used for patents describing the geometry, flow-conditioning elements, or mounting arrangements of these connections.
[F04D 29/44] Fluid-guiding means, e.g. diffusers -- This group covers devices that direct or regulate fluid flow in non-positive displacement pumps, such as diffusers, vanes or other guiding elements that shape the fluid stream. It includes details, component parts or accessories for these fluid-guiding means.
[F04D 29/441] especially adapted for elastic fluid pumps -- This CPC subclass refers to parts and accessories specially adapted for pumps that handle elastic fluids, such as hydraulic or pneumatic systems. It is a subcategory of non-positive-displacement pumps within the broader group of positive-displacement machines for liquids. It covers design features that enable the pump to move compressible media efficiently.
[F04D 29/442] rotating diffusers -- Rotating diffusers are turbine-style vanes that spin within a pump housing to impart kinetic energy to liquid, converting rotational motion into a directed fluid flow. Used in non-positive-displacement pumps, they help increase discharge pressure, smooth flow and reduce turbulence, serving as a key component or accessory in such pump designs.
[F04D 29/444] Bladed diffusers -- F04D 29/444 covers bladed diffusers, stationary vaned or channeled rings positioned downstream of a pump impeller that convert the fluid's kinetic energy into static pressure and direct flow toward the volute. These components are classified under mechanical-engineering non-positive-displacement pumps as detailed accessory parts that improve pump efficiency and flow uniformity.
[F04D 29/445] especially adapted for liquid pumps -- F04D 29/445 covers details, component parts, or accessories of non-positive-displacement pumps that are especially adapted for liquid pumps.
[F04D 29/447] rotating diffusers -- Rotating diffusers are components of non-positive-displacement pumps that convert the rotational motion of an impeller into a swirling fluid flow, increasing pressure and smoothing discharge. They are used as accessory parts in centrifugal or axial-flow pump designs to improve hydraulic efficiency.
[F04D 29/448] bladed diffusers -- F04D 29/448 covers bladed diffusers, which are stationary or rotating components that direct fluid flow and increase pressure in non-positive-displacement pump systems. They are used as detail parts or accessories in hydraulic machinery to improve efficiency and flow uniformity.
[F04D 29/46] adjustable -- F04D 29/46 concerns adjustable elements of component parts or accessories for non-positive-displacement pumps. It includes mechanisms that allow variation of pump geometry, flow-rate or pressure settings, such as adjustable impellers, throttling devices or speed-control units used to fine-tune pump performance.
[F04D 29/462] especially adapted for elastic fluid pumps -- F04D 29/462 is a CPC subclass under Mechanical Engineering for non-positive-displacement pumps that includes details, component parts or accessories specifically designed for elastic fluid pumps, such as those handling compressible liquids or gases in specialized pumping mechanisms.
[F04D 29/464] adjusting flow cross-section, otherwise than by using adjustable stator blades -- The F04D 29/464 subclass covers ways to vary the flow cross-section in non-positive-displacement pumps without using adjustable stator blades. It includes devices such as variable-diameter sleeves, movable liners or other mechanisms that change the effective passage area to control pump output. This pertains to details, component parts or accessories for such flow-adjusting features.
[F04D 29/466] especially adapted for liquid fluid pumps -- This subclass covers technical details, component parts and accessories that are specially adapted for liquid-fluid pumps, such as specialised seals, impellers, casings, bearings or control mechanisms designed to improve the efficiency, reliability or handling of the pumped liquid.
[F04D 29/468] adjusting flow cross-section, otherwise than by using adjustable stator blades -- This subclass covers features for varying the flow cross-section in non-positive-displacement liquid pumps without employing adjustable stator blades, such as adjustable orifices, throat diameters, or flow-path geometry changes. It relates to component parts or accessories that modify the pump's hydraulic passage to control capacity or pressure.
[F04D 29/48] for unidirectional fluid flow in reversible pumps -- This CPC subclass (F04D 29/48) covers components or accessories that ensure unidirectional fluid flow in reversible pumps, i.e., devices that let fluid move only one way even when the pump runs backward. It applies to non-positive-displacement pump designs and includes items such as check valves, one-way flow mechanisms and related parts.
[F04D 29/483] especially adapted for elastic fluid pumps -- The CPC subclass F04D 29/483 covers details, component parts, or accessories especially adapted for elastic fluid pumps, which are a type of non-positive-displacement pump used to move liquids. [F04D 29/50] for reversing fluid flow -- F04D 29/50 classifies components or accessories that cause fluid to flow in the opposite direction in non-positive-displacement pumps, such as check valves, flow-reversing mechanisms, and similar devices used in positive-displacement machines for liquids.
[F04D 29/503] especially adapted for elastic fluid pumps -- The F04D 29/503 classification covers components or accessories that are specially adapted for elastic fluid pumps, a type of non-positive-displacement pump that moves liquids by elastic deformation. [F04D 29/52] for axial pumps -- F04D 29/52 relates to axial-type pumps, a subclass of non-positive-displacement pumps used for moving liquids. It is used to index inventions that improve performance, sealing or control in these elastic-fluid pumping systems.
[F04D 29/524] shiftable members for obturating part of the flow path -- This code covers movable components that act as sealing or blocking elements within a pump's flow channel, such as sliding gates, rotors, or adjustable valves that can be shifted to open or close part of the passage. These shiftable members are used to regulate fluid flow or to create a variable flow path in non-positive-displacement pumps.
