[F05B 2240/57] Seals -- The code F05B 2240/57 designates patents concerning sealing devices used in wind, spring, weight or inertia-type motors and similar liquid-driven machines. It falls under the mechanical engineering section for indexing schemes relating to engines or pumps, specifically the subclass for such motor types.
[F05B 2240/571] Brush seals
[F05B 2240/572] Leaf seals -- F05B 2240/572 covers leaf-type sealing elements that are attached to rotating shafts or other moving parts to prevent fluid leakage in machines such as pumps, compressors and turbines. The classification pertains to designs where flexible leaves or lamellae slide against a housing surface to maintain a seal while accommodating rotation or axial movement.
[F05B 2240/60] Shafts -- F05B 2240/60 designates shafts employed in machines or engines that drive liquids, situated within the indexing scheme that groups wind, spring, weight, inertia or similar motor types. It belongs to the Cooperative Patent Classification for mechanical engineering, under the broader category of indexing schemes for engines or pumps.
[F05B 2240/61] hollow -- F05B 2240/61 covers hydraulic or pneumatic actuators whose piston-rod or shaft is hollow, allowing a conduit for fluid, cables or other lines to pass through the moving member while the device transmits power to liquids or gases. This subclass is used for machines such as pumps, compressors or engines where a hollow rod improves functionality or integration with auxiliary systems.
[F05B 2240/62] flexible -- F05B 2240/62 classifies machines that use a flexible energy-storage element-such as a spring, elastic band or other deformable component-to provide the driving force for a wind, spring, weight, inertia or similar motor that powers a pump or compressor handling liquids. The entry covers the design and integration of such flexible elements within these motor-driven hydraulic or pneumatic systems.
[F05B 2240/63] Glands for admission or removal of fluids from shafts -- F05B 2240/63 covers glands that provide a seal while allowing fluid to be admitted to or removed from rotating shafts, used in engines, pumps and similar machines. These components prevent leakage and protect bearings while permitting lubrication, cooling or drainage.
[F05B 2240/70] Slinger plates or washers -- F05B 2240/70 classifies slinger plates or washers that are attached to rotating shafts or pistons in liquid-handling machines to impart centrifugal force, control fluid flow or generate pumping action. These components are used in engines, pumps or mixers that operate by wind, spring, weight or inertia mechanisms.
[F05B 2240/80] Platforms for stationary or moving blades -- F05B 2240/80 covers platforms that support stationary or rotating blades in machines such as wind-turbine rotors, fans or other blade-driven devices. These platforms may be fixed or mobile and are designed to hold the blades in proper alignment, allow adjustment, transport or integration with the surrounding structure.
[F05B 2240/801] cooled platforms -- F05B 2240/801 designates cooled platforms incorporated in machines or engines for liquids that use wind, spring, weight, inertia or similar motor principles. The entry covers platforms equipped with cooling systems to dissipate heat generated during operation, thereby improving efficiency and longevity of the liquid-handling equipment.
[F05B 2240/85] Electrical connection arrangements -- Group F05B2240/85 is impacted by reclassification into group F03D80/301 . Groups F05B2240/85 and F03D80/301 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[F05B 2240/90] Mounting on supporting structures or systems -- This CPC subclass F05B 2240/90 concerns the way wind, spring, weight, inertia or similar motors and liquid-handling engines are attached to a supporting structure, such as frames, brackets or foundations, to secure the machine in a fixed position for operation.
[F05B 2240/91] on a stationary structure -- This CPC subclass covers wind, spring, weight or inertia driven motors that are incorporated into liquid-handling machines where the motor is mounted on a fixed, non-moving structure instead of a moving vehicle or vessel. It applies to stationary installations such as pumping stations, water-treatment plants and other fixed hydraulic equipment.

It falls under mechanical engineering indexing schemes for engines or pumps, specifically those using hinged mechanisms to transmit motion or control fluid flow.

The entry indexes systems where the driving mechanism is situated on the earth's surface rather than being submerged, elevated or integrated within the liquid conduit.

It falls under the indexing scheme for engines or pumps and specifies the integration of a lifting arrangement as part of the motorized system.

[F05B 2240/94] on a movable wheeled structure -- F05B 2240/94 designates inventions where a movable wheeled structure is employed as part of a wind-, spring-, weight- or inertia-driven motor that powers a liquid handling machine such as a pump or engine. The classification captures arrangements that use the motion of wheels to generate or transmit mechanical energy for moving fluids.
[F05B 2240/941] which is a land vehicle -- F05B 2240/941 is a subclass of the CPC relating to mechanical devices that use wind, spring, weight, inertia or similar motors in machines or engines for liquids, specifically when the overall system is a land vehicle. It covers patents where such unconventional power sources are employed to drive hydraulic or fluid-based propulsion or auxiliary functions in cars, trucks, railcars or other terrestrial transport. This entry falls under mechanical engineering indexing schemes for engines and pumps.
[F05B 2240/95] offshore -- The F05B 2240/95 entry covers offshore applications of wind, spring, weight, inertia or similar motor types used in machines handling liquids. It includes components and installations for offshore wind turbines, wave-energy converters and other marine-based fluid-driven power devices.
[F05B 2240/96] as part of a wind turbine farm -- F05B 2240/96 covers the use of wind turbines as components of a wind turbine farm, addressing the collective arrangement, integration, and coordination of multiple turbines within a single installation. It includes the layout, interconnection and shared infrastructure that enable the turbines to operate together as a system.
[F05B 2240/965] as part of a water turbine farm -- F05B 2240/965 covers elements that are installed or operated as part of a water turbine farm, such as auxiliary equipment, control systems or structural components that support the collective operation of multiple water turbines. The entry falls under the mechanical-engineering indexing scheme for engines and pumps that use wind, spring, weight, inertia or similar motors to drive liquid-handling machines.
[F05B 2240/97] on a submerged structure -- F05B 2240/97 covers the use of wind, spring, weight, inertia or similar non-conventional power sources to drive machines or engines for liquids when the apparatus is integrated with a submerged structure such as a underwater vessel, platform or foundation. It falls within the mechanical-engineering subclass dealing with indexing schemes for engines or pumps.
[F05B 2240/98] which is inflatable -- This CPC entry covers pumps or fluid-moving devices that employ an inflatable element such as a bladder or bag, which expands and contracts to displace liquid. It applies to machines or engines handling liquids that are driven by wind, spring, weight, inertia or similar motors, and is part of the mechanical-engineering indexing scheme for engines and pumps.
[F05B 2240/99] characterised by colour or colour patterns -- F05B 2240/99 covers pumps, engines or related mechanical devices that are distinguished by their colour or colour patterns, typically for visual identification, status indication or aesthetic purposes. The classification applies when the colouring is an integral part of the component's design or function.
[F05B 2250/00] Geometry -- F05B 2250/00 covers the geometric aspects of machines or engines for liquids that are driven by wind, springs, weights, inertia or similar sources. It indexes the shape, dimensions and spatial arrangement of components such as rotors, pistons or chambers in these power-transmission devices. This subclass helps classify patents dealing with the physical form of such fluid-driven mechanisms.
[F05B 2250/02] variable -- F05B 2250/02 covers machines or engines for liquids that employ wind, spring, weight, inertia or comparable motor principles where a key operating parameter-such as speed, displacement, pitch or other characteristic-is variable, allowing the device to adjust performance in response to changing conditions. This sub-class falls under mechanical engineering indexing schemes for engines or pumps and refines the broader category of variable-parameter motor systems.
[F05B 2250/10] two-dimensional -- This CPC subclass F05B 2250/10 covers two-dimensional geometry aspects of wind, spring, weight or inertia motor driven machines or engines for liquids, within mechanical engineering's indexing schemes for engines or pumps. It is used to classify patents that describe the planar geometric characteristics of such fluid-power devices.
[F05B 2250/11] triangular -- F05B 2250/11 triangular designates the use of triangular geometry in wind, spring, weight or inertia-type motors that power machines or engines for liquids, as indexed within the broader mechanical-engineering classification.
[F05B 2250/12] rectangular -- The CPC entry F05B 2250/12 designates features that have a rectangular shape in the indexing scheme for wind, spring, weight, inertia or similar motors used in liquid-working machines or engines. It falls under the mechanical-engineering hierarchy that classifies engines and pumps, specifically relating to geometric forms of components. This code helps identify patents that employ rectangular geometry in such motor systems.
[F05B 2250/121] square -- The code F05B 2250/121 designates the term square as a geometric indexing element for machines or engines handling liquids, particularly those driven by wind, springs, weight or inertia. It is employed to classify components such as housings, rotors or vanes that have a square shape within the broader mechanical-engineering subclass.
[F05B 2250/13] trapezial -- F05B 2250/13 is a CPC subclass in the mechanical-engineering section that indexes geometrical features of liquid-handling machines or engines powered by wind, springs, weights, inertia or similar forces. The entry "trapezial" covers components or parts that have a trapezoidal shape, such as chambers, blades or casings, used in these types of pumps or engines. It is part of the indexing scheme for engines and pumps within the broader class for mechanical power sources.
[F05B 2250/131] polygonal -- The F05B 2250/131 "polygonal" entry is an indexing-scheme subclass for wind, spring, weight, inertia or similar motors applied to machines or engines handling liquids, and it covers arrangements where the moving or rotating elements are shaped or positioned in a polygonal configuration, such as polygonal cams, bodies or rotor profiles that generate the indexed motion.
[F05B 2250/132] hexagonal -- F05B 2250/132 hexagonal classifies mechanical components of liquid-handling machines that incorporate a hexagonal shape, such as housings, rotors or ducts, within the broader indexing scheme for wind, spring, weight or inertia motors in engines and pumps.
[F05B 2250/14] elliptical -- F05B 2250/14 is a CPC subclass in the mechanical-engineering section that indexes geometric features of liquid-handling machines, specifically those driven by wind, spring, weight, inertia or similar motors. The entry covers elliptical shapes or motions used in such engines or pumps.
[F05B 2250/141] circular -- The CPC code F05B 2250/141 designates features of wind, spring, weight, inertia or similar motors that employ a circular geometry, such as circular chambers, rotors or guide paths, within machines or engines handling liquids. It is used to index patents where the circular shape is a defining characteristic of the motor's operation or construction.
[F05B 2250/15] spiral -- The CPC subgroup F05B 2250/15 covers spiral components in liquid-handling machines such as pumps, compressors and turbines, including spiral casings, vanes, chambers or passages that guide fluid flow. It applies to designs where a spiral geometry creates progressive displacement or pressure change within the device.
[F05B 2250/16] parabolic -- F05B 2250/16 classifies mechanical devices that employ a parabolic geometry in the design of wind, spring, weight, inertia or similar motors used for moving liquids. It covers engines, pumps or related machines where a parabolic shape governs the motion path, force transmission or fluid flow, distinguishing these configurations from other geometric arrangements.
[F05B 2250/17] hyperbolic -- F05B 2250/17 covers liquid-handling machines or engines that use a hyperbolic motion or cam profile, such as pistons or pumps driven by wind, springs, weight, inertia or similar forces, to achieve a characteristic displacement or pumping action.
[F05B 2250/18] patterned -- F05B 2250/18 covers liquid-handling machines or pumps that employ a patterned geometric arrangement of components, such as blades, vanes or chambers, to influence fluid flow or motor action. It is used for indexing devices that use wind, spring, weight, inertia or similar motive forces where the pattern of the geometry is a defining technical feature.
[F05B 2250/181] ridged -- F05B 2250/181 designates fluid-driven machines that use rigid ridges or ribs on moving components to pump, move or seal liquids, a subcategory of wind, spring, weight or inertia motors for liquid engines. The ridged surfaces create displacement or improve flow within the engine's chamber, distinguishing these devices from smooth-walled counterparts.
[F05B 2250/182] crenellated, notched -- The CPC subclass F05B 2250/182 designates devices in mechanical engineering for pumps or liquid-handling machines that use wind, spring, weight, inertia or similar motors, where the moving part has a crenellated or notched geometry. It falls within the indexing scheme for engines or pumps employing such motor types.

Such configurations are used to increase turbulence, improve pressure distribution or reduce pulsations in pumps and similar engines. The classification captures designs that deliberately employ a zigzag shape in conduits, chambers or moving parts.

This subclass is part of the mechanical engineering indexing scheme for engines or pumps.

[F05B 2260/203] by transpiration cooling -- F05B 2260/203 classifies liquid-driven machines, such as engines or pumps, that employ transpiration cooling to control temperature. The method involves a cooling fluid passing through a porous surface or material, removing heat as the fluid is drawn through the device, and is used in various mechanical engineering applications to improve performance and durability.

[F05B 2280/702] Reinforcements -- Group F05B2280/702 is impacted by reclassification into groups F03D1/0679 , F03D1/0681 , F03D1/0682 and F03D1/0684 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[F05C] INDEXING SCHEME RELATING TO MATERIALS, MATERIAL PROPERTIES OR MATERIAL CHARACTERISTICS FOR MACHINES, ENGINES OR PUMPS OTHER THAN NON-POSITIVE-DISPLACEMENT MACHINES OR ENGINES -- F05C is a CPC indexing scheme that groups patents dealing with the selection, characterization or testing of materials and their properties for use in machines, engines or pumps, but it does not cover non-positive-displacement devices. It helps locate inventions where material performance is a key technical feature.
[F05C 2201/00] Metals -- F05C 2201/00 is the CPC group that indexes the use of metals as material choices for machines, engines and pumps, covering their properties and characteristics in the context of mechanical engineering. It falls under the broader scheme for material-related indexing in engine and pump subclasses, excluding non-positive-displacement devices.
[F05C 2201/02] Light metals -- F05C 2201/02 classifies indexing schemes that identify light metals-such as aluminium, magnesium or titanium alloys-used in machines, engines or pumps. It falls under mechanical-engineering categories that organize material properties for components other than non-positive-displacement engines.
[F05C 2201/021] Aluminium -- F05C 2201/021 is a CPC sub-group that indexes patents dealing with aluminium as a material used in the construction of pumps, engines or related mechanical devices. It falls under the broader scheme for material properties in machines, specifically within the metals category.
[F05C 2201/023] Beryllium -- The CPC entry F05C 2201/023 indexes beryllium as a material used in mechanical engineering components such as engines, pumps and other machines. It falls under the broader scheme for material properties and characteristics applied to metallic parts, helping classify inventions that exploit beryllium's lightweight strength, high-temperature stability and corrosion resistance.
[F05C 2201/025] Boron -- The F05C 2201/025 entry designates the use of boron as a material in machines, engines or pumps, covering its properties such as hardness, corrosion resistance and high-temperature performance when alloyed with other metals. It falls under indexing schemes for engine-related components that focus on material characteristics, specifically within the broader category of metals for mechanical engineering applications.
[F05C 2201/026] Lithium -- The CPC entry F05C 2201/026 designates lithium as a metal material considered in indexing schemes for mechanical engineering components such as engines and pumps. [F05C 2201/028] Magnesium -- F05C 2201/028 classifies the use of magnesium as a material in machines, engines or pumps. It falls under mechanical engineering, specifically the indexing scheme for material properties of such equipment, excluding non-positive-displacement machines. The entry signals the relevance of magnesium's characteristics-such as low density and high strength-to-weight ratio-to the design and construction of mechanical devices.
[F05C 2201/04] Heavy metals -- F05C 2201/04 designates the use or presence of heavy metals in components of machines, engines or pumps, focusing on material properties and performance. It covers alloys, coatings or treatments involving metals such as lead, mercury, tungsten or bismuth that affect durability, wear resistance or thermal behavior.

It falls under mechanical-engineering indexing schemes for material characteristics of machines, engines or pumps.

It falls under mechanical engineering indexing schemes for metal materials in non-positive-displacement engines and pumps.

The entry applies to conventional positive-displacement devices where molybdenum's strength, corrosion resistance or high-temperature behavior is relevant.
[F05C 2201/0412] Titanium -- F05C 2201/0412 identifies titanium as a material option for machines, engines or pumps, detailing its properties and use in such equipment. It is part of the indexing scheme that classifies material characteristics within the broader mechanical-engineering category, excluding non-positive-displacement devices.
[F05C 2201/0415] Zirconium -- The CPC code F05C 2201/0415 identifies zirconium as a material chosen for engines, pumps or other mechanical devices, where its specific characteristics such as high corrosion resistance, strength at elevated temperatures, and low neutron-capture cross-section are relevant to the performance or durability of the equipment.

It is part of the indexing scheme for material characteristics in mechanical engineering, focusing on the use, treatment or properties of these metals within such equipment.

This subclass groups gold with other metals employed for durability, conductivity or corrosion resistance in engine-related parts.

It falls under mechanical engineering indexing schemes for material-properties of machines, engines or pumps.
[F05C 2201/0445] Austempered ductile iron [ADI] -- Austempered ductile iron (ADI) is a heat-treated cast iron with a bainitic-martensitic microstructure that combines high strength, good ductility and wear resistance. In the CPC it falls under F05C 2201/0445, the subclass for material-property indexing of metals used in engines, pumps or other machines, excluding non-positive-displacement devices. This code groups ADI with other specialized metal materials for mechanical-engineering applications.

[F05C 2253/06] Amorphous -- F05C 2253/06 covers patents that deal with amorphous (non-crystalline) materials used in machines, engines or pumps, focusing on their material properties or characteristics. The entry is part of the indexing scheme for material properties in mechanical engineering.

[F05C 2253/083] Directionally-solidified crystalline structure -- The entry F05C 2253/083 classifies the material property of a directionally-solidified crystalline structure, where grains are aligned during solidification to give anisotropic strength and fatigue resistance. This indexing scheme applies to machines, engines or pumps that use such engineered materials to improve performance and durability.
[F05C 2253/0831] monocrystalline -- F05C 2253/0831 designates monocrystalline materials as a material characteristic for machines, engines or pumps, excluding non-positive-displacement types. [F05C 2253/10] Syntactic -- F05C 2253/10 classifies inventions that use syntactic materials-composites containing hollow micro- or nano-particles embedded in a matrix-to achieve specific mechanical or physical properties in engines, pumps or related machinery. [F05C 2253/12] Coating -- F05C 2253/12 classifies inventions that apply protective or functional coating layers to components of machines, engines or pumps, focusing on the material properties or characteristics of the coating rather than the mechanical design of the device. It covers processes, compositions and treatment methods that modify surfaces to improve durability, corrosion resistance, friction or heat-transfer performance. This entry is part of the indexing scheme for material characteristics in mechanical engineering.
[F05C 2253/14] Foam -- F05C 2253/14 classifies patents that address foam as a material or material characteristic in machines, engines or pumps. It covers the selection, preparation or performance effects of foamed substances used in such mechanical equipment.
[F05C 2253/16] Fibres -- F05C 2253/16 covers fibers as a material characteristic for machines, engines or pumps. It falls under the indexing scheme for material properties in mechanical engineering.
[F05C 2253/18] Filler -- F05C 2253/18 designates the use of filler materials in mechanical engineering components such as engines or pumps, where the filler is added to alter properties like strength, wear resistance, thermal conductivity or corrosion behavior. It covers the classification of substances introduced into a base material to achieve specific performance characteristics without constituting a separate structural element.
[F05C 2253/20] Resin -- F05C 2253/20 pertains to the use of resin as a material in engines or pumps, addressing its properties and characteristics that affect performance and durability. [F05C 2253/22] Reinforcements -- F05C 2253/22 Reinforcements covers the use of structural or material reinforcements in machines, engines or pumps to increase load-bearing capacity, stiffness, wear resistance or fatigue life. It includes added ribs, inserts, layers, fibers or other strengthening elements applied to components that are not part of non-positive-displacement devices.
[F05C 2253/24] Heat treatment -- F05C 2253/24 covers heat-treatment processes applied to the materials of machines, engines or pumps, such as heating, quenching, annealing or tempering, to modify their mechanical or physical characteristics. It pertains to any treatment intended to improve strength, hardness, ductility or resistance to wear in components that are not part of non-positive-displacement devices.
[F05D] INDEXING SCHEME FOR ASPECTS RELATING TO NON-POSITIVE-DISPLACEMENT MACHINES OR ENGINES, GAS-TURBINES OR JET-PROPULSION PLANTS -- F05D is the CPC indexing scheme that groups patents dealing with non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines, jet-propulsion plants and related engines. It covers design, operation, control and auxiliary systems for these devices, distinguishing them from positive-displacement compressors or pumps. The class aids in locating technologies that produce power by continuous fluid flow rather than volume displacement.
[F05D 2200/00] Mathematical features -- F05D 2200/00 (Mathematical features) covers the use of mathematical models, equations, simulations, optimisation methods and control-theory techniques applied to non-positive-displacement machines, gas-turbines and jet-propulsion plants. It includes analytical and numerical representations of performance, thermodynamic cycles, fluid dynamics and system behaviour that aid design, analysis and control of these engines. This subclass groups the purely mathematical aspects rather than specific hardware components.
[F05D 2200/10] Basic functions -- F05D 2200/10 covers the fundamental operations of non-positive-displacement machines such as engines, gas turbines and jet-propulsion plants, including the generation of power, thrust or rotational motion that constitutes the basic function of these devices. It pertains to the essential purpose these machines serve rather than specific structural or control features.
[F05D 2200/11] Sum
[F05D 2200/12] Subtraction -- F05D 2200/12 designates the use of subtraction as a mathematical operation in the control, monitoring or diagnostic methods for non-positive-displacement machines, including gas-turbines and jet-propulsion plants. The code applies to implementations where a value is subtracted from another to compute differences, error signals or performance parameters within such engine systems.
[F05D 2200/13] Product -- The CPC entry F05D 2200/13 classifies inventions concerning the product of non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines, jet-propulsion plants and similar engines, focusing on the overall device rather than its individual components or subsystems. It is used to index patents that deal with the complete engine or turbine as a product, including its performance and functional characteristics.
[F05D 2200/14] Division -- F05D 2200/14 pertains to non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants in which the working fluid or power is divided among multiple paths, stages or components. It includes indexing schemes for features that split flow, allocate thrust or share energy within a single engine system.
[F05D 2200/15] Inverse -- The term "inverse" designates the use of inverse mathematical relationships or transformations in the analysis, modelling or control of non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants, enabling calculation of unknown quantities from measured performance data.
[F05D 2200/20] Special functions -- This subclass covers patents that employ special mathematical functions in modelling, analysis or control of non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines or jet-propulsion plants. It includes the use of Bessel, Legendre, hypergeometric or other advanced functions for describing fluid dynamics, thermodynamic cycles or performance characteristics.
[F05D 2200/21] Root -- The entry denotes the mathematical concept of a root - the solution of an equation - as it applies to indexing schemes for non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines and jet-propulsion plants. It classifies documents that address root-finding methods, characteristic roots of system equations, or root calculations used in performance analysis of these engines.
[F05D 2200/211] Square root -- The CPC entry F05D 2200/211 designates the use of square-root calculations as a mathematical feature in the analysis, control or performance modelling of non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines, jet-propulsion plants and related pump or engine systems.
[F05D 2200/212] Cubic root -- F05D 2200/212 designates the use of cubic-root mathematical operations in the analysis, control or design of non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines and jet-propulsion plants, where the calculation assists in performance modelling, speed regulation or thermodynamic calculations.
[F05D 2200/22] Power -- F05D 2200/22 covers the mathematical treatment of power in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines, jet-propulsion plants and similar engines. It includes formulas, calculations and theoretical models for power output, efficiency and related performance parameters.
[F05D 2200/221] Square power -- F05D 2200/221 - Square power - covers indexing of patents that treat the calculation of power where the result is obtained by squaring a parameter such as velocity, pressure or flux in non-positive-displacement machines, gas turbines, or jet-propulsion plants. The code is used to locate technical disclosures that employ this mathematical relationship for performance analysis or design.
[F05D 2200/222] Cubic power -- F05D 2200/222 pertains to the mathematical description of cubic power, i.e., power that varies with the cube of a governing variable such as rotational speed or fluid flow rate, as applied to non-positive-displacement machines, gas-turbines and jet-propulsion plants. It is used to index technical features where power output is expressed by a cubic relationship in the context of engine and pump engineering.
[F05D 2200/23] Logarithm -- The CPC subclass F05D 2200/23 "Logarithm" groups patent elements that use logarithmic functions in the design, control or analysis of non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines, jet-propulsion plants and related engines, where logarithms support calculations, performance modelling or data processing. It is part of the mechanical-engineering indexing scheme for engines and pumps.
[F05D 2200/24] exponential -- F05D 2200/24 identifies the use of exponential mathematical functions in the indexing of non-positive-displacement machines, gas turbines and jet-propulsion plants. It groups documents that apply exponential relationships for describing flow, pressure, temperature or efficiency characteristics within these engines.
[F05D 2200/25] Hyperbolic trigonometric, e.g. sinh, cosh, tanh -- F05D 2200/25 classifies inventions that employ hyperbolic trigonometric functions such as sinh, cosh or tanh in the analysis, control or design of non-positive-displacement machines, including gas-turbines and jet-propulsion plants, typically for modeling thermodynamic cycles, flow characteristics or stability equations.
[F05D 2200/26] trigonometric -- The code F05D 2200/26 identifies patents that apply trigonometric functions in the analysis, design, or control of non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines, jet-propulsion plants and related engines. It is used within the CPC indexing scheme for mechanical engineering to capture mathematical features that involve sine, cosine, tangent or related calculations.
[F05D 2200/261] Sine -- The CPC entry F05D 2200/261 "Sine" covers the use of sine-wave mathematical functions for describing or controlling periodic phenomena in non-positive-displacement machines, such as gas-turbines and jet-propulsion plants, including aspects of engine and pump operation that rely on sinusoidal timing, signals or motion.
[F05D 2200/262] Cosine
[F05D 2200/263] Tangent -- The CPC subclass F05D 2200/263, titled Tangent, pertains to mathematical features used in the analysis of non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines and jet-propulsion plants. It includes the use of tangent functions, angles or line approximations in performance, flow or thermodynamic calculations for these engines.
[F05D 2200/264] Cotangent -- In the CPC, code F05D 2200/264 classifies the use of the cotangent mathematical function in the analysis or design of non-positive-displacement machines, especially gas-turbine and jet-propulsion plants, within the broader mechanical-engineering indexing scheme.
[F05D 2200/30] miscellaneous -- F05D 2200/30 is a CPC subgroup dealing with miscellaneous aspects of non-positive-displacement machines, gas-turbines and jet-propulsion plants that are not covered by more specific headings. It includes peripheral or auxiliary features such as control, monitoring, diagnostics, auxiliary drives, and other technical elements that do not fall under other indexed schemes.
[F05D 2200/31] odd
[F05D 2200/32] even -- The CPC code F05D 2200/32 marks patents that reference the mathematical property of an even function or even symmetry within non-positive-displacement machines, gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants. It enables indexing of inventions where this symmetry is a key analytical characteristic.
[F05D 2200/33] bigger or smaller -- F05D 2200/33 covers aspects of gas-turbine or jet-propulsion plants that relate to the relative size of components, such as design choices for larger or smaller sections, scaling of parts, or dimensional variations within the machine. It is used to index inventions that modify overall or local dimensions to affect performance, efficiency, or integration in non-positive-displacement engines.
[F05D 2200/34] biggest or smallest -- This CPC subclass F05D 2200/34 covers features that identify the extreme size characteristics - such as the maximum or minimum dimensions, capacity or power - of non-positive-displacement machines, gas turbines or jet-propulsion plants. It is used to index patents that specify the biggest or smallest physical or performance parameters of these engines.
[F05D 2200/35] first -- The code F05D 2200/35 identifies technical aspects concerning the first stage of a compressor, turbine or similar component in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants, grouping these features within the broader indexing scheme for engines and pumps in mechanical engineering.
[F05D 2200/36] last -- F05D 2200/36 classifies inventions that pertain to the final or last stage of a non-positive-displacement machine such as a gas-turbine or jet-propulsion plant, including the arrangement of the last rotor, stator, nozzle or exhaust components. It is used to index technical features that address performance, cooling or flow control in that concluding stage of the engine cycle.
[F05D 2210/00] Working fluids -- F05D 2210/00 classifies inventions concerned with the working fluids employed in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines, jet-propulsion plants and related engines or pumps, covering the type, composition, supply, handling and conditioning of the fluid that provides the energy for the machine's operation.
[F05D 2210/10] Kind or type -- F05D 2210/10 is a CPC subclass within the mechanical engineering group that indexes non-positive-displacement machines, gas-turbine and jet-propulsion plants by the kind or type of working fluid employed. It classifies patents according to whether the fluid is a gas, liquid, steam, combustible mixture or other specific medium used to transfer energy in these engines.
[F05D 2210/11] liquid, i.e. incompressible -- F05D 2210/11 classifies the use of liquids-treated as incompressible working fluids-in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines, jet-propulsion plants and similar engines. It falls under the indexing scheme for aspects of engines or pumps that address fluid properties and behavior in these systems.
[F05D 2210/12] gaseous, i.e. compressible -- F05D 2210/12 classifies inventions that employ a gaseous, i.e. compressible, working fluid in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines, jet-propulsion plants or other similar engines. It covers the handling, flow, compression or expansion of gases used to generate mechanical power in these devices.
[F05D 2210/13] mixed, e.g. two-phase fluid -- F05D 2210/13 concerns machines or systems that employ a mixed working fluid, typically a two-phase liquid-vapour mixture, in non-positive-displacement engines, pumps, gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants. It covers the handling, circulation, control or utilization of such fluids within the mechanical engineering context.
[F05D 2210/132] Pumps with means for separating and evacuating the gaseous phase -- The code F05D 2210/132 covers pumps that incorporate a device to separate and evacuate any gaseous phase present in the working fluid, allowing continuous liquid transfer while removing entrained gas. It is part of the mechanical-engineering indexing scheme for non-positive-displacement machines, gas-turbines and jet-propulsion plants, and applies to systems where gas-laden liquids must be pumped efficiently.
[F05D 2210/14] Refrigerants with particular properties, e.g. HFC -- F05D 2210/14 covers refrigerants that possess specific characteristics, such as hydrofluorocarbons, used as working fluids in non-positive-displacement machines, gas-turbines, or jet-propulsion plants. The subclass groups substances selected for particular thermodynamic or chemical properties relevant to cooling cycles in these engine types.
[F05D 2210/20] Properties -- F05D 2210/20 covers the physical and thermodynamic properties of the working fluids used in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines, jet-propulsion plants and related engines. It includes data on density, viscosity, specific heat, compressibility, thermal conductivity and other characteristics that influence the performance and efficiency of these machines.
[F05D 2210/30] Flow characteristics -- F05D 2210/30 concerns the flow characteristics of working fluids in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines and jet-propulsion plants, covering aspects like velocity distribution, turbulence, pressure drop and related fluid-dynamic behaviour.
[F05D 2210/31] with Mach-number kept constant along the flow -- F05D 2210/31 classifies devices for engines, gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants in which the working-fluid flow is managed so that the Mach number remains constant along the flow path. It falls under the mechanical-engineering scheme for indexing non-positive-displacement machines and related fluid-dynamic aspects.
[F05D 2210/32] Pressure kept constant along the flow -- This CPC subclass covers devices or methods where the pressure of the working fluid is kept constant along its flow path in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines or jet-propulsion plants. It relates to the control or regulation of fluid pressure in these engines.
[F05D 2210/33] Turbulent flow -- F05D 2210/33 covers turbulent flow in the working fluids of non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines, jet-propulsion plants and related engines. It classifies inventions that address the characteristics, measurement or control of turbulence within these fluid-power systems.
[F05D 2210/34] Laminar flow -- F05D 2210/34 classifies patents dealing with laminar flow of working fluids in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines, jet-propulsion plants and related engines. The code is used for inventions that control, analyze or exploit smooth, orderly fluid motion to improve performance or efficiency.
[F05D 2210/40] Flow geometry or direction -- F05D 2210/40 covers features of the flow path in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines and jet-propulsion plants, focusing on how the working fluid is guided, turned or directed within the engine. It includes geometric arrangements and directional control of the fluid stream.
[F05D 2210/41] upwards due to the buoyancy of compressed air -- F05D 2210/41 pertains to devices or processes that achieve upward movement by exploiting the buoyancy of compressed air as a working fluid. It is used for non-positive-displacement machines such as certain gas-turbine or jet-propulsion systems where lift is generated by the lighter-than-air gas rising.
[F05D 2210/42] Axial inlet and radial outlet -- The F05D 2210/42 subclass addresses machines-such as gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants-where the working fluid enters the rotor axially and exits radially. It is used to index designs of non-positive-displacement engines that employ this axial-inlet/radial-outlet flow arrangement.
[F05D 2210/43] Radial inlet and axial outlet -- F05D 2210/43 pertains to non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines or jet-propulsion plants in which the working fluid enters the rotor radially and leaves it axially, describing the flow path layout of the engine.
[F05D 2210/44] bidirectional, i.e. in opposite, alternating directions -- F05D 2210/44 pertains to working-fluid systems in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines or jet-propulsion plants where the fluid moves in opposite or alternating directions. The code covers bidirectional flow arrangements, including mechanisms that periodically reverse flow for performance or control purposes.
[F05D 2220/00] Application -- F05D 2220/00 designates patent documents that deal with the practical use or implementation of non-positive-displacement machines, including gas-turbine and jet-propulsion plants. It encompasses applications of these engines in various contexts such as power generation, propulsion or auxiliary functions. This subclass is part of the broader indexing scheme for engines and pumps within mechanical engineering.
[F05D 2220/10] in ram-jet engines or ram-jet driven vehicles -- F05D 2220/10 classifies technical features of ram-jet engines and vehicles powered by ram-jet propulsion. It falls under the CPC section for non-positive-displacement machines, gas-turbines and jet-propulsion plants, within the broader mechanical-engineering indexing scheme for engines and pumps.
[F05D 2220/20] within closed fluid conduits, e.g. pipes -- The CPC code F05D 2220/20 relates to the handling of fluid flow inside closed conduits such as pipes or ducts in non-positive-displacement machines, including gas turbines and jet-propulsion plants, covering features, arrangements and methods that influence the movement, control or heating of fluids within sealed channels.
[F05D 2220/30] in turbines -- This CPC subgroup classifies inventions relating to turbines used in gas-turbine or jet-propulsion plants, covering design, construction, operation, control and auxiliary systems of such rotating machines. It falls under mechanical engineering, indexing schemes for engines or pumps, and the non-positive-displacement machine category.
[F05D 2220/31] in steam turbines -- F05D 2220/31 is a CPC indexing subclass that deals with aspects of steam turbines within the broader group of non-positive-displacement machines, such as gas-turbines and jet-propulsion plants, and forms part of the mechanical-engineering hierarchy for engines and pumps.
[F05D 2220/32] in gas turbines -- F05D 2220/32 is a Cooperative Patent Classification code used by the EPO and USPTO to index inventions concerning non-positive-displacement machines, specifically gas-turbine and jet-propulsion plants. It falls under the Mechanical Engineering section and refines the broader indexing scheme for engines and pumps. This subclass groups patents dealing with the design, operation or improvement of gas-turbine technology.
[F05D 2220/321] for a special turbine stage -- F05D 2220/321 covers a special turbine stage in gas-turbine or jet-propulsion plants, classified under mechanical-engineering schemes for non-positive-displacement machines. The entry pertains to unique design features of a turbine stage such as blade geometry, cooling arrangements, or variable-flow mechanisms that differ from conventional stages.
[F05D 2220/3212] the first stage of a turbine -- F05D 2220/3212 covers the initial or first stage of a turbine in gas-turbine or jet-propulsion systems, including the design, arrangement and operation of that stage. It falls under the indexing scheme for non-positive-displacement machines in the mechanical engineering CPC hierarchy.
[F05D 2220/3213] an intermediate stage of the turbine -- This subgroup of the CPC deals with the part of a turbine system that lies between the primary compression or combustion zone and the final power-extraction stage, covering designs, arrangements and functions of intermediate turbine stages in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines and jet-propulsion plants.
[F05D 2220/3215] the last stage of the turbine -- It pertains to the design, construction and operation of the final rotor stage of gas-turbine or jet-propulsion engines, including blade geometry, cooling, sealing and aerodynamic optimisation of that stage. It also covers accessories directly attached to the last stage such as the disc, vanes and related control mechanisms.
[F05D 2220/3216] for a special compressor stage -- F05D 2220/3216 designates inventions that involve a special compressor stage used in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants. It falls within the mechanical-engineering indexing scheme for engines and pumps, specifically covering unique compressor configurations.
[F05D 2220/3217] for the first stage of a compressor or a low pressure compressor -- The CPC code F05D 2220/3217 covers inventions concerning the first stage or low-pressure stage of a compressor, typically in gas-turbine, jet-propulsion or other non-positive-displacement machines. It addresses design, arrangement or control of that stage, including inlet guide vanes, rotors and associated flow-control features. The entry lies within the mechanical-engineering hierarchy under indexing schemes for engines and pumps.
[F05D 2220/3218] for an intermediate stage of a compressor -- F05D 2220/3218 covers features related to an intermediate stage of a compressor in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbine or jet-propulsion plants. It falls under the mechanical-engineering indexing scheme for engines or pumps, specifically for aspects of non-positive-displacement machines.
[F05D 2220/3219] for the last stage of a compressor or a high pressure compressor -- F05D 2220/3219 refers to features of the last stage of a compressor or a high-pressure compressor used in gas-turbine or jet-propulsion systems. It falls under the mechanical-engineering subclass that indexes non-positive-displacement machines, engines, and pumps.
[F05D 2220/323] for aircraft propulsion, e.g. jet engines -- This CPC subgroup classifies inventions concerned with aircraft propulsion systems, specifically jet engines and related gas-turbine devices used to generate thrust for airplanes. It falls under the non-positive-displacement machine category and deals with the design, operation, or improvement of such propulsion units.
[F05D 2220/324] to drive unshrouded, low solidity propeller -- F05D 2220/324 covers mechanical arrangements for powering unshrouded propellers that have a low blade-solidity, typically used in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines or jet-propulsion plants. The classification addresses drive concepts that enable efficient rotation of such open-type propellers without a surrounding duct.
[F05D 2220/325] to drive unshrouded, high solidity propeller -- The F05D 2220/325 subclass covers arrangements for powering an unshrouded propeller whose blades have a high solidity, meaning a large blade area relative to the disc area. It applies to non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbine or jet-propulsion plants that drive such propellers directly without a surrounding duct.
[F05D 2220/326] to drive shrouded, low solidity propeller -- F05D 2220/326 covers mechanisms that provide power to a shrouded, low-solidity propeller, typically used in gas-turbine or jet-propulsion systems where the propeller is enclosed by a duct or shroud to enhance thrust and reduce noise. The classification includes designs for driving such propellers directly or through gearing, and addresses related control and support structures. It falls under indexing schemes for non-positive-displacement machines within mechanical engineering.
[F05D 2220/327] to drive shrouded, high solidity propeller -- F05D 2220/327 covers systems that power a shrouded propeller with high blade solidity, typically used in gas-turbine or jet-propulsion applications. It falls under the mechanical-engineering indexing scheme for non-positive-displacement machines, linking engine and pump technologies to specialized propulsion devices.
[F05D 2220/328] providing direct vertical lift -- F05D 2220/328 covers methods or devices that achieve a direct vertical lift of fluids, typically using aerodynamic or thermodynamic forces rather than traditional positive-displacement pumping. It applies to engines, gas-turbines, or jet-propulsion systems where fluid is raised vertically for combustion, cooling or thrust generation.
[F05D 2220/329] in helicopters -- The CPC entry F05D 2220/329 classifies subject matter concerning the use of non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbine or jet-propulsion plants when installed in helicopters. It covers design, integration, control and performance aspects of turbine or jet engines that power rotary-wing aircraft.
[F05D 2220/34] in ram-air turbines ("RATS") -- The subclass F05D 2220/34 covers ram-air turbines (RATs), compact wind-driven generators mounted on aircraft or other vehicles that extract energy from the airstream to supply electrical or hydraulic power, often used as emergency or auxiliary power sources for gas-turbine and jet-propulsion systems.
[F05D 2220/36] specially adapted for the fan of turbofan engines -- The CPC subclass F05D 2220/36 pertains to features that are specially adapted for the fan of turbofan engines, including geometric, aerodynamic, structural or cooling modifications that improve fan performance, integration with the engine nacelle or drive system. It falls under non-positive-displacement machines in the indexing scheme for engines and pumps.
[F05D 2220/40] in turbochargers -- The CPC code F05D 2220/40 covers turbochargers, which are devices that increase the pressure and density of intake air for internal-combustion engines, gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants. It falls within the indexing scheme for non-positive-displacement machines and relates specifically to the components and arrangements that boost engine performance by compressing the working fluid.
[F05D 2220/50] for auxiliary power units (APU's) -- F05D 2220/50 designates auxiliary power units (APUs) that supply secondary or standby energy in vehicles, aircraft, ships or other installations, typically as small gas-turbine or non-positive-displacement engine systems. The code covers the structural and functional aspects of these supplemental power sources within the broader indexing scheme for engines and pumps.
[F05D 2220/60] making use of surplus or waste energy -- F05D 2220/60 concerns the utilization of surplus or waste energy in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines, jet-propulsion plants and related engines. It covers methods that recover, convert or reuse excess heat, pressure or kinetic energy generated during operation to improve overall efficiency.
[F05D 2220/62] with energy recovery turbines -- F05D 2220/62 covers non-positive-displacement machines, such as gas-turbine or jet-propulsion plants, that incorporate a turbine for recovering waste energy from exhaust gases or steam and feeding it back into the system to improve overall efficiency, as in combined-cycle or recuperative turbine arrangements.
[F05D 2220/64] for domestic central heating or production of electricity -- F05D 2220/64 belongs to the mechanical-engineering section of the Cooperative Patent Classification and covers non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants used for domestic central heating or for generating electricity. It is nested under indexing schemes that relate to engines, pumps and other power-producing devices. This subclass specifically addresses applications where the machine provides heat or power rather than mechanical drive.
[F05D 2220/70] in combination with -- F05D 2220/70 identifies patents that describe a non-positive-displacement machine, gas-turbine or jet-propulsion plant used together with another component or system. The code is applied when the invention is presented "in combination with" such a machine, helping locate combined-technology disclosures within the mechanical-engineering indexing scheme.
[F05D 2220/72] a steam turbine -- F05D 2220/72 covers steam turbines, rotating machines that convert the energy of high-pressure steam into mechanical work. It is placed within the indexing scheme for non-positive-displacement machines, gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants under the broader mechanical-engineering class.
[F05D 2220/722] as part of an integrated gasification combined cycle -- This subgroup covers features of integrated gasification combined cycle plants, where a gasifier produces syngas that feeds a gas turbine and a heat-recovery system for electricity generation. It includes arrangements, controls and auxiliary equipment that integrate the gasification unit with the combined-cycle power block. The focus is on non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbine-driven power plants.
[F05D 2220/74] a gas turbine -- F05D 2220/74 is the CPC subclass for gas turbines within the mechanical engineering group that indexes non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbine and jet-propulsion plants. It covers the construction, arrangement and operation of gas-turbine engines, including components like compressors, combustion chambers and expanders.
[F05D 2220/75] equipment using fuel having a low calorific value, e.g. low BTU fuel, waste end, syngas, biomass fuel or flare gas -- F05D 2220/75 covers devices that operate on fuels with a low calorific value, such as low-BTU fuel, waste-derived gas, synthesis gas, biomass gas or flare gas. It is part of the mechanical-engineering indexing scheme for non-positive-displacement machines, gas turbines and jet-propulsion plants, defining equipment adapted to use these low-energy-content fuels.
[F05D 2220/76] an electrical generator -- The CPC subgroup F05D 2220/76 covers electrical generators that are integrated with or driven by non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbine or jet-propulsion plants. It is part of the mechanical engineering class dealing with indexing schemes for engines and pumps, specifically the scheme for non-positive-displacement machines. The code F05D 2220/76 designates this subject.
[F05D 2220/762] of the direct current (D.C.) type -- F05D 2220/762 designates features of non-positive-displacement machines, gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants that operate with a direct-current (D.C.) type power source or control system, covering DC generators, motors and related circuitry used in such engines.
[F05D 2220/764] of the alternating current (A.C.) type -- F05D 2220/764 indexes features of non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants that are powered by or operate with alternating current (A.C.) electrical systems, covering design, control and performance aspects specific to A.C.-type operation.
[F05D 2220/7642] of the synchronous type -- F05D 2220/7642 designates the subclass of non-positive-displacement machines, such as gas-turbine or jet-propulsion plants, that use a synchronous-type rotor, meaning the rotor rotates in step with the frequency of the driving power source.
[F05D 2220/7644] of the asynchronous type, i.e. induction type -- F05D 2220/7644 is a CPC subclass that indexes aspects of non-positive-displacement machines, specifically the asynchronous or induction type of engines, gas-turbines and jet-propulsion plants. It falls under the mechanical engineering hierarchy, grouping technical features related to induction-type rotating-machine designs.
[F05D 2220/7646] Double fed induction generators (DFIGs) -- F05D 2220/7646 classifies double-fed induction generators, which are electrically excited machines that receive input power from both the stator and a rotor winding via slip-rings, enabling variable-speed operation and active control of reactive power in applications such as wind-turbine drives and other non-positive-displacement machines.
[F05D 2220/766] via a direct connection, i.e. a gearless transmission -- F05D 2220/766 covers non-positive-displacement machines, gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants that transmit power through a direct, gearless connection between rotating parts. It includes couplings, shafts or similar mechanisms that convey torque without intermediate gearing.
[F05D 2220/768] equipped with permanent magnets -- The code refers to mechanical devices that operate without positive displacement and incorporate permanent magnets in their construction or operation, such as gas-turbine or jet-propulsion components that use magnetic fields for fluid handling or rotor support. The classification falls under indexing of engines and pumps within mechanical engineering.
[F05D 2220/77] of the linear type -- F05D 2220/77 covers linear non-positive-displacement machines and engines, such as linear motors or linear piston devices, that are employed in gas-turbine or jet-propulsion plants. It falls under the mechanical-engineering indexing scheme for engines and pumps, specifically for machines that do not operate on positive displacement.
[F05D 2220/80] in supersonic vehicles excluding hypersonic vehicles or ram, scram or rocket propulsion -- F05D 2220/80 classifies technical aspects of engines used in supersonic vehicles that employ conventional gas-turbine or jet propulsion, deliberately excluding hypersonic, ram-jet, scram-jet and rocket propulsion systems.
[F05D 2220/90] in vehicles adapted for vertical or short take off and landing (v/stol vehicles) -- F05D 2220/90 relates to the application of non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants in vehicles specifically designed for vertical or short-take-off and landing, covering helicopters, tilt-rotor aircraft and other V/STOL craft.
[F05D 2230/00] Manufacture -- The CPC subclass F05D 2230/00 covers manufacturing methods and steps specifically applied to non-positive-displacement machines such as engines, gas-turbines and jet-propulsion plants, including the fabrication of components, assembly techniques and related process technologies. It is part of the indexing scheme for engine-related mechanical engineering inventions.
[F05D 2230/10] by removing material -- This CPC subgroup covers manufacturing processes that form parts of non-positive-displacement machines, such as gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants, by removing material. It includes methods like machining, grinding, milling, laser ablation or other subtractive techniques used to shape engine or pump components.
[F05D 2230/11] by electrochemical methods -- The code F05D 2230/11 refers to manufacturing of non-positive-displacement machines, gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants by electrochemical techniques such as electroplating, electroforming, anodic oxidation or related processes. It covers the use of these chemical-electrical methods to form or treat engine components, turbine parts or propulsion system elements during production.
[F05D 2230/12] by spark erosion methods -- F05D 2230/12 pertains to manufacturing processes for non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants that use spark erosion, also known as electrical discharge machining. The entry is part of the CPC hierarchy under mechanical engineering, indexing schemes for engines or pumps, and the specific scheme for non-positive-displacement engines.
[F05D 2230/13] using lasers -- F05D 2230/13 covers the use of laser technology in the manufacture of non-positive-displacement machines, gas-turbine or jet-propulsion components, including laser cutting, welding, surface treatment or material modification applied during production of these engine-related parts. It is part of the indexing scheme for mechanical engineering processes.
[F05D 2230/14] Micromachining -- F05D 2230/14 covers micromachining techniques used in the manufacture of non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines, jet-propulsion plants and other related engines or pumps. The entry falls under the mechanical-engineering indexing scheme for engines and pumps, specifically focusing on precise, small-scale material removal processes.
[F05D 2230/18] Manufacturing tolerances -- This CPC subclass deals with the definition and control of dimensional and geometric tolerances applied during the manufacturing of non-positive-displacement machines, gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants. It covers methods, criteria and specifications for allowable variation in parts and assemblies to ensure performance and safety.
[F05D 2230/20] essentially without removing material -- F05D 2230/20 designates manufacturing operations on non-positive-displacement machines-such as gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants-where the workpiece is processed without any material being removed. The entry covers procedures like forming, sintering, welding, heat treating, coating or other surface-altering treatments that modify properties but leave the overall mass essentially unchanged.
[F05D 2230/21] by casting -- This CPC subclass F05D 2230/21 covers the manufacturing of non-positive-displacement machines, gas turbines and jet-propulsion plants by casting, meaning the shaping of metal engine components through molten metal solidification. It belongs to the mechanical-engineering hierarchy for indexing schemes related to engines and pumps.
[F05D 2230/211] by precision casting, e.g. microfusing or investment casting -- F05D 2230/211 pertains to the manufacture of non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants using precision casting techniques like micro-fusing or investment casting. The entry falls under the mechanical engineering indexing scheme for engines and pumps.
[F05D 2230/22] by sintering
[F05D 2230/23] by permanently joining parts together -- F05D 2230/23 pertains to manufacturing methods that permanently join parts of non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants, using techniques like welding, brazing, or adhesive bonding. It falls under the mechanical-engineering hierarchy for indexing schemes related to engines, pumps and similar devices.
[F05D 2230/232] by welding -- F05D 2230/232 covers manufacturing steps for non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbine or jet-propulsion engines that involve welding operations. It is an indexing sub-category within the mechanical-engineering class for engines and pumps, focusing on the fabrication of these power-plant components by welding techniques.
[F05D 2230/233] Electron beam welding -- F05D 2230/233 designates manufacturing processes that use electron beam welding to join metal parts, especially in non-positive-displacement machines, gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants. It includes the use of focused electron beams under vacuum, the related equipment, parameters and control methods.
[F05D 2230/234] Laser welding -- The CPC class F05D 2230/234 concerns laser welding methods used in the manufacture of non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants. It includes laser-based joining of engine or pump components, typically by directing a high-energy beam to melt and fuse material without filler metal.
[F05D 2230/235] TIG or MIG welding
[F05D 2230/236] Diffusion bonding -- Diffusion bonding is a solid-state joining technique in which two or more metal or ceramic components are pressed together at elevated temperature, allowing atomic diffusion across the interface without melting. It produces near-net-shape, high-strength joints used in the manufacture of non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbine and jet-propulsion components.
[F05D 2230/237] Brazing -- F05D 2230/237 covers brazing methods used to join parts of non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbine and jet-propulsion engines, focusing on the melting of a filler metal to create a metallurgical bond without melting the base components. It is classified under manufacturing processes for these engine types within the mechanical-engineering CPC hierarchy.
[F05D 2230/238] Soldering -- The CPC entry F05D 2230/238 covers soldering techniques applied in the manufacture or repair of non-positive-displacement machines such as engines, pumps, gas-turbines and jet-propulsion plants, focusing on methods of joining components by melting a filler metal. It pertains to mechanical-engineering processes that involve assembling or maintaining these types of equipment.
[F05D 2230/239] Inertia or friction welding -- The CPC code F05D 2230/239 pertains to inertia or friction welding, a joining technique that uses relative motion and heat generated by friction or kinetic energy to fuse metal parts without melting the base material. It is applied mainly in the manufacture of engines, turbines and other mechanical components where high-strength bonds are required.
[F05D 2230/24] by extrusion -- F05D 2230/24 covers the manufacture of non-positive-displacement machines, such as gas-turbine or jet-propulsion components, by extrusion methods. It belongs to the mechanical engineering indexing scheme for engines, pumps and related devices, specifying extrusion as a production technique for these types of machinery.
[F05D 2230/25] by forging -- F05D 2230/25 designates the forging of components for non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbine, jet-propulsion or other similar engines. The subclass covers forging processes applied to parts like rotors, blades, casings and other structural elements used in these high-speed power units.
[F05D 2230/26] by rolling -- F05D 2230/26 classifies manufacturing steps for non-positive-displacement machines, gas turbines or jet-propulsion plants that employ rolling techniques, such as forming shafts, rotors or casings by passing material through rollers. The entry is part of the indexing scheme for engine-related mechanical engineering processes.
[F05D 2230/30] with deposition of material
[F05D 2230/31] Layer deposition -- F05D 2230/31 refers to manufacturing processes for non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines or jet-propulsion plants that involve layer deposition techniques. The code covers methods like additive manufacturing, coating, or spraying of material layers onto engine components to form functional surfaces or structures.
[F05D 2230/311] by torch or flame spraying -- The code F05D 2230/311 concerns the manufacturing of parts for non-positive-displacement machines, gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants by applying a coating using a torch or flame-spraying process, wherein the material is heated and projected onto the surface to form a protective or functional layer.
[F05D 2230/312] by plasma spraying -- F05D 2230/312 refers to the manufacturing of non-positive-displacement machines, gas turbines or jet-propulsion plants in which a coating is applied by plasma spraying, a process that melts and propels powder particles with a high-temperature plasma jet onto a substrate. The resulting coating can serve protective, thermal-barrier or functional purposes on engine or turbine components.
[F05D 2230/313] by physical vapour deposition -- F05D 2230/313 denotes manufacturing processes for non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines or jet-propulsion plants that employ physical vapour deposition to apply coatings or functional layers to engine components.
[F05D 2230/314] by chemical vapour deposition -- The CPC subclass F05D 2230/314 relates to the manufacture of non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines or jet-propulsion plants using chemical vapour deposition to form components or protective coatings; it is part of the mechanical-engineering indexing scheme for engine and pump technologies.
[F05D 2230/40] Heat treatment -- F05D 2230/40 covers heat-treatment operations used in the manufacture of non-positive-displacement machines, gas turbines or jet-propulsion plants, specifying methods of heating and subsequent cooling of metal components to achieve desired mechanical properties. It falls within the broader indexing scheme for engines and pumps in the mechanical-engineering category of the CPC.
[F05D 2230/41] Hardening -- The CPC subclass F05D 2230/41 covers hardening treatments applied during the manufacture of components for non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbine or jet-propulsion engines. It includes processes that increase surface or bulk hardness of parts like turbine blades, combustor liners or casing sections, typically by heat-treatment, carburizing, nitriding or similar techniques.
[F05D 2230/411] Precipitation hardening -- F05D 2230/411 identifies manufacturing processes that apply precipitation hardening to metal parts of non-positive-displacement machines, gas turbines or jet-propulsion plants. It covers heat-treatment cycles, alloy additions and cooling regimes used to increase strength and wear resistance of engine components.
[F05D 2230/42] by hot isostatic pressing -- F05D 2230/42 designates manufacturing of non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbine or jet-propulsion components by hot isostatic pressing. The process involves applying high temperature and isostatic gas pressure to consolidate powdered metal or ceramics into dense, near-net-shape parts, improving strength and dimensional accuracy.
[F05D 2230/50] Building or constructing in particular ways -- The CPC subclass F05D2230/50 covers methods and arrangements for building or constructing non-positive displacement machines such as gas turbines or jet-propulsion plants in particular ways, focusing on manufacturing aspects. It belongs to the indexing schemes for engines and pumps within mechanical engineering.
[F05D 2230/51] in a modular way, e.g. using several identical or complementary parts or features -- This subclass concerns the manufacture of non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines or jet-propulsion units in a modular fashion, whereby the device is assembled from multiple identical or complementary parts or features. It covers design and production methods that employ repeatable modules to build the overall engine.
[F05D 2230/52] using existing or "off the shelf" parts, e.g. using standardized turbocharger elements -- This subclass addresses manufacturing techniques that employ pre-made or commercially available components-such as standardized turbo-charger elements-in the construction of non-positive-displacement machines, gas turbines or jet-propulsion plants, rather than using custom-designed parts.
[F05D 2230/53] by integrally manufacturing a component, e.g. by milling from a billet or one piece construction -- F05D 2230/53 covers the manufacture of components for non-positive-displacement machines, gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants by integral methods, such as machining a single piece from a billet or using one-piece construction. The focus is on producing the part as a solid unit rather than assembling multiple separate elements.
[F05D 2230/54] by sheet metal manufacturing -- F05D 2230/54 pertains to the manufacture of non-positive-displacement machines, such as gas-turbines or jet-propulsion units, using sheet-metal fabrication techniques. It covers processes like stamping, forming, welding or assembling sheet metal components that form the structural or functional parts of these engines. The subclass groups methods where the primary material is sheet metal rather than castings or forgings.
[F05D 2230/60] Assembly methods -- F05D 2230/60 pertains to methods for assembling non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines and jet-propulsion plants, covering manufacturing steps, sub-assembly techniques and the integration of components within these engines. It falls under mechanical engineering, specifically the indexing scheme for engines and pumps, and focuses on the manufacture of such machines.
[F05D 2230/61] using limited numbers of standard modules which can be adapted by machining -- This subclass relates to manufacturing non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines or jet-propulsion plants by employing a limited set of standard modules that are later adapted through machining to meet specific design requirements. It covers modular construction methods where interchangeable parts are finished by machining to create the final engine or pump.
[F05D 2230/64] using positioning or alignment devices for aligning or centring, e.g. pins -- The entry covers manufacturing steps that use positioning or alignment devices, such as pins, to align or centre components of non-positive-displacement machines, engines, gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants. It is classified under mechanical engineering, indexing schemes for engines or pumps, specifically the manufacturing sub-category.
[F05D 2230/642] using maintaining alignment while permitting differential dilatation -- The CPC subclass F05D 2230/642 covers manufacturing methods for non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants that keep components aligned while allowing differential thermal or mechanical expansion. It indexes designs employing flexible or compliant features to accommodate dilatation without loss of alignment.
[F05D 2230/644] for adjusting the position or the alignment, e.g. wedges or eccenters -- F05D 2230/644 covers mechanisms used to adjust the position or alignment of components in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants, for example using wedges or eccentrics. It falls under the mechanical engineering section dealing with indexing schemes for engines and pumps.
[F05D 2230/68] using auxiliary equipment for lifting or holding -- It relates to manufacturing processes for non-positive-displacement machines, gas turbines or jet-propulsion plants where auxiliary equipment is used to lift or hold parts during assembly, maintenance or servicing. The classification covers lifts, jigs, fixtures and other support devices that aid handling of components.
[F05D 2230/70] Disassembly methods -- F05D 2230/70 covers techniques used to take apart non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines and jet-propulsion engines during manufacturing or maintenance. The classification includes systematic procedures for separating components, removing assemblies, and preparing parts for inspection, repair or recycling.
[F05D 2230/72] Maintenance -- The CPC subclass F05D 2230/72 covers maintenance activities for non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbine and jet-propulsion engines during manufacturing, including procedures, devices or systems used to inspect, service, repair or overhaul these engines to preserve performance and safety.
[F05D 2230/80] Repairing, retrofitting or upgrading methods -- This CPC subclass F05D 2230/80 covers methods for repairing, retrofitting or upgrading non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines and jet-propulsion plants, focusing on manufacturing processes that restore or improve performance.
[F05D 2230/90] Coating -- F05D 2230/90 covers methods and apparatus for applying protective or functional coatings to non-positive-displacement machines such as engines, gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants, focusing on the manufacturing stage of these devices. It includes processes that deposit, adhere or treat surface layers to improve performance, durability or resistance to wear, corrosion or heat.
[F05D 2240/00] Components -- F05D 2240/00 designates the structural parts and accessories of non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines and jet-propulsion units, including rotors, stators, combustors, casings, seals and ancillary fittings. The entry groups together any component that directly contributes to the mechanical integrity or functional operation of these engines without covering the complete machine itself.
[F05D 2240/10] Stators -- F05D 2240/10 covers stators, the stationary components that direct fluid flow or support rotating parts in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines and jet-propulsion plants. It pertains to the design, mounting and arrangement of these fixed blades or vanes within the engine architecture.
[F05D 2240/11] Shroud seal segments -- F05D 2240/11 covers shroud seal segments, which are removable or replaceable sealing elements installed on the shroud of turbines, compressors or jet-propulsion units to limit leakage between rotating and stationary components and maintain aerodynamic efficiency. These segments are typically made of metal, ceramic or composite materials and are designed for non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines and jet-propulsion plants.
[F05D 2240/12] Fluid guiding means, e.g. vanes -- This CPC subclass F05D 2240/12 covers fluid-guiding components such as vanes, guide blades or stator elements used in non-positive-displacement machines, gas-turbines and jet-propulsion plants to direct and shape the working-fluid flow. It is part of the mechanical-engineering indexing scheme for engines and pumps.
[F05D 2240/121] related to the leading edge of a stator vane -- F05D 2240/121 covers features of the leading edge of a stator vane in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants. It includes the geometry, material selection, manufacturing techniques and cooling or protective measures applied specifically to that portion of the vane. This sub-group is part of the indexing scheme for engine-related mechanical engineering inventions.
[F05D 2240/122] related to the trailing edge of a stator vane -- This CPC code covers inventions concerning the trailing edge of a stator vane in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines or jet-propulsion plants. It includes designs, cooling methods, shaping, or aerodynamic features that improve performance, durability, or efficiency of the vane.
[F05D 2240/123] related to the pressure side of a stator vane -- F05D 2240/123 is a subclass in the Cooperative Patent Classification for mechanical engineering that indexes aspects of non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines and jet-propulsion plants. It specifically covers components located on the pressure side of a stator vane, which influence airflow and pressure distribution within the turbine.
[F05D 2240/124] related to the suction side of a stator vane -- F05D 2240/124 classifies features of the suction side of a stator vane in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants, covering the shape, aerodynamic profile, cooling or flow-control elements that influence the incoming airflow to the rotor. It is used to index inventions that modify or improve the suction-side geometry or its interaction with the surrounding flow in these engine types.
[F05D 2240/125] related to the tip of a stator vane -- This CPC subclass covers features concerning the tip region of a stator vane in gas turbines or jet-propulsion plants, such as its geometry, cooling provisions, sealing arrangements and wear protection.
