It is used to index patents that address the design, material selection or manufacturing of stator-vane tips in non-positive-displacement machines.
[F05D 2240/126] Baffles or ribs -- F05D 2240/126 designates baffles or ribs employed in non-positive-displacement machines, including gas turbines and jet-propulsion plants, where the components serve to direct, control, or stabilize fluid flow within the engine or pump assembly.
[F05D 2240/127] Vortex generators, turbulators, or the like, for mixing -- This CPC subgroup covers vortex generators, turbulators and similar devices installed in non-positive-displacement machines, gas turbines or jet-propulsion plants to create controlled vortices or turbulence that enhance fluid or gas mixing, improve combustion, heat transfer or flow uniformity.
[F05D 2240/128] Nozzles -- F05D 2240/128 designates the class of nozzles employed in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines and jet-propulsion plants, covering their design, geometry, flow-control features and integration with engine or pump systems.
[F05D 2240/1281] Plug nozzles -- F05D 2240/1281 - Plug nozzles covers plug-type flow-restricting or thrust-producing devices used in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbine or jet-propulsion engines. The classification applies to components that shape or accelerate gas flow by a centrally mounted plug within a nozzle passage.
[F05D 2240/129] Cascades, i.e. assemblies of similar profiles acting in parallel -- F05D 2240/129 classifies cascades, which are assemblies of identical or similar blade profiles arranged side-by-side to create parallel flow passages. The entry applies to non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbine and jet-propulsion engines, where such cascades are used to direct or compress fluid streams.
[F05D 2240/14] Casings or housings protecting or supporting assemblies within -- F05D 2240/14 covers protective casings or housings that shield or support internal assemblies of non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines, jet-propulsion plants and other similar engines. The classification falls under mechanical engineering, specifically indexing schemes for engines and pumps, and focuses on structural components that safeguard moving parts within these power-generation systems.
[F05D 2240/15] Heat shield -- F05D 2240/15 designates protective heat-shield components for non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines and jet-propulsion plants, covering structures that shield engine parts from high-temperature gases, prevent thermal damage, and maintain aerodynamic or mechanical integrity during operation.
[F05D 2240/20] Rotors -- F05D 2240/20 designates rotors employed in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants. It is part of the mechanical-engineering hierarchy for indexing schemes related to engines or pumps, covering rotor components that generate or transmit rotary motion in these types of machines.
[F05D 2240/24] for turbines -- F05D 2240/24 is a CPC entry that covers turbines used in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines and jet-propulsion plants. It falls under the mechanical-engineering subclass for indexing schemes relating to engines or pumps. The code is used by the EPO and USPTO to classify patents concerning turbine components and their operation.
[F05D 2240/241] of impulse type -- F05D 2240/241 designates impulse-type non-positive-displacement machines or engines, such as impulse turbines used in gas-turbine and jet-propulsion plants. The classification addresses the design, components and operation of these impulse devices within the broader mechanical-engineering indexing scheme.
[F05D 2240/242] of reaction type -- F05D 2240/242 covers components of non-positive-displacement machines-such as gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants-that operate on the reaction principle, where thrust is produced by accelerating the working fluid through the device rather than by mechanical displacement.
[F05D 2240/243] of the Archimedes screw type -- F05D 2240/243 covers machines of the Archimedes-screw type, i.e., helical-screw rotors that convey fluids by axial movement along the screw or convert fluid flow into rotary motion. These devices are used in water lifting, sewage handling, and small hydro-electric generation and are classified under non-positive-displacement engines and pumps.
[F05D 2240/30] Characteristics of rotor blades, i.e. of any element transforming dynamic fluid energy to or from rotational energy and being attached to a rotor -- F05D 2240/30 classifies patents dealing with the design and performance features of rotor blades or similar rotating elements that convert dynamic fluid energy to rotational energy and vice-versa, such as those in gas-turbines, jet-propulsion units and other non-positive-displacement machines. It includes aspects like blade geometry, aerodynamic shaping, materials, cooling, and other characteristics that affect efficiency and operation.
[F05D 2240/301] Cross-sectional characteristics -- F05D 2240/301 refers to the cross-sectional characteristics of components used in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines and jet-propulsion plants, covering dimensions, shapes and profiles of parts that affect fluid flow and mechanical performance. This subclass groups patents that disclose how the geometry of blades, ducts, nozzles or other sections influences efficiency, pressure distribution or vibration in such engines.
[F05D 2240/302] characteristics related to shock waves, transonic or supersonic flow -- F05D 2240/302 covers the characteristics of shock waves and transonic or supersonic flow in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines and jet-propulsion plants, focusing on their aerodynamic and thermodynamic behavior. It is part of the indexing scheme for engines and pumps within the broader mechanical-engineering classification.
[F05D 2240/303] related to the leading edge of a rotor blade -- A classification for non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants that specifically covers features, design considerations or manufacturing steps concerning the leading edge of a rotor blade. It is used to index inventions dealing with aerodynamic shaping, material selection, cooling or wear-resistance of that blade portion.
[F05D 2240/304] related to the trailing edge of a rotor blade -- This sub-group covers inventions concerning the design, shaping, cooling or aerodynamic treatment of the trailing edge of rotor blades in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbine or jet-propulsion engines. It is applied to patents that address trailing-edge serrations, cooling passages or other modifications intended to improve efficiency or reduce noise.
[F05D 2240/305] related to the pressure side of a rotor blade -- F05D 2240/305 is a CPC subclass within the mechanical engineering section that deals with features of the pressure side of rotor blades used in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines and jet-propulsion plants. It covers design, cooling, surface treatment and aerodynamic aspects that affect the high-pressure surface of the blade.
[F05D 2240/306] related to the suction side of a rotor blade -- This group concerns features or measures that affect the suction side of rotor blades in machines such as gas-turbine or jet-propulsion engines and other non-positive-displacement machines. It covers aerodynamic shaping, cooling, surface treatment or control devices intended to influence the low-pressure surface of rotating blades.
[F05D 2240/307] related to the tip of a rotor blade -- F05D 2240/307 classifies inventions that deal specifically with the tip of a rotor blade in turbomachines such as gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants, covering aerodynamic shaping, cooling, protective treatments, tip-clearance control and other related features. It is part of the CPC indexing scheme for non-positive-displacement machines under mechanical engineering.
[F05D 2240/31] with roughened surfaces -- This subclass covers inventions concerning non-positive-displacement machines, gas turbines or jet-propulsion plants that incorporate components with deliberately roughened surfaces, typically to improve heat transfer, lubrication, wear resistance or coating adhesion.
[F05D 2240/35] Combustors or associated equipment -- F05D 2240/35 designates combustors and related equipment used in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines and jet-propulsion plants. It falls within the Mechanical Engineering section of the CPC, under indexing schemes for engines and pumps, specifically the scheme for aspects of non-positive-displacement engines.
[F05D 2240/36] Fuel vaporizer -- F05D 2240/36 designates fuel vaporizers used in non-positive-displacement engines, gas turbines and jet-propulsion plants. The class covers devices that atomise or vaporise liquid fuel before combustion, including vaporisation chambers, heating elements and injection systems designed to produce a combustible gas-phase fuel for these machines.
[F05D 2240/40] Use of a multiplicity of similar components -- F05D 2240/40 relates to the use of a plurality of similar components in non-positive displacement machines such as gas turbines or jet-propulsion plants, covering configurations where several identical or alike parts perform the same function within the engine. It falls under the mechanical engineering indexing scheme for engines and pumps.
[F05D 2240/50] Bearings -- F05D 2240/50 falls under the CPC class for indexing schemes relating to engines or pumps, specifically for non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines or jet-propulsion plants. The subgroup covers bearings used in these machines, including types, arrangements, and lubrication systems designed to support rotating shafts under high speed and temperature conditions.
[F05D 2240/51] Magnetic -- F05D 2240/51 - Magnetic covers inventions that employ magnetic fields or magnetic components to transmit torque, provide bearing support, or otherwise drive or control non-positive-displacement machines, pumps, gas-turbines or jet-propulsion units, such as magnetic couplings and magnetic-bearing arrangements.
[F05D 2240/511] with permanent magnets -- F05D 2240/511 covers components of non-positive displacement machines, gas turbines or jet-propulsion plants that incorporate permanent magnets, such as magnetic bearings, rotor exciters, or integrated magnet-based power-generation units.
[F05D 2240/515] Electromagnetic -- F05D 2240/515 is a CPC subclass in the mechanical-engineering section that indexes electromagnetic aspects of non-positive-displacement machines, including gas turbines and jet-propulsion plants, covering electromagnetic components, control or power-transfer methods used in such engines.
[F05D 2240/52] Axial thrust bearings -- F05D 2240/52 pertains to axial thrust bearings employed in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbine and jet-propulsion engines. The classification covers bearings that bear axial loads on rotating shafts, typically using hydrodynamic or hydrostatic lubrication, and are engineered to sustain high thrust forces in high-speed engine environments.
[F05D 2240/53] Hydrodynamic or hydrostatic bearings -- F05D 2240/53 pertains to hydrodynamic or hydrostatic bearings employed in non-positive-displacement machines such as engines, gas turbines and jet-propulsion plants, where a fluid film supports rotating shafts, providing low friction, high load capacity and precise positioning.
[F05D 2240/54] Radial bearings -- The CPC subclass F05D 2240/54 covers radial bearings used in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines, jet-propulsion plants and other engines. It is part of the mechanical-engineering group that indexes schemes for engines and pumps, focusing on components that support rotating shafts.
[F05D 2240/55] Seals -- F05D 2240/55 covers sealing elements used in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbine or jet-propulsion engines. It includes designs that prevent leakage of fluids or gases between moving and stationary parts, and addresses materials, configurations and mounting methods suited to high-temperature and high-speed environments.
[F05D 2240/56] Brush seals -- Brush seals are sealing devices that employ a dense array of flexible bristles to create a low-friction barrier against fluid leakage in rotating machinery. They are commonly used in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbine and jet-propulsion engines to improve efficiency and control internal pressure differentials. This CPC entry (F05D 2240/56) falls under mechanical engineering for indexing schemes relating to engines and pumps.
[F05D 2240/57] Leaf seals -- F05D 2240/57 - Leaf seals are flexible, thin metal or composite elements positioned in turbine or compressor stages of gas-turbine and jet-propulsion engines to limit flow leakage between rotating and stationary components. Their design reduces aerodynamic losses, improves pressure-ratio and overall engine efficiency, and they are commonly mounted on rotor or stator surfaces as part of sealing systems for non-positive-displacement machines.
[F05D 2240/58] Piston ring seals -- F05D 2240/58 covers piston ring seals, which are annular metal or composite components fitted around a piston to prevent the passage of gases, liquids or combustion products between the cylinder wall and the piston. These seals are used in internal-combustion engines, compressors and other non-positive-displacement machines to maintain pressure and improve efficiency.
[F05D 2240/581] Double or plural piston ring arrangements, i.e. two or more piston rings -- F05D 2240/581 covers piston assemblies that use two or more rings on a single piston, known as double or plural piston-ring arrangements. The code lies within the indexing scheme for non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbine and jet-propulsion engines, linking it to broader mechanical-engineering classifications for engines and pumps.
[F05D 2240/59] Lamellar seals -- F05D 2240/59 covers lamellar seals, which are sealing arrangements formed from overlapping flexible plates that slide over each other to restrict fluid leakage in rotating equipment such as gas-turbines, jet-propulsion units and other non-positive-displacement machines. The code belongs to the mechanical-engineering section of the CPC, under indexing schemes for engines, pumps and related power-plant components.
[F05D 2240/60] Shafts -- F05D 2240/60 classifies shafts used in non-positive-displacement machines such as engines, pumps, gas-turbines and jet-propulsion plants. It covers the design, arrangement and functional features of rotating shafts that transmit power or motion within these mechanical systems. This entry is part of the mechanical-engineering indexing scheme for engines and pumps.
[F05D 2240/61] Hollow -- The CPC code F05D 2240/61 is in the mechanical-engineering section for non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines and jet-propulsion plants and identifies patents that deal with hollow components, including shafts, ducts, casings or other internal-void parts used in those engines.
[F05D 2240/62] Flexible -- F05D 2240/62 designates flexible components employed in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants, covering elements that can bend or deform to meet operational stresses. It falls within the indexing scheme for engines and pumps under mechanical engineering.
[F05D 2240/63] Glands for admission or removal of fluids from shafts -- F05D 2240/63 designates glands that admit or discharge fluids along rotating shafts, providing a seal while allowing fluid flow and is used in engines, pumps, gas-turbine or jet-propulsion plants.
[F05D 2240/70] Slinger plates or washers -- F05D 2240/70 covers slinger plates or washers used in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines and jet-propulsion plants to generate a fluid spray or impart momentum to a working fluid. These components are mounted on rotating shafts to fling liquid or gas outward for cooling, atomisation or thrust augmentation.
[F05D 2240/80] Platforms for stationary or moving blades -- F05D 2240/80 covers platforms that support stationary or rotating blades in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbine and jet-propulsion units. These structures provide mounting, alignment and structural reinforcement for blade rows within the engine or pump, enabling correct aerodynamics and load transfer.
[F05D 2240/81] Cooled platforms -- F05D 2240/81 refers to the cooling of platforms that support rotating components in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines, jet-propulsion units or other similar engines. The classification covers designs and methods for removing heat from these bearing or support structures to maintain performance and prevent overheating.
[F05D 2240/90] Mounting on supporting structures or systems -- The CPC entry F05D 2240/90 covers arrangements for attaching non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines, jet-propulsion units or related engines to supporting structures or systems. It includes brackets, foundations, mounts, vibration-isolating devices and other means of securing the machine to a chassis, frame or building.
[F05D 2240/91] on a stationary structure -- This CPC subgroup indexes inventions that concern the fixed structural elements of non-positive-displacement machines, gas turbines or jet-propulsion plants, such as housings, frames, supports and mounting arrangements that are integral to a stationary installation. It covers the design, arrangement and attachment of these machines to a permanent structure rather than the moving parts of the engine itself.
[F05D 2250/00] Geometry -- The F05D 2250/00 Geometry subclass groups patent material that deals with the shape, dimensions and spatial arrangement of components in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines, jet-propulsion units and related engines. It covers design of casings, rotors, blades and flow passages where geometric features influence performance.
[F05D 2250/10] Two-dimensional -- F05D 2250/10 covers the use of two-dimensional geometry for defining the shape or motion of components in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines and jet-propulsion plants, including the layout of flow passages, blade profiles or other planar design features relevant to engine performance.
[F05D 2250/11] triangular -- F05D 2250/11 designates a geometric subclass covering triangular arrangements of components in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines, jet-propulsion plants and similar engines, where the layout or flow path is defined by a triangular configuration. This falls under the indexing scheme for engines and pumps within mechanical engineering.
[F05D 2250/12] rectangular -- The F05D 2250/12 entry designates patents that address rectangular geometric features of non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines, jet-propulsion plants and related engine components. It covers the design, arrangement or shaping of parts that are rectangular in cross-section or overall form.
[F05D 2250/121] square -- The F05D 2250/121 entry classifies geometric features of square-shaped elements in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines, jet-propulsion plants and related engines, covering design, analysis and dimensional aspects of square geometry within these systems.
[F05D 2250/13] trapezoidal -- The code F05D 2250/13 designates geometric features of trapezoidal shape in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines, jet-propulsion plants and similar engines. It belongs to the mechanical-engineering indexing scheme for engines and pumps, specifically the geometry subclass for these machines.
[F05D 2250/131] polygonal -- This CPC entry (F05D 2250/131) covers geometric aspects of non-positive-displacement machines such as engines, gas turbines or jet-propulsion plants, specifically relating to polygonal shapes. It is placed under mechanical engineering, indexing schemes for engines or pumps, and the geometry subclass.
[F05D 2250/132] hexagonal -- The code F05D 2250/132 designates the use of a hexagonal geometry in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants. It belongs to the indexing scheme for aspects of engines and pumps within the mechanical engineering section of the CPC.
[F05D 2250/14] elliptical -- F05D 2250/14 covers the use of elliptical geometry in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines and jet-propulsion plants, including elliptical rotor or blade shapes and related design features. It is part of the indexing scheme for mechanical engineering topics on engines and pumps.
[F05D 2250/141] circular -- F05D 2250/141 designates circular geometric features in the indexing scheme for non-positive-displacement machines, gas-turbines and jet-propulsion plants. It is applied to components whose shape or arrangement is circular, such as rotors, ducts or housings, within the broader mechanical-engineering class.
[F05D 2250/15] spiral -- F05D 2250/15 "spiral" classifies the geometry of spiral-shaped components used in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines and jet-propulsion plants, covering spirally twisted flow passages, casings or rotors that affect fluid movement.
[F05D 2250/16] parabolic -- This subgroup pertains to the use of a parabolic shape in the geometry of non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants, covering aspects like combustion chambers, nozzles or flow paths that employ a parabolic profile. It is part of the indexing scheme for engines and pumps within mechanical engineering.
[F05D 2250/17] hyperbolic -- F05D 2250/17 designates the hyperbolic geometry subcategory within the CPC index for non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines and jet-propulsion plants. It falls under the mechanical engineering hierarchy, specifically indexing schemes for engines and pumps, and addresses geometric aspects of these devices.
[F05D 2250/18] patterned -- F05D 2250/18 is a CPC subgroup in the mechanical-engineering section that deals with the geometric aspects of patterning in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines and jet-propulsion plants. It is part of an indexing scheme for engines and pumps, focusing on the layout, arrangement or surface patterns that affect performance or manufacturing of these devices.
[F05D 2250/181] ridged -- The F05D 2250/181 "ridged" entry groups patent documents that disclose or claim ridged geometries-raised linear or patterned surfaces-on components of non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines, jet-propulsion plants, or other engines, where the ridges influence fluid flow, heat transfer, or structural characteristics.
[F05D 2250/182] crenellated, notched -- The subclass F05D 2250/182 covers designs for non-positive-displacement machines, such as gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants, in which components feature crenellated or notched geometrical surfaces. These patterned edges are used to modify airflow, pressure distribution or cooling characteristics within the engine.
[F05D 2250/183] zigzag -- The CPC entry F05D 2250/183, titled "zigzag", classifies geometric features of non-positive-displacement machines, gas turbines or jet-propulsion plants that involve a zigzag-shaped flow or structural path, such as serpentine channels, ducts or blade arrangements designed to alter fluid direction or improve mixing. This sub-group falls under mechanical engineering indexing schemes for engines and pumps.
[F05D 2250/184] sinusoidal -- F05D 2250/184 denotes patents that involve sinusoidal geometric features in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants, covering components whose shape follows a sinusoidal curve for blades, flow passages or other structural elements. This subclass is used to index designs where the sinusoidal form influences fluid dynamics or mechanical performance.
[F05D 2250/185] serpentine-like -- F05D 2250/185 designates a serpentine-like geometry applied to non-positive-displacement machines, engines, gas turbines or jet-propulsion plants. It falls under the mechanical-engineering indexing scheme that classifies geometric aspects of these power-producing devices. This entry helps identify components or flow paths shaped in a winding, snake-like form.
[F05D 2250/19] machined -- F05D 2250/19 designates the geometry of machined components in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants. It covers the shape, dimensions, surface finish and related machining details of parts that are produced by cutting, turning, milling or similar processes.
[F05D 2250/191] perforated -- F05D 2250/191 classifies the geometric features of perforated components used in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbine and jet-propulsion plants, covering the shape, size, pattern and arrangement of holes in parts like combustor liners, cooling passages and exhaust ducts. It is part of the indexing scheme for engines and pumps.
[F05D 2250/192] bevelled -- The F05D 2250/192 entry classifies patents that describe non-positive-displacement machines, gas-turbine or jet-propulsion components whose geometry includes bevelled surfaces, edges or angles, such as bevelled shafts, gears, blades or casings. It is used to index inventions where the bevelled shape is a key functional feature.
[F05D 2250/193] milled -- F05D 2250/193 classifies milled components in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas-turbines or jet-propulsion engines, relating to the geometric features of parts produced by milling processes.
[F05D 2250/20] Three-dimensional -- F05D 2250/20 covers three-dimensional geometry-related aspects of non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines and jet-propulsion plants, including spatial layout, volume modelling and dimensional analysis of their components.
[F05D 2250/21] pyramidal -- This CPC entry F05D 2250/21 relates to the geometric features of pyramidal structures in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines or jet-propulsion plants, covering the shape, arrangement or configuration of pyramid-shaped components within the engine or pump system.
[F05D 2250/22] parallelepipedal -- F05D 2250/22 designates devices whose functional or structural elements are configured in a parallelepipedal shape, such as rectangular or box-like chambers, passages or housings used in non-positive-displacement engines, gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants. The entry groups patents that exploit the geometric characteristics of a parallelepiped for improving flow, combustion or mechanical performance.
[F05D 2250/221] cubic -- The CPC entry F05D 2250/221 classifies patent elements that involve cubic geometry in non-positive-displacement machines such as gas turbines and jet-propulsion plants. It is used to index designs where a cubic shape influences the engine or pump structure.
[F05D 2250/23] prismatic -- F05D 2250/23 designates the geometric aspects of prismatic elements in non-positive-displacement machines, including gas-turbine and jet-propulsion plants. It covers the shape, configuration and dimensional relationships of prismatic components as they relate to engine or pump operation.
[F05D 2250/231] cylindrical -- F05D 2250/231 designates the cylindrical geometry of components in non-positive-displacement machines, gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants. It is used to index patents that involve cylindrical rotors, housings or flow passages.
[F05D 2250/232] conical -- A conical geometry refers to a shape that tapers smoothly from a broader base to a narrower tip, forming a cone. In the context of non-positive-displacement machines, gas-turbines or jet-propulsion plants, such geometry may be used for flow-guiding passages, nozzle sections or rotor-blade housings to influence fluid dynamics, pressure distribution and efficiency.
